chapters.title: Chapter 2748: I’Ll Vanquish It Right Away!
chapters.body:
Chapter 2748: I’Ll Vanquish It Right Away!

This wasn't Zu An’s first experience with such a situation. The Ocean races’ Crystal Palace was also a city encapsulated in a transparent light barrier.

However, this place was much deeper than the Ocean races’ Crystal Palace. Perhaps he'd passed out at some point, but he had a vivid impression of that enormous whirlpool. He had to be at least a hundred thousand kilometers deep now.

Even Earth’s deepest ocean trench was only eleven kilometers deep, and that was already a depth that humans struggled to conquer. Meanwhile, he was now tens of thousands of times deeper. Any expert would be crushed to death if the ocean water fell on them. 

Zu An took another look at the transparent barrier above him. He couldn’t imagine the power that would be needed to create such a huge space so deep in the ocean.

Just then, he heard a moan. Only then did he realize that Isabella was still in his arms. She'd grabbed him when the frigate was dragged into the whirlpool.

Zu An felt her trembling body and couldn’t bring himself to abandon her, so he embraced her. He hadn’t expected them to remain together despite having fallen this far down.

Just then, Isabella regained consciousness and realized that her hands and legs were still wrapped around Zu An. Her face reddened, and she mumbled as she unwrapped herself from his body, “B-big brother Zu, t-thank you.”

Zu An didn’t mind it. “Thank your luck instead of me. I’m surprised we’re fine despite having fallen so deep into the ocean.”

Before he passed out, he vaguely sensed a flowing current propping up his body. That could be why they hadn't been smashed to bits.

Just then, he heard groans as more people emerged from the water and slowly regained consciousness. They warily surveyed their surroundings, and they couldn’t help but be amazed by the ocean ceiling above them.

A horrified Alfred frantically looked around. When he spotted Isabella, he anxiously ran over and exclaimed, “Big miss, I can’t be more relieved that you’re safe!”

Isabella eyed Zu An in embarrassment. “Big brother Zu saved me.”

Alfred grabbed Zu An’s hand and shook it vigorously. “You’re truly the savior of our Universal Conglomerate. I don’t know how many times you have saved our big miss now. I’ll report this matter to my superiors. You’ll be the most esteemed friend of our Universal Conglomerate.”

Zu An smiled. “It’s thanks to Isabella’s great fortune that we survived such a huge fall.”

Zu An’s humility only made Alfred more fond of him. Our big miss happens to be of a marriageable age, and our old master is looking for a son-in-law. It’d be perfect if the two of them could get together. It’s a pity he has wives back in his world, but that isn’t a big problem. We’ll accept his current wives as concubines… 

After answering Alfred’s question, Zu An anxiously searched the area. When he finally spotted the person he was looking for standing not too far away, he breathed a sigh of relief. He walked over and asked, “When did you regain consciousness?”

Tingxue was dry despite having been caught inside the whirlpool. Her clothes were artifacts, which had a certain level of resistance against water and fire. But even if they weren’t water-resistant, she could easily dry her clothes just by channeling her ki.

“I woke up slightly earlier than you,” she replied impassively.

“You should have woken me up. Did you find anything?” Zu An asked as he assessed their surroundings.

Tingxue eyed Isabella. “The two of you were hugging so tightly. I didn’t want to get in your way.”

Zu An was speechless. He was surprised as this was Tingxue’s first time showing interest in something unrelated to her.

“We should be on the ocean bed. There are some buildings in the distance. I was thinking of heading over to take a look after you regained consciousness,” Tingxue said. 

Zu An looked over and saw structures that resembled buildings, but they were so far away that he couldn’t be certain.

Meanwhile, more people arrived on shore. 

Yani did a head count. They had lost more than half of the crewmates—given their weak cultivation, it would be hard for them to survive. Most of the guests, except for the unlucky ones who had been on the other side of the frigate, had survived. 

The others also noticed the buildings in the distance and decided to investigate them. 

Yani instructed the remaining crewmates to remain in the vicinity and construct a temporary camp, so they would have a place to retreat to should they encounter trouble ahead. The guests had no qualms with this course of action; they even complimented her meticulousness.

Thus, Zu An and the others began their adventure.

“Uncle Alfred, why is the ocean water not falling? Is it some kind of formation?” Isabella asked.

“This exceeds the scope of formations and technology. The only ones who can pull off such a feat are water-type deities and those who possess ocean control powers like young master Zu.”

Zu An shook his head. “I might be able to create such a pocket temporarily, but this place has clearly been around for ages. I can’t maintain one for that long.”

He could control the ocean with the Crown of the Ocean Deity, but it would also deplete his ki. There was no way he could maintain such a pocket in the long run. 

Their conversation sparked thoughts in the other guests. Chances are that there is some water-type divine artifact here. It’ll be a jackpot if I can obtain it… 

But Yani poured a pail of cold water over them. “There’s another possibility. A universal deity could be behind this.”

The hearts of the guests shuddered. Indeed, a universal deity could easily perform such a feat. They thought about their earlier encounter with Death Envoy Alati. Could this be where the Deity of Death died?

But there was no death energy in the vicinity. 

Despite being under the ocean, their surroundings looked no different from being on land; it was just that the forests were made up not of trees, but fossils of ocean plants. Along the way, they saw a lot of bizarrely-shaped skerries[1] that induced chills in them. 

Isabella instinctively grabbed Zu An’s hand and leaned toward him. This was the only way she could feel any sense of security. Alfred quietly chuckled at that sight while walking behind them.

Tingxue suddenly halted. “Do you hear that?”

Yani replied, “I hear chirping sounds.”

The other guests heightened their wariness. They didn’t dare to let their guard down in this environment, though they didn’t think any monster could pose a threat, given the experts present here. 

A black beetle emitting a golden luster suddenly appeared amid the skerries. It seemed to be examining them curiously.

“It’s just a little bug.” Many guests breathed a sigh of relief. 

Alfred, on the other hand, first narrowed his eyes in uncertainty before extreme fear gripped his heart. “Run! It is a Soul Gnawing Beetle!” As he cried out, he grabbed Isabella and dashed off. 

Due to Isabella grabbing his arm, Zu An was dragged along. 

A brawny guest frowned. “It’s just a measly bug. Watch as I vanquish it!”

He grabbed the bug and torched it with a flame. The fire was so intense that it could have instantly incinerated any beetle.

But a mere instant later, his smile froze. He first noticed his flame diminishing, then felt a sharp pain in his palm. A lump beneath his skin traveled up his arm, and wherever it passed, the corresponding part of his body withered. 

1. small rocky island ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2749: Slashing Out A Nebula
chapters.body:
Chapter 2749: Slashing Out A Nebula

The brawny man screamed. Flames ignited all over his body, leaving no question that he was a fire wielder. He'd been helpless earlier when they were surrounded by ocean water, so he'd thought he ought to reveal a hand here to show his worth. Little had he expected this damned beetle to be this formidable. 

He quickly severed his withered arm, but he was still a step too slow. The beetle had already slipped into his body. 

He anxiously channeled his fire ability to its full extent in hopes that the intense heat would incinerate the beetle, but to his horror, the raging flames he channeled swiftly extinguished. It was as if there was a black hole in his body devouring his flames.

It went without saying that the terrifying beetle was the culprit.

How is this possible? How can my almighty flames fail to incinerate a mere bug?

He would never get an answer to that question. His screams died down, and it came to a point where his withered body could no longer support its weight and fell to the ground. 

“What’s that?!” the old shaman woman exclaimed in horror. 

Alfred grimly replied, “It’s a Soul Gnawing Beetle. It devours all energies, be it ki or mana. That fellow tried to incinerate the Soul Gnawing Beetle, but his flames did nothing but nourish it.”

The others gulped. A being that can devour all energies? Isn’t it invincible then?!

“Does that beetle have no weaknesses?” Isabella asked.

“All beings are bound to have a weakness, but no one knows the Soul Gnawing Beetle’s weakness because no one has ever survived after encountering them.”

More chirping sounds echoed. 

Pah!

The brawny man’s chest suddenly burst open. A black beetle crawled out, followed by a huge army of little beetles. 

The crowd felt chills run down their spine. It reproduced so much within such a short time?

The Soul Gnawing Beetle flapped its wings, producing sharp whistling sounds.

A moment later, similar sounds came from the other skerries. More black beetles emerged from the cracks within the rock, creating a hellish sight for anyone who suffered from trypophobia[1]. Isabella nearly passed out from the sight. 

The other guests dared not dawdle anymore and immediately fled. They regretted not being born with more limbs so that they could flee faster.

The Soul Gnawing Beetles were alarmed by the crowd’s movements and charged over. There were so many beetles that it was impossible to avoid all of them. It didn’t take long for a guest to be rushed down by the beetles.

It was the person who had attempted to scout the way earlier with talisman figures. He hurriedly conjured an army of birds to devour the Soul Gnawing Beetles.

But there were so many beetles that they overran the birds in a mere second. The birds cried in agony as they transformed back into paper talismans and were devoured clean. The talisman user also let out a miserable scream as a wave of Soul Gnawing Beetles washed over him.  

Several guests tried to rescue him. Despite having heard from Alfred that the Soul Gnawing Beetles could devour all energies, they still wanted to give it a try.

The results were disappointing, though. The Soul Gnawing Beetles devoured all the attacks without a hitch. In the meantime, the beetles got to another guest and siphoned her dry.

But their efforts weren't all futile. They discovered that physical attacks worked on the beetles, but the attacks had to involve direct physical contact. Sword ki and the like wouldn’t work.

Unfortunately, there were simply too many Soul Gnawing Beetles, and these beetles were capable of gnawing through weapons as well. Several guests lost their weapons in such a manner and burst into tears.

Zu An was glad he hadn’t whipped out the Tai’e Sword. Otherwise, Mi Li could have lost her home. He'd dealt with enemies with such traits before, but none of them had been as formidable as the Soul Gnawing Beetles.

The guests gathered here were from different worlds, and their attacks were of all kinds and patterns, but the Soul Gnawing Beetles still devoured them with ease.

All of a sudden, the crowd grimly halted their footsteps. The path ahead had been blocked by Soul Gnawing Beetles. In fact, they were completely surrounded. 

Cornered, the guests unleashed all their methods. Isabella even whipped out all sorts of artifacts from her bag, but none of them worked against the Soul Gnawing Beetles.

Zu An’s heart quivered. I do have an idea, but I don’t know if it’ll work.

Faced with the encroaching Soul Gnawing Beetles, Tingxue said, “I’ll deal with them.” She took a step forward.

That provoked the Soul Gnawing Beetles, and they furiously charged at her. 

“Be careful!” Zu An was worried. How could he not be after witnessing other guests being siphoned dry by the Soul Gnawing Beetles?

With a crisp reverberation, Tingxue drew her sword. It sliced through the world, tearing open a void. 

The crowd suddenly felt cold and desolate, as if there was no meaning to life. By the time they snapped out of their trance, the terrifying Soul Gnawing Beetles were nowhere to be seen.

“Where did those beetles go?” Isabella asked in astonishment.

“I have exiled them to another dimension,” Tingxue calmly replied.

The crowd frowned. That didn’t feel like any normal dimension—it resembles the nebula. In the nebula’s voidlike space, the Soul Gnawing Beetles won’t have anything to devour.

The crowd looked at Tingxue in shock. What’s her background? How can she slice open a pathway to the nebula just like that?

Yani clenched her fists. She was tempted to spar with Tingxue, but she dropped that thought, knowing they weren’t out of danger yet.

Tingxue walked at the forefront, and the other guests followed her. There was no reason not to clutch onto that extra thick thigh dangling in front of them.

Isabella remained by Zu An’s side. She whispered, “Big brother Zu, you’re incredible to have befriended someone like that.”

Alfred was speechless. That’s a pair of absurdly rose-tinted glasses you’re wearing, big miss. You’re crediting that to him, too?

“I don’t know if she thinks of me as a friend.” Zu An sighed. She’s even colder than Yan Xuehen.

After the earlier crisis, the guests were exceptionally vigilant. 

Some time later, Tingxue suddenly stopped. Yani frowned as well.

“Haven’t we been here before?”

1. Trypophobia is an aversion to the sight of repetitive patterns of holes or bumps, triggering intense feelings of disgust, fear, or discomfort ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2750: Disappearance
chapters.body:
Chapter 2750: Disappearance

“You’re right. We have been here.”

The guests gathered here were top-notch experts. Despite the difficulty in distinguishing the skerries, they still swiftly spotted the resemblance in their surroundings.

“No wonder these skerries look so weird. It’s a labyrinth.” The guests turned to Zu An, wondering if his key would provide them guidance once more.

Zu An shook his head as he waved the key around. “It’s not providing guidance anymore. Its purpose could be to guide us out of that fog, trigger the whirlpool, and bring us into this space.”

Another guest asked, “Miss Isabella, what about the East Fish?”

Isabella pulled out a fish tank from her bag. The East Fish was still lying belly-up in the water. She felt so embarrassed and awkward that she was tempted to smash the fish tank on the floor.

Yani suggested, “Why don’t we split up and explore the area? Those who reach a dead end can just return here.”

Zu An frowned. “It’s dangerous to split up in a labyrinth.”

He hadn’t watched so many horror movies in his previous life for nothing. It was always the same pattern. As soon as the protagonist’s party split up, the monster lurking in the shadows would tear them apart one by one.

Yani replied, “We can split into groups. With our cultivation, we can look out for one another as long as we don’t stray too far away.”

Isabella spent a moment in thought before pulling out a bunch of communication devices from her bag. “Each group will take one communicator. This will allow us to remain in contact and alert one another of danger right away. These communicators are resistant to interference, and each one has enough energy to last a decade in adverse environments.”

Yani took a communicator. “I’ll go alone and scout the way up front.” She had proven her strength. No one was opposed to her going solo.

Seeing that, Tingxue also said, “Then I’ll scout this path.” She grabbed a communicator and headed down another path.

Zu An wanted to go with Tingxue, but there were many other people remaining who needed to be grouped up. He could only put aside his personal feelings. In the end, he formed a group with Alfred and Isabella.

The other guests also formed groups of their own and began exploring the area. They remained in contact via the communicators, reporting their movements. 

Zu An observed the surroundings along the way. He noticed that the skerries resembled formations, but they weren’t quite complete. Despite his high proficiency in formations, he was still unable to decipher them right away.

Just then, Isabella suddenly remarked, “Hasn’t it been a while since we last heard from big sister Tingxue?”

Zu An was startled. 

Unlike the others, who were afraid of encountering danger and reported in every few steps, the reticent Tingxue only reported in once in a while. He had already gotten used to it and didn’t pay much attention to it anymore. It was only when Isabella pointed that out that it struck him that it had been a long while since he last heard from Tingxue. 

So, he tried calling for Tingxue via the communicator, but the latter didn’t reply. His heart sank. Tingxue might be cold, but she won’t ignore me under such circumstances. Something must have happened to her. 

He quickly headed back and traced Tingxue’s footsteps to find her.

Isabella quickly chimed in, “Don’t worry, big brother Zu. The communicators are able to locate one another.” 

She fiddled with the communicator, and a minimap appeared on screen. It indicated the locations of the other communicators. Two communicators were swiftly approaching them, whereas Tingxue’s remained still.

Zu An’s expression turned even more grim. He continuously used his continuous movements skill until he arrived at the location corresponding to Tingxue’s communicator.

It was a circular clearing surrounded by a wall made up of skerries. There was no way out of here aside from the path he'd entered through. Tingxue’s communicator was lying in front of the wall. 

“What happened?”

“Is Miss Tingxue all right?”

The other groups arrived just then.

Zu An ignored them and headed up to the skerry wall to examine the area. Meanwhile, Isabella filled the others in about the situation.

“How could she disappear without any warning?” A guest was perplexed.

“We didn’t hear anything.”

Yani walked up to Zu An and asked, “Did you find anything?”

Zu An shook his head. “There don’t seem to be any mechanisms here.” So, he drew his sword, intending to blast open the skerry wall in front of him.

Yani stepped forward. “Let me do it instead.”

Having witnessed Yani’s strength, the others had no qualms with letting her do the work. 

Yani took a breath so deep her chest puffed up, then hurled a fist. The force behind her punch appeared to vacuum away all the air in the area. The crowd narrowed their eyes in fear, knowing they couldn’t have survived this punch if it was directed at them. 

But something even more shocking happened—the skerry wall didn’t budge! Instead, Yani’s arm fractured with a crack, and her fist turned bloody. 

Isabella yelped in horror. She reached into her bag and pulled out a few pills. “Big sister Yani, take this!”

“Thank you.” Yani took the pills. Her arm was soon as good as new. She turned to the crowd and explained, “The wall is imbued with a mysterious power of high stature. It’s not limited to just this wall, but all the skerries we passed by along the way.”

None of the guests were fools. Why would they observe the rules of a labyrinth if they could have just smashed their way out of it? They had tried smashing the skerries, but couldn’t break them. 

Zu An frowned. He inspected the other wall surfaces, but he couldn’t gather any clues from them.

Yani walked over and said, “Don’t worry too much. Miss Tingxue is strong. I’m sure she can protect herself.”

The other guests voiced their agreement. Even Isabella and Alfred nodded as well.

Tingxue’s feat of slashing open the nebula had been too astonishing. Even the strongest experts they had met were, at most, on par with her. It was unlikely anyone could corner her, unless she encountered a universal deity.

Zu An finally calmed down. Yes, Tingxue isn’t Yan Xuehen. She’s an expert, even in the vast Myriad Worlds. She might be in a quandary right now, but that doesn’t mean she’s in danger. She’s a Nebula envoy. If it comes to that, she can just open the nebula and leave. 

“We found something, and we believe it will lead us to the exit,” the guest with the golden compass said with a smile. “The way in reveals the way out. When we stepped back and took a second look at the skerries, we noticed their arrangement utilizes 256-bit encryption. We have ideas on how to decrypt it, but we rushed here after hearing your shouts. With more time, I’m sure we can find the way out.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2751: Waking Up
chapters.body:
Chapter 2751: Waking Up

Since Tingxue was nowhere to be found, the crowd decided to leave the labyrinth first before deciding their next step.

Zu An had no choice but to go with them. He left marks along the way, hoping Tingxue could follow his lead and escape once she was freed from her quandary.

Soon, the crowd returned to where they had been. There, they noticed some changes in the skerries compared to before.

One guest lifted his golden compass, and the runes on its surface began to change endlessly. He said, “Such an encryption method might stump most civilizations, but it isn’t a problem for me.”

He had been injured on the frigate, but thanks to Isabella’s pills, he'd recovered swiftly. I embarrassed myself in front of the big miss once. This is my chance to restore my reputation!

His hands swiftly rearranged the floating golden runes so quickly that they left afterimages. The surrounding skerries, influenced by his movements, began moving around. As they shuffled around, a path slowly emerged; afterward, the skerries vanished.

“You’re amazing!” Isabella exclaimed in amazement. 

“It’s my honor to receive Miss Isabella’s admiration.” The guest performed a knight’s bow. Fearing Isabella had forgotten him, he introduced himself once more. “It’s all thanks to Miss Isabella’s medicine. If not for that, I, Divine Calculator, wouldn’t have recovered that quickly otherwise.”

Isabella replied with a sweet smile, “We’re in the same boat now. It’s only right for us to help each other out.”

Disappointment flickered through Divine Calculator’s eyes. He looked at how intimately Isabella was holding Zu An’s arm. She’s treating me no differently from other guests. She favors him more right now because he hogged the limelight earlier, but if I prove myself, I should catch her eye as well.

Thus, Divine Calculator said, “I’ll lead the way. This is my field of specialty.” Without waiting for Isabella’s answer, he strutted forth.

Isabella turned to Zu An and sighed. She had encountered far too many of these situations.

The others wouldn’t object to someone scouting the path for them, though.

Of course, Divine Calculator wasn't a reckless person. He understood the dangers of leading the way, so he fiddled with his compass and scanned the path forward with rays of golden light. He turned back to smile at Isabella. “Big miss, I am known for my prophetic ability. The compass will warn me if there are any dangers ahead.”

Zu An frowned. He suddenly felt uneasy, but Divine Calculator wasn’t reacting in the slightest. Am I overthinking things?

Just then, Divine Calculator suddenly stopped for no reason. He turned around in confusion. “Huh? Is there no more road…”

He looked forward and saw there was still a road ahead. Confusion first flickered through his eyes, then his pupils widened in horror. “What the hell! Why did I stop moving?”

The others were hurriedly stopped in horror. “What’s wrong?”

Divine Calculator was flailing his limbs around even though there was nothing there.

“I feel something gluing…” Before Divine Calculator could finish speaking, he was suddenly dragged into the sky. 

The crowd hurriedly looked up, but the sky above them was pitch-black. They couldn’t see anything at all.

“Be careful!” Yani shouted.

The others also got into battle stances. 

The next second, Divine Calculator reappeared before the crowd.

“What the hell, Divine Calculator?!” a guest roared. 

No one among the crowd had sensed anything amiss earlier, and Divine Calculator had returned safely by himself. It felt as if he was playing a prank on them.

Divine Calculator began walking toward the guest.

“What are you doing? Don’t come here!” the guest shouted in fright. 

There was something weird in the way Divine Calculator walked. It felt unnerving. 

Zu An said, “Be careful. He’s dead.” As the master of the Netherworld, he was particularly attuned to death.

“What?” 

The crowd was startled, as Divine Calculator looked no different from before, and he was still moving normally. 

Just then, Divine Calculator revealed an eerie smile and lurched at the guest.

Luckily, the guest had prepared himself after hearing Zu An’s warning, and knocked Divine Calculator back with a stick. 

But while Divine Calculator was flying backward, he suddenly defied physics and lurched forward once more. His head bent toward his back and his limbs curved unnaturally as he charged the guest at a shocking speed.

Pah!

Yani bashed her fist into Divine Calculator, causing his body to be smashed into many bits.

But the shattered fragments suddenly stitched back together into his ragged form. Despite Divine Calculator's unseemly condition, he relentlessly charged at the guest who had berated him.

The sight left the crowd with goosebumps. Isabella even shrieked in horror.

“Be careful!” Alfred shouted as he shoved Isabella aside.

The next second, however, Alfred disappeared as well.

“Uncle Alfred!” Isabella shouted in horror.

“There’s something above us!” Zu An warned the others with a shout. 

Unfortunately, their divine sense was unable to perceive what was above them.

Isabella hurriedly reached into her bag and pulled out something resembling a torchlight. She flicked it on, and a brilliant light illuminated the area. 

The crowd finally saw what was above them. It was a colorful spider that spanned the size of a football field! The area was covered in transparent spiderwebs that, for some reason, could be seen clearly under Isabella’s light.

Thus, they finally understood why Divine Calculator was behaving in such a bizarre manner. Those transparent threads were controlling him!

The crowd saw Alfred with swollen muscles like the Hulk, looking nothing like the sickly old man he was a second ago. He was fighting desperately to prevent the threads from wrapping around him, but despite his best efforts, he could only slow the process. He was unable to save himself.

Isabella quickly reached into her bag, pulled out a pistol, and fired at the spiderweb.

The pistol was small, but the bullet it fired emanated a phoenix’s cry as a gigantic fiery bird lurched at the spider. The webs it touched along the way melted.

The other guests were stunned. The sheer power of the pistol was comparable to the forbidden fire spells of magic worlds. 

“DON’T!” Yani shouted, but it was too late.

Pah!

The fiery bird crashed into the spider, raising a fiery inferno. But it was swiftly extinguished without leaving the slightest singe on the spider’s fur. 

The spider opened dozens of eyes all along its body, and terrifying pressure crashed down from above. 

Everyone shuddered in fear. It felt as if a universal deity had descended before them. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2752: Finding A Way Out Of Danger
chapters.body:
Chapter 2752: Finding A Way Out Of Danger

When they saw the spider above them, the crowd was initially startled, but they weren’t too frightened. The guests who had joined this expedition were experts from the Myriad Worlds, wielding skills surpassing their peers. They had encountered all kinds of monsters before. 

It was creepy to see Divine Calculator being controlled by the spiderwebs, but it wasn't beyond what they had already seen before. Besides, the spider had caught Divine Calculator off guard, so it wasn't too surprising for its ambush to have succeeded.

It was only when Isabella fired that bullet at the spider that the crowd realized they had underestimated their enemy. It turned out that the spider had been asleep, and it had killed Divine Calculator out of sheer instinct. 

Now that it was awake, the pressure it emanated was cataclysmic. 

These guests would still consider putting up a fight against the ex-Death Envoy Alati, but they couldn’t muster the courage to fight this spider. Just looking at it made their souls tremble. Thus, those who remained turned tail and fled. No matter how powerful the Universal Conglomerate and the Acadias were, those groups couldn’t be more important than their lives. 

Their abrupt movements alarmed the spider, however. They were quickly bound by threads, and the more they struggled, the tighter the threads became. They screamed in horror, “Save me!”

But no one had the spare capacity to save anyone right now. 

Zu An was desperately trying to avoid the transparent threads flying around. If not for the predictions from the Book, he would have been caught by spider threads by now.

Isabella wanted to pull out other weapons from her bag, but the spider above had already recognized her as the one who had fired at it earlier. Its chelicerae[1] fell down upon her from the sky. In that instant, she looked like an ant facing a giant’s fist. She froze in place, gripped by extreme fear that rendered her incapable of reacting. 

Zu An used the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra to pull her over to his side, but the chelicerae followed her as if they had already locked onto her. At this moment, he understood why Isabella had stopped moving earlier. It felt as if the entire world was crushing him, sealing off his vitality, stamina, blood, and heartbeat. The only thing that could function was his mind.

But such a state would only plunge one further into despair.

Just then, Yani hurled her fist at the chelicerae with a roar. It was a fist that could tear even space, but the chelicerae only shook a little.

Fortunately, this disturbance was enough for Zu An to regain his mobility. He quickly retreated with Isabella via instantaneous movement.

Yani hurled her fist once more to strike the spider directly, but her arm got caught in the transparent webs instead. She was temporarily stuck in place, during which the spider wrapped her limbs with more spider threads. Soon, she couldn’t budge anymore. 

“Brother, please escape with our big miss!” Alfred struggled with all his might, but he couldn’t break free of his restraints. He was wrapped in a thick cocoon and shouted his final words before being fully sealed.

The other guests were already wrapped so tightly that they couldn’t let out any noise.

“Uncle Alfred!” Tears streamed down Isabella’s face. She'd thought that given their strength and the treasures they had on hand, this trip would just be another interesting experience. She hadn't thought they would encounter such a powerful monster that would cost Alfred his life. 

Zu An grabbed her hand and fled. Isabella exclaimed, “Big sister Yani is still there…”

Zu An turned around and saw Yani struggling in the spiderweb. “We can’t save her now. If we don’t run now, all of us will die.”

Isabella bit her lip, knowing they couldn’t do aynthing to save the others. She whipped out a few items from her bag to construct layers of barriers against the spider.

But the spider’s chelicerae continued to reach directly toward her. Isabella’s array of defensive artifacts were top-notch, but the chelicerae pierced through them like hot knife slicing through butter.

Isabella felt the same despair she'd experienced earlier. Her body froze in place. She desperately wanted to escape but was unable to do so. She couldn’t even close her eyes out of fear.

However, Zu An was still able to move. 

A beautiful river reflecting countless civilizations suddenly appeared behind them, standing in the way of the chelicerae thrusting toward them. The chelicerae struck the river, but were unable to pierce through it. A light, fleeting hum echoed—it didn’t sound human.

Zu An made use of this opportunity to escape with Isabella.

The spider was infuriated, but its chelicerae were stuck in that bizarre river. It could only roar angrily. Just then, however, the hair on its chelicerae fell onto the ground and transformed into countless smaller spiders that frenziedly chased the duo.

“Big brother Zu, what’s that ability? How did you stop that monster?” Isabella was awed.

Zu An was surprised as well. That had been the Annals of History, one of the two abilities he had received from Deity of Memory Meng not too long ago. It allowed him to use the river of history to block an enemy’s fatal attack. If not for that, they would have been in danger. 

He was about to answer that question when Isabella suddenly yelped. Her torchlight had revealed countless small spiders chasing after them. Thus, Zu An quickly lifted Isabella up and escaped via Rainbow Morph.

But somehow, the small spiders persistently followed him no matter how he flitted around. He couldn’t shake them off no matter how he tried.

Just then, a voice echoed from the shadows, “Head in my direction!”

Hearing a feminine voice in an empty, dark space sent shivers down Isabella’s spine. Is it a ghost? But on second thought, the spider was far more terrifying than any ghost. 

Even so, Zu An didn’t hesitate to run toward the voice. 

An arm suddenly emerged from the darkness and dragged him in. The shadows rippled as if they were passing through a spatial passageway. After they passed through, the ripples vanished, and the shadow melded into the surrounding darkness. 

Dozens of spiders scuttled over and combed the surroundings, but they could no longer sense their target’s aura. 

Meanwhile, Zu An and Isabella found themselves in a cave that had a high ceiling over fifty meters high. They sensed a presence with them and switched on the torchlight.

The third party stared intently at Isabella and Zu An’s linked arms and remarked, “You’re a heartless man. It hasn’t even been that long, but you've already gotten together with another woman.”

Isabella hurriedly retracted her hand, and her face flushed red. 

Zu An stared at the other party and huffed, “I need an explanation.”

The person before him was none other than Firework.

—

Translator Thought:

The Book (The Book of Prophecies) existed in history, too. 

Lu Sheng was an official tasked by Qin Shihuang (Ying Zheng) to find the elixir of immortality, but he failed. So, he created the Book of Prophecies and presented it to Qin Shihuang, saying that it had come from beyond the seas and possessed mystical powers.

1. The mouthparts of a spider. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2753: The Truth
chapters.body:
Chapter 2753: The Truth

Contrasting with Zu An’s grim expression was Firework’s beaming smile. “Shouldn’t you look happier? We've finally reunited after such a long time!”

Zu An coldly uttered, “Why should I be happy? Do you know how many people have died trying to save you?”

“Those people harbored ulterior motives. They died because they weren’t strong enough.” Firework was unfazed. 

Zu An frowned. Even though those guests did harbor ulterior motives, her words were still displeasing to hear. 

Isabella got angry and roared, “Uncle Alfred didn’t harbor ulterior motives, but he met with danger trying to save you. How can you utter such cold words!”

She rarely yelled at others, but she was truly angry this time. 

Firework realized she had gone overboard with her joke, so she quickly said, “Calm down, Isabella. Your old butler might not be dead yet.”

“What do you mean?” Isabella wiped her tears as she asked in confusion.

“The spider might have bound them, but it won’t kill them just yet,” Firework replied. 

“How do you know?” Isabella recalled the earlier sight of Divine Calculator being slaughtered by the monster, and that made her face turn pale. 

“Because you escaped. The monster has plenty of reasons to keep them alive as hostages,” Firework explained. 

“Would that bloodthirsty monster have such considerations?” Isabella hoped for that to be the case, but she rationally doubted that possibility. 

“Big miss, do you think a being that powerful would have no intelligence?” Firework sighed. 

Isabella widened her eyes in realization. That spider was the strongest monster she had ever seen. An entity of this level would be a powerhouse even in the Myriad Worlds. There was no way it would lack intelligence. 

Firework turned to Zu An and said, “Big brother Zu, I'm touched. You took such a huge risk to save me.”

Hearing her coy words, Zu An retracted his hand and asked, “Are you in cahoots with that sea monster?”

He hadn’t suspected anything initially, but once he calmed down, he realized there were many dubious points about this incident. 

In the first place, it didn’t make sense for such a huge sea monster to approach the beach without alarming any of Dreamland’s experts. It was also weird for the sea monster to hold someone hostage. Furthermore, Firework was no weakling, so for her to have been captured so easily… 

There were many anomalies regarding this issue, and he had already thought them through along the way. But considering their prior interactions, he didn’t think Firework would attempt to harm him, so he'd decided to play along and see how the situation unfolded. 

He hadn’t expected the journey to be so fraught with dangers. 

“The sea monster is indeed my friend. We staged this show to lure you here. Otherwise, you would have turned into a zombie at the Dream Gathering by now.” Firework harrumphed.

“What?” Isabella was taken aback. “Are you saying the Dream Gathering is a scheme?” 

“Of course. It’s a play staged to reap the attendees’ souls. Isn’t it laughable how many experts across the Myriad Worlds are aware of its dangers, but they're unable to rein in their greed?” Firework sneered. 

Isabella hurriedly turned to Zu An with astonished eyes, only to see him composed, as if he had guessed as much.

“That can’t be right. Dreamland hosts the Dream Gathering once every millennium. It’s one of the most famous gatherings in the Myriad Worlds. How could other powers remain oblivious if there is a problem with it?” Isabella was confused. 

“The previous Dream Gatherings were the real deal, but the same cannot be said about those hosted in the recent millennium. Do you know who I am?” Firework asked.

Isabella glanced at Zu An, knowing the two were friends. 

But Zu An had only met Firework on the spaceship. He had no way of knowing if the information she'd shared with him was correct or not.

“Let’s see. I’m guessing someone from Dreamland told you that they would be holding their big miss’ coming-of-age ceremony soon, and the Dream Gathering would be held after,” Firework said self-deprecatingly.

A thought popped into Zu An and Isabella’s minds. “You're Dreamland’s big miss?”

“That’s right.” Firework smiled bitterly. “But to the current Dreamland, that is of no significance…”

“If you’re Dreamland’s big miss, who was the person in the hotel?” Isabella found the situation absurd.

“It’s a fake, of course!” Firework sneered. “Everyone you see in Dreamland is an impersonator!”

“Does big sister Yani know about it, too? She saved us many times during our journey here. She’s the guard captain. I don’t think she’d turn a blind eye to the situation,” Isabella asked. 

“Yani?” Firework scoffed. She glanced at Zu An. “I bet he already knows what’s going on. Someone like him wouldn’t leave a beauty in the lurch and escape alone.”

Isabella looked at Zu An with confused eyes.

Zu An said, “There is something wrong with Yani.”

After he received the Eye of Truth from the Deity of Memory Meng, he'd returned to the frigate and scanned Yani and all the crew members. To his astonishment, all of them were fakes. But he hadn't known what Yani’s motives were, so he'd chosen to hold his tongue.

But he still didn’t trust Firework yet. The latter hadn’t told them everything yet. “You said you’re Dreamland’s first miss, but Dreamland started meeting with problems a thousand years ago, yet you’re only having your coming-of-age ceremony now. There’s a discrepancy in your age.”

Firework looked at him with an unreadable smile. “How old do you think I am?”

Zu An assessed her. She looks at most sixteen or seventeen.

Firework finally answered the question. “Our Dream Clan has existed since time immemorial. We have been around since before the ascension of the universal deities. There are several races like ours in the Myriad Worlds.”

Isabella nodded. As the Acadias’ first miss, she had heard about such races.

Firework looked at Zu An with a mischievous smile. “Unlike short-lived humans like you, our race enjoys a long lifespan. We barely reach adulthood at a thousand years of age.”

Zu An was speechless. You’re even older than my grandmother’s grandmother!

“What happened to Dreamland?”

“My mother died an early death. My father doted on me and refused to remarry, until he suddenly met that woman a thousand years ago. She's beautiful and knows how to charm a person’s heart. My father hasn't been the same since meeting her. He no longer spends time with me and spends his day fawning over that woman.” Firework recalled the past. 

“At the start, I was happy for my father, thinking he'd been alone for too many years. Had I known that woman was a venomous scorpion, I would have stopped them.” She gritted her teeth.

“Ever since that woman became Dreamland’s matriarch, bizarre things began happening. Old subordinates loyal to my father suddenly vanished, but they would return shortly after. No one paid it any heed at the start, but thinking back, they must have already become her puppets.”

Isabella asked, “Who’s that woman?”

“You know her, and you have become familiar with her in recent days,” Firework sneered.

A person popped into their minds, and the thought left Zu An and Isabella with goosebumps.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2754: Life Creation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2754: Life Creation

“Yani!” Isabella thought about the woman who could shatter space. Even though she was beautiful, it was hard to imagine her as a homewrecker. 

Zu An was perplexed as well. He hadn't thought Yani would be into such things.

Firework shook her head. “She approached my father as Diodora.”

Both Zu An and Isabella thought about the seductive female elder who'd appeared at the hotel. Ah, that fits the impression better. 

“Hold on.” Isabella thought of a problem. “You said it was someone we have become familiar with recently. Only Yani accompanied us on this voyage.”

“That’s because Diodora and Yani are the same person,” Firework replied with a sigh.

Isabella was stunned. She couldn’t see those two completely different women as the same person. “How is that possible? They appeared in the same place simultaneously, and Yani came out to the ocean with us while Diodora remained in the hotel to host the other guests. If they are the same person, how can she pull that off?”

“That’s her special ability, Life Creation. She's capable of conjuring countless clones. In fact, everyone you've seen in Dreamland, except for the outsiders, is a puppet she conjured.”

The thought that all the staff members she'd met were just clones made shivers run down Isabella’s spine. “I can’t believe no one has been able to see through her tricks. Isn’t her ability too powerful?”

The guests visiting Dreamland were experts from different civilizations. One couldn’t hope to deceive them all with just ordinary clones. Yet not a single person had seen through her act.

Zu An asked, "Isn't Life Creation an ability rivaling that of the universal deities?”

Destruction was far easier than creation. The creation of life would normally be an ability in the domain of universal deities. 

Firework shook her head. “The lives created by universal deities are sentient beings who are capable of growth, perhaps to the extent of becoming universal deities themselves in the future. In contrast, the lives created by Yani are superficial. They aren’t true living beings. Their souls and wills are merely extensions of her own.”

Zu An had met many people in Dreamland thus far, but it had never occurred to him that they were all clones. To play so many characters at once… Isn’t she afraid of developing dissociative personality disorder? 

“If she's already replaced everyone in Dreamland, how did you escape from her?” Zu An stared at Firework intently. 

“I wasn’t in Dreamland for the last thousand years. My father had sent me away, perhaps because he was worried that I’d get in the way of him and the demoness,” Firework said with a sigh. “It happened that a Beauty worshiper thought I conformed to the dao of beauty and had been inviting me to study with her. My father took this chance to send me to the Deity of Beauty’s academy. That move unexpectedly saved my life.”

“How did you know about what happened in Dreamland?” Isabella asked.

“None of the participants who attended the Dream Gathering a thousand years ago left. We've had participants who relished in the event and were unwilling to leave, but never had all the participants been unwilling to leave before,” Firework said. She continued with a melancholic look, “Several people in the academy asked me about it, but I was confused too. It occurred to me that it had been a long time since my father contacted me, so I tried contacting him through a projection artifact. He acted the same as before, but I knew it wasn’t him.”

Zu An nodded. No matter how good someone’s disguise was, they couldn’t hope to fool their closest kin. He had tried impersonating others, but he had always struggled to fool those who were close with the impersonated.

“Perhaps noticing my skepticism, she sent assassins into the Deity of Beauty’s academy to kill me. Fortunately, perhaps we were too far from Dreamland, or she was afraid of the experts in the academy, so the assassins weren’t too strong. I managed to escape alive. However, I didn’t dare to harbor hope since. I left the Deity of Beauty’s academy to investigate what happened to my homeland.” Firework looked fearful. “I never expected her to wield such a strong ability. I barely escaped with my life many times…”

Zu An could imagine how dangerous the situation had been. 

“I've tried verifying it many times over the years, but my final conclusion is that everyone in Dreamland is a puppet.” Firework had always been all smiles, but she couldn’t bring herself to smile at this moment.

“What do you intend to do? Aren’t you leaping straight into danger now?” Zu An asked. 

“I planned to use the Dream Gathering to scout my father’s whereabouts. The staff members she conjured might be her clones, but not all of them are linked to her central consciousness. With my aura concealment artifacts, as long as I'm not spotted by the more important figures in Dreamland, she shouldn’t notice me,” Firework explained. “But I don’t have the power to exact vengeance alone, so I was intending to borrow the strength of the experts from the Myriad Worlds. But I didn’t expect the Dream Gathering to be held so soon. There was no time to convince the other guests of the truth, so I could only pretend to be captured by a sea monster to lure you all here, lest you be harmed by the Dream Gathering."

Zu An frowned. “What if I hadn’t taken the risk to save you?”

“You’d have died in vain if you were that heartless.” Firework scoffed. She hopped over and grabbed Zu An’s arm. “I knew I didn’t judge you wrongly. You're a true friend. The same goes for big sister Tingxue, too. She might appear cold on the surface, but she still willingly came all the way here to save me.”

Isabella felt stifled, especially when she saw Firework grabbing Zu An’s arm. It feels as if I've been toyed with.

“Unfortunately, Tingxue went missing. I don’t know how she’s faring now.” Zu An was worried.

“That woman was intimidated by big sister Tingxue’s strength, so I had to separate big sister Tingxue from the rest of the group. Don’t worry. Considering her strength, big sister Tingxue has to be safe. It’s only a matter of time before she breaks free,” Firework consoled him.

Zu An was relieved to hear that Tingxue was safe. “So, what will happen at the Dream Gathering? Why will all the attendees die?”

Firework sighed. “We, the Dream Clan, have the unique ability of dreamweaving. We can weave beautiful dreams that our guests relish. Many people refuse to wake up from them of their own volition. Since that demoness took control of Dreamland, she's been exploiting this opportunity to devour our guests’ souls, causing our guests to die amid their dreams.” 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2755: Main Body
chapters.body:
Chapter 2755: Main Body

“She devours their souls?” Isabella’s face paled in fright. 

Zu An was disappointed. “Your Dream Clan’s ability is to weave dreams? So, it’s all fake?”

He'd come here to find ways to help Chu Chuyan and Mi Li, as well as to see if there was a way he could reunite with the ancient Mermaid Queen and the Goddess of Wu Mountain. If the Dream Clan was only capable of weaving dreams, that wouldn’t solve his problems. What was the point of only finding solutions in his dreams?

Firework indignantly retorted, “Dreamweaving isn’t meaningless. It comforts wounded souls and brings peace and solace to those who have been through too much. Our dreams also guide one toward the future they seek.”

Zu An narrowed his eyes. “Your dreams can guide one toward a certain future?”

At this point, Isabella interjected, “Big brother Zu, I have heard about their ability and even asked my father about it. Their ability is indeed extraordinary. It is on par with powerful divination methods. They first ascertain the outcome before influencing causality toward it.” 

Fearing her explanation was too vague, she listed an example, “If the Dream Clan declares that someone is going to die, that person’s enemies might be compelled to do everything they can to kill them. Or if the clan declares that someone is going to live, even if that person is terminally ill, they might encounter all kinds of fortuitous encounters that will eventually save their life.”

“How mysterious…” Zu An wasn't unfamiliar with such divination methods. His ‘Keyboard Come’ turned his declarations into reality. He asked the crucial question, “Can you fulfill all dreams?” 

“Of course not,” Firework replied bitterly. “That demoness would have been dead by now if it were that easy. We can only guide a person’s dream, and the process will consume one’s karma. The greater the wish, the more karma is required. Furthermore, if one fails to fulfill the dream, one will suffer a severe backlash.”

Zu An nodded. It was the same for ‘Keyboard Come’; it was just that he hadn’t figured out what the standard for quantification was.

“What is karma?” he asked. He wondered if ‘Keyboard Come’ depleted a similar resource. If he could make sense of this, he could better control ‘Keyboard Come’ and not risk disintegration each time. 

Firework explained, “Many believe that karma is about doing good and accruing virtue, but heaven is dispassionate. In truth, kindness may carry no karma, whereas committing evil deeds like going on a massacre may result in a lot of karma. Karma is generated based on whether one's deeds are beneficial to the world.

“If an Annihilation worshiper goes on a massacre on an overpopulated and overdeveloped planet, this might seem like an evil deed, but from the world’s point of view, it is considered a good deed.”

Zu An fell into deep thought. These words had opened his eyes, as he had never considered things from that perspective. It reminded him of the concept of ‘heaven’s will’, which the people of the Cultivation World often talked about. It wasn't necessarily tangible, but it most definitely existed. 

If a world can possess a will, how can an entire universe not possess a will, too? Perhaps universal deities might even be a manifestation of some will.

Isabella asked, “Have you gathered any news about your father yet?”

“Through my skills and years of investigation, I have gathered some news. My father should be alive. That demoness likely has him imprisoned somewhere. I wanted to lure big brother Zu and big sister Tingxue here to help with the investigation, but I never expected to draw the demoness here as well,” Firework said with a sigh.

“It’s a good thing she was captured by the spider. You don’t have to worry about her anymore,” Isabella consoled her, though that also reminded her about Alfred’s capture and her expression darkened. 

“That demoness wouldn’t die that easily. The spider merely captured a clone. It won’t affect her main body.” Firework harrumphed.

“Where’s her main body?” Isabella was confused. “Do we have to kill all the staff members in Dreamland to kill her for good?”

Firework shook her head. “I don’t know where her main body is either. I've been investigating her for many years now, but I was unable to trace her background or the origin of her abilities.”

Zu An suddenly spoke up. “I know where her main body is.”

“You do?” Firework was perplexed. She had investigated this matter for many years, but to no avail, yet Zu An had uncovered it within days of arriving at Dreamland?

Faced with the two women’s curious gazes, Zu An looked in the direction they had come from and said, “Her main body is right here.”

“Right here?” Firework’s hair stood on end. She glanced at Isabella, wondering if the latter had been controlled by the demoness as well. 

Isabella also stared at Firework in horror. She instinctively stuck closer to Zu An. 

Taking in their reactions, Zu An laughed. “It’s not what you’re thinking. Yani is that spider outside.”

“What?!” The two women exclaimed in horror, especially Firework. She had seen Yani fighting valiantly against the spider earlier, so she struggled to come to terms with the idea that they were the same entity.

“I have an ability that allows me to see through all facades. From the first time I met Yani, she was already a demon spider.” 

After Zu An obtained the Eye of Truth, he'd inspected everyone on board. That was why he knew something was amiss with Yani and the crew members. He'd seen a spider silhouette behind Yani, but didn’t understand what it meant at first. It was only after seeing the terrifying demon spider that he'd made sense of the situation. 

“Big brother Zu, how do you have so many special abilities?” Isabella was amazed.

She had met many experts who were far stronger than Zu An, but none of them had as many methods as him. Perhaps he appeared weak, but he wouldn't necessarily lose to those experts in an all-out fight!

“Spider. Puppets. Control…” Firework looked shaken up. “You’re right. Why did I never think of that? But why is she doing all of this when she’s already this powerful?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2756: Sandwiched
chapters.body:
Chapter 2756: Sandwiched

Zu An replied, “Even powerhouses have their own problems. She might be strong, but she’s bound to have enemies who are as strong as her, not to mention the universal deities.”

The two women agreed. Their information was limited. There was no point speculating about the demoness’ motive.

“Why don’t we rescue my father first? We’ll stand a better chance against the demoness with his strength and knowledge,” Firework said with a hopeful gleam in her eyes.

“Have you found your father’s whereabouts?” Zu An agreed that her father, as Dreamland’s master, would have a better idea as to how they could quell the demoness. 

“I don’t have a concrete location yet, but I have some clues,” Firework replied. 

“Alright. Let’s try rescuing your father first.” Zu An knew there was no other solution at hand. That demon spider transcended the concept of strong and weak. There was no way they could defeat it, so their only option was to find other solutions.

Isabella meekly asked, “Will you help me save Uncle Alfred after we rescue your father?”

“That goes without saying!” Firework exclaimed. “We’re in the same boat now. We’ll definitely save Uncle Alfred after rescuing my father.”

Firework’s guarantee brought Isabella relief. Isabella perked herself up and said, “Alright, let’s go rescue your father!”

Firework led the two of them down a convoluted path, until they finally arrived before a door. She peeked outside the door and cursed, “Damn. There are many small spiders outside.”

Zu An took a look outside. There were indeed many little spiders scuttling around, searching for their whereabouts. 

“These little spiders don’t seem too strong. Why don’t we…”

Before Isabella could finish her sentence, Firework interjected, “That won’t do. Their consciousness is linked to the demon spider. If we attack the small spiders, the bigger spider will immediately be alerted to our location. That’ll spell the end of us.”

“Are there any other paths?”

“There’s no way to go around this path.”

“I have some concealment artifacts.”

“That won’t work. These spiders don’t rely on vision. Normal concealment artifacts won’t conceal our presence from that demoness.” 

Isabella looked defeated. “I guess we can only wait for the small spiders to leave on their own accord.”

But the three of them knew that was unlikely to happen.

“I have an idea,” Zu An said. 

Isabella exclaimed in delight, “You’re incredible, big brother Zu.”

Even Firework was amazed. How is this fellow so resourceful?

Zu An spent a moment in thought before asking, “Isabella, do you have artifacts that can completely remove light from the surroundings?” It’s a pity Manman isn’t here, or else I could have used Fu Hao’s owl statue[1].

“I have something like that, but didn’t big sister Firework mention earlier that the little spiders don’t rely on vision?” Isabella pulled out a black jewel from her bag. Their surroundings were immediately plunged into darkness.

Zu An was overjoyed. Isabella’s bag is truly like Doraemon’s pouch. It has everything inside!

“I don’t know if it’s enough to fool the demon spider. Let me give it a try first.”

He had the ability to meld into the darkness, such that no one could find him in a dark environment, but the demon spider was so strong that he wasn’t confident he could fool the other party with it.

Firework and Isabella exclaimed, “Be careful!”

Even so, they understood that they were in dire straits, and they had no choice but to take risks. Isabella handed the black jewel to Zu An and was shocked to see him disappear right before her eyes. 

The two women exchanged delighted looks. There might be a chance.

Moments later, Zu An returned and stowed away the black jewel, thus revealing himself. He said with a voice tinged with excitement, “I just gave it a try. The spiders couldn’t see me.”

It was already dark outside, so the spiders hadn’t noticed anything amiss even when he used the dark jewel.

“How did you do it?” Firework asked excitedly.

“I have an ability that allows me to meld into darkness.”

“How many more abilities do you have that we don’t know about?”

An ability that could deceive the demon spider’s senses had to be of the highest caliber. This man carried many secrets, and that made him fatally charming to them. 

“But what about us?” Isabella asked with a pitiful frown. “We don’t have such an ability.”

Zu An pulled out a cloak from his Brilliant Glass Bead and said, “Don’t worry, I’ll take you out. It might be a bit uncomfortable, but bear with it.”

He had once successfully carried Shang Liuyu with him using this method.

He had also considered putting an item far away and taking the two women out of here using the position swap skill, but he'd decided against that. He had to extend his divine sense to use his position swap, and that could alert the demon spider to their presence. 

Firework excitedly climbed onto Zu An’s back and said, “This looks fun. Isabella, you can take the front.”

“All right.” Isabella’s face reddened, and she bit her lip. She knew this wasn’t the time to be kicking up a fuss, so she slipped into Zu An’s arms. His warmth made her face redden further, and her heart couldn’t stop beating. 

Zu An took in the fragrances of both women, and it stirred something in his heart. Even the coolest man would struggle to remain calm when he was sandwiched between two youthful and vibrant ladies.

He had planned to carry both women in front, but Firework had climbed onto his back and was hugging him like a sloth. He decided to let her do whatever she wanted. He took in a deep breath and told himself not to focus on the amazing buoyancy pressing against him. 

“Don’t make any noise later on.”

“Orh~” the two women replied in unison.

Zu An took out the black jewel and plunged the surroundings into darkness. He melded into the darkness and walked out. 

He had to proceed slowly because the area was filled with little spiders. Perhaps they wouldn't be able to sense him, but if he touched them carelessly, they would notice him right away. Furthermore, there were invisible spider threads hanging along the corridor. Touching them would alert the demon spider as well. 

Luckily, he had a strong constitution, and he'd scouted the traps earlier. Otherwise, it would have been a struggle to carry these two women along while making precise movements.

He suddenly felt an itch from behind. Firework was writing on his back, “You have an amazing waist.”

He'd had to bend his body at a gravity-defying angle earlier to avoid a dense congregation of spiderwebs.

Zu An was speechless. This lass sure is something to joke under such circumstances. Luckily, we’re almost out of this corridor.

Just then, a slightly larger spider sensed something and looked in their direction.

For a moment there, Zu An’s blood nearly froze.

1. Chapter 556: Pei Mianman obtained it from a tomb, and it allows her to see in the dark. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2757: A Special Bridge
chapters.body:
Chapter 2757: A Special Bridge

Despite knowing it made no difference, Zu An held his breath and kept completely still, fearing that his movements would further alarm the slightly larger spider.

The spider stood there, looking in their direction with an entire row of eyes. Moments later, it began moving toward the three of them.

Zu An was horrified. He stared intently at the spider, not daring to make a reckless move. 

The spider soon arrived before him, looking confused. It extended its chelicerae over. 

Zu An barely avoided the spider’s chelicerae by leaning backward. He could sense their hairs less than a centimeter away from him. Even so, he maintained his composure and didn’t make any overly large movements, lest the other party sense something. 

The spider moved its chelicerae around the vicinity, which Zu An dodged by carefully maneuvering his body. In the end, the spider didn’t find anything, lost interest, and scuttled off elsewhere. 

Zu An didn’t move right away. He waited for the spider to fully depart before flitting out of the corridor. When they were finally out of danger, he put Isabella and Firework down. Both their faces were as red as apples. The earlier intimate contact was too much stimulation for them. 

“That scared me!” Isabella patted her chest. Her cultivation was high enough to see through absolute darkness, and she'd gotten a clear enough view of the earlier situation to understand how close of a shave they'd had earlier.

“I thought that demon spider found us!” Firework stuck out her tongue. She regretted teasing Zu An earlier, which had nearly resulted in them being discovered.

“It’s a relief we survived the ordeal. I don’t think there are any spiders here.” Zu An scanned their surroundings. There were no spiders ahead of them; only fog. 

This fog was different from the one they'd encountered on the ocean. Zu An felt a burning sensation on his skin, which was astounding considering his body’s sturdiness and poison resistance. The fog had to be extremely poisonous.

“Careful. This miasma corrodes one’s ki, flesh, and blood, reducing one to a pile of bones.” Firework spotted a few skeletons lying not too far away. Those had to be people who had reached this point but died to the miasma. 

Isabella took out three jewels from her bag. “These are Poison Warding Beads. They should provide you with immunity to most poisons in the Myriad Worlds.”

They'd worked on all known poisons to date, but there were too many unknown things in the Myriad Worlds, so she didn’t dare to speak of it in absolute terms. 

Firework was awed. “I’d expect no less from the Universal Conglomerate’s big miss. You have all kinds of treasures stashed away.”

Isabella’s face reddened. “Don’t tease me, big sister Firework. Aren’t you Dreamland’s big miss as well? Dreamland is a place countless in the Myriad Worlds look forward to.”

“But Dreamland has fallen. A demoness has taken control of it,” Firework said with a sigh. 

“I’m sorry, big sister Firework…” Isabella was apologetic for mentioning something upsetting. 

“Don’t worry about it. I've gotten used to it. I should be thanking you for helping me instead.” Firework quickly regained her smile. 

Thanks to the Poison Warding Beads, they were able to travel within the miasma without suffering from its poison. Thus, they explored the area and quickly found their surroundings weirdly familiar.

“Another labyrinth?” Isabella frowned. The earlier labyrinth had nearly traumatized her. They had barely found the exit, only to bump into that terrifying demon spider.

Zu An shook his head. “It’s not a labyrinth. It’s just a trick of the eye.”

Proper labyrinths couldn’t be easily overcome unless one understood the trick behind them. This place was different. It merely induced a sensation that made one feel as if one were moving in circles.

Such tricks would be harder for others to unravel, but Zu An had the Eye of Truth. He could see through all trickery and deception. Worried that the two ladies would lose their way, he grabbed their hands and led the way.

“Are you taking advantage of Isabella now?” Firework teased.

Isabella’s face heated up. “Aren’t you holding his hand too?”

Firework was unfazed. “We’re close. It’s normal for us to hold hands.”

Isabella bit her lip, not knowing how to reply.

“Stop teasing her.” Zu An glared at Firework.

Firework stuck out her tongue as she turned her attention to the road he walked. Zu An paid no heed to the convoluted paths and walked straight in a single direction. Twice, the ladies noticed him leading them into a wall, but for some reason, they didn’t bump into it. The wall turned out to be an illusion, and there was a path through it. 

It didn’t take long for the miasma to scatter. The convoluted labyrinth disappeared, replaced by a massive underground rift valley. The area beneath was cloaked in darkness, as if it were a pathway into the netherworld.

The rift valley was shrouded in demonic air, death energy, and similar energies that were uncomfortable to the living. Their divine senses could only sense what was within a fifty-meter radius. A bizarre wind blew, carrying with it terrifying noises reminiscent of demonic murmurs. 

Those noises made Isabella shudder. She pressed closer to Zu An and grabbed his arm. 

Zu An scanned the rift valley. The rift was so wide that he couldn’t see what was on the other side. He could only see eight chains extending from one side to the other, as if they were bridges linking both ends. 

He couldn’t recognize the material the chains were made out of, but he could tell they were a precious material. They were densely inscribed with ancient runes that subtly moved as if they were breathing. They felt old and solemn.

“Are you sure your father is imprisoned here?” Zu An asked Firework. For some reason, he felt intimidated by the chains. 

“I've investigated this place for many years, but the opposite side of the rift valley is the only place I have yet to check. If the demoness has imprisoned him, that is the best place for her to do so,” Firework said.

She could also tell this place was dangerous.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2758: Choosing Life And Death
chapters.body:
Chapter 2758: Choosing Life And Death

“Should I head to the other side of the chain to take a look first?” Firework looked around but couldn’t find another path. In the end, she had to set her eyes back on the eight chains. It wasn't a real bridge, but with her cultivation, crossing a chain wouldn’t pose a challenge.

Zu An stopped her. “We can’t see what lies beneath the rift valley, and there’s something amiss about the chains, too. It’s too dangerous to cross it.”

“We won’t achieve anything standing around like this.” Firework’s worry about her father’s safety stoked her anxiety.

“I have an idea.” Isabella reached into her bag and pulled out a metal sphere. “This is a Planet Detector. It can withstand extreme environments, from a star's heat to the depths of space. Its unique material also allows it to withstand an expert’s full-powered attack for an hour. We can use it to scout the situation.”

Zu An took a second look at her bag. How can such a small bag store so many treasures? As expected of the Acadias’ big miss!

Firework hugged her and said teasingly, “I’m starting to get tempted by your bag. So much so that I want to snatch it over.”

Isabella shook her head. “It won’t work even if you snatch it from me. This Bag of Treasures is bound to my soul. Only I can open it.”

Firework was speechless. A long time later, she retorted, “As expected of the Acadias’ big miss. You’re so innocent that I can’t bear to bully you.”

Isabella blinked in confusion. She didn’t understand what those words meant.

Her Planet Detector flew above the rift valley and emitted a red light to scan the environment. Soon, Zu An and the others received some images. 

It was then that an anomaly happened. A light wind blew, and the Planet Detector, crafted to withstand extreme environments, tried its best to maintain its position. Even so, it only lasted a second before crumbling into countless parts that fell into the rift valley.

Firework was intrigued. “Didn’t you say it could withstand the attacks of an expert for an hour?”

Isabella’s face reddened. She'd still been bragging about her gear’s functionality a second ago, only for it to unravel to a light wind right away. She spent a moment in thought before taking out the Wind Quelling Pearl. “Let me try with this.”

Zu An stopped her. “I’ll do it.”

Isabella wasn't weak by any means, but she'd grown up in a sheltered environment and lacked ferocity and tenacity. It was too much to ask her to do something this dangerous on the spur of the moment. 

Isabella handed the Wind Quelling Pearl to him. “Big brother Zu, you’re more capable than me. I’m sure you can do it.”

Before Zu An stepped onto the chains, Firework suddenly stopped him. “Hold on, big brother Zu. Have you wondered why there are eight chains here instead of one?”

Zu An widened his eyes. Indeed. Anyone with the means to reach this place can’t be ordinary. If they wanted to make a bridge, a single chain would have sufficed. Yet there are eight chains here. Do seven of the chains lead to doom, and only one leads to our destination? 

The first thing he thought about was the Eight Trigrams, but there was no other commonality between that and the eight chains other than the number eight.

“Big brother Zu, can you see the right path with your Eye of Truth?” Isabella asked. 

Zu An shook his head. “These chains are real objects and not illusions.”

“What should we do? There might not be a second chance if we choose the wrong chain.” Isabella panicked. They had witnessed the fate of her Planet Detector. Even if the Wind Quelling Pearl could suppress the wind, there could be other threats awaiting them on the chains. 

Just then, Firework began singing. It was a peculiar song with bizarre intonations, but it induced a feeling of relaxation in the listeners. 

Zu An looked at the golden notes gathering around him and quickly understood what was going on. “Is it a blessing?”

Firework smiled sweetly. “Have you forgotten I’m from Dreamland, too? I just used an ability passed down within my clan. I've just bestowed you with great fortune. I’m sure you’ll be able to choose the right chain now.”

Zu An remembered that the Dream Clan had the ability to consume their karma to influence cause and effect to help others achieve their goals.

“But your karma…” Zu An was worried. Those who depleted their karma would be condemned to terrible luck. The backlash wasn't something to be joked about.

“Don’t worry, I can take it,” Firework replied with a smile. “Our ability only guides cause and effect, but it doesn’t ensure the outcome. To increase the chances of success, you’ll have to employ your own methods and do everything you can to improve the odds."

Zu An nodded. Basically, the Dream Clan’s ability greatly increased one's chances of success, but nothing would happen if one remained idle. Only by doing everything within one’s means to fulfill the theoretical possibility of success would the effect of the blessing be magnified. 

Zu An tried to analyze the chains. He was adept at formations, and he suspected the eight chains were imbued with formations. The runes on each chain were different, so they could each have different effects. Those effects would be triggered once he stepped on the chains. 

Too bad my knowledge of runes is only superficial at best. If Xie Daoyun were here, given her experience as Yumen Beiqing, she might just be able to read the runes and derive the functions of the ones on each chain. 

Wait, with her main body being a talisman paper, Jing Teng might also see through the runes. 

Unlike his other lovers, he'd been able to bring Jing Teng into Dreamland as it was unlikely Dreamland’s staff would find her, given her main body was a talisman paper; not to mention that she was hiding in the blessed cave inside the pendant. 

He was about to call Jing Teng out when he remembered the two ladies he was with. Ugh, it might be another bloodbath. And there’s no guarantee she can read the runes…

Hold on, I have The Book!

The Book was capable of prophesizing future danger. Wasn’t his current one-in-eight selection the perfect place for it to shine?

So, he walked to the leftmost chain and raised his foot. An image of him plummeting into the rift valley surfaced in his head.

Not this one.

He moved on to the second chain and did the same. He saw himself going berserk and his soul being siphoned dry, eventually ending up as a mindless zombie.

Zu An was speechless. Not this one either!

While The Book could foretell the future, it could only read what would happen in the next few seconds. It was likely due to the Dream Clan’s blessing that he could peer into the future so clearly.

For the third chain, he saw his flesh being sliced away by an invisible power until he was reduced to a skeleton.

He did the same for the remaining chains, and they all showed him a tragic way to die. By the time he was done testing the eighth chain, his face paled in horror. “How can this be?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2759: White Bones
chapters.body:
Chapter 2759: White Bones

“What’s wrong?” The two women were taken aback by his reaction.

Zu An explained severely, “I have the ability to foresee danger, and with Firework’s blessing, I was able to see further ahead than I usually can. I foresaw my death on every chain I stepped on, and every death was as tragic as could be.”

“How’s that possible?” Isabella’s face paled. She grabbed Zu An’s arm. “You shouldn’t go then. It’s a path of certain death.”

Firework frowned. “It doesn’t make sense for all eight paths to lead to death. Big brother Zu, try to look back on what you saw. Do none of the routes provide a hope of survival?”

Zu An shook his head as he shared his deaths for each of the eight chains. He then said, “If there’s a chain that offers a glimmer of hope, it would be the first one. I didn’t witness my death after plummeting into the rift valley. There might be a shot at surviving after falling in there.”

Firework stopped him. “No, that won’t do. The rift valley is too dangerous. Even its wind carries immense destructive power. You’ll die a tragic death if you fall in there.”

“Indeed!” Isabella exclaimed. She held onto Zu An, afraid that he would recklessly attempt the first chain.

“But we’ll be dead meat if we return without giving it a try,” Zu An replied worriedly. It hadn’t been easy for them to get here, and the terrifying demon spider was waiting for them behind. They didn’t stand a chance against the spider. He was the type to choose to gamble instead of facing certain death.

“Big brother Zu, aren’t you capable of instantaneous movement and swapping positions? Can’t you move to the other side right away?” Isabella suggested. 

“Those skills are limited to the range of my divine sense, but I’m only able to extend my divine sense across fifty meters here. It isn’t enough for me to reach the opposite side.” Zu An shook his head.

Isabella crumbled into despair.

Firework pondered about it and asked, “Big brother Zu, is it possible that one of the chains doesn’t lead to certain death? Perhaps the vision you saw wasn’t the end. Try to look back on what you saw. It might be possible that you came back to life in one of the visions.”

Zu An widened his eyes as he remembered the words Yumen Beiqing had left him back then: ‘The end can also be the beginning’. He should have understood that better than anyone, being a Time Scribe as well as an Enigma and Chaos envoy.

Indeed. What I saw might not necessarily spell death. Let me see which situation I could possibly come back to life in. 

Realization struck him moments later.

“Allow me a moment of silence.” Zu An walked up to the cliff and stared at the chains contemplatively.

Firework and Isabella held their breaths and kept completely silent lest they disrupt him. 

In truth, Zu An wasn't doing any thinking. Instead, he took out his Spring and Autumn Brush and Time Annal[1]. He wrote a sentence in it. Moments later, he walked up to the third chain.

Seeing this, Isabella exclaimed with a quivering voice, “No! Your flesh will be sliced off and you’ll turn into a skeleton!”

Even Firework asked, “Are you sure that’s the right chain?”

Zu An replied, “I practice a unique cultivation skill. I won’t die even when reduced to a skeleton.”

The Primordial Origin Sutra granted him tremendous regenerative prowess, whereas his position as the Netherworld’s master gave him a deeper grasp of death than anyone else. 

“That might be true elsewhere, but it’s too dangerous here.” Isabella’s eyes were filled with concern.

Zu An agreed that this place couldn’t be evaluated with common sense. To be safe, he squeezed out a droplet of blood essence and handed it to Isabella. “If a mishap happens, put this blood essence droplet on my skeleton. It will bring me back to life.”

Isabella bit her lips and nodded. I’ll guard this blood essence droplet with my life.
