“Since you've made up your mind…” Firework bit her lips as she activated her Dream Clan’s blessing once more. “…I’m sure you’ll succeed!” 

A golden light shrouded Zu An. All their lives were at stake here, so he didn’t stand on ceremony. He patted her shoulder before making his way to the third chain. He lifted his foot and stepped on it. 

The old, grayish chain lit up, bringing slight illumination to the thick darkness. Runes flowed across its surface like water.

Unfortunately, the light appeared to have drawn the notice of something beneath the rift valley. Several eerie red lights glowed below. Sometimes, there were also flickering green lights that resembled the eyes of terrifying behemoths. This made the sinister environment feel even more stifling and nerve-wracking.

Isabella nervously followed Firework to the edge of the cliff. They didn’t dare breathe loudly, fearing that they would alarm Zu An. They clenched their fists so tightly that their nails were digging into their flesh. 

A strange wind blew up from the rift valley. Recalling that was how her Planet Detector had shattered, Isabella was so nervous that her heart thumped up into her throat. 

Zu An lifted the Wind Quelling Pearl he'd received from Isabella. The destructive wind assailed him, but it couldn’t breach the space within a meter of him. In the end, it slowly dissipated.

The two women on the cliff’s edge exhaled in relief. It’s good that the Wind Quelling Pearl works.

Zu An continued his journey.

More gusts of wind blew up from the rift valley, each one stronger than the previous. However, they were unable to breach the safety zone created by the Wind Quelling Pearl. Eventually, the wind died down as if it understood the futility of its endeavor.

Even so, Zu An showed no relief, as the wind dying down suggested that the attacks were coming from a sentient being. Could there be something below?

But it was too late for regrets. He could only muster his courage and march on. 

As he advanced further, the runes on the chain grew brighter. Zu An felt immense pressure crushing him, so much so that it eroded his cultivation. He should have been strong enough to easily flit across even a metal wire, but with the unraveling of his cultivation, the stress he was under amplified greatly. He felt as if he was marching with a mountain on his back. 

The previously stable chain shook violently, and it looked as if he would fall at any moment. Furthermore, he felt countless malevolent gazes staring at him from the shadows around him. Fear, regret, and despair gripped his mind. 

I shouldn’t have come here. Why did I take such a huge risk to save someone else’s father? Maybe it’s not too late to turn back.

… 

Doubts gushed into his mind, and they only made the chain shake more.

“Big brother Zu!”

“Be careful!”

Zu An suddenly heard the familiar cry of a bird. Hundredwarble unfurled its wings and soared into the sky. The miscellaneous voices distracting him slowly quieted down, and his eyes regained clarity. 

I’m not just saving someone else’s father. I’m saving myself, too!

His willpower had been tempered by countless life-and-death situations. It didn’t take long for him to dispel unnecessary thoughts and continue on with stable footsteps.

But Isabella clutched her mouth. She saw Zu An’s flesh disappearing with each step that he took, such that even his bones could be vaguely seen. 

1. Chapter 2631. These two tools are used together to allow him to turn whatever is written into reality, but it has to be within reasonable grounds. It’s similar to Keyboard Come, but he doesn’t have to pay a price straight away. Instead, he risks suffering a backlash if the outcome is not ‘plausible’ enough. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2760: Chang’E And The Goddess Of Wu Mountain
chapters.body:
Chapter 2760: Chang’E And The Goddess Of Wu Mountain

To Isabella’s bewilderment, Zu An continued advancing as if he was unaware of his disappearing flesh. She shouted, “Big brother Zu! Big brother Zu!”

No matter how she shouted, Zu An didn’t respond. He had entered another space. He was no longer walking on the chain; instead, he was on the moon. The surroundings were frigid, and the space was barren except for a nearly withered osmanthus tree.

There was a palace in the distance, and he was excited to see it. It was the Frost Palace.

He had been wanting to meet Chang’e, also known as the ancient Mermaid Queen Gong Suyin, once more. He ran over, but to his astonishment, he couldn’t open the door no matter how he pushed. 

“Suyin! Suyin!” He knocked on the door and shouted. 

Moments later, a perplexed voice echoed, “Who are you?”

Zu An was excited to hear that familiar voice. “Suyin, I am Zu An!”

“Zu An?” The person inside murmured the name. Moments later, she replied, “I don’t know you.”

Zu An was taken aback, but he quickly realized what was going on. “I’m sorry, Suyin. I know you’re angry at me, but I have been looking for ways to return, albeit to no avail. It’s only now that I’m able to reach you.”

His heart was filled with guilt. It didn’t occur to him how he had gotten here in the first place.

The door finally opened, and a woman dressed traditionally walked out. She had well-sculpted eyebrows reminiscent of a painting, and her robes fluttered gracefully. She was ravishing.

The sight of her made Zu An gasp. He leaped forward to hug her. 

But the other party dodged with a cold expression. “Please have some self-respect, sir!”

“Suyin, it’s me!” Zu An was appalled.

“Suyin… You kept calling me Suyin, but I’m afraid you've gotten the wrong person. I’m not Suyin. I am Chang’e,” the beautiful woman replied with a frown. “But Suyin sounds familiar. I appear to have heard the name somewhere.”

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat as he realized she wasn't putting on an act. “You are Chang’e, but you are also the ancient Mermaid Queen Gong Suyin…”

He told her about their transmigration into the past, how she had become the Chang clan’s saintess, and how she had been forced to consume the immortal medicine and ended up being trapped in the Frost Palace. 

“It’s an interesting story…” The beautiful woman nodded.

Noticing her composure, Zu An’s heart sank. “It’s not a story. It’s what happened between us.”

The woman eyed him. “Your words spark some memories in me. I have been waiting for someone all these years. Are you that person?”

Zu An became excited once more. “That’s right! Have you remembered it?”

“I do remember it. I remember how grueling the first thousand years were. Every time I heard the wind blow and the grass rustle, I’d think you had returned. But each time I opened the door, there was no one there. It was an endless cycle of disappointment.”

“I’m sorry.”

“You don’t have to apologize. It’s all in the past.”

“…”

“The following ten thousand years weren’t easy either. By then, I had stopped rushing to the door whenever I heard any movements outside because I feared disappointment.”

“Another hundred thousand years passed afterward. I thus lost all expectations. I knew you would never come, but I still continued to wait out of habit. Even our image started to blur in my mind. I tried to draw you, but I could never get your face.

“In the next three hundred thousand years, I forgot about my wait and our past. I grew accustomed to my life in the Frost Palace.”

Her words plunged Zu An’s heart into ice. 

“I’m sorry, Suyin. You waited in agony for so many years because I came too late.” Zu An looked distraught. That was three hundred thousand years! What a despair-inducingly long time that was!

The beautiful woman shook her head. “You don’t have to apologize to me. It has all come to pass. I have grown accustomed to all these things. Time can indeed heal all pain. I no longer feel pain or excitement when I recall the past; there is only serenity.” 

Zu An wanted to say something, but the beautiful woman turned around and left. The door slowly closed. “You should leave. That was an immature relationship from three hundred thousand years ago. I have lived for far too long; the days I spent with you are insignificant in comparison. The current me harbors no feelings for you. Farewell.”

The moment the door closed, Zu An felt as if someone had plunged a blade into his heart. 

He couldn’t criticize Gong Suyin. What promise could possibly withstand the trial of three hundred thousand years? He only had himself to blame for losing her and failing to find her for so many years. 

The next second, the world warped. He found himself transported from the lifeless Frost Palace to a verdant mountain shrouded in clouds. The grass and trees here felt familiar. His heart skipped a beat. This is Wu Mountain. 

He anxiously rushed to the mountain peak to look at the beautiful Goddess Peak.

“You’re finally here,” a gentle voice said with a sigh. 

Goddess Peak turned around to look at him as if it had come to life. 

Zu An asked with a quivering voice, “Is it you, Yaoji?”

“Have you forgotten me…” the voice sounded begrudging.

“No, I was afraid you would forget me.” Zu An recalled his earlier encounter at the Frost Palace, and that filled his heart with pain.

“How could I ever forget you? I have been waiting for you. But I am stuck in this ugly form now.” The Goddess Peak sounded downtrodden. Dark clouds appeared around the Wu Mountain and drizzled, as if the goddess herself was weeping. 

“Are you unable to revert to your human form?” Zu An asked fearfully. 

“Too much time has passed. Has it been three hundred thousand years? I envy Chang’e. She consumed the immortal medicine and had an eternity to wait for you, but I was unable to live that long. I could only morph into the Goddess Peak and await your arrival.

“But still, it has been too long. My sentience is about to dissipate,” the Goddess Peak lamented.

“What?!” Zu An hadn’t expected such an outcome for their reunion. He cast his avatar to expand his body, so he could embrace the Goddess Peak as if it was the Goddess of Wu Mountain.

“Don’t weep. I am glad that I was able to meet you right before I dissipate.” Her voice was as gentle and ethereal as ever. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2761: The Undying Skeleton
chapters.body:
Chapter 2761: The Undying Skeleton

“How can I save you? Will an immortal medicine work?” Zu An thought of the Divine Dragon Immortal Medicine and the other legendary immortal medicines. The only thought on his mind was how he could save Yaoji.

“I’m a mountain peak now. What can an immortal medicine do to help?” It sounded as if the Goddess of Wu Mountain was smiling at him. “Ah Zu, don’t grieve for me. I’m already happy that you could make it here before my final moments.”

Zu An embraced her tightly as tears rained down his cheeks. “It’s all my fault. I should have found you earlier. Suyin has forgotten everything, and now you’re leaving me too…”

“Don’t blame her. I remembered because I morphed into a mountain peak, but it’s different for her. She might have consumed the immortal medicine and attained eternal life, but over time, her memories were bound to fade. It’s normal for her to forget about the history you shared and the feelings she had for you.”

“I never blamed her. I only blame myself.” 

Zu An wanted to say more, but the Goddess Peak suddenly morphed into the appearance of the Goddess of Wu Mountain. She gently placed her hand on Zu An’s cheek and said, “Farewell, my love…”

Zu An was alarmed. He reached out to grab her, but he failed to grab anything. His lover dissipated into countless specks of starlight and vanished into thin air. His outstretched hand froze in midair. Despair crashed down on him. 

Perhaps he'd accomplished much in his life, but what was the point if he couldn’t even save the women he loved? He might as well die.

As this thought surfaced, his body began to disintegrate. His flesh and blood broke down, but he no longer cared. There was no longer joy in life, so why should there be fear in death?

His mistake had caused Chang’e and the Goddess of Wu Mountain to wait for a long time. Chang’e no longer remembered him, and the Goddess of Wu Mountain had passed away. 

I've kept her waiting alone for such a long time, so how can I let her leave alone?

He watched as his body broke down into a skeleton, but he only felt at ease.

So be it…

But to the spectating Isabella and Firework, they felt as if their world was on the verge of collapse. To them, Zu An had been walking along the chain when he suddenly trembled as if he had encountered something shocking. His body and flesh began to disintegrate, revealing his bones.

The plan had been for Zu An to use his unique cultivation skill to repair his body, but he stood motionless, as if he had given up on himself.

Isabella shouted in a panic, but no matter how she strained her throat, her voice didn’t seem to reach Zu An. 

Firework went over to her side and spoke commandingly, “Hand his blood essence to me.”

“But…” Isabella hesitated. I promised big brother Zu to guard his blood essence with my life. Big sister Firework might not be an outsider, but…

Before she could finish her thought, Firework interrupted, “Do you still want to save him?!”

Isabella gritted her teeth. She could think of no other options, so she handed the blood essence over. 

Firework took the blood essence and rushed down the chain, straight toward Zu An. 

Sensing her entry, a furious wind blew toward Firework.

“Be careful!” Isabella shouted. Firework wasn't holding onto the Wind Quelling Pearl, and they had all witnessed what the bizarre wind was capable of. 

Firework twisted her waist in a show of unbelievable flexibility, dodging the bizarre wind in the nick of time. But being on the chain, she was limited in where she could step. Her large movement resulted in a misstep, and she began plummeting into the rift valley. 

“AH!” Isabella cried in horror.

But Firework’s foot hooked onto the chain, and using that as a point of leverage, she pulled herself back onto the chain. 

Isabella didn’t have time to celebrate her turnaround as she shouted a warning, “Be careful, the wind is coming again!”

The earlier encounter appeared to have given Firework a better grasp of how the wind worked. Instead of making reckless movements, she waited for the wind to approach her before leaping up and twirling a few times in the air to dodge the wind.

Feeling provoked, several more gusts of wind swept up from the rift valley, threatening to tear her into pieces.

Isabella was initially worried, but her eyes soon gleamed with awe.

Firework moved with incredible lightness and flexibility, as if her body had no bones. Somehow, she managed to dodge every attack by the closest shave. Her movements were so graceful that it looked as if she were dancing. 

Isabella was amazed. As expected of someone pursuing the dao of Beauty. Her movements are such a pleasure to look at.

By then, Firework had already reached Zu An’s side. She dripped the blood essence onto the forehead of his skeleton. 

Zu An was embracing the Goddess Peak when he suddenly realized something. He had rushed to visit Wu Mountain after returning to the present, and the Goddess of Wu Mountain had left him with a message. She'd said that she often visited the Frost Palace to chat with Chang’e. If that was the case, how could Chang’e have forgotten about him?

No, this doesn’t make sense. How could a true promise disintegrate because of time? My relationship with Chang’e transcends time and generations, so how could she forget about me so easily?

This must have been the fear I was harboring in my heart. I was afraid that I’d lose the two of them over the passage of time. 

Chang’e consumed the immortal medicine, so I wasn’t afraid of her death but of her forgetting. The Goddess of Wu Mountain didn’t take the immortal medicine, so I was afraid of her death.

I remember it now. I was crossing a chain on the rift valley. This must be an illusion conjured by the runes on the chain.

Feeling a warm tinge on his forehead, he hurriedly channeled the Primordial Origin Sutra to repair his collapsing body. Houtu had bestowed him with a droplet of Shaman blood back then. Shamans boasted tremendous life force, and they could heal their bodies with just a single droplet of blood essence. Thus, it didn’t take him long to make a full recovery.

It was only when he saw Firework that he realized she had saved him. “Thank you.”

He thought back about everything that had just happened and felt fear. 

If I hadn’t left that droplet of blood essence behind, if Firework hadn’t risked danger to save me, and if I hadn’t written those words in the Time Annal, I might have been dead by now.

Qi Yaoguang’s prophecy of me turning into bones should have been referring to this.

The eight chains suddenly glowed with a reddish-orange light, and then transformed into whips that flailed at the two of them as if they had come to life.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2762: The Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2762: The Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation

The reddish-orange whips flailed from all directions like divine retribution. Their sheer momentum caused space to freeze up, making it impossible to avoid them.

“Be careful, big brother Zu!” Firework flitted behind Zu An and used her back to block the whip coming from the most challenging angle.

Pah!

The strike ripped her robe open, and her blood and flesh splattered. Her fair skin was charred black, as if singed by intense flames. 

More whips flew in their direction. Zu An took Firework in his arms and hurriedly dodged them. 

There should only have been eight whips, yet they covered the area. Despite his movement skills and special abilities, not even Zu An could dodge all of them. To make things worse, even the chain he was stepping on was flailing around like a whip, moving up and down like a wave and robbing him of stable footing. This forced him to jump to and from different chains.

All those factors made it more difficult for him to keep up. He could only hold Firework in his arms and use his body to protect her from the whips reminiscent of divine retribution. 

Pah!

One of the whips struck Zu An, inflicting him with immense pain that hurt not just his body but his soul as well. Goosebumps rose across his body. 

He had sustained many injuries in his lifetime, including from whips. The one that left him with the strongest impression was Chu Huanzhao’s Wailing Whip. But the pain inflicted by this whip was a thousand times greater!

Despite his resilience, he still let out a groan. It had to be even worse for Firework.

More whips flailed over. Zu An quickly used Annals of History, the ability bestowed upon him by Deity of Memory Meng. Glimmering with starlight, the river of history appeared around him. Firework was enchanted by the river’s beauty.

The reddish-orange whips fell into the river of history. 

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. The river of history had stopped even the terrifying demon spider’s attack, so it likely wouldn't have a problem blocking these attacks.

The next second, however, he widened his eyes in astonishment. The whips flailed through the river of history and struck his body, nearly knocking his soul out. 

What is wrong with these whips? Why is the river of history unable to stop them? There are at least tens of thousands of years to the river, if not more! Are these whips forces that transcend time?

Zu An had no time to ponder this. He could only endure the whips while doing everything he could to avoid them. He had already attempted flight, but it was impossible to do so above the rift valley.

No matter how he dodged, the whips continued their assault relentlessly. He realized that the only thing he could do was to try his best not to fall into the rift valley while withstanding the whips. 

He simultaneously channeled the Heaven-Devouring Sutra and the Primordial Origin Sutra, the former to absorb the whips’ destructive prowess and the latter to heal his injuries.

Even so, he still quickly reached his limit. The pain inflicted on his soul was unbearable.

Firework felt a droplet of blood falling on her face. She raised her head to look at the man embracing her. His dashing face was distorted by intense pain, and she could smell charred flesh coming from him. She fell into a daze.

“Why do you treat me so well?” she whispered.

Zu An couldn’t answer the question. He didn’t even hear her words, as his mind was already strained to its limit. 

Just then, a golden tortoiseshell silhouette appeared around him. Isabella had been scouring her bag in a fluster and finally pulled out a set of defensive armor. That greatly lifted the burden on Zu An, and he finally had the energy to think about how he could overcome this crisis. He noticed that the eight flailing chains were extending from the shadows.

An idea popped into his mind. While a chain was striking him, he grabbed it and used its returning force to propel him and Firework forward. He was planning to use it to travel to the opposite side of the rift valley!

But the other chains flailed over, refusing to give him a chance to escape. If he didn’t let go, the other chains would cut him to pieces. As a result, he had no choice but to let go. 

But his eyes gleamed. He noticed there was a skill he could learn from the chain—the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation!

This must be the formation behind the eight chains. If I can grasp the formation, I might be able to control the chains. Otherwise, I’ll be whipped to death.

He wanted to learn the formation, but when he saw the astronomical Rage points required for it, he was gripped by despair. 

The Keyboard system swiftly notified him that there was a connection between the Netherworld Book of Life and Death and the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation, which greatly reduced the amount of Rage points needed to a million.

Even so, that was beyond Zu An’s means. He only had 97,460 Rage points left. 

Do I have no choice but to be struck to death by this formation?

Foreseeing his impending death, an idea suddenly popped into Zu An’s mind. He shouted at Isabella, “Do you have an artifact that provides soul energy?”

Over the years, he'd slowly come to understand that Rage points were the manifestation of a person’s soul energy. A person’s soul fluctuated the most when feeling anger, making it the ideal moment to harvest their soul energy. Given the Universal Conglomerate’s influence, they would likely know about soul energy.

Isabella was panicking when she suddenly heard those words and shouted, “Yes, I do!”

She reached into her bag and pulled out a black card. Zu An had no idea how she did it, but the card suddenly appeared in his hand the next moment. 

He was taken aback. This card resembled the credit cards he had seen in his previous life, just that it was made of a special material. This thing can provide me with soul energy?

But there was no time for him to think. He invoked the Keyboard System’s ability to learn the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation. His Rage points were emptied right away, but just when he felt a force dragging his soul out, the black credit card in his hand suddenly glowed and paid the remaining 902,540 Rage points required. 

Before Zu An had time to be amazed, a formation blueprint appeared in his head, and he widened his eyes in enlightenment. He swiftly formed a series of hand seals while murmuring an obscure incantation.

The whips froze in midair. The runes on the chains reorganized themselves, causing the reddish-orange light to slowly vanish and be replaced by a gentle glow. At the same time, the chains settled down, and a light barrier formed between them, giving rise to a bridge that extended into the distance.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2763: Prison Of White Bones
chapters.body:
Chapter 2763: Prison Of White Bones

Zu An stood on the bridge firmly, gasping for air. The relentless attacks had finally stopped. 

Overjoyed, Isabella ran over. “Big brother Zu!” She stuffed all kinds of medicine into his mouth.

Zu An burst into laughter. She’s truly a kind one. He could heal up quickly enough himself, but he didn’t want to turn down her goodwill, not to mention that her pills were indeed useful. 

He initially intended to take the pills from her, but she fed them straight into his mouth. 

The touch of his hot lips made Isabella’s heart skip a beat. It would be even more awkward for her to abruptly retract her hand, so she suppressed her embarrassment and asked, “Big brother Zu, are you feeling any better?”

“Of course. I received your kind sentiments and blessings, and your medicine works like a charm.” Zu An regretted saying those words right away. People tended to run their mouths after surviving a crisis, and he was too used to teasing his lovers, so he blurted those words out. 

It wasn’t appropriate for him to say those words to Isabella, who was like a little sister to him. 

Isabella’s heart beat quickly. She hurriedly retracted her hand and said, “I’m glad you’re fine, big brother Zu.”

Firework scoffed, “I’m injured too. Why don’t I get any medicine?”

“S-Sorry.” The embarrassed Isabella fumbled to take out a pill and hand it over. 

Firework sighed. “You fed the pill to him earlier, but you’re passing it to me now. Isn’t this discrimination?”

“Ah…” Isabella’s face reddened. She felt so ashamed that she wanted to hide in a hole.

Zu An glared at Firework and said, “Stop teasing the young lady. Hurry up and circulate your energy.”

Firework glanced at him. “How much longer do you intend to carry me? I’m a young lady as well. You’re making me flustered. I can’t calm down to circulate my energy.” 

Zu An was speechless.

It belatedly occurred to him that he was still hugging Firework tightly. He'd been too focused on protecting her, so releasing her had slipped his mind, even after the formation was resolved. Only when she mentioned it did he notice the soft sensation pressing against him.

How voluptuous. Her figure doesn’t feel like that of a young lady.

“My apologies.” Zu An hurriedly released her. 

Isabella chuckled. Seeing how even big brother Zu was being teased by Firework, she felt a bit better.

“It would've been fine even if you didn’t release me. I wouldn’t mind.” Firework smiled. She still remembered how he had protected her desperately earlier.

Isabella’s heart jolted. Is big sister Firework confessing to big brother Zu? She nervously glanced at Zu An, and she breathed a sigh of relief when she noticed a lack of response on his part. Then, she quickly berated herself for having such a thought. 

Zu An coughed lightly. “Since we've all recovered, let’s continue our journey.”

Isabella’s medicine was extremely useful. His bone-deep injuries had recovered, his soul had been nourished, and his mental state was much better than before. 

The two women nodded and followed him to the opposite side. 

Isabella was awed by the bridge of light. Firework asked out of curiosity, “How did you subdue the eight chains?”

“I have an ability that allows me to grasp the powers of those I touch,” Zu An scanned their surroundings for threats as he replied.

“How can there be such a formidable ability in the world? Doesn’t that mean you can grasp every ability there is out in the world?” Firework was taken aback.

“It isn’t that easy. I have to pay a price for the powers I learn through this ability. The stronger the power, the greater the price I’ll have to pay. The earlier chains are a good example. If not for Isabella’s card, I wouldn’t have been able to grasp their power and decipher this formation.”

Zu An passed the card back to Isabella. “Thanks for the card. I nearly forgot to return it to you.” 

Isabella replied with a smile, “You should hold onto it for the time being, big brother Zu. We aren’t out of danger yet. This card provides you with not just credit but common currencies in different worlds, and it also resolves the negative effects of certain skills. It should be useful.” 

Firework’s eyes bulged. “What’s your credit limit?”

“I have no credit limit.”

“???”

“I got this card from my grandfather. I don’t think there's a limit to it. He told me that, with the Universal Conglomerate’s wealth, there is nothing we can’t afford in the world.” Isabella innocently revealed what her grandfather told her back then. She wasn't even trying to boast.

Firework was rendered speechless. “I’m envious. She’s flexing without knowing it.”

She was also a renowned scion in the Myriad Worlds, but her financial ability was nowhere near on par with that of the Universal Conglomerate, especially with Dreamland’s current crisis.

“I’m not flexing.” Isabella was embarrassed. “Big brother Zu, you saved my life. I would have given this card to you in return, but my grandfather bound it to my soul, so I’m unable to pass it on to anyone else. However, you should have no trouble using it as long as I’m with you.” 

Zu An smiled. “I’m happy to be able to use it here. Thank you.”

He had to expend Rage points for many of his abilities. Now that he had depleted his Rage points, this card was even more valuable to him. 

It had to be one of the greatest treasures in the Myriad Worlds. Isabella was too young to protect a treasure of this caliber, so her grandfather had bound the card to her soul for her safety. 

When she saw that he didn’t mind, Isabella breathed a sigh of relief. 

While they were chatting, the trio reached the opposite end of the rift valley. The terrain resembled a mountain that had its upper half sliced off, leaving a flat plain on top. It was surrounded by the deep rift valley.

A white bone cage sat at the center of the flat land, and in it sat a dashing and scholarly, middle-aged man. He bore some resemblance to Firework. His eyes were tightly shut, as if asleep. 

“Father!” Firework rushed over as she called out. 

The man had eight chains coiled around his arms and legs, but perhaps due to Zu An unraveling the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation, the chains dissipated into starlight and vanished. The man’s eyelashes fluttered, as if he was about to regain consciousness.

Zu An went over and secretly asked, “Are you sure he’s your father?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2764: Found
chapters.body:
Chapter 2764: Found

“Of course. I wouldn’t fail to recognize my father. What’s wrong?” Firework asked.

“Didn’t you say the demon spider could clone anyone? What if he…” Zu An was skeptical because the white bone cage looked eerie, as if it was intended to seal off some demonic creature.

“That demoness likely imprisoned my father here because she wants to obtain Dreamland’s secrets from him. If this were her clone, she could have used him to fool us right from the start. There would have been no need for her to imprison him here,” Firework replied with a smile. “If you're still worried, I’ll ask some questions to verify his identity later on.”

While they were speaking, the imprisoned master of Dreamland woke up. He was initially confused when he opened his eyes and saw Firework, but then his eyes lit up in delight. He stood up in excitement and exclaimed, “Firework, what are you doing here?”

“Father, I’m here to save you.” Firework dropped her usual light-hearted attitude, and her eyes brimmed with tears. She couldn’t wait to shatter the white bone prison and leap into his arms. 

“My good daughter…” Tears also flowed down the eyes of Dreamland’s master. 

Worried, Zu An scanned Dreamland’s master with the Eye of Truth but didn’t find anything anomalous. Only then did he breathe a sigh of relief.

Dreamland’s master noticed his gaze and asked out of curiosity, “My good daughter, who is your friend over there?”

Firework’s face reddened. She pulled Zu An over to introduce him. “Father, this is Zu An. It’s thanks to him that I was able to get here to save you.”

Heartened, Dreamland’s master looked at Zu An with gentle eyes. “What an outstanding youth you are.”

Firework was embarrassed. “Father, you should be thanking him instead of saying such nonsense.”

Dreamland’s master laughed heartily. “My daughter has grown up. You’re mature now.”

Isabella felt uncomfortable. It didn’t sit well with her to see her big brother Zu meeting another woman’s parent. 

Fortunately, Firework noticed it and quickly introduced her, too. “Father, this is Isabella, the Acadias’ big miss. She also contributed a lot to your rescue.”

Dreamland’s master bowed to Isabella. “I have long heard about your grandfather. I didn’t expect his granddaughter to be so outstanding as well. Dreamland will remember this favor.”

Isabella quickly responded with a greeting. “Uncle, you know my grandfather?”

Dreamland’s master smiled. “Who doesn’t know about the Acadias of the Universal Conglomerate? It’s just that we have never had the chance to meet. I’ll make sure to pay your family a visit in the future to relay my gratitude.”

After trading some pleasantries, Zu An got to the point and said, “Elder, we're currently being pursued by the demon spider. Do you know of any way to deal with her?”

Upon hearing the words ‘demon spider’, the expression of Dreamland’s master warped. 

“That vile demoness! She made us suffer so much! We’re in a forbidden land that has been passed down through generations. There’s a powerful formation here that can suppress her, but the formation has been idle for too long, so we’ll have to reactivate it.”

“How do we reactivate the formation?” Zu An asked.

“Come with me.” Dreamland’s master stretched his limbs before standing up to lead the way. 

Zu An hurriedly followed him. 

They soon arrived before a circular metal platform. Profound patterns reminiscent of a dark red blood moon were inscribed on its surface.

A red jewel embedded in the center of the headdress worn by Dreamland’s master lit up and shot a light beam at the blood moon, causing it to glow. The moon's color turned as vibrant as fresh blood, and the light reflecting off it made it seem as if it was indeed filled with fresh blood.

A loud rumble echoed, as if some hidden gears had begun spinning. The ground trembled, and Isabella instinctively leaned against Zu An for security. 

“Come up.” Dreamland’s master beckoned them to step on top of the circular platform, and the circular platform began descending. 

Looking at the stone walls around them, the group quickly realized they were riding on an elevator. Dreamland’s master was probably the only person in the world who knew how to activate it. 

Their surroundings grew darker the deeper they descended, but the elevator’s tiles continued emanating a vibrant red glow. It felt eerie. 

Zu An suddenly recalled the destructive wind that had risen from the rift valley earlier and asked, “Elder, what lies beneath the rift valley?”

Dreamland’s master shook his head. “I don’t know. Our ancestors only tasked us with the mission to guard this place. If not for the demoness, I probably would have never come here in my lifetime.”

Zu An fell silent. 

Dreamland has been guarding this place all this time, but they don’t know what they're guarding. Does he really not know, or is he reluctant to reveal it to an outsider? No matter which case it is, this place has been running amok since a thousand years ago. 

I wonder if it’s due to the demon spider’s infiltration. 

Some time later, the elevator reached the bottom. The door in front of them automatically opened, revealing a long, well-lit passageway. Zu An couldn’t help but feel as if he had entered a sci-fi movie.

While traveling through the passageway, Dreamland’s master said with a tinge of nervousness, “It’s my first time here as well. Our ancestors strictly forade us from coming here unless it was a matter concerning Dreamland’s life and death. My predecessors have probably never been here, either. But with the demoness having massacred everyone in Dreamland, there’s no better time than now.”

Zu An put up his guard. Isabella took out a couple of defensive artifacts and handed them around. After all the dangers they had been through, they were now more vigilant than ever. 

Surprisingly, there were no traps along the way. They soon arrived at a semi-spherical room covered in giant screens. None of the screens lit up, likely because they had run out of energy after a long time.

Dreamland’s master walked up to a metal pillar that was around his height. A crystal sphere sat on top of the pillar. He formed hand seals while murmuring an obscure incantation. Then, he slit his finger and dripped his blood essence on the sphere. 

It then lit up and scattered light around. The screens began projecting live images of the surrounding area, such as the devastating storm outside, the fog ocean that warped directions, the labyrinths, and the rift valley Zu An and the others had just overcome.

But what caught their attention were the scuttling little spiders and a massive figure on one of the screens.

All of a sudden, the demon spider sensed something and looked over. Even across the screen, the group still felt her suffocating pressure.

Dreamland’s master was horrified. “Oh no! She has found us. It’ll take her no more than thirty breaths to find this place. We have to activate the formation to seal her right away!”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2765: True Identity
chapters.body:
Chapter 2765: True Identity

Zu An and the others were startled. The demon spider had left a deep impression on them. Even if they gave it their all, they still wouldn’t stand a chance against it. 

They had Dreamland’s master on their side now, but the latter was in a severely weakened state after being imprisoned for a long time. It was unlikely he could put up a fight against the spider. 

“What do we have to do to seal the demon spider?” Isabella asked.

“The seal should have been activated when the nine stars were aligned, so we could tap into the natural energies of the world to channel the formation. But we don’t have the luxury of waiting now. We can only use the Dream Clan’s bloodline power to suppress that demoness for now and wait for a better time to seal her off completely.”

Dreamland’s master and Firework slit their fingers and infused their blood essence into the crystal, which glowed with a brilliant red light. The ground quaked as if a formation was whirring to life. 

A sharp shriek echoed in the distance. The demon spider appeared to have sensed an impending crisis and started rushing over.

“This isn’t good!” Dreamland’s master exclaimed. “This formation draws energy from five earth veins located across this forbidden land, but two of them have been corrupted and energy is no longer flowing through them. I can’t activate the formation.”

“We’ll handle that!” Zu An said.

Dreamland’s master knew there was no time to waste. “All right. I’ll teleport you over with this formation disc. I’ll try to buy as much time as possible, but you’ll have to make haste.”

His fingers moved so quickly that they became a blur. He activated a series of ancient formations that hindered the demon spider rushing across the screens. The spider tore through the formations in an instant, but Dreamland’s master used his authority to relentlessly create more formations to hinder it. 

“I won’t be able to stall it for long.” Dreamland’s master spat blood. His face was pale, and sweat rained from his forehead. 

Firework’s eyes were closed, her face pale. Her father was in a weakened state, so she needed to channel more blood essence and power into the formation to compensate for him.

Zu An and Isabella’s surroundings blurred as they were teleported to different corners of the forbidden land. 

Zu An stood before an earth vein resembling a majestic dragon. The area was rich with energy, so much so that he could greatly raise his fighting prowess if he utilized the earth vein’s energy using One Drop of Heaven’s Essence[1].

But he quickly dropped that thought. The demon spider’s strength transcended conventional notions of strong and weak. This wasn't a gap he could bridge with just better utilization of his skills. 

He looked up at the vein. There was a reddish-black mass there resembling a massive heart. Pure earth veins wouldn’t take on such a color. This must be the corruption Dreamland’s master spoke about.

Approaching the corrupted mass filled his mind with negative emotions of violence, bloodthirst, and death. If not for the strong mental fortitude he had built up from the storms he had weathered, he would have lost his senses.

Knowing the demon spider could arrive at any moment, he didn’t waste time and began cleansing the demonic energy using the method taught by Dreamland’s master. His Primordial Origin Sutra harnessed the ability to cleanse demonic ki, so it didn’t take him long to expel a pitch-black aura from the lump, leaving behind a pristine red earth vein.

He wondered if things were going well on Isabella’s side, but he quickly realized his worry was unfounded and laughed as he remembered the mountain of treasures she had in her bag. She might even be more efficient than me. 

Now that the earth vein was cleansed, he couldn’t help noticing that the massive earth vein contained a plate with a keyhole in the center.

At the same time, something was glowing from his chest. He took it out. It was the key he had received from Alati. The key emanated a beam of light at the keyhole, and the plate lit up. 

Even a fool could tell that the key belonged to the hole. Thus, Zu An walked up to the plate, only to suddenly stop when a thought flashed through his mind.

Many things had happened since they entered this forbidden land, and there had been a nudging feeling in his head that something was amiss. However, crises had kept happening one after another, leaving him no time for contemplation.

Unease simmered in his heart. He knew the demon spider could arrive at any moment, but he still refrained from making a move. He had a feeling that if he didn’t figure things out first, there was a good chance he might make a decision he would regret.

“Little brother, you need to quickly unlock it for the earth vein’s energy to flow. I can’t activate the formation otherwise,” Dreamland’s master said.

Zu An looked upward and asked, “You can see what I’m doing here?”

“Of course. I am in the forbidden land’s control room. Didn’t you see it earlier?” Dreamland’s master replied. 

Zu An remembered the display screens he had seen earlier. “Why didn’t you mention that a key was required to unlock the earth vein earlier?”

“It’s my first time here as well, and I didn’t know you had that key. My knowledge about this forbidden land comes from my predecessors, so it can be off,” Dreamland’s master replied with a frown. “You seem to be doubting me?”

Zu An didn’t answer the question. “I don’t think I've heard Firework speak for a while now. Is she still fine?”

Firework said, “Big brother Zu, I couldn’t speak earlier because I was focused on channeling the formation.”

“But you can speak now?”

“It’s a bit better now that you have cleansed the earth vein’s corruption. Big brother Zu, we don’t have time. Hurry up and unlock the earth vein’s energy.”

Zu An sighed. “Firework, why do you have such a high opinion of me? You expressed tremendous goodwill to me right from the start even though I was just a countryside bumpkin from a fringe world.”

“I already told you—I am a Beauty worshiper. From the moment I saw you… Gah! Big brother Zu, do you have to make me say such words in front of my father?” Firework’s embarrassment could be heard in her voice. Zu An could imagine her stomping her feet shyly.

Zu An continued the questions. “What’s the situation on Isabella’s side?”

“She has cleansed the corruption on that side, too. You’ll be able to meet her once you return.” Firework sounded anxious. “Our formations can’t stall that demoness for much longer. We only have ten seconds left. You need to hurry!”

But Zu An still refused to move. “Firework… Or should I call you Diodora, or Yani?”

1. First introduced in Chapter 1266, this book exists in real life. It's one of the foundational texts for your Bazi (Eight Characters) divination, and it talks about Yin-Yang, Five Elements, Ten Heavenly Stems, Twelve Earthly Branches, etc. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2766: Show Of Hands
chapters.body:
Chapter 2766: Show Of Hands

An unnerving silence loomed.

Moments later, Firework’s voice called out, “What are you saying, big brother Zu? How can I be that demoness?”

Zu An sighed. “You don’t need to hide it anymore. I only regret not realizing it earlier.”

A light distortion manifested not far away from where Zu An was, and Firework emerged from there. She was as beautiful as ever, but she showed none of her usual cutesy demeanor. She felt cold and lofty, as if she had become someone else.

“When did you see through me?” ‘Firework’ asked coldly.

The air grew viscous. A familiar pressure bore down on Zu An, trampling on his final sliver of hope. “I wouldn't have expected someone of your stature to lie low for so long. You’re a devoted actor.”

Zu An was impressed by how this major boss had the patience to play a sweet character and address him as big brother Zu. He'd thought he was a good actor, but he was nothing compared to 'Firework'.

“Is it the Eye of Truth? No, that can’t be. I'm holding onto a universal deity-level artifact. Your Eye of Truth shouldn’t be able to see through me.” ‘Firework’ frowned as she wondered where she had given herself away.

Zu An hadn’t obtained the Eye of Truth when they first met, so he couldn’t have figured her out then. It was later that the Deity of Memory Meng had paid Zu An a visit. Other guests might not have known what was going on, but she was strong enough to sense something amiss.

That was especially so when Zu An looked at Yani after returning. She knew right away that Zu An had figured out Yani’s identity, but she hadn’t panicked. She'd decided to play along and let Zu An think that he had it all figured out. 

Later, when she reunited with him as Firework, she'd made sure to carry the universal deity-level artifact along. Only such an artifact could deal with a universal deity’s ability. Just as she had expected, Zu An failed to notice the anomaly. 

Everything should have gone according to plan. Where did I go wrong?

“I was already skeptical when you approached me on the spaceship,” Zu An said. There was no glee on his face despite his having figured out the mystery.

“You suspected me over that? Isn’t it normal for a young lady to be attracted to a dashing guy, not to mention a capable one?” ‘Firework’ was perplexed. “You’re obviously someone who often attracts the attention of the opposite sex, so why would you single me out?”

“Because you’re too mysterious.” Zu An sighed. “Someone with your looks and strength is bound to have no lack of suitors. I saw no reason for you to be drawn to me. Initially, I thought you coveted my Wonderpoint World, but I quickly realized that wasn’t the case.”

“Wonderpoint Worlds might be an invaluable treasure to others, but that means nothing to me,” ‘Firework’ replied proudly. “But I should have given you an apt reason afterward—I was seeking help to save my father. Did that pique your suspicion?”

“Indeed. Your actions were peculiar. You showed no interest in Dreamland and were instead more eager to drag me to the beach, after which you then feigned anger, dove into the ocean alone, and ended up being captured by a sea monster.”

“That’s all? But that fits within ‘Firework’s’ eccentric persona. I don’t see anything amiss with that.” ‘Firework’ remained confused.

Zu An eyed ‘Firework’. “You seem to enjoy acting.”

“Of course.” ‘Firework’s’ eyes gleamed. “While my clones can impersonate others, they can’t fool close kin and friends if there are discrepancies in their personality, speech, and gestures. That would be my greatest failure!”

“That worked against you. Do you remember asking me, as Yani, how much I knew about Firework? You mentioned that it was suspicious how she was captured near the hotel’s beach.” Zu An chuckled. “Yes, you were merely playing each character according to their personality and circumstances. But your words were so convincing that, for the first time, I began pondering my relationship with Firework.”

‘Firework’ looked stifled. “I didn’t expect my thoroughness to work against me. But did you suspect me just because an outsider said a couple of words? I didn’t think you trusted me so little.”

“There’s more to it, of course. Later, when we reunited in this forbidden land, you revealed that your main body was capable of creating many clones and had replaced everyone in Dreamland.” Zu An briefly paused as his expression turned grim. “It was then I wondered that, if everyone in Dreamland had been replaced, was there a chance you were a fake, too?

“But your story about studying abroad was reasonable, and your motive of wanting to rescue your father made sense. I didn’t see anything amiss through my Eye of Truth either, so I didn’t think too much of it.” Zu An looked frustrated at his carelessness.

“So what made you suspect me?” ‘Firework’ remained confused.

“I couldn’t figure out why the demon spider refused to kill your father and instead chose to imprison him here,” Zu An said. “If the demon spider could replace everyone in Dreamland without anyone in the Myriad Worlds noticing, it should have been able to figure out the secrets this place held, too. I didn’t think Dreamland’s master could have overcome its methods.”

“There could have been a restriction on his memories, preventing me from taking forceful measures.” ‘Firework’ wasn’t satisfied with Zu An’s explanation. She didn’t think there were any discrepancies here.

“I didn’t discard that possibility, but the demon spider had proven itself to be a highly resourceful being. Since it had already impersonated everyone in Dreamland, surely it could impersonate Firework or someone close to Dreamland’s master to extract his secrets? It seemed inefficient to just imprison him here without doing anything.”

At this point, Zu An added with a harrumph, “I’m guessing Dreamland’s master is one of your clones, too.”

“You’re a smart one, but you still haven’t revealed the real reason you suspected me.” ‘Firework’ sneered. Her appearance began to change swiftly, switching between the weakened Dreamland’s master, the valiant Yani, and the seductive Diodora. “You were beating around the bush, refusing to get to the point. You were planning to use the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation against me, right?”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2767: The Real Firework
chapters.body:
Chapter 2767: The Real Firework

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat.

‘Firework’ sneered. “You listed many reasons, but they were all just chasing shadows. I was also reflecting on where I went wrong, and the only thing I could think of was on the rift valley’s chain bridge.”

“Indeed.” Since things had already gotten to this point, Zu An decided to speak the truth. “I was moved when you took the first whip for me. Which man wouldn’t be when a beauty puts herself on the line for him?

“But when I put my earlier suspicions together and considered the situation, a thought occurred to me. Could the chains have been targeting not me, but you? You took the first whip and misled me into thinking that you were protecting me, and that prompted me to foolishly protect you from the remaining attacks.”

There was actually another important factor at play here—Mi Li’s absence. Perhaps she'd been in hibernation, but with all the dangers he had been through, it didn’t make sense for her to still remain asleep. The only explanation he could think of was that her appearance would draw attention and plunge him into greater danger. She could only signal for him to resolve the crisis by himself via her silence. 

They had been together for so long that words weren’t necessary for them to convey their thoughts. 

Of course, Zu An wasn’t so foolish as to reveal that to ‘Firework’.

‘Firework’ smiled after hearing his analysis. “You’re smart, but it’s not a good thing to be too smart. I was getting lonely after spending so much time alone. It’s rare for someone to catch my eye. If you were more foolish and more cooperative, I could have taken you in as my plaything. You would have been happy for a long time.”

“I’d have liked to feign ignorance as well, but I couldn’t hold it in.” Zu An sounded regretful.

“That’s not an issue. I can erase your memories. I’d still be your Firework, and you’d be my big brother Zu. How does that sound?” ‘Firework’ looked at Zu An playfully. 

Even though this man is weak, I’m sure he’ll be quite a different experience. I don’t mind spending more time with him. It’s rare to meet someone this interesting. 

“I’d rather live with my eyes open. Even if it doesn’t end well, I think it still beats living in ignorance.” Zu An shook his head. “Besides, I've been chosen by the Deity of Memory. I wouldn’t dare to relinquish my memories that easily.”

‘Firework’s’ expression darkened. “Do you think I’d be intimidated just because you brought up Meng?”

Zu An’s heart thumped. 

She didn’t hesitate to call the Deity of Memory by name! And it sounds like they know each other. How can she be this fearless…

Universal deities were transcendent entities. They could sense disrespect, even in private settings, and that could result in divine retribution. That was why the Myriad Worlds tread very carefully around universal deities to avoid blasphemy.

Sensing Zu An’s confusion, ‘Firework’ harrumphed. “Don’t expect Meng to save you. Even universal deities are reluctant to approach this place.”

“Oh? What is this place?” Zu An asked. Supposing that was true, this place had to be more than just Dreamland’s forbidden land. Universal deities wouldn’t fear a mere Dreamland.

“Why don’t you take a guess? I’ll reward you if you get it right.” Despite still looking like a youthful lady, ‘Firework’s’ tone and demeanor had changed to that of a mature older sister instead. 

Zu An thought about everything he had encountered en route, and his mind gradually cleared. “This place could be related to the Deity of Death. There are rumors floating around that the Deity of Annihilation has killed the Deity of Death, or that the Deity of Death has gone missing. Regardless, there’s no question he's gone missing. Could this be where he lies?”

“As expected of someone who caught my eye. Congratulations, you’re right.” ‘Firework’ smiled, looking beautiful but dangerous.

“Didn’t you promise a reward if I get it right?”

“What do you want?”

“Can you return Isabella to me?”

“Don’t you think it’s unwise to speak up for another woman in my presence?” ‘Firework’ clicked her tongue. “Ah, why didn’t you suspect Isabella to be one of my clones?”

The thought of Isabella brought some warmth to Zu An’s heart. “You wouldn’t have sent us to two separate earth veins if she was your clone. You’d have kept her with me to control me.”

“It would indeed have been troublesome to impersonate that lass. Her bag contains too many treasures,” ‘Firework’ remarked. “In particular, that black card of hers is a marvel. It synergizes well with you. As a safety precaution, I figured it would be best to separate you two.”

She turned to Zu An and said, “I advise you not to try anything funny. You have interesting skills, but I doubt you can pay the price for them without her card forking out the cost.”

Zu An responded with a bitter smile. That had indeed been his intention. He'd contemplated using Isabella’s card to pay the price for his ‘Keyboard Come’, but even though the card was in his hand, he couldn’t use it without her approval.

“Is the real Firework dead?” he asked.

“Why? Feeling sorry for her?” ‘Firework’ paced to and fro, as if she were a cat toying with a mouse. 

“She did bring me some happy memories. I’m guessing you used her real personality as a model, right?” Knowing that the real, bubbly Firework was dead made Zu An a bit melancholic. 

“Hmph, I was the one who brought you those happy memories, not her!” ‘Firework’ harrumphed in displeasure. 

You have successfully trolled Firework for +199… +199… +199…

Zu An was surprised to see the name on his long-inactive system. “Your name is really Firework?”

Firework was taken aback. “How did you know? Do you have the ability to see through a person’s true name as well? Hold on, you have a soul-related skill, and you’re able to figure out a person’s true name. I remember hearing about a skill like that…” She suddenly clutched her head as if in pain.

Zu An was startled. Does she know about the Keyboard System?

Having reached his current level and met experts from different worlds, he no longer thought that the Keyboard System was a typical cheat often seen in his previous life’s webnovels. It was likely to be an artifact, the most powerful kind at that.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2768: Irresistible Terms
chapters.body:
Chapter 2768: Irresistible Terms

Zu An had never been able to determine the origin of the Keyboard System. Even Mi Li seemingly had no idea.

Perhaps she knew, but withheld the information from him. Secrets posed danger in this world, after all. Perhaps Mi Li thought he wasn’t strong enough to know such things yet.

He understood where she was coming from, but he couldn’t curb his curiosity. It seemed the demon spider knew something. It was the strongest entity he had ever met aside from the universal deities, so it could know some secrets. It would be a worthwhile trip if he could acquire some intelligence from it.

“It should be related to a universal deity.” Firework lifted her head. “To think I can’t recall it. Either too much time has passed, or a universal deity has tampered with that piece of memory.”

She reassessed Zu An. “I wouldn’t have expected someone as weak as you to carry a secret concerning a universal deity. That would explain the skills you have.”

“A universal deity…” Zu An fell into deep thought. 

Was the Keyboard System created by a universal deity? Who could it be? So far, I've met the Deity of Chaos and Enigma Mi Li and the Deity of Memory Meng, and they were fairly amicable. Could they be behind the Keyboard System?

While Zu An was in contemplation, Firework suddenly appeared next to him, traced his cheeks with her finger, then propped up his chin. “It may have just been a whim earlier, but now, I intend to make you my plaything.”

Feeling her cold touch, Zu An replied, “You haven’t answered my question. What happened to the real Firework?”

“Tsk tsk. I didn’t expect you to care so much about me. Don’t you already know my name is Firework?” Firework chuckled, her voice echoing in the room. She was dangerous, yet seductive.

“No, you aren’t Firework.” Zu An shook his head. How can this flirtatious woman possibly be the youthful and bubbly Firework?

Firework fell silent. A moment later, she asked, “Did you really fall in love with that lass? Even though you know she was a fake?”

Zu An shook his head. “No, I believe she exists. Many things can’t be faked.”

His words angered Firework. “How am I any less than her? Not only can I take her personality, but I can also turn into other women as well.”

Her body expanded into a two-meter-tall beauty. “You appear to be fond of Yani. You snuck glances at her and ogled her thighs. You don’t need to be secretive about it anymore. It’s now within your reach.”

Zu An calmly replied, “It’s normal for a gentleman to show interest in a lady, not to mention someone that outstanding. It would be abnormal for me not to look.”

“Aren’t you thick-skinned?” ‘Yani’ harrumphed. “Am I not beautiful? Why don’t I see desire in your eyes?”

Zu An fell silent. 

“Does this physique intimidate you now that you’re seeing it close up?” ‘Yani’ laughed. Her physique changed once more, morphing into the graceful and ravishing Elder Diodora. “Then what do you think about Diodora? She’s a famed beauty in Dreamland. You saw how guests across the Myriad Worlds were mesmerized with her at the hotel. Just give the word, and she’ll become your dream lover.”

Zu An sighed. “But aren’t they all you?”

“What’s bad about me?” Firework harrumphed. “If you have me, you can have any woman in the world. I can turn into whoever you like; you can have your way with anyone you like in bed, all at the same time.”

“It’s a tempting offer. No man can turn down such temptation,” Zu An remarked.

“I hear a but.” Firework frowned.

Zu An sighed. “Yani and Diodora are beautiful in their own ways, but I prefer that bubbly, youthful Firework. Can you tell me what happened to her now?” 

Surprise flickered across Firework’s eyes, but her gaze soon turned cold. “She’s dead.”

“You killed her?”

“Indeed. Do you plan to avenge her?”

“I would, but I am no match for you,” Zu An replied with a sigh. He had been simulating ways to defeat Firework in his mind, but none of them worked. The difference in strength was too great.

“Hmph. Men are all talk,” Firework grumbled.

“It sounds like you've been hurt by a man before,” Zu An remarked. 

Firework’s face darkened. “Don’t you think you’re talking too much? It’s time for you to do what you have to do. I’ll spare your life on account of the time we have spent together.”

“You want me to be your plaything?” Zu An frowned.

“You can also choose to be devoured instead.” Firework smiled. “You know my main body. There’s nothing female spiders relish in more than devouring their male partners.”

Zu An said, “…There’s one more option. You can let Isabella and me go.”

Firework scoffed, “Are you mad, or are my ears failing me? Do you think you get to choose?”

“I’ve been thinking about it. You’re probably trapped here by some powerful formation, and everything you've done was in order for me to free you. That means you require my help. If so, there should be room for negotiation,” Zu An replied with a smile.

Firework narrowed her eyes. “You’re a smart man, but have you not considered the possibility that you've already released the seal?”

Zu An widened his eyes in shock. “The rift valley’s Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation?”

“Indeed. That formation was relentlessly striking me, but you put your life on the line to protect me. Being protected by you made me feel something I've never felt before. I wouldn’t have wasted my breath on you if not for that,” Firework scoffed, and the surroundings cooled. 

Zu An felt goosebumps, but he didn’t cower. “You’re lying. If you'd been released, you wouldn’t have had to urge me to release this formation under the pretext of cleansing the earth vein.” 

“Hah, men. At this point, do you still think you’re indispensable?” Firework waved her hand, and the key Alati had entrusted to Zu An appeared in her hand. She walked up to the heart-like stone and inserted the key.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2769: Nebula’S Envoy
chapters.body:
Chapter 2769: Nebula’S Envoy

Zu An’s expression darkened. It’ll be over if she opens the seal. 

He instinctively activated the position swap to pull her away from the seal, but his body suddenly turned icy cold the moment he activated the skill. He was still stuck to the spot, and he was unable to move at all!

Firework laughed. “Big brother Zu, we've been together for some time now. I've seen all your skills. Do you think I’d fall for your tricks? Your position swap is an incredible skill, but it has a weakness. Your divine sense has to reach its target first, but that’s as good as offering it up for me to grab.”

Zu An’s heart plunged. This woman is too strong, not to mention she's been with me all this time and has seen many of my skills. I don’t stand a chance against her. If only I'd seen through her ploy earlier…

Firework continued, “You do have many interesting skills that someone of your cultivation shouldn’t have. Let me see what else you have, lest I get caught off guard later on.”

She extended her divine sense into Zu An’s mind. He felt a surge of cold infiltrating his thoughts. His Hundredwarble’s ability reflexively counterattacked.

Firework harrumphed. “A paltry trick!”

Hundredwarble shattered, and Zu An felt a sharp pain in his mind. Blood seeped from his mouth and nostrils.

“Why put yourself through this suffering? Since you don’t stand a chance against me, you should just sit back and relax. It hurts me to see you in pain,” Firework said as she wiped off the blood on his face.

She should have been a distance away, but somehow, her hand reached Zu An’s face. Yet there were no signs of her arm stretching like a rubber arm. This bizarre spatial distortion felt incomprehensible.

Cold sweat trickled down Zu An’s back. Every cell in his body was warning him of danger, but the earlier misstep had sealed his fate. With his divine sense suppressed, he couldn’t activate any of his other abilities. Once Firework learned about his other skills, he wouldn’t stand a chance at all. Not to mention he had many secrets that mustn’t be leaked.

“Oh? You are hiding a secret. Let me see what it is.” Intrigued, Firework’s divine sense dug deeper into Zu An. 

All of a sudden, there was a spatial distortion, followed by a brilliant cold flash. Firework’s arm dropped to the ground. 

A gorgeous woman dressed in snowy clothes stepped out from the distortion with an air of nonchalance. It was Tingxue. 

Her intervention created an opening for Zu An to retract his divine sense. He exclaimed in delight, “Miss Tingxue!”

Tingxue directed a nod at him before coldly turning to Firework. “You expelled me into the void. We should settle the score.”

FIrework chuckled. Her body glowed, and her arm regenerated instantly. “As expected from Nebula’s envoy. Your prowess is formidable.”

Zu An was startled. Despite knowing Tingxue was related to the Deity of Nebula, he hadn’t expected her to be a Nebula envoy. Unlike other universal deities, the Deity of Nebula was known to not have any envoys.

He turned his attention back to the crisis at hand. Worried that Tingxue wasn’t aware of the latest developments, since she had been missing for a long time, he warned, “Be careful. She’s the sealed demon spider. Firework, Yani, Diodora, and all the other people in Dreamland are her clones.”

Tingxue remained unfazed.

Firework clicked her tongue. “It’s true that all who join Nebula lose their emotions. How boring.”

“I might not have my emotions, but I intend to exact vengeance for this grudge. I considered you a friend, and I don’t like being deceived,” Tingxue coldly spat.

“I am grateful that you thought of me as a friend and risked your life to save me,” Firework replied with a grin. “That’s why I chose to banish you instead of hurting you. I knew you would be fine. Look, didn’t you return safe and sound?”

Zu An remarked, “I bet you banished her because you witnessed her ability to tear open the void and were intimidated by her strength.”

Tingxue hadn’t drawn her sword on the frigate. While her strength was amazing nonetheless, it wasn't enough to threaten the demon spider. It was only after arriving at the forbidden land that Tingxue had drawn her blade against the Soul Siphoning Beetles. 

The demon spider had likely felt threatened by that, and thus sent Tingxue away.

If the demon spider is intimidated by Tingxue, we might stand a chance against her if we join forces. 

Firework noticed Zu An’s excitement and sneered, “Do you honestly think you stand a chance against me? Indeed, Tingxue was stronger than I expected, so I had to banish her lest she get in the way of my plan. But I regained most of my powers after you deciphered the Eight Barren Netherworld Formation. You won’t be a match for me even if you join hands.”

Zu An was speechless. Thinking about how he had risked his life to protect her earlier, he felt so ashamed that he wanted to find a hole to hide himself in.

Tingxue didn’t waste her breath talking and instead slashed with her sword.

It was an attack strong enough to slice a world apart, but Firework stopped it between her slender fingers. “Didn’t I say you aren’t a match for me? I’ll spare you on account of our past ties…”

Tingxue flicked her wrist and rotated her blade. Even Firework had no choice but to release her fingers. Tingxue thrust her blade forth once more like a merciless machine. 

Firework harrumphed. She raised her hand and lightly tapped with her fingertip. Each time she moved, she never failed to block the incoming sword. 

Meanwhile, her other hand moved the key into the keyhole. Once she lifted the final seal, she would regain her full strength, thus completely assuring her victory. She wasn't worried about Zu An, as she had wrapped the key with her divine sense. He couldn’t hope to stop her.

But the key suddenly dropped to the ground right as it was about to enter the keyhole.

Taken aback, Firework immediately turned around to look at Zu An, only to see a copper coin rotating on his palm. It was the Treasure Sinking Coin!

The key could be considered a treasure. Once it was out of Firework’s hand, it could be influenced by the Treasure Sinking Coin.

Zu An rushed to the seal and grabbed the key. His presence suddenly flared, emanating an aura that intimidated even Firework.

Firework sensed that Zu An was charging up for some kind of terrifying move. She didn’t think he was strong enough to threaten her, but considering all the strange moves he had, she didn’t dare let her guard down either. She flicked Tingxue aside and heightened her vigilance. 

The next second, Zu An grabbed Tingxue’s hand and said, “Let’s go!”

The two of them vanished into thin air. 

This was the move he had learned from the True Demon Emissary—Bravado. It involved convincing the enemy that one was going to put one’s life on the line, but the goal was to create an opening to escape.

    ——

T/N: Tingxue means ‘Listening to Snow’ in Chinese. 

I could have translated it into English, as per the norm for the Myriad Worlds, but I kept it as it is because both her name and Yan Xuehen’s contain ‘Xue (Snow)’, and there appears to be a link between the two characters (not a spoiler).

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2770: This Is Love
chapters.body:
Chapter 2770: This Is Love

The True Demon Emissary wasn't strong by the Myriad Worlds’ standard, but he'd had his fair share of fortuitous encounters. Bravado was a skill he'd learned that disregarded the gap in strength to cause a lapse in the opponent’s judgment. 

That very skill was at the level of a law.

Caught off guard, Firework fell for it. She was initially taken aback before she was assaulted by a mix of anger and embarrassment. She hadn’t expected to be fooled by a mere ant. 

You have successfully trolled Firework for +233… +233… +233…

“You want to escape?” 

She grasped the fabric of space, and astonishingly, she found where Zu An was.

Zu An was taken aback. In the brief opening created by Bravado, he'd quickly activated a spatial teleportation to flee from the scene. It shouldn’t have been possible for anyone to chase him once he slipped into the fabric of space. 

But Firework wasn't limited by such laws. She reached right into the fabric of space and grabbed him.

Tingxue immediately reacted with a slash. Zu An also took out the Poisonous Prick to secretly stab Firework’s hand. 

“Hmph, is that the dagger of certain death? Big brother Zu, I know what you’re capable of.” Firework mocked him with a coy laugh. “I showed you mercy, but you sought to claim my life. Don’t you think you’re being heartless here?”

Her hand moved with incredible dexterity, flicking aside Tingxue’s sword and dodging Zu An’s assault to land on his shoulder. 

“Heh, I’ve caught you… AH!” Firework suddenly retracted her hand with a yelp.

Zu An exploited this opportunity to finish the teleportation. 

Firework stared at the prick on her finger. It was seeping black blood. She murmured with an awful complexion, “What kind of poison is this? To think it can hurt and inflict pain on me!”

She expelled the poisonous blood with a wave of her hand. It splattered and corroded a hole in the floor. 

Her face was livid for a while before she burst into manic laughter. “Little man, you have caught my attention!”

Space distorted at the rift valley, and Zu An and Tingxue appeared by the cliff. 

Tingxue awkwardly pulled back her hand before asking in confusion, “What did you use to hurt her earlier?”

Even her world-severing slashes struggled to hurt Firework, yet Zu An had managed to hurt her. That miserable yelp earlier reflected how much pain she'd been in.

Zu An noticed her movements and felt down despite knowing she wasn’t Yan Xuehen. Not only do they look identical, but even their hands are equally cold.

He pulled out a pitch-black stinger and explained, “This is the Scorpion King’s tail sting. It was rumored that its poison would be potent enough to kill any expert in the Myriad Worlds, though I doubt that woman can be killed by it.”

Tingxue nodded in agreement. “She is very powerful.”

A coy laugh echoed. “You know me well, big brother Zu.”

Zu An and Tingxue widened their eyes in shock.

Firework slowly descended from the sky as if walking down an invisible staircase. Her fair, smooth feet left gentle ripples in space, resembling blossoming flowers heralding her descent. Zu An might have swooned if not for the tremendous pressure she emanated.

“How did you catch up with us so quickly?” Zu An was taken aback.

He had grasped the essence of space in the Monster World, and Yumen Beiqing had generously imparted her knowledge to him in bed. His understanding of spatial teleportation was extremely high. He'd thought that he could shake off Firework with this, but she still caught up with them in the blink of an eye.

“Spatial manipulation is an easy feat for me, not to mention you’re still within the forbidden land,” Firework explained with a smile as she eyed their surroundings. “But isn’t that your goal? You’re hoping to lure me back to the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation.”

Worried that he would be suppressed by Firework, Zu An immediately activated the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation. The eight chains on the bridge turned reddish-orange as they lashed at Firework as if they were whips.

Firework harrumphed. “Do you think those whips are what stopped me all these years?”

She raised her fair hand and simultaneously grabbed all eight whips. They turned crimson, and runes furiously flowed across their surfaces. They were channeling all their strength to break free of Firework’s grip.

But it was to no avail. The whips couldn’t even char her skin. 

“It would have been one thing for you to seal me when I was in a weakened state, but do you think I’d let you walk over me now that I’m out?” Firework flicked her hand, and the imposing chains shattered and fell into the rift valley.

Zu An was speechless. He had set the teleportation destination here because he could control the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation. He definitely hadn’t expected it to be destroyed right away. 

I took so many blows for you when you could have destroyed the formation so easily… 

Firework smiled. “Those whips caused you so much pain earlier. I've helped you get even with them. Aren’t you happy?”

More like horrified!

Tingxue frowned. “It seems almost as if she has fallen in love with you?”

Zu An was speechless. Is this the time to be wondering about that?

Surprisingly, Firework murmured in confusion, “Hm? Something seems off. While I do harbor goodwill for you, this seems overboard. Did you use some kind of weird ability on me?”

Zu An said nothing.

He had an ability from the Golden Phoenix called ‘Love Is More Solid Than Gold’. It made women fall in love with him. Coincidentally, he'd only used that ability against Yan Xuehen because the latter was after his life. That made him eye Tingxue with guilt-ridden eyes.

Since then, he'd vowed not to use that despicable skill. It was underhanded to exploit someone else’s feelings like that. 

That said, he wasn't an inflexible person. He would still use the ability if he was cornered.

He had indeed considered using it. It was a pity he didn’t have the Heiress Ball of Delights[1] anymore, so his only option was to use ‘Love Is More Solid than Gold’.

But he hadn’t even used that power yet. What was going on?

1. This item converts pain inflicted by a richer woman into sheer bliss ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2771: Conditions
chapters.body:
Chapter 2771: Conditions

Even Tingxue looked at Zu An with a frown. Why does this feel weirdly familiar?

Firework also suspected that Zu An had used some weird skill on her.

Faced with their suspicions, Zu An was forced to respond, “Have you never felt warmth in your life?” 

Firework’s expression darkened. “Rubbish! That has nothing to do with that!”

Zu An looked at Firework with eyes of pity. “I have an ability called ‘Love Is More Solid Than Gold’ that induces such effects, but I never used it against you. Your reaction likely stems from a lack of love in your life. That’s why you catch feelings quickly whenever someone treats you well.”

Tingxue’s expression flickered upon hearing the words, ‘Love Is More Solid Than Gold’, but walking down Nebula’s path had made her forget many things. Her miscellaneous thoughts scattered as ripples, and her mind reverted to its previous impassiveness. 

Firework, on the other hand, reacted explosively, “I lack love? What a joke! Countless men queue up just to lick my feet. Powerhouses vie to send me precious treasures. Do you know how many people would die on my behalf? Yet you say I lack love?” 

Her vehement response evoked a sigh from Zu An. “You know that isn’t love.”

Firework fell silent. She knew that as well. Some were afraid of her and wanted to get into her good books. Others coveted her beauty. Regardless, they had their motives for approaching her. 

Nothing compared to the moment Zu An protected her from the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation. He'd held her so tightly in his arms that she hadn’t suffered the slightest damage other than the first whip. His desperation to protect her had been pure, untainted by any desires. That was why she was moved. 

But there was no way she would admit to that. She proudly replied, “You arrogant brat! Just because I favor you doesn’t mean you can run your mouth as you please. I’m merely showing you magnanimity in view of the time we have spent together. How dare you lecture me? You must be tired of living!”

Firework’s aura flared, unleashing pressure comparable to that of a universal deity. 

Tingxue frowned. She gripped her sword tightly, ready to launch a counterattack.

Breathing suddenly became difficult for Zu An. He barely held on by simultaneously using One Drop of Heaven’s Essence to tap into the earth vein’s energy and using the Heaven-Devouring Sutra to absorb more energy. 

Just then, a spiderweb appeared in the sky. 

A cold gleam flashed. Tingxue brandished her sword and sliced the web in two. 

But it appeared to have a life of its own. It transformed into countless serpents that lunged at Tingxue and Zu An.

Tingxue brandished her sword without rest, tearing down wave after wave of attacks. However, during a momentary lapse in attention, a web coiled around her wrist and stalled her movements. 

That opening was more than enough. In just an instant, she was thoroughly wrapped up, to the point where she could no longer move.
