Zu An wasn’t faring any better. He unleashed the White Lotus Flame and the Phoenix Fire, thinking it could curb the spiderweb. However, it was completely unaffected by flames. That left him with no choice but to dodge the threads, but he was still caught in the end.

Firework smirked. “Let’s see where you can run now.”

Tingxue stared at Firework nonchalantly, her bright eyes slowly becoming murky like the nebula. She was charging up for a powerful move.

Firework frowned. “What a troublesome foe a Nebula envoy is…”

She was about to make her next move when Zu An suddenly spoke up. “You just want the key to release the seal, right? I’ll hand the key to you if you promise to free Tingxue, Isabella, Alfred, and the others.”

Firework burst into laughter. “I didn’t expect you to be a sentimental person. By being this soft-hearted, you’ll only bring harm upon yourself and your loved ones.”

Zu An pointed out, “Those people gathered here to save you. You shouldn’t repay goodwill with enmity.”

“They joined the expedition under the pretext of saving me, but all of them harbored ulterior motives. Only a fool like you would believe their words.” Firework harrumphed as her face turned cold. “I am the type of person to repay goodwill with enmity, anyway. You’re now all my prisoners. What right do you have to negotiate with me?”

“I am indeed no match for you, but I can bring you down with me. I hope you don’t force my hand,” Zu An calmly replied.

Firework frowned. “Oh? Are you trying to intimidate me again?”

Zu An shook his head. “It’s my last resort. I’ll have to pay a hefty price to invoke it. I’ll lose my life, and my soul might be condemned for all eternity. You've shown me mercy several times now. I don’t want to go to that extent, but if you continue cornering me, I’ll have no choice.”

He wasn’t even sure if Keyboard Come would work on Firework. She was so powerful that he risked being destroyed before the skill could even take effect. But he had no other option. He could only take a gamble.

“Are you threatening me?” Firework’s expression darkened. The surrounding air turned cold. 

“It’s not a threat, but advice from an old friend. I''ve already thought things through. It doesn’t matter to me whether you escape from your seal or not; I have no interest in maintaining order in the Myriad Worlds.” 

Zu An had gotten a rough grasp of Firework’s temperament. She was more of a carrot person than a stick person. It wouldn’t be wise to agitate her. That said, those were his earnest thoughts. 

Even if Firework was a heinous villain, the Myriad Worlds were large enough for her to wreak havoc upon. Besides, the responsibility of dealing with such a powerful entity was supposed to fall on the universal deities and not a small fry like him. 

Firework smiled. “That’s more like it. At least you aren’t an inflexible fool. I’d expect no less from a man I regard highly. Hand over the key, and I’ll release them.”

Zu An opened his palm and revealed the key.

Firework was surprised. “Aren’t you worried about me reneging on my promise?”

Zu An smiled. “I might not have spent much time with you, but I believe you aren’t that kind of person.”

Of course, he wouldn’t hesitate to bring Firework down with him if she reneged, but it was unnecessary to voice such jarring words.

Firework was pleased. “You’re getting more and more likable. All right, I’ll give you my word.”

The key appeared in her hand, and the cocoon around Tingxue and Zu An unraveled. She pulled a cocoon out from the fabric of space—it was Isabella, with only her face showing. 

Upon seeing Zu An, her lips quivered and her eyes reddened. “Big brother Zu, be careful! Firework is…”

“Stop acting coy. He knows everything.” Firework harrumphed. This lass irks me. 

Zu An consoled her. “Don’t worry, Isabella. It’s all right now.”

Firework flung her sleeves. “Come with me!”

She tore the fabric of space and headed to where the seal was. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2772: Awakening
chapters.body:
Chapter 2772: Awakening

Zu An replied grimly, “You've already obtained the key. Why do you need us to go with you?”

Firework coldly replied, “Can’t I just have you come with me? Didn’t you say I was lonely and empty? The least you can do is to accompany me.”

Zu An was stunned. Didn’t expect my words to come back and bite me so quickly.

As soon as Isabella broke free of her restraints, she ran to Zu An’s side to grab his arm, but Firework’s earlier ferocious glare made her hesitant to do so.

“Don’t worry, it’s fine now.” Zu An patted her hand to console her. He then turned to Firework and asked, “What about the others? Didn’t you agree to free them?”

“The sight of them irks me, so I didn’t take them with me. I've released them elsewhere,” Firework replied coldly.

Zu An was worried that she had secretly eliminated them, but he didn’t dare to point that out lest she be provoked by that question. That could prompt her to kill them for real if they weren’t dead yet. So, he could only suppress his curiosity.

Isabella tugged his sleeves and secretly asked him what was going on.

“I made a deal with her… Firework is one of her identities, and so is Yani…” Zu An roughly explained the situation. In truth, he was confused as well. What’s that woman’s real identity? And what’s the deal with Firework?

Isabella was startled by the revelations. At the same time, she was amazed. “Big brother Zu, you’re amazing to have overcome this sure-death crisis.”

She'd experienced Firework’s strength, and she couldn’t find any solutions against her despite her resourcefulness. Yet Zu An had somehow found a way to calm Firework. 

Firework, who was walking in front, didn’t turn around, but she could see Isabella’s twinkling eyes. She knew no man could resist a young lady’s gaze of admiration. She would have killed Isabella if the latter were a scheming green tea bitch[1], but the girl was earnest. She couldn’t find a reason to make a move. 

Isabella had sincerely come here to save her, and had even been calling her ‘big sister’ along the way…

Tingxue is much better. She’s cold, lofty, and doesn’t flirt with Zu An at all. 

Zu An, Tingxue, and Isabella walked through the spatial passageway opened by Firework and returned to the heart-shaped stone. 

Firework was about to insert the key when she thought about something and tossed the key to Zu An. “You do it.”

Zu An asked, “…Why are you letting someone else release your seal?”

Firework smiled. “You also know that it’s a seal targeted against me. What if there are some special measures embedded in it against me? I’d be courting my own death.”

Zu An had to concede that her words made sense. Being on the weaker side here, he had no choice but to take the key and march up to the stone.

“Be careful!” Isabella reminded him.

Despite Firework’s light-hearted demeanor, she stared intently at Zu An in case he attempted something. She was so focused on him that she overlooked Isabella’s ‘green tea bitch-esque’ demeanor.

Zu An chuckled. He took a deep breath before inserting the key. A brilliant light shone from the stone, making it appear like a beating heart. 

At the same time, the rift valley where the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation was located emitted a red light, as if an entity had opened its eyes. No, it felt as if the rift valley was just a small part of its eye.

A desolate aura settled over the forbidden land, emanating pressure that was of even higher stature than Firework. 

Firework was the first to sense the changes. She instantly appeared beside Zu An, grabbed his collar, and exclaimed, “You brat! What did you do?”

Tingxue hurriedly drew her sword and brandished it. 

Isabella also ran over while searching her bag for an artifact that could help Zu An.

Zu An was dumbfounded. “I didn’t do anything. I merely followed your instructions and inserted the key.”

“Impossible. This is the seal restraining me, so why would it awaken Him…” Firework had maintained a lofty demeanor ever since she revealed her identity, as if everything was in her grasp. This was her first time panicking. 

“Who are you talking about?” Zu An asked.

Just then, a blinding white light burst out from the heart-like stone and devoured everything, including him, Tingxue, and Isabella. 

Firework was appalled. She tried to escape, but a mysterious force held her in place. What should have been an easy feat for her under any other circumstances became nigh impossible. She fought back with all her might, but her body was still devoured by the white light.

Despair seeped into her eyes as she screamed, “NO!”

She had schemed for countless years and was finally on the verge of breaking free, but everything had come crashing down on her at the last moment. She felt indignant, but that didn’t stop her from vanishing in the white light. 

The white light soon faded. All four of them disappeared as if they had never been there. 

… 

When Zu An was devoured by the white light, he tried escaping via Rainbow Morph, but he quickly realized it didn’t work. It felt like his means were being restricted by some compelling force.

Before he knew it, he had already been transported from the dark, forbidden land to a world of bleak whiteness. He searched his surroundings, but Tingxue, Isabella, and even Firework were nowhere to be seen. 

In the distance were winding stairs formed out of white clouds that led into the sky. The world was empty, except for the cloud stairs. This appeared to be a sign for him to scale them. 

Zu An had experienced countless bizarre events in his life. Since there were no other clues, he decided to advance. 

So, he walked to the stairs, only to lurch in shock. He'd been mistaken. The stairs weren't made out of clouds, but skulls stitched together. It merely looked like clouds from afar. 

Unlike normal, gloomy skulls, these skulls emanated pristine white light as if they were the most exquisite works of art. It was just unnerving to see so many skulls gathered in one place.

As if sensing his arrival, the skulls suddenly turned to him and chanted, “Go up… Go up…”

Zu An was speechless.

Being the Netherworld’s master, this wasn't his first time seeing talking skulls, but it was terribly unsettling to be simultaneously stared at and talked to by tens of thousands of skulls. 

He took a breath to calm himself before stepping on the chanting skulls and making his way up into the sky. 

1. Green tea bitch refers to people who pretend to be innocent but are actually manipulative. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2773: Death’S Mystery
chapters.body:
Chapter 2773: Death’S Mystery

“Ugh! This fellow is very heavy.”

“Audacious, who dares to step on this exalted one’s head?”

“Wake up. You’re already dead!”

“What? I have conquered worlds and dominated universes. How can I possibly be dead?”

“His illness is flaring up again.”

The surrounding skulls shook their heads.

… 

Zu An’s head hurt from the skulls’ chattering. 

They had to have been top-notch experts back when they were still alive. They retained an imposing air despite being reduced to skulls, so much so that even their chattering was as potent as a barrage of mental attacks. Those who lacked mental fortitude would have long lost their minds from the simultaneous remarks of the tens of thousands of skulls.

Zu An pulled himself together. He considered using the Deity of Memory’s ‘Annals of History’ to banish these mental attacks into the River of History, but on second thought, the person at the end of these stairs could very well be antagonistic to Meng. It was best not to expose himself just yet. So, he channeled the Unshakable Daoist Manual to empty his mind as he continued scaling the stairs. 

As he advanced, his flesh and blood gradually disappeared. It didn’t take him long to be reduced to a skeleton. The impact between his bony feet and the skull stairs produced eerie clacking sounds.

Zu An remained composed. He didn’t feel any pain despite having been turned into a skeleton. His mind remained clear. As he inspected his current form with his divine sense, he couldn’t help but marvel at the machinations of fate. 

Qi Yaoguang had prophesied that he would be reduced to a skeleton. He'd thought that the prophecy had already been fulfilled at the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation, but who could have thought this would be it instead?

He had no idea how long he had been climbing for, as time seemed to lose its meaning here. Eventually, he arrived at the top of the stairs. 

At the top was a throne made out of white bones. But even calling it a throne felt like an insult, as no throne in the world was as domineering as it. Even the majestic skulls weaved into the stairs quivered in the face of the white bone throne, as if they were peasants before the emperor. 

The throne was empty, but the aura lingering on it was frightening. No one would dare take it lightly. 

Zu An was taken aback. He recognized that unique aura to be divinity. He'd encountered something similar when he met the Deity of Chaos and Enigma and the Deity of Memory. The nature of the auras was different, but they shared a similar air of divinity. 

“You’re here,” a dispassionate voice echoed. 

Zu An raised his head and saw a pair of red eyes in the sky. Each eye extended beyond the horizon. They were so huge that even planets looked insignificant before them, let alone individuals.

Somehow, Zu An didn’t feel pressured by the eyes; if anything, he felt some intimacy toward the other party. He found this feeling inexplicable.

Zu An bowed. “Are you the Deity of Death?”

“You know me?” The eyes looked at Zu An.

Zu An sighed. “I would have died a long time ago if I couldn’t even figure this out.”

He had met a Death envoy in the ocean, and later, he'd heard a lot about the Deity of Death. There were also the overly conspicuous skull stairs, as well as his subsequent transformation into a skeleton. Everything was related to Death.

The massive red eyes in the sky rippled. “It's been a long time since anyone called me that.”

It looks like the rumors about the Deity of Death’s demise are untrue, Zu An thought.

“I am dead,” a voice echoed from the sky.

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. Shit. I nearly forgot that universal deities can hear thoughts. Worried that the other party would read more of his thoughts, he quickly changed the topic and asked, “Can the Deity of Death really die, too?”

“Do you find it ludicrous?”

“I dare not.”

“It goes without saying that the Deity of Death can die as well,” the voice calmly replied. “Nothing is fairer in the world than Death. Be it the strong or the weak, the wealthy or the poor, all beings are bound for death. I may be the Deity of Death, but I am not beyond this law myself.”

Zu An felt as if he had an epiphany, but that feeling quickly slipped away from him. “I heard rumors that you were ambushed by the Deity of Annhilation, and that’s why you…”

 Since he was currently in the Deity of Death’s presence, he wasn’t worried about catching other universal deities’ gazes.

“The Deity of Annihilation is powerful, but he doesn’t have the means to kill me,” the Deity of Death replied proudly. 

Zu An was perplexed. But you’re dead.

The Deity of Death was enraged by his thought. “The Deity of Annihilation wasn’t the only one who made a move.”

Zu An was astonished. It sounds like a huge secret at play here. I hope it isn’t another riddle. So, he got to the point and asked, “Did the Deity of Annihilation join hands with other universal deities?”

“It wasn’t just one or two of them. All of them were involved.” The Deity of Death dropped a bombshell. 

Zu An was flabbergasted. He wouldn’t have been surprised if the Deity of Annihilation had a helper, but to think all the other universal deities were in on it. Wow, the Deity of Death must have been very hated. Good thing I didn’t use…

He quickly cut his thoughts short. 

“What are your thoughts on the universal deities?”

“They are the strongest entities in the Myriad Worlds, the end points of their respective paths. Do you think such omnipotent beings would accept death?”

Those words provided enough clues for Zu An to figure out the other universal deities’ motive for killing the Deity of Death. Beings who had reached the top would be even more reluctant to accept death. 

Only by slaying the Deity of Death, the embodiment of death, could one be eternal.

“We are fated.”

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. Damn! I’d have celebrated if I heard that before learning the truth. This is a huge fortuitous encounter, a chance to inherit the Deity of Death’s legacy. But the Deity of Death was lynched to death by other universal deities! I might end up dead before I know it if I get involved with this karma.

“This is fate. You can’t escape it.” The Deity of Death knew his thoughts but didn’t lose his temper. “I sense death energy from you. You’re a Deity of Death in a minor world.”

Zu An was stunned. Indeed, he was the Netherworld’s master in the World of Cultivation, so that technically made him a Deity of Death as well. 

“That was the reason I guided you here,” the Deity of Death said with a hint of excitement.

Zu An widened his eyes. It suddenly occurred to him that he was only able to enter this forbidden land because of Death Envoy Alati’s key… 

He'd thought this was all Firework’s scheme, but from the looks of it now, Firework was just an unknowing pawn in the Deity of Death’s karma. 

“Is that powerful demon spider your worshiper, too?” Zu An asked.

“No, she’s the gatekeeper of this forbidden land.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2774: An Impossible Trial
chapters.body:
Chapter 2774: An Impossible Trial

“Gatekeeper?” Zu An was startled. Wasn’t Firework sealed here? How did she become the gatekeeper?

“She’s also a pitiful one,” the Deity of Death calmly said. “She pledged allegiance to a certain universal deity to exact vengeance, but that resulted in the demonification of her body. In the end, she could only stay here to keep watch over me. She’s a prisoner as much as a warden.”

Zu An was taken aback. He hadn’t expected Firework to have such a background. So, which universal deity did Firework pledge allegiance to?

“Pardon my candid question. I understand she is strong, but does she truly wield the strength to keep watch on a universal deity?”

“I’m dead,” the Deity of Death stated impassively as if he wasn’t talking about himself. “I’m merely a sliver of consciousness, and her job is to keep that sliver from leaving this land.”

Zu An looked at the eyes in the sky. He’s a universal deity indeed. Even a sliver of his consciousness commands such great power. Just approaching him strips me of my flesh and blood. 

“May I ask which universal deity Firework pledged allegiance to that resulted in her demonification?” he asked.

“This information is too dangerous for you right now. The closer you approach, the easier it will be for you to land in her plight,” the Deity of Death warned.

Zu An’s heart quivered. The Deity of Death had divulged so much information to him that he'd nearly forgotten how dangerous secrets were. 

“If it’s not too rude, may I ask why you have summoned me here?” Since he had no inclination to remain here as a skeleton for the rest of his life, he had to advance the conversation. 

“We are fated. You’re the most likely candidate I have met in the past ten thousand years to inherit Death’s will. Are you willing to take on this legacy?” The pair of massive, elongated red eyes stared at him.

Zu An felt inexplicable pressure weighing on him. He meekly asked, “What will happen if I refuse?”

“You’ll become one of them.” The red eyes glanced at the stairs of skulls.

Zu An was dumbfounded. He immediately changed his tune and said, “It will be my utmost honor to receive the revered Deity of Death’s teaching.”

You must be kidding me!

The Deity of Death was lynched to death by other universal deities. If I inherit his will, I’d potentially draw the ire of other universal deities. But that will at least be a problem to deal with in the future. 

Right now, if I don’t accept the Deity of Death’s offer, I’ll die on the spot. It’s obvious what I should choose!

“You’re indeed fated with the path of Death. I shall open a trial for you. Clear it, and you shall inherit my legacy and the Dao of Death,” the red eyes in the sky said. 

Zu An was dumbstruck. “I have to do another trial? Can’t you just impart your legacy to me?”

“Of course not,” the red eyes sternly replied. “It’s the rule I set before my death. Not even I have the power to change it. What you’re inheriting is not just my legacy but the Dao of Death as well. You have to clear the trial to earn Death’s recognition.”

“What if I fail the trial?” Zu An frowned.

“You’ll become one of them.” The Deity of Death glanced at the stairs of skulls once more. 

Zu An was stunned. You must be kidding me! Even if I accept the trial, there’s still a huge chance of me becoming a foothold for others?

“Almost all of the skulls you see were powerhouses in their respective worlds. They coveted my legacy, but they failed the trial. Tread carefully. I have expectations for you. Don’t let me down,” the Deity of Death spoke concernedly.

Zu An stared at the endless stairs of skulls with his mouth agape. “So many people have challenged your trial?” Isn’t he sealed here? How did so many people find their way here?

“I may be dead, but Death persists. Death senses those inclined to the Dao of Death, no matter where they are, and guides them here.” The Deity of Death harrumphed. “There has been no lack of attempts from ambitious fools and spies from other universal deities to usurp my authority over Death, but they all became a part of that staircase.

“Hm? You seem intent on keeping your mind empty. Are you withholding something from me?” The Deity of Death suddenly eyed Zu An warily. 

“There’s no way I could hide anything from a revered universal deity like yourself,” Zu An hurriedly replied. “If you have expectations of me, why don’t you offer me some clues to help me out?”

“That’s out of the question.” The Deity of Death rejected the request.

Zu An gestured to the endless stairs of skulls. “You said these people were powerhouses from their respective worlds; their strength and wits are bound to surpass mine. If they all failed, no matter how arrogant I am, I can’t say that I have the confidence to clear the trial.”

The Deity of Death impatiently interjected, “What are you trying to say? This is Death’s trial. Obtaining the Deity of Death’s legacy means there’s a high chance you may grow to become a universal deity. Those who strive for such heights should be prepared to fall to their deaths.”

“Is there a chance this trial is impossible to clear? So many people have attempted it and failed. If there’s a way to clear the trial, someone should have found the method by now.”

“There is a way to clear this trial, but these people aren’t fated with Death, so they can only return to Death’s embrace.”

“When did you prepare this trial?”

“What do you mean?”

“Was it before or after you were ambushed by the other universal deities?”

The Deity of Death fell silent. Even though he was just a sliver of consciousness, it didn’t take him long to fathom a possibility. 

Zu An took this chance to push his case. “They ganged up on you to be freed from Death’s restraints. Why would they allow someone else to inherit your legacy?”

“You’re suggesting that they might have tampered with the trial?”

“I don’t know the other universal deities. You should know their temperament far better than me.” Zu An didn’t dare to gossip about other universal deities behind their backs, so he could only subtly lead the Deity of Death toward his conclusion.

The two elongated red eyes in the sky trembled. “Those motherfuckers! That sounds like something they are capable of! ￥%#@*&…”

What followed was a long string of furious curses. Zu An, despite having inherited Fleeting Baldie’s knowledge of languages, struggled to understand many of the phrases used, but he didn’t need to understand the language to figure out that there was a high concentration of expletives in the words. 

After cursing for a long time, the Deity of Death finally regained his composure and said, “There’s not much I can tell you due to the rules I set back then. You won’t be the only one entering the trial. Your three companions will be there as well…”

Zu An said, “…Firework is far stronger than me. How can I be a match for her if she enters too?”

“You don’t have to worry about that. I have taken that into consideration. That world limits all supernatural abilities.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2775: A Familiar World
chapters.body:
Chapter 2775: A Familiar World

“Forbids all supernatural power…” Zu An was surprised. 

He hadn’t expected the trial to have such a peculiar setting. They had cultivated for so long that they were already accustomed to their superhuman powers. It was unnerving to suddenly be robbed of their powers and reverted to mortal status.

“I have a question. You said that I was fated with death, so why are you bringing them into the trial as well?” Zu An was confused.

Putting aside the fact that having Firework in the trial would make things far more dangerous for him, he was also reluctant to put Isabella and Tingxue in danger. No one had been able to clear the trial thus far, so there was a huge chance of them becoming part of the skulls in the stairs.

I’m sure their skulls would be delicate and beautiful… Pui pui pui, I shouldn’t be thinking about that now!

The Deity of Death replied, “There’s a chance they could be favored by the Dao of Death. Not many people have been able to get here, and the path of Death needs an inheritor. To boost the chances of finding the right person, the trial must involve everyone who makes their way here.”

Zu An was speechless. You’re just scattering your net as wide as possible. But looking at the stairs of skulls, it feels like quite a lot of people have reached this place. 

He hurriedly cleared his mind and asked, “Is that all? Isn’t there more useful information?”

“I have made an exception by offering you two pieces of information. You should be satisfied!”

“What two pieces of information?!”

“One, the trial takes place in a world where supernatural powers are limited. Two, your companions will be participating in the trial as well.”

Zu An was stunned. He would have been cursing in his mind if he hadn’t known that the Deity of Death could read minds. The information offered was worthless!

“These two pieces of information aren’t helpful in the trial. Aren’t you looking for someone to inherit Death’s will? You can offer me more help since we’re fated.”

“That won’t do. The trial has to be impartial.”

“But the trial has likely been tampered with by other… entities! Challengers like me would be severely handicapped if we entered the trial without prior information. That goes against your principle of having a fair trial.”

The red eyes in the sky shook. Seconds later, he replied with a sigh, “I have met no lack of sharp-witted fellows who tried to gather information from me over the years, but you’re the first one to convince me. Alright, I’ll reveal another piece of information to you. I noticed that the world this trial is set in has some relation to you.”

Zu An was first overjoyed before confusion set in. “How is it related to me?”

The Deity of Death had prepared this trial to select his successor. He couldn’t fathom how the trial’s world could have anything to do with him.

Hold on, did he read my memories?

“The trial’s world is grounded in what I heard from a good friend. That friend was the death god of his respective world, and the stories about his world left a deep impression on me. So, when I rushed to create the trial’s world, I used his world as the backdrop.”

Zu An’s heartbeat quickened—it was bizarre how he still had such sensations even though he was only a skeleton now. “Was your friend called the Necropolis Emperor?”

“No, he’s known as Yama in his world.”

Zu An was stunned. There are indeed ten Yamas in the Netherworld. I wonder which one it was. It’s a pity all the Yamas and the ghost guards were gone by the time I took over the Netherworld, so there’s much I don’t know about it.

“He remains impartial before the world, thus establishing a fair order.”

A figure appeared in Zu An’s mind. But just as he was about to probe further, the Deity of Death said, “I have revealed too much. You have to enter the trial now, or else you’ll be sanctioned by the restriction I imposed back then.”

Zu An felt the world spin around him, as if he had been pulled into a massive temporal vortex. Some time later, everything fell silent. 

He found himself lying on a bed. He pulled the blanket off and breathed a sigh of relief after confirming he wasn’t a skeleton anymore. Despite retaining his sense of self as a skeleton, who could get used to such a body?

A feeling of weakness suddenly gripped him, and he nearly fell to the ground.

He had indeed lost his cultivation. He had considerable vitality compared to his peers, but he was indescribably weaker than his usual self. To make things worse, his massive divine sense had vanished. He now had to rely on his eyes and ears, and that made him feel crippled. 

He tried to execute his skills, but none of those overpowered moves could be used. He even tried summoning Mi Li, but there was no response. 

It makes me feel so helpless to be a mortal…

The only silver lining was that Firework had been turned into a mortal as well. She was bound to have lost more than him. That realization lifted his spirits a little.

He grabbed a copper mirror to scrutinize his appearance. When he saw that he still had his own face, he breathed a sigh of relief.

He proceeded to survey the surroundings. His room wasn't opulent, but it was tidy and well-furnished. This suggested that the identity he'd taken on in this world was from a decent background.

Hold on. Copper mirror? And this layout resembles medieval shows as well. Have I been transported into one of the older Chinese dynasties? No wonder the Deity of Death said the trial’s world was related to me.

To think that he could read my memories. I feel as if I was stripped bare before him. 

He suddenly remembered the impartial Yama whom the Deity of Death mentioned. It wasn't too difficult to figure out that Yama’s identity. 

The Song Dynasty’s Bao Zheng was known for his impartial and upright nature. The populace lauded him as Justice Bao. It was often rumored that he dealt with mortal cases in the day and interrogated souls at night. He was also believed to have become a Yama after his death, and there was indeed such an impartial figure in the Netherworld. 

Am I in the Song Dynasty?

Hold on. I've been to the Xia, Shang, Qin, and Tang Dynasties’ dungeons. If this is the Song Dynasty, could this be an Unknown Region, too? But this is the Deity of Death’s trial. Surely it can’t be that coincidental, right?

Just then, someone knocked on the door. “Third Young Master, are you awake? The old master and old madam are calling for you. They have something they would like to discuss with you.”

Zu An was surprised. Third Young Master? 

Who am I? Am I Sword God Xie Xiaofeng? No, Xie Xiaofeng isn’t from this dynasty. 

He opened his door, and the eyes of the servant outside lit up. “Third Young Master, you’re amazing. You recovered quickly after sustaining such severe injuries!”

“Was I injured…” Zu An was perplexed. He considered asking where he was and who his parents were, but he was worried he would give himself away. Thus, he decided to withhold his questions and let things run their course first.

Soon, he was brought to a remote room. As soon as he stepped in, a beautiful middle-aged woman grabbed his hand and worriedly asked, “Polu, how are your injuries?”

Zu An was speechless.

—

Guo Polu is a side character from Return of the Condor Heroes (a very famous Wuxia work in China that almost everyone knows about), and the story is set in the Song Dynasty.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2776: The Secret Plan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2776: The Secret Plan

While Zu An was heading over, he'd noticed wounded soldiers bandaging themselves and black smoke rising from the distant city center. The scent of blood and gunpowder lingered in the air.

That led him to think he was from a general clan, and they had just fought a war. Judging from the wounded soldiers all around, they appeared to have lost the battle.

But the beautiful middle-aged woman calling him ‘Polu’ made his mind come to a halt. That name was terribly familiar. The first thought that came to his mind was Guo Jing and Huang Rong’s son, Guo Polu. 

I thought dungeons emulated history. Why is there novel content in them as well?

On second thought, a universal deity could grasp all knowledge and culture about a world in an instant. It wouldn’t be surprising for them to combine different elements to create a unique trial. 

“I’m fine now,” Zu An replied. 

Had the middle-aged woman not mentioned it, he wouldn’t have noticed it. There were indeed parts of his body where blood flowed more slowly, but those could hardly be considered an injury. Even the lightest injury he'd sustained in recent years was far more severe than this. 

But it struck him that his current body was different from his usual superhuman one. He couldn’t compare them using the same standards. 

“You must have been diligently practicing Quanzhen Inner Energy[1] in recent years to have recovered so fast.” The stern middle-aged man sitting in the center chair finally revealed a heartened smile. 

“It could be the Nine Yin Manual[2]. I imparted it to him recently,” the middle-aged woman said.

“Rong’er, you dote on our children too much. It’s still too early for Polu to be practicing the Nine Yin Manual. It might work against him,” the middle-aged man replied sternly.

Zu An was silent, but that affirmed his guess.

The couple in front of him were Guo Jing and Huang Rong from the Legend of the Condor Heroes, and his identity was that of a side character who had only appeared on Guo Xiang’s birthday, Guo Polu.

Hold on, I thought supernatural powers were banned? Why is the setting a Wuxia world?

But then again, compared to the powers displayed in the Cultivation World and the Myriad Worlds, I guess martial arts are hardly supernatural abilities. They can, at most, be considered physical training. 

Taking a closer look, the couple before him was beyond middle-aged. Guo Jing’s hair was turning white. Huang Rong had maintained her complexion better, but wrinkles could be seen at the corners of her eyes. Time had still left its mark on her. 

Zu An couldn’t help remembering a famous phrase—’beauties are like famous generals; their gradual withering is not meant for the world’s eyes’. But then it quickly struck him that Huang Rong was now his mother, and that made him feel terribly awkward.

Huang Rong harrumphed. “The Mongols have already invaded the Central Plains. I ought to impart everything I can to him while I can.”

Guo Jing fell silent. The atmosphere in the room became grim.

Zu An was taken aback. He hadn’t expected to hear such depressing words from top experts in this world. 

Hold on. He suddenly remembered the reflection he'd seen in the copper mirror earlier. I should be in my twenties or early thirties in this world. Guo Jing and Huang Rong have been guarding Xiangyang[3] against the invading Mongols in the south. Could it be… 

“Guo’er won a decade of peace for us by assassinating the Mongol’s Möngke Khan[4], but the Mongols have made a comeback and have erected their base camp right outside Xiangyang. The imperial court’s resources have dried up from reinforcing Xiangyang. It’s unlikely we can turn the tide of the battle.” Guo Jing’s resolute eyes looked downcast. 

“Big brother Jing, you have already done your best. Xiangyang would have succumbed years ago if not for you. This time, the Mongols invaded Persia, cutting off the imperial court’s access to the Silk Road. On top of that, the Mongols are also harassing our southwest border over at Dali. Our other battlefronts are struggling. This is not a problem you can resolve alone,” Huang Rong consoled him. 

Guo Jing sighed. He understood all those things, but a defeat was still a defeat. In this case, no justification would make a difference. 

Huang Rong changed the subject and said, “Polu, Xiang’er, we called you here today to plant a seed for the future.”

It was only then that Zu An noticed a young lady sitting in the corner of the room. She had a delicate appearance, exuding an air of sweetness and valiance. She was Huang Rong’s second daughter, Guo Xiang, though they looked nothing alike. 

Of course they don’t look alike. Zu An looked at the young lady, who was staring intently at him, and chills ran down his spine. 

She was Firework! Just as Zu An's appearance hadn’t changed, Firework’s appearance remained the same as well. It turned out that they were all roleplaying here. 

“Second sister…” Zu An greeted Firework. 

“Heh. Yes, my good third brother.” The young lady chuckled.

Zu An was speechless. This sarcastic tone. No doubt about it. She’s Firework.

Huang Rong frowned. “What’s wrong with you two? Why do your tones sound so weird?”

Zu An and Firework were startled. Neither of them knew the criteria for clearing and failing this trial. There was a chance they would be eliminated if their identities were exposed. They didn’t dare risk it, so they decided to play their roles.

But Firework knew next to nothing about this world. She was still too shaken up from losing her immense powers to quickly adapt to the situation.

Fortunately, Zu An quickly changed the subject. “We were curious as to why our big sister isn’t here.”

Huang Rong shook her head. “Too much is at stake here; Fu’er is not sharp-witted enough to participate in this plan.”

Firework was impressed by Zu An’s swift reaction. He’s a sharp one. So, we have a big sister named Fu’er in this world?

Zu An had to hold back his laughter. I guess Guo Fu’s reputation is truly terrible. 

At the same time, he understood why the Deity of Death had said he had an advantage in this trial. Firework, Tingxue, and Isabella knew nothing about this world’s backstory, and that was bound to be to their disadvantage.

“All right, let’s get back to the topic at hand,” Huang Rong said. “We have tried everything against the Mongols in recent years, but we have no choice but to conclude that fate is siding with the Mongols now. The Central Plains’ downfall is set in stone. It’s nigh impossible to fight against fate.

“But fate will not side with them forever. Just as quickly as the grassland tribes have conquered the world on horseback, they will eventually fall off their high horses and lose the world. It might take decades or even centuries. The only thing we can do is to plant a seed for the future, so that our descendants can reclaim the world once the tides no longer favor the Mongols.”

“Our secret plan is named the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan[5]!”

1. The Quanzhen Sect is a nominally righteous daoist sect in the story, though they play an antagonist role. ☜

2. The Nine Yin Manual is one of the legendary skills in the series that even top experts seek to obtain ☜

3. Xiangyang was an important fortress city in the Song dynasty, used against the Mongols. ☜

4. Khan is the leader of the Mongolian tribes. ☜

5. This term is literally translated as ‘Following providence to slay the dragon plan’. The dragon that they are seeking to bring down here refers to the Mongols. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

Return of the Condor Heroes is set in the final years of the Song Dynasty, with elements of Wuxia. 

It follows the hero Yang Guo and the Guo family, who rise against the odds and become heroes who attempt to stop the inevitable downfall of the Song Dynasty to the Mongols. 

Yang Guo is the protagonist of the story. He was an orphan and was raised by the Guo family, but their relationship was beset with conflicts. 

From the Guo family, there are:

  • Patriarch Guo Jing + Matriarch Huang Rong (an honest but dumb man who is supported by his highly intelligent wife). They have three children:

    • Guo Fu (A willful and self-righteous woman)

    • Guo Xiang/Firework (Intelligent, adventurous, and outgoing)

    • Guo Polu/Zu An (Side character)

Power level-wise, Yang Guo and Guo Jing are among the Five Greats (S-tier), the five strongest experts in the series.

Huang Rong would probably be A-tier. 

Guo Fu, Guo Xiang, and Guo Polu are at most C-tier.

This arc’s timeline is right after Return of the Condor Heroes, and before Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber (which is another popular Wuxia novel from the same author).

chapters.title: Chapter 2777: Soul-Crushing Interrogation
chapters.body:
Chapter 2777: Soul-Crushing Interrogation

Zu An was stunned.

This plan had only been briefly touched on in the original series. A hundred years passed before it became known to the world. Zu An had never thought he would become a part of this plan.

“We shall follow heaven’s will and slay the dragon known as the Yuan Mongols!” Huang Rong said with a determined look. “Since ancient times, no dynasty has remained eternally strong. An empire built by nomads is bound to be unstable. So, we have to make preparations. We will tide through the darkness and bring light and order back to the world!”

Firework finally snapped out of her daze. “How do you intend to do that?”

Huang Rong frowned. Don’t you think your tone is rude here? 

She'd had a scare giving birth to Guo Xiang, so she favored Guo Fu a bit more. Guo Fu was dense, but she was caring and sweet-tongued as well. In contrast, Guo Xiang was eccentric, and it was hard to read her thoughts.

But ultimately, Guo Xiang was still her child… 

Thus, Huang Rong suppressed her displeasure and explained, “We intend to forge two godly weapons.”

Firework frowned. Her cultivation had been sealed, but she had the brains to figure out that a world devoid of supernatural powers couldn’t possibly make truly powerful weapons. To hope to change fate with just two mortal weapons was a pipe dream. 

Are the people of this world so shallow? And of all people, they had to be my parents. Can’t I kill them?

The intelligent Huang Rong keenly sensed Guo Xiang’s contempt. She was annoyed but relieved at the same time. Such an important responsibility can only be entrusted to someone as smart as Xiang’er. Fu’er is too dumb for this.

“Big brother Jing, why don’t you explain it to them?” The meticulous Huang Rong deferred to her husband out of respect. 

Guo Jing was more concerned about the success of the plan. “We’ll forge two weapons, the Heavenly Sword and the Dragon Slaying Saber. We’ll hide the Book of Wumu, which contains the crystallization of King Yue’s military insights, in the Dragon Slaying Saber. When the tides turn, the heroes of the world will rally the Han[1] to overthrow the Mongols. The Book of Wumu will teach them warfare, so that we can triumph in every battle.

“To prevent the Dragon Slaying Saber from falling into the wrong hands and scourging the populace, we’ll place the Nine Yin Manual into the Heavenly Sword. This way, we can nurture powerful martial artists capable of assassinating tyrants and corrupt officials, so that they will have to treat their subjects kindly.”

Guo Jing spoke passionately, as if he could already see the bright future ahead.

Huang Rong looked at Guo Jing gently. Despite being in his fifties, his heroic spirit remains as charming as ever. 

While the couple was feeling moved to tears, Firework suddenly interjected, “I have a question.” 

Guo Jing smiled. “Xiang’er, you are involved in this plan as well. Feel free to speak your mind. We should work out the kinks to increase our chances of success.”

Firework frankly asked, “You should be well-versed in the Book of Wumu, right… Father?”

Under normal circumstances, she wouldn’t have even glanced at this insignificant being. It killed her a little inside to call Guo Jing ‘father’. But this was the Deity of Death’s trial, and she had no idea what awaited her if she failed the trial, so she dropped her obstinacy.

“Of course,” Guo Jing replied right away.

“Then, why don’t you lead the army and win every battle against the Mongols?” Firework looked straight at Guo Jing with large eyes.

Guo Jing’s face turned bright red. He stuttered, “Perhaps due to my lacking aptitude, I’m unable to grasp its essence.”

Zu An was amused. Firework is delivering critical strikes with her questions. 

Huang Rong knew Guo Jing wasn't good with words, so she explained on his behalf, “You rascal. No matter how well-versed your father is with military tactics, our Great Song doesn’t have the resources to fight against the Mongols. As the saying goes, even a skilled housewife struggles to cook anything up without grains.

“Also, I said earlier that fate is siding with the Mongols. Their army is unstoppable right now. But a hundred years from now, when fate no longer favors them, the inheritor of the Book of Wumu will be facing a completely different situation.”

Entities like Firework had a deeper understanding of fate. Fate could make everything go smoothly, as if the world was coordinating things to ensure one’s success, but it could also spell the doom of even the mightiest heroes in the world. 

Huang Rong’s words rang true.

“Father and mother, you have learned the Nine Yin Manual. Why don’t you assassinate the Mongol tyrant?” Guo Xiang probed on. “If even the two of you can’t do that, there’s no guarantee the next person who learns the Nine Yin Sutra will be stronger than you. How can you expect them to be the executor of tyrants?”

Guo Jing and Huang Rong were speechless.

Guo Jing murmured in shock, “Rong’er, were we wrong? Is this plan doomed to fail right from the start? Is there no way to pass down the seeds of the Han?”

Upon seeing his heartbroken appearance, Huang Rong become agitated. “Xiang’er, you shouldn’t be talking nonsense for something as important as this!”

Firework calmly replied, “I'm not talking nonsense. I’m considering the feasibility of your plan.”

She had no idea what the criteria for clearing the Deity of Death’s trial was, but it had to be related to this plan. However, if it was that simple, someone would have succeeded by now. Naturally, she ought to understand every detail of the plan.

Huang Rong was angered by Guo Xiang’s talking back. She was starting to regret involving the latter in this plan.

It was then that Zu An interjected, “I think it’s feasible. Fate is siding with the Mongols now. Their armies are unrivaled, and many experts have pledged allegiance to them. No matter how strong our… father and mother are, it’d be hard for them to assassinate the Khan under the protection of so many experts.

“But a hundred years from now, when fate no longer sides with the Mongols, things won’t be as smooth for them. The people will rise up against them, and competent individuals will no longer side with them. Someone who wields the Heavenly Sword and has mastered the Nine Yin Manual will have room to do much more.”

Huang Rong was heartened. “Polu, you have always been reticent, so I didn’t expect you to have thought things through so clearly. Your father and I can finally put our hearts at ease.”

Firework spent a moment in thought before acknowledging Zu An’s explanation. “We have the Book of Wumu and the Nine Yin Manual. What about the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber?”

Her willingness to cooperate evoked a sigh of relief from Huang Rong. “It all falls on Guo’er.”

Huang Rong looked at Firework with sympathetic eyes and said, “Xiang’er, I know you have been traveling around in search of Guo’er. You’re already an old maiden now…” Had I known this would happen, I wouldn't have been so guarded against Guo’er back then.

Firework stayed silent. She had no idea what was going on or who Guo’er was at all. 

Huang Rong reined in her feelings and said, “We need your help here. You should also know that there have been conflicts between Guo’er and our family. You’re the only one he’s not averse to. We need you to persuade him to offer his Xuan Metal Sword to forge the godly weapons.”

1. The Han have been the dominant and majority ethnicity of ancient China across multiple dynasties. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

Some context to the grudge between the Guo Family and Yang Guo:

Guo Jing and Huang Rong were opposed to Yang Guo’s romance with Xiaolongnu because a master-disciple romance defies tradition. 

Huang Rong told Xiaolongnu that if she cared about Yang Guo, she shouldn’t let him carry a bad name, causing the couple to become estranged.

Later, Guo Fu severed Yang Guo’s arm.

Guo Jing was a sworn brother to Yang Guo’s father, but he and his wife later played a role in killing Yang Guo’s father because the latter had helped the Mongols against the Han.

Guo Jing took in and raised Yang Guo because he was sworn brothers with Yang Guo’s father, but Huang Rong didn’t like Yang Guo due to his father's identity and remained distant.

chapters.title: Chapter 2778: Do You Submit?
chapters.body:
Chapter 2778: Do You Submit?

Firework was confused, but Zu An understood the context. He stepped in and spoke up on her behalf. “Second sister has searched for Hero Yang for many years now, but to no avail. He might be avoiding her intentionally.”

Huang Rong looked at Firework with pity. “Guo’er and Miss Long faced many tribulations before their love finally bore fruit. They don’t want to be interrupted, which is why Guo’er has been avoiding Xiang’er. But if he sees you with your second sister, he should understand that Xiang’er is seeking him not to court him, but for more important business.”

With her sharp wits, it wasn’t too difficult for her to decipher Yang Guo’s intentions. She had refrained from voicing that aloud to spare her daughter’s dignity, hoping that the latter would back down after hitting a wall. She hadn’t expected her daughter to be this obstinate, devoting the golden years of her youth to searching for Yang Guo.

Firework finally grasped what was going on. So I’m just a total simp in this world? Audacious! There is no guy in this world who's worth me simping over!

She turned to Zu An. How does he know the background of this world? Did he arrive here earlier than me?

Huang Rong turned to Zu An. “Polu, I want you to accompany your second sister to Mount Zhongnan’s Tomb of Living Dead[1] to see if Guo’er is there. As long as he’s willing to meet you, I believe he should be willing to offer his Xuan Metal Sword for this plan.”

Zu An nodded. “Is there anything else I should do?”

“Your father and I will be devoting our energy to condensing the Nine Yin Manual and the Book of Wumu. The space within the two godly weapons will be limited, so we’ll have to pick our words carefully,” Huang Rong said with a frown. This was easier said than done.

Guo Jing instructed, “The Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan is confidential. You mustn’t let anyone else know about it.”

Huang Rong added, “Polu, I know you care greatly for your big sister, but you mustn’t let her know about this.”

Zu An was, once again, impressed by how low Huang Rong’s impression of Guo Fu was. 

After leaving the hidden chamber, Firework pulled Zu An over to her room. Guo Jing and Huang Rong smiled, heartened by the siblings’ good relationship.

Upon reaching her room, Firework pushed Zu An onto the bed before planting her foot in between his thighs. She looked down on him with a dangerous glint in her eyes.

Zu An remarked, “We're twins in this world. It will be terrible if others see us like that…”

Firework harrumphed. “Cut the crap and tell me what’s going on.”

Zu An carefully moved her foot aside before sitting upright. “Don’t you already know what’s going on?”

“Why did my cultivation suddenly disappear? This body is too weak!” Firework couldn’t get used to the immense nerf she'd suffered.

“You don’t know? This world forbids all supernatural powers.” Zu An was surprised.

“Why is there such a setting?” Firework frowned.

I guess the Deity of Death wasn’t lying to me, after all. The information he revealed to me beforehand is at least worth something. That lifted Zu An’s spirits a little. “It could be to make the trial fairer. We would be slaughted by you otherwise.”

“Do I look that scary to you?” Firework was displeased.

Shouldn’t you have some self-awareness? Zu An thought.

“Hold on. When did you enter this trial?” Firework asked. 

“We came in together,” Zu An replied in confusion, intrigued by the intent of her question.

“That can’t be. You know a lot more about this world than I do,” Firework said icily. 

Even though they'd been devoured by the white light at the same time, there were plenty of methods in this world to alter the flow of time, meaning Zu An could have spent years in here before her. 

“I was lucky. This world has some connection to my previous world,” Zu An replied. I wonder where Tingxue and Isabella are, and what identities they've taken on in this world.

“Oh?” Firework’s eyebrows shot up. “That old scamp Death opened a backdoor for you!”

Zu An found it hard to deny that. 

“What’s going on here? They mentioned Mongols, Great Song, and some Yang Guo fellow. Hurry up and fill me in on the details,” Firework urged.

Zu An looked at her weirdly. “Why would I do that?” 

Speaking of which, I still have no idea what the condition for clearing this trial is. Do I need to ensure the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber plan goes smoothly? But Firework appears to be in the same camp as me. 

If the plan goes smoothly, will I inherit the Deity of Death’s legacy or will she? Surely we can’t both inherit the legacy, right?

Also, those who failed the trial are doomed to become skulls on the stairs. What if Tingxue and Isabella aren’t in the same faction as me? Will they die if I clear the trial?

Firework clenched her fists, the sounds of her cracking knuckles echoing loudly. “Do you think you can defy me just because this world bans supernatural powers?”

Zu An chuckled. “Why should I obey you when this world bans supernatural powers?”

Bam!

A fist flew into his abdomen, smashing him into the wall.

“You didn’t give a warning! That’s so unsportsmanlike!” Zu An got up and fought back. 

He thought he'd have an advantage over Firework, as the latter would likely be unaccustomed to her mortal body given how strong she had been. Given they were the same age and had learned the same martial arts, he, with a man’s physique, was supposed to have the upper hand. 

They quickly traded ten blows. 

Zu An first started out confident. He possessed a Wonderpoint World’s World Law Beacon, and that granted him a deeper understanding over martial arts and world laws than even those who were far stronger than him. In fact, if they were pulled down to his level, he'd thought that he could rival even universal deities. 

But this was the moment he realized he was a frog in a well. 

Given his vast knowledge, it only took him the blink of an eye to assimilate all of Guo Polu’s martial arts. He could even exert strength far surpassing that of the original Guo Polu. 

But Firework didn’t pale in comparison. Her grasp of martial arts even appeared to be superior!

Zu An struggled right from the start, and several blows even breached his defense and struck him squarely. However, he wasn’t the type to give up easily. He gritted his teeth and continued trading blows with Firework while contemplating her moves. 

Some time later, Firework found an opening and pressed his hands down to the bed. 

“Do you submit?” Firework asked gleefully.

“I don’t!” Zu An wriggled his body. He was attempting to pincer her with his thighs to flip her over and pin her down. 

But Firework easily neutralized his attempts to turn the tables with precise movements. She even found a chance to pin him down completely, stopping him from making any moves.

“You should finally submit now, no?” Firework asked.

Now that she had lost her supernatural powers, her stamina was much lower; just the earlier fight was enough to make her breathless. Now that she had pinned him down, it suddenly struck her just how close they were. 

Just then, the door opened. “Xiang’er, Polu, why did mother call… What are you doing?”

1. This is the Ancient Tomb Sect’s base, where Xiaolongnu first met Yang Guo and trained him. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2779: Where’S The Risk?
chapters.body:
Chapter 2779: Where’S The Risk?

Chapter 2779: Where’s the Risk?

A gorgeous lady with crescent eyes and pink cheeks stood by the doorway. 

She wore an extravagant blue damask[1] shirt with leopard skin at the collars. Such clothes would have appeared excessive on most women, but not her. On the contrary, it felt as if only such luxurious clothes were worthy of her beauty. 

Zu An swiftly realized she was Guo Jing and Huang Rong’s oldest daughter, Guo Fu. It was commonly said that she had inherited her father’s head and her mother’s beauty. 

Guo Fu’s mind had frozen up. She'd come here to scout the situation, curious to know why her parents had called her siblings but not her. She hadn’t expected to stumble upon this sight as soon as she opened the door.

Firework was sitting on Zu An’s body, her hands pinning down his. As there was no longer a vast disparity in their strength, she needed to exert her full force to keep him in place. As a result, their bodies were pressed against each other. Their faces were so close to each other that it looked as if they would kiss the next second. 

What are they doing? I thought Xiang’er liked Yang Guo? 

Maybe she’s feeling lonely from years of failing to court Yang Guo, so she's seeking other men for company. But most men don’t even catch her eye, so she must have decided to go for her little brother instead.

Countless thoughts surged into Guo Fu’s mind, causing her already dull brain to stall to a halt. 

Firework and Zu An had been too busy fighting to pay heed to their positions. It was only when they saw Guo Fu’s horrified expression that they realized how inappropriate their current posture was.

Firework suddenly became aware of Zu An’s air of masculinity. His heat was palpable even through their clothes. 

Meanwhile, Zu An clearly felt Firework’s shapely and youthful body. Even though Firework had never shied away from physical contact, this was the first time they had gotten into such an intimate posture. 

The two of them quickly backed off. Firework awkwardly tidied her clothes and hair. “This rascal refused to listen to me. I was teaching him a lesson.”

She looked at Zu An tauntingly. “Do you submit?”

Zu An harrumphed. “Of course not. We’ll have a rematch another day.”

Despite his words, he was impressed with Firework. Her grasp of combat was at a transcendent level. Their brief clash had taught him much more than his fights with other experts. 

Firework was also impressed by Zu An. She had spent some time with him now, and that gave her a good grasp of his strength. Even though he had several interesting skills, she hadn’t thought he was much of a fighter, especially considering his low cultivation.

While this world forbade supernatural powers, it wasn't as if she had been born strong. She had climbed a step at a time, undergoing countless life-and-death battles before she finally reached her current level. Her higher cultivation also granted her a deeper insight into the utilization of strength.  

She'd thought that she could easily subdue Zu An even without tapping into her supernatural powers, but the battle turned out to be a surprisingly close one. How long has it been since I had such a tough battle?

Looking at how composed those two were, Guo Fu’s face reddened. How can I think of my siblings in such a light? What a sin! But she wasn't the type to admit her mistakes, so she put on a stern appearance and reprimanded them. “You aren’t young anymore. You should watch your boundaries even when fighting with each other. What if others see you like that?”

Firework’s eyebrows shot up. A mere ant dares talk to me like that?

Zu An quickly stepped in to clear the air. “Big sister, what brings you here?” He was worried Firework would murder Guo Fu in a moment of recklessness. That would turn their mission into hell mode. 

“I heard that father and mother called you over. Did something happen? Why didn’t they call me as well?” Guo Fu sounded displeased. 

“We made a breakthrough recently, so father and mother called us over to review our martial arts,” Zu An explained. “Big sister, are you interested in training? Shall I talk to them on your behalf?” 

“Forget it.” Guo Fu waved her hand, not wanting to suffer by practicing martial arts. Zu An’s explanation had dispelled her curiosity. Worried that her younger siblings would see through her cowardice, she put on her big sisterly airs and instructed, “If you want to spar, do it in the training field. It’s unseemly to fight in the bedroom.”

She turned around and left, but Zu An hurriedly asked, “Big sister, where’s brother-in-law?”

“He’s too busy dealing with the Beggar Sect’s[2] affairs to accompany me. How annoying,” Guo Fu grumbled before leaving.

Firework asked, “Why did you suddenly ask about our brother-in-law?”

Someone who could grow to her extent couldn’t possibly be dumb. She was already getting into the flow of things. She knew Zu An wouldn’t ask an insignificant question.

Zu An hesitated, but considering they were in the same boat, he figured he should still share critical intelligence. “Our brother-in-law is Yelu Qi. His father, Yelu Chucai, was a member of the Liao royal family. When the Liao Country fell, they were left to wander the world. Yelu Chucai later became a powerful Mongolian official, wielding influence comparable to a prime minister.”

Firework was taken aback. “You’re saying they'll betray Great Song to join the Mongols?”

Zu An shook his head. “I don’t know. This is a world created by the Deity of Death, so it might not necessarily follow the trajectory of history. Yelu Qi doesn’t exist in real history, either.”

“That can’t be said for sure. You’re a Memory worshiper, so you should be well-versed with the tricks Time Scribes use. When a character doesn’t appear in history, they're either unimportant, or have been erased for some reason,” Firework sneered.

Zu An was also perplexed as to where the true danger was. The plan didn’t look too difficult to pull off, so it was baffling to him why everyone who came before him had failed. 

Does the trial have nothing to do with whether the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan succeeds? Or is there some compelling reason causing the plan to fail?

He racked his brain and concluded that the biggest threat was the Yelu Family. Yelu Chucai had to have contributed greatly to the Mongols to have become such a powerful official in the future.

The Yelu Family’s close ties with the Guo Family made it likely for them to learn about the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan. In fact, Zu An even suspected Guo Fu’s motive for probing them for information. 

Guo Jing and Huang Rong had secretly called them over to not alert Guo Fu, yet she'd still learned about it. Did Yelu Qi instigate her to gather information from the Guo Family, and she foolishly did so without knowing she was being exploited?

1. A woven patterned silk fabric ☜

2. The Beggar Sect is a righteous power that helped the Song Dynasty resist the Mongols. It is a massive sect, consisting of beggars across cities that form a huge intelligence network. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2780: Variables
chapters.body:
Chapter 2780: Variables

“Do you think the key to the trial is completing the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan?” Zu An asked Firework. She was highly intelligent, evident from how she had woven an elaborate scheme and played them like a fiddle. 

“It’s probably not that simple.” Firework returned the favor since Zu An had offered her some intelligence. “Think about it. How can the Deity of Death choose between us if the plan is completed? I’ve never heard of a universal deity’s legacy being shared between two people.”

“Could it be based on contribution? Whoever contributes more wins,” Zu An asked. 

Instead of answering the question, Firework asked, “You should know that those who fail the trial will face death, right?”

“Indeed.” As he recalled the stairs of skulls, Zu An’s heart became heavy. 

“If your contribution theory is true, those who are going to lose have no reason to help others. They will try their best to drag others down and foil the plan. It’s more satisfying for everyone to fail together than to let one person win,” Firework said coldly.

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. That’s true. One can never underestimate what humans are capable of when pushed into a corner.

“We might appear to be in the same boat, but that doesn’t mean we’ll be able to join forces.” Firework paused before continuing coldly, “Only one can clear the trial. Those who fail the trial are doomed to die.”

Zu An’s heart was chilled. Tingxue and Isabella are here as well. Will we have to eventually turn against one another? But he quickly calmed down and said, “There should be a way out. I’ll find a way for all of us to survive.”

Firework scoffed, “What naivete. When you try to save everyone, it’s more likely that you won’t save anyone at all.” 

Despite her skepticism, there was a small part of her that trusted him. She had witnessed his tenacity in tackling and overcoming seemingly impossible situations… But she quickly snuffed out that thought. This is a universal deity’s trial. There’s no way to bend the rules. 

“Let’s work together. Even if we’re destined to become enemies, it’s still beneficial for us to join forces for now.” Zu An didn’t want to engage in infighting. There was no way he could clear this trial if someone as smart and strong as Firework was determined to sabotage him.

“What’s the point of such superficial partnership? I’d rather not work together if we’ll eventually have to turn against each other,” Firework said coldly as she stood up and left. She intended to clear the mission by herself.

“What if I find a way for us all to survive?” Zu An shouted.

Firework stopped by the doorway. Without turning around, she scoffed, “Empty promises hold no meaning.”

“It’s not an empty promise,” Zu An said. “I am very familiar with this world. That’s an advantage no one else has ever had.”

“That only means you have a higher chance of clearing this trial; it doesn’t mean you can save the others.” Firework finally turned around, revealing a dangerous gleam in her eyes. “Now that I think about it, you’re my greatest rival here. I ought to kill you first.”

She might have lost her supernatural powers, but given her grasp of martial arts, she could still pull it off.

Zu An’s expression darkened. “You’ve overlooked something crucial. Not everyone would coincidentally stumble upon this trial with their comrades. Why do you think they still failed?”

Firework widened her eyes and fell into deep thought. 

Such opportunities are hard to come by. Not everyone who entered this trial would have been friends, and some of them might have even been alone. The latter would have been highly beneficial, as the challenger wouldn’t have to worry about being sabotaged.

But that only made it even more baffling as to why no one had cleared the trial to date. 

Zu An continued, “You already know that the other universal deities ganged up on the Deity of Death and killed him. It should be safe to assume that they don’t wish to see the rise of another Deity of Death. The easiest way to do so would be to tamper with the trial.”

“That’s possible. Those fellows are capable of that,” Firework narrowed her eyes and sneered. She appeared to harbor animosity against the universal deities. “But that only makes the trial harder to clear. It just shows that it’s more likely for the four of us to die, not for the four of us to survive.”

Zu An shook his head. “Other universal deities tampering with this trial introduces variables, giving rise to more possibilities.”

Firework was silent for a few seconds before finally conceding with a nod. “There is some sense to your words. I hope you do find a way to ensure everyone’s survival.”

Zu An and the other two women had, to some extent, helped her. While she wouldn’t sacrifice herself to save them, if there was a way for all of them to survive, she wasn’t inclined to let them die, either. 

She quickly added, “Our priority should be to find Tingxue and Isabella, right?”

Zu An shook his head. “This might just be a trial world, but it’s still a huge place. Searching for them here without supernatural powers is as good as searching for a needle in a haystack.”

“Are you going to abandon them?”

“Of course not. We’re in the Deity of Death’s trial; I’m sure fate will somehow bring us together. What’s more important is for us to find Yang Guo and obtain his Xuan Metal Sword.”

“You’re more familiar with this world. Where should we look for them?”

“The Beggar Sect has a huge intelligence network, with eyes and ears all over the world. However, Yelu Qi is the current sect leader. We’ll risk feeding crucial information to him if we go through the Beggar Sect.” Zu An thought about it before adding, “Let’s make a trip to the Tomb of Living Dead first.”

“What kind of place is that?”

“Pack your things. I’ll explain it to you on the way.”

The two of them quickly made preparations before bidding farewell to Guo Jing and Huang Rong and secretly leaving Xiangyang City. 

Looking at the huge army of Mongol soldiers stationed opposite the river, Firework grimly remarked, “The Mongols are indeed at the peak of their power. That far surpasses the troops we have at Xiangyang. If not for the river, Xiangyang wouldn’t have lasted this long.”

“Our destination is part of Mongolian territory. Let’s disguise ourselves first to avoid trouble,” Zu An suggested. 

Firework replied, “There’s no need for that. I might have lost my supernatural power, but I am strong enough to protect myself in this world. Not even Guo Jing is a match for me.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2781: Eternal Pain
chapters.body:
Chapter 2781: Eternal Pain

Zu An was speechless. But he had no objection to that. The so-called martial arts experts in this world had a shallow understanding when it came to utilizing energy. 

For instance, by tapping into ‘Guo Polu’s’ memories, he'd immediately grasped the Quanzhen Sect, Peach Blossom Island, and Beggar Sect’s inner energy methods and could even use them at a higher efficiency.

Less needed to be said about martial arts moves. He understood the openings in every move with just a single glance, not to mention he could still employ the moves he already knew, it was just that they were no longer reinforced by ki.

While supernatural powers were restricted in this world, they still retained their memories and judgment. Even the strongest skill in this world, the Nine Yin Manual, was easy for him to grasp. Guo Jing and Huang Rong thought it was beyond him, but he had already grasped the gist of it.

The Nine Yin Manual was ingenious, living up to its reputation as a supreme martial art in this world. Unfortunately, this world was limited by the absence of ki, so those dwelling in this world could never hope to reach the level of a cultivator.

To higher entities like them, the Nine Yin Manual was like an innovative application of elementary school level concepts to resolve rudimentary problems. As ingenious as it was, these rudimentary problems could be easily circumvented upon becoming a higher being. Due to that, the manual wasn't noteworthy in the Myriad Worlds. 

That said, it wasn't completely useless. The restriction of supernatural powers meant Zu An couldn’t use most of his techniques. The Nine Yin Manual allowed him to maximize what was possible with his current mortal body. 

Firework, with her deeper grasp of energy utilization, was likely more skilled at modifying the Nine Yin Manual to maximize her strength. With their combined prowess, unless they foolishly marched into the enemy camp, it was unlikely anyone could pose a danger to them. 

…Murphy’s law struck, however.

Firework suddenly decided to scout the Mongolian camp without any warning. Zu An hurriedly followed her, worried that a mishap would befall her.

It wasn’t tough for them to sneak into the camp. They snuck into a watch tower and made quick work of the sentries inside before examining the camp’s layout. 

In the end, Firework shook her head and sighed. “If they were in a normal mansion, I could have easily assassinated their general, but in a camp like this, even if I succeeded in my assassination, I would still end up getting surrounded and killed by the enemy soldiers.” 

Killing the enemy commander would have been the most efficient way to alleviate Great Song’s crisis, but she wasn’t so noble as to lay down her life for that cause.

“You shouldn’t let your guard down. A few of the Five Greats[1] of this world once snuck into the Mongols’ barracks and nearly lost their lives after being encircled.” 

If they were at their usual level, Zu An and Firework could have massacred the enemy troops, but with their strength capped, brute force wouldn’t work.

“You rascal! How many more things have you not told me?!” Firework glared at Zu An. 

After they agreed to work together, Zu An had shared the background of this world with her, but here he was, dropping a piece of information she knew nothing about!

“I’m not intentionally withholding information from you. This scene just so happened to trigger my memories. People like us have been through so much that it’s hard to remember everything at hand,” Zu An replied. 

They were sticking close to each other so they could speak as softly as possible, lest they draw attention to themselves. They were so close that Zu An could feel Firework’s breathing and smell a light fragrance coming from her.

Zu An’s heart was momentarily moved, but he quickly remembered that she was a spider demoness. What am I thinking?

“There’s something weird about your gaze.” Firework frowned. It’s uncomfortable not having my supernatural powers, or else I could have sensed his thoughts.

Zu An coughed. “Let’s retreat. We should prioritize finding the Xuan Metal Sword.”

Firework ignored him. “Do you think we could clear the trial if we circumvent the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan and move straight to assassinating the Mongolian Khan?”

She could already tell that there were many ways the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan could go awry, and it would be best to circumvent it.

Zu An jolted in shock. “Don’t be reckless. Fate is favoring the Mongols right now. Many experts are serving the Mongolian Khan. It would be nigh impossible to pull it off even if we join forces; it’d be no different from courting death.”

Firework harrumphed. “Coward.”

She kicked one of the dead sentries, and the latter flew out of the watchtower and crashed into the ground.

Zu An was stunned.

That naturally caused an uproar. Many soldiers rushed over right away. Firework fearlessly leaped down the watchtower and valiantly fought the soldiers. 

Zu An thought that it was a foolish move, but he had to admit that her valiance was charming, like a rose blooming on the battlefield. Her disposition resembled Yani’s. Perhaps this was her true personality.

He cut his thoughts short and joined the battle.

More soldiers arrived, but the two of them were no ordinary experts. Their judgment and fighting prowess far surpassed this world’s Five Greats, and there was a natural rapport between them. They covered each other’s blind spots and protected each other from harm. They managed to slaughter a path out of the Mongolian camp and escape. 

The Mongolian soldiers were startled. The general hurriedly wrote a letter to report the incident to his superiors. 

Zu And Firework fled into a stream. They'd survived the crisis, but due to the restrictions on their supernatural powers, they had still incurred considerable injuries.

While Firework was cleaning her injuries, Zu An grumbled in displeasure, “That was very rash. We could have snuck away quietly.”

They could have slipped away without alarming anyone, but Firework just had to kick the sentry out of the watchtower and draw the attention of the Mongolian soldiers. 

“You know nothing at all. Do you know the first thing a tiger ought to do upon arriving in a foreign environment?” Firework scoffed. Before Zu An could speak a word, she continued, “It’s to pick a fight with the animals there. Only then will it know its standing in the new environment.”

Zu An had told her about the Five Greats and the power levels in this world, but those were theoretical. The easiest way to know what she was up against was to get into a fight.

She had wanted to spar with Guo Jing, but the latter was her ‘father’ and could suspect something if she suddenly became strong enough to beat him up. So, she'd instead decided to pick a fight with the Mongols to gain a rough grasp of her standing. 

Zu An looked at her with pity. “You’re instinctively thinking like a wild beast even though you used to be a human. What happened?”

Firework’s nonchalant expression warped in fury. She flitted to him, grabbed his neck, and exclaimed, “You bastard! Where did you hear that from?”

1. The five strongest people in this world, with two of them being Guo Jing and Yang Guo ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2782: Woo Her
chapters.body:
Chapter 2782: Woo Her

Zu An hadn’t expected such an intense reaction. He quickly pushed her hand aside and retreated. “Are you out of your mind?”

Firework didn’t chase him, but she glared at him icily and asked, “How did you know?”

“The Deity of Death told me before I entered this trial,” Zu An replied. “He said you were a pitiful person. What drove you to make such a huge sacrifice?”

“What a joke! Someone who died is pitying me?” Firework harrumphed. “You’re in no place to pity me. Don’t blame me for getting nasty if you dare mention that again.”

She pulled down her clothes and stomped away. 

Whatever happened back then must have hurt her greatly. If she ever opens up about that to me, it would mean that she's finally opened her heart to me and considers me a true friend. 

“How long did you guard the forbidden land?”

“None of your business.”

“It must have been a long time. Did you feel lonely?”

“Hmph. You must have forgotten about my ability. I can have countless clones. I can single-handedly create a country, so how could I possibly be lonely?”

“But they're all you. Wouldn’t the bustle just make you feel emptier?”

“You darned brat! Are you asking for a beating?”

“There’s nothing much to do on the way, so I figured we could chat.”

Firework suddenly turned to him and asked, “Why don’t you talk about your past then?”

“Sure. What do you want to know?” Zu An smiled radiantly, showing his teeth.

Firework harrumphed. “How many wives do you have?”

“…” Zu An hadn’t expected this to be her first question, but he still answered it. “I have an official wife whom I properly wedded, but I have lovers as well.”
