He suddenly fell into a daze. Come to think about it, I did go through the marriage ceremony with the ancient Mermaid Queen after she became the Chang clan’s Saintess…

Firework continued asking, “Is the person who resembles Tingxue your official wife?”

Zu An replied, “No, she’s my wife’s master.”

“Oh?” Even though Firework was still fuming just a moment ago, Zu An’s response piqued her interest. “Tell me what happened.”

Zu An was speechless. The desire to gossip is innate even for ridiculously powerful entities. However, he didn’t mind talking about himself, since he sought reconciliation. He told her about how Yan Xuehen had tried to kill him to end her disciple’s secular attachments, as well as how they'd eventually gotten together. 

Firework was thoroughly entertained. Halfway through the story, she suddenly asked, “She spared you because you used that ‘Love is More Solid Than Gold’ skill, right?”

Zu An sheepishly replied, “I knew I wouldn’t be able to hide it from you. I was cornered and couldn’t find any other way out, so I could only resort to such despicable methods.”

“It’s not despicable. All is fair in a fight for survival. It’d be foolish to withhold your skills when your life is on the line,” Firework said with a smile. All of a sudden, her expression darkened. “Did you secretly use that skill on me as well?”

Zu An rolled his eyes. “I already told you I didn’t. Why would I have revealed that skill if I planned to use it against you?”

“It could be reverse psychology,” Firework sneered. 

“So, are you in love with me?” Zu An asked. 

Firework was stunned. Perhaps she'd arrogantly declared that she would make him her plaything, but that was because she had a favorable impression of him after spending some time together. He was merely different from other guys.

An entity like her wouldn’t fall in love with anyone. 

Zu An continued, “You don’t need to worry about that skill—it isn’t omnipotent. It’s not just because of that skill that Xuehen and I got together. Many things happened in between that led to our eventual union.”

Firework heaved a sigh of relief. “That better be the case. I’ll say it upfront. If you dare to use that skill against me, I’ll rip you to shreds even if I fall in love with you. Your lovers will be next.” 

Zu An’s expression turned cold. “You need not worry about that. I have no interest in spiders.”  He flung his sleeves and walked away.

Even though he had expressed goodwill for the sake of working together, Firework had still threatened not just him, but his loved ones as well. That was beyond what he could tolerate. If that was going to be the case, he would rather not work with her. 

“Spider?” Firework was so maddened that one could almost see steam puffing from her nostrils. If not for the restriction on supernatural powers, she would have shown Zu An the true horror of spiders.

You have successfully trolled Firework for +444… +444… +444…

Shortly after they parted ways, Zu An sensed footsteps behind him. He turned around and frowned. “What are you doing here?”

Firework beamed at him. “Don’t be so stingy. I came back because I was worried about you.”

Zu An frowned. She’s speaking in that girlish voice she used when we first met again. “Which one is the real you? Aren’t you worried that you might catch schizophrenia if you keep switching between the two?”

“My mind isn’t that weak, and these are all my real personalities,” Firework smiled. “Which one do you prefer?”

“I prefer sincerity.” Zu An couldn’t tell which of her personalities were real, and he couldn’t be bothered to figure that out either. 

“I’m already being very sincere by offering to make you my plaything. I don’t even spare a glance at most men.” Firework jogged to his side. “Aren’t you going to consider me? Having me is as good as having all the women in the world. I can turn into anyone you want.”

Hmph, you brat. I’ll make you fall in love with me hard before dumping you. Let’s see how arrogant you’ll be then!

Zu An looked at her and asked, “You don’t know the way to the Tomb of Living Dead, do you?”

Firework’s smile stiffened up. “What a tactless man you are. How did you get so many women to fall in love with you?”

“Because we treat one another with sincerity.”

Firework snorted. “Ridiculous. That's just what simps say to comfort themselves. I don’t believe that you haven't met a woman who tried to exploit your feelings for her.”

Zu An shook his head. He couldn’t be bothered to waste his breath. 

Firework continued prattling along the way. It felt as if the two of them had returned to the time when they first met. They traveled for half a month before reaching the foot of Mount Zhongnan.

Much to their surprise, many Mongol martial artists were gathered around the perimeter of Mount Zhongnan. From their conversations, Zu An learned that a Mongol prince had learned that there was a ravishing beauty at Mount Zhongnan and wanted to woo her. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2783: Exposed
chapters.body:
Chapter 2783: Exposed

Firework nudged Zu An with her shoulder. “Are they talking about Xiaolongnu? You told me she was the number one beauty in this world.”

Zu An scoffed, “Xiaolongnu is beautiful, but think of her age. Even if she maintains her appearance well, I don’t see how she can make a Mongol prince go to such an extent for her.” 

Meeting the aged Huang Rong had driven home the fact that Xiaolongnu wasn’t young anymore for him. 

“Then who’s the beauty they’re talking about?” Firework was intrigued. 

“I don’t know.” Zu An was confused. 'Return of the Condor Heroes' started with Huodu[1] bringing an army of experts to the Chongyang Palace to request marriage with Xiaolongnu. To think that history would repeat itself decades later. 

He knew that he was in a trial world and the events were likely fabricated, but it still left him feeling wistful.

“I’m curious about that woman now. How beautiful must she be to drive a prince to do this?” Firework suddenly thought about something. “Could it be Tingxue or Isabella?”

Putting the gorgeous Tingxue aside, Isabella was beautiful as well. The Mongol prince could be a lolicon[2].

“Let’s take a look.” Zu An’s eyes lit up. It would be great if we could reunite with the two of them.

The two of them began scaling Mount Zhongnan. Along the way, they saw buildings covered in algae and Quanzhen disciples with dead looks in their eyes. The Quanzhen Sect used to be the number one sect in the world, but they've fallen into decline. 

The Quanzhen Sect had previously fought against the Mongols, but its disciples had now grown accustomed to seeing Mongol soldiers strutting in and out of the Quanzhen Palace. Mount Zhongnan must have fallen into Mongol hands, leaving the Quanzhen Sect with no choice but to surrender to the Mongols. 

“Quanzhen Sect’s teachings are very aligned with cultivation. Its founder must have been a formidable individual. It’s a pity this world is devoid of ki, so his cultivation methods are only theories at most,” Firework said. This was her evaluation from eavesdropping on bits and pieces of the disciples’ training along the way. 

“Their founder, Wang Chongyang, is an amazing man. He’s the only one whom the Five Greats recognized as number one,” Zu An replied. 

“Ah, so he’s the Central Divinity[3]. He’s not all talk,” Firework said, though it wasn't a very high evaluation.

The two of them secretly followed the trail of the Mongol soldiers and soon arrived at the mountain behind the Quanzhen Sect. 

Firework asked in confusion, “Is the Ancient Tomb Sect located on the mountain behind the Quanzhen Sect?”

“It is.” Zu An found the irony in the situation amusing. They were searching for the Xuan Metal Sword to deal with the Mongols, but it just so happened there was a group of Mongols here, too.

There was an opulently dressed young man surrounded by a group of Mongol soldiers. The young man shouted at the mountainous slope, bragging about how noble his liege was and imploring the lady to come out and meet him.

“That prince appears to be the incumbent Mongol Khan’s most doted-on son, their future ruler?” Firework swiftly grasped the gist of the message.

Zu An nodded. “He seems to be Kublai Khan’s son, Prince Yan Zhenjin.”

After the previous Mongol Khan died in battle, Kublai Khan had taken over his mantle. Zhenjin was the oldest son he had with his empress, and was currently the most likely candidate to inherit the imperial throne.

To think such a person would travel southward in search of some beauty. With his stature, he should be able to get all the beauties he wants in the world. 

Firework was thrilled. “If we capture this fellow, won’t Xiangyang be saved? We won’t have to carry out that nonsensical Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan anymore.”

She keenly sensed that a huge pitfall was awaiting them if they carried out the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan, and that it would be best if they went down a different path.

Zu An frowned. “He’s bound to have many experts accompanying him. We shouldn’t make a reckless move.” 

He could already sense several strong auras around the prince. Unfortunately, his senses were too suppressed here to gauge their strengths from afar. 

“Hmph, you’re too indecisive. Wait here. I’ll settle it,” Firework sneered as she strutted forth.

Zu An couldn’t stop her, though he was also curious to know what her plan was. 

Instead of charging in recklessly, Firework calmly made her way over. Even though her features were sweet, her strut was graceful and seductive, reminiscent of Dreamland’s Diodora. 

Zu An was amazed. She wasn't boasting when she said she could play all kinds of women. That’s probably how she grasped her ability. 

Firework's approach alarmed the Mongol soldiers, and they instinctively reached for their swords. However, upon seeing it was a beauty, they breathed a sigh of relief. 

“Miss, who are you looking for?” a middle-aged Quanzhen daoist asked. 

This was the Quanzhen Sect’s turf. It would spell disaster for them if a mishap were to befall Prince Zhenjin here. 

Judging from his clothes, he should be in the upper echelons of the Quanzhen Sect, perhaps from the ‘Zhi’ generation[4].

Zu An had previously introduced some of the key members of the Quanzhen Sect to Firework, but she couldn’t be bothered to remember them.

“You’re asking who I am looking for? Weren’t you the one looking for me? I’m here now. You can state your business with me.”

The crowd exchanged looks. They'd followed their prince here in search of a ravishing beauty, and looking at Firework… She was indeed worthy of being called that. 

The Mongol prince was indeed drawn to her. “You’re as beautiful as the rumors say…”

A few Mongols traded conflicted looks. This woman doesn’t fit the description of the woman our prince is looking for… but our prince was throwing a tantrum at being ignored earlier, so it might be good to have a substitute.

As the Mongol prince walked over, Firework’s smile brightened. He has some experts accompanying him, but as long as he takes three steps closer, I’ll be able to hold him hostage. Those experts won’t be able to do a thing at all.

It was then a Mongol soldier suddenly spoke up in their native tongue, causing Prince Zhenjin to halt his footsteps. An expert stepped in front of him and glared at Firework, saying, “You’re the girl we held hostage back when Möngke Khan died!”

Firework was confused. She had no idea what had given her away. 

Zu An facepalmed. He hadn’t expected veteran Mongol soldiers who participated in the Xiangyang battle many years ago to be here.

Back then, Guo Xiang had been tied up atop a platform to serve as a hostage. Given her appearance, it was hard for anyone who had seen her to forget her. The death of Möngke Khan by a flying stone only made that incident more memorable. 

“That’s the girl the Golden Wheel Reverend wanted to take as his disciple? Her physique is indeed extraordinary. Girl, why don’t you come under me? Let’s study Esoteric Buddhism’s Pleasure Tantra[5] together.” An old lama[6] dressed in a red kasaya robe stepped forward.

1. A Mongol prince who serves as an antagonist in the story. ☜

2. Lolicon is a Japanese term referring to people attracted to smaller-sized girls, though they don’t necessarily have to be children. ☜

3. The Five Greats are named with a direction and a descriptor, such as East Heretic and North Beggar. ☜

4. Those in the same generation carry the same character in their names. ☜

5. The proper translation is Annutarayoga Tantra. A portion of this tantra celebrates sex between a male and female practitioner, though it is more than that. ☜

6. A monk who practices Tibetan Buddhism ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2784: Amazing Woman
chapters.body:
Chapter 2784: Amazing Woman

His words made the smiling Firework’s expression turn cold, and she launched a kick. She was so fast that it produced a sonic boom. 

The middle-aged lama had been admiring Firework when his eyes widened in shock. He wasn't a grandmaster for nothing, though. He formed a hand seal, and his presence suddenly felt heavy like a mountain. 

A loud thud ensued as Firework’s leg whipped into the middle-aged lama. 

The lama hurriedly defended himself with his arm, but he was still pushed fifteen steps back before he regained his balance. 

“Red Sun Reverend, are you sure you’re up to par? You often brag about your hand seals, but you can’t even catch a lass’ kick?” 

A man carrying a jade flute at his waist laughed. He had a young face, but his hair was all white. He looked dashing in a feminine way, but there was an unnerving glint in his eyes. 

A flirtatious woman voiced her agreement. “Aiyo! Senior Brother, the Red Sun Reverend might just be going easy on the young miss. It’s called playing the long game!”

The Red Sun Reverend’s face turned bright red. He had proclaimed himself as Esoteric Buddhism’s number one expert ever since the Golden Wheel Reverend died in Xiangyang City, sometimes even expressing his disdain toward the Golden Wheel Reverend. He believed he was better than a failure who had died in enemy hands.

Yet he was pushed back so much by a lass whom the Golden Wheel Reverend had wanted to take as his disciple back then. If this became widely known, he would lose respect in the Mongols’ upper echelons.

His expression turned livid. Without any hesitation, he charged at Firework to continue the battle, determined to restore his honor before the Prince of Yan. The latter was likely to inherit the mantle of Khan in the future. Being underestimated by the prince would spell the end of his career.

Firework’s eyebrows shot up. “Interesting.”

While she hadn’t used her full strength for the earlier kick, she was still surprised that he had withstood it. 

Zu An was perplexed. Red Sun Reverend? Why does that name sound so familiar? That’s a character that appeared in one of Huang Yi’s novels[1], right?

Why is he here? Was there such a person in the history of Esoteric Buddhism?

Zu An was baffled. How did the Deity of Death design this trial world?

The Red Sun Reverend was shocked. He dished out thirty moves in succession, but somehow, his opponent neutralized every single attack with ease. 

His signature move, Death Seal, was known for its unpredictability. He could instantly switch between light and heavy blows, which made it difficult for his opponents to deal with his attacks. His opponents would swiftly exhaust themselves and eventually reveal an opening, which he could exploit and claim victory.

Yet this lass had perfectly neutralized his earlier barrage of attacks. It was as if she could see through his inner energy circulation and predict his move… but that was impossible!

With the Prince of Yan and his peers watching this battle, the Red Sun Reverend didn’t dare hold back. His hands began moving so fast that shadows appeared in their wake. This was his other signature skill, Nine Characters Mantra Hand Seal.

Unmoving Foundation Seal. Great Vajra Seal. Outer Tiger Seal. Inner Tiger Seal. Outer Bind Seal. Inner Bind Seal. Wisdom Seal. Sun Disc Seal. Treasure Vase Seal.

His hand seals moved so quickly that he felt that he was exerting 120% of his usual strength. Never had he utilized his Nine Characters Mantra Hand Seal to this extent. 

Yet the lass easily unraveled his hand seals. She didn’t even use any profound moves, relying on simple punches, kicks, and pokes. Her moves weren't particularly fast either, but somehow, she still tore down his attacks.

Firework nodded in approval. 

The person who created this cultivation skill must have realized that the body was a vessel for salvation, containing the world’s secrets. The body could commune with the world through hand seals and inner energy circulation pathways to borrow the world’s energy. It was half a step into the road of cultivation. 

Unfortunately, the ki concentration in this world was abysmal. It was like climbing trees in search of fish—a lesson in futility. 

Initially, the Mongol soldiers were still laughing at the Red Sun Reverend for embarrassing himself, but their expressions slowly turned grim. The first kick might have caught the Red Sun Reverend off guard, but he was going all out now, yet he was still unable to do anything about the lass.

She was only using common moves, but somehow, they neutralized the Red Sun Reverend’s profound martial arts. It looked almost as if an elder was guiding a junior along. 

This should have been impossible!

They were from rival factions, but they knew how strong the Red Sun Reverend was. It wasn't overly arrogant of the Red Sun Reverend to put down the Golden Wheel Reverend. Many Mongol soldiers had met the Golden Wheel Reverend and knew the two were experts of the same caliber. 

Yet such a powerful expert couldn’t even curb a young woman?

One of Prince Zhenjin’s aides leaned over and whispered something to him. Moments later, the prince smiled. “Ah, so you’re the daughter of the Golden Saber Prince Consort[2]. I can see where you inherited your valiance from. Given our old ties, there’s no need for us to resort to blows. Shall we have a temporary ceasefire?”

The Red Sun Reverend took this chance to retreat. “Since Your Highness has said so, I’ll temporarily cease the battle.”

Firework harrumphed. She was too proud to give chase.

The Red Sun Reverend secretly wiped off his cold sweat. I nearly humiliated myself today.

“I heard you are likely to become the next Khan. Do your words hold weight in Mongolia?” Firework asked Prince Zhenjin.

“You’re flattering me, Guo second miss. My father is the one who decides who becomes the next Khan.” Prince Zhenjin clasped his fist toward the north. “But my words do carry some weight in Mongolia. Guo second miss, feel free to speak your mind if you need anything. I might be able to offer you my assistance.”

Firework wasn’t so foolish as to tell Prince Zhenjin that she had come here in search of the Xuan Metal Sword to deal with Mongolia. “Get your soldiers out of Xiangyang, and you are not to invade Great Song in the future.”

Prince Zhenjin was initially taken aback before revealing a smile. “Pardon me, Guo second miss, but the invasion of Great Song is the collective will of several generations of Khans and Mongolia’s upper echelon. It’s not something I can change alone. Why don’t you advise your father to surrender to Mongolia instead, so we can avoid bloodshed?”

Thinking his words weren't convincing enough, he quickly added, “Most of my teachers are Han, and I have the highest appreciation for the Han culture and system of governance. I promise that I’ll treat your people with kindness after you surrender to us. Your people will live better lives under us than under the Song imperial court.”

Firework’s expression turned cold. “If you can’t call the shots, I’ll hold you hostage and see how much Hubilie[3] cares about his son!”

She charged forth as she said those words. 

The Mongol soldiers were already prepared against that. They stepped forward to stop her. But Firework was a tiger in a flock of sheep; normal soldiers couldn’t hope to deter her.

Witnessing her valiance, Prince Zhenjin remarked, “To think there’s such an amazing woman in the world. If I can have her as my wife, my trip will have been worthwhile.”

1. The novel was adapted into a drama called ‘Lethal Weapons of Love and Passion’. The Red Sun Reverend is one of the top experts of Esoteric Buddhism, and he stood his ground against the other experts in the novel. ☜

2. Guo Jing once fought for the Mongols and was engaged to their princess, but he defected when he realized they were going to invade the Song dynasty ☜

3. Kublai Khan’s name ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2785: The Plan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2785: The Plan

A pale-faced elder grimly warned, “Be careful, Your Highness. She’s an astonishingly powerful martial artist. Even the Red Sun Reverend wasn’t a match for her.”

The Red Sun Reverend harrumphed, but he didn’t retort against that remark. The woman’s fighting prowess was indeed at a ridiculous level. 

“The wilder the steed, the greater the satisfaction in conquering it.” Prince Zhenjin’s eyes gleamed with excitement. 

He had come here to meet the legendary beauty, but the woman before him was also gorgeous and carried a unique air of wildness. The latter piqued his interest more than the legendary beauty. 

Meanwhile, the Mongol soldiers toppled like domino pieces, with none withstanding more than a single blow from Firework. The Quanzhen Sect’s daoists feared being implicated by this scuffle, so they tried to persuade Guo Xiang to step down. They thought that given the ties between the Quanzhen Sect and the Guo Family, the Guo second miss would step down.

But Firework wasn’t the real Guo Xiang. She ignored them and even sent a Quanzhen daoist flying. 

That enraged the Quanzhen Sect. They quickly constructed the Big Dipper Formation to confront her. 

Firework, with her keen eye, easily saw through the formation’s crux and sneered, “That’s an interesting formation, but it’s a pity you've failed to grasp its essence.”

She dismantled the Big Dipper Formation with ease. 

The faces of the Quanzhen daoists crumbled in despair. 

They knew that they were lacking compared to the Quanzhen Seven Masters, as well as the already deceased Yin Zhiping and Zhao Zhijing. But they had been training diligently in recent years, and they'd thought they could at least protect their sect with the Big Dipper Formation. 

Only now did they realize how weak they were. 

“Looks like the Quanzhen Sect is going to disappear in our generation.”

That was the thought dominating the minds of the Quanzhen Sect’s upper echelon.

Seeing that neither the Mongol soldiers nor the Quanzhen daoists could stop Firework, the experts surrounding Prince Zhenjin finally made a move. 

While Firework was busy fighting others, the flirtatious woman who had mocked the Red Sun Reverend earlier suddenly flicked a silk ribbon at Firework’s arms and legs. While the ribbon looked flimsy, it was a lethal weapon that viciously claimed the lives of the unsuspecting.

The white-haired man also made a move. He lifted his jade flute and blew a peculiar melody. This was his skill, Soul-Stirring Demonic Melody. His melody seeped right into the heart, evoked one’s lust, disrupted their psyche, and led them toward a fatal cultivation deviation. 

“What a beautiful lady you are. Do you have a man you’re interested in? Why don’t I teach you how to give a man an unforgettable night?” the flirtatious woman’s voice echoed loudly.

The two martial siblings specialized in such coordinated attacks. The flirtatious woman’s seduction was amplified by her senior brother’s demonic melody. 

Even though the lass was a formidable martial artist, it was obvious at a glance that she was inexperienced in the realm of physical pleasure. It would be difficult for her to withstand such temptations.

If they could apprehend this lass, especially after she had defeated so many experts, it would surely raise the Prince of Yan’s opinion of her. His Highness might even allow me to serve him… 

The more the flirtatious woman thought about it, the more excited she became. 

“What a shabby tune!” Firework harrumphed. 

Her voice resounded like a thunderous roar in the white-haired man’s head, causing him to spurt blood. His glee gave way to horror. 

At the same time, Firework stepped on the silk ribbon and twisted it with her foot, causing the flirtatious woman to fly through the air and slam into the charging Mongol soldiers like a hammer. The flirtatious woman spat blood, sustaining injuries so severe that she couldn’t fight anymore. 

“Bai Sun, His Highness may be in danger if we let the lass to continue take us down one by one. Why don’t we join hands to subdue her?” The Red Sun Reverend was intimidated by what he was seeing. He dared not charge in alone, but it would be a different story if he could secure the help of Daoist Bai Sun and the others. 

Even if the lass started training in her mother’s womb, there was no way she could withstand the combined prowess of both him and Daoist Bai Sun.

Daoist Bai Sun’s initial thought was to turn down the offer, thinking it was beneath him to gang up on a lass. But considering he had yet to accrue any merit ever since joining Prince Zhenjin, and Prince Zhenjin was clearly interested in the woman, this could be a good chance for him to score some points. 

So, he snickered and said, “Alright.”

After securing Daoist Bai Sun’s help, the Red Sun Reverend got a huge boost of morale. He leaped into the air, his puffed-up red kasaya robe making him resemble a red sun. 

“Someone who has already lost to me still dares challenge me?” 

It didn’t take long for Firework to force the Red Sun Reverend back, but a green figure suddenly flashed toward her from the side to ambush her. She felt a cold aura even before the enemy arrived before her. 

Firework was startled. This was hardly the strongest ice skill she had faced, but she'd had her almighty powers then. Stripped of her overwhelming power in this world, she could still overcome most attacks with her ingenious moves, but in a pure clash of energy, she was at a disadvantage. 

She twisted to dodge the attack, but she was still forced to meet palms with Daoist Bai Sun. Both parties retreated several steps. 

Firework shuddered. A tinge of gray appeared on her palms, slowly creeping up her arms. She frowned. His ice palm also contains lethal poison?

“Hahaha! The Xuanming Divine Palm lives up to its reputation!” The Red Sun Reverend was overjoyed. There was no way he would let Firework catch a breather. He charged in and rained attacks down on her. 

Daoist Bai Sun snuck attacks in from the side, pressuring Firework with his Xuanming Divine Palm. 

Firework channeled her inner energy to expel the ice poison while coping with the aggression of two top experts. She was no longer as domineering as before, but her composure remained.

Prince Zhenjin remarked, “What incredible martial arts and disposition. It’s my first time seeing a woman like that.”

“Your Highness, you can put your heart at ease. Her martial arts are formidable, but she is limited by her age. We can subdue her if we use our inner energy instead of our martial arts against her,” a monk observing the battle said. 

Unlike other monks, his hair wasn't shaved. His hairstyle resembled that of the Western Xia[1] people, where the center was shaved and the sides were braided.

“I’m awed by your sharp insight, master. Once we conquer Great Song, I’ll assign you as Jiangnan’s Chief of Religious Affairs[2],” Prince Zhenjin said in delight. 

“Thank you, Your Highness.”

The situation suddenly went awry. A Mongol soldier suddenly charged at Prince Zhenjin and took him hostage. 

While Firework was distracting everyone else, Zu An had snuck into the Mongol soldiers’ ranks, holding his position and biding his time for a chance to strike Prince Zhenjin.

“You’re courting death!” the monk from Western Xia sneered as he thrust his palm at Zu An’s head. It’s rare for anyone to be as powerful as that lass. There can’t be another expert of that caliber in this world!

1. Also known as the Tangut Empire. ☜

2. ]The monk is Yang Lianzhenjia, a famous monk in the subsequent Yuan Dynasty, known for propagating Tibetan Buddhism further. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2786: Assault
chapters.body:
Chapter 2786: Assault

Chapter 2786: Assault

Prince Zhenjin was of lofty stature. There was no lack of experts protecting him.

The de facto leader of his entourage of experts was a Western Xia monk named Yang Lianzhenjia. He'd defected to Mongolia after Mongolia conquered Western Xia. 

He was a prodigy in Buddhist studies and martial arts. He was from Esoteric Buddhism, but he belonged to a different school than the Red Sun Reverend. He didn’t bother vying over the position of Esoteric Buddhism’s number one expert with the Red Sun Reverend, for he was already known as the Southern Desert’s number one expert.

As such, he had the highest position among the experts protecting Prince Zhenjin. That was also why he could address the prince in a casual tone as if they were friends. 

He hadn’t made a move despite Firework’s incredible show of power, as he knew his primary mission was to protect Prince Zhenjin. 

He was initially unfazed when another figure suddenly charged out of nowhere at the prince. He thought that it was ridiculous enough for a woman so young to be so skilled in martial arts—there couldn’t be a second one in this world—but his eyes nearly popped out of their sockets the next moment.

That figure easily subdued all the Mongol soldiers standing in his path, arriving before Prince Zhenjin in the blink of an eye.

Yang Lianzhenjia couldn’t remain still anymore. He thrust his palm forth to jolt the figure aside. 

To his astonishment, however, the figure easily neutralized his palm strike. Then, a bizarre force hurled him backward, causing him to crash into the Mongol soldiers rushing in to reinforce them.

The figure continued charging at Prince Zhenjin.

Alarmed, Yang Lianzhenjia shouted, “Master of the Vajra School, protect His Highness!”

“Alright!” 

An unseemly monk stepped forward to protect Prince Zhenjin. He formed a simple palm seal that caused his body to glow with a golden light. 

The figure seeking to reach Prince Zhenjin was Zu An. He couldn’t stop Firework from making a move, so he could only sneak into the midst of the Mongol soldiers while she was distracting the others. As soon as a chance emerged, he hadn’t hesitated to make a move. 

While he didn’t agree with Firework’s recklessness, he had the same idea in mind. As long as they could hold Prince Zhenjin hostage, they could disregard the dangerous Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan.

Everything proceeded smoothly. 

He barged through the Mongol soldiers before they had time to react. He successfully launched a sneak attack against the lama protecting Prince Zhenjin, knocking the latter aside. He also didn’t think the monk with discolored patches on his head could stand in his way. With his prowess, he should have been able to send the monk flying within a couple of moves. 

But much to his surprise, the monk was unfazed by his attacks. It was as if he were a mountain that couldn’t be moved. 

He was referred to as the Master of the Vajra School earlier. Could it be the Indestructible Vajra Divine Art?

Zu An finally realized he had made a miscalculation, but it was all too late. Yang Lianzhenjia had already recovered from the earlier shove and was rushing in with other experts to protect Prince Zhenjin. This left Zu An with no choice but to retreat to Firework’s side. 

“You waited so long just for this farce?” Firework scoffed.

Zu An replied bitterly, “I missed my chance. I didn’t expect there to be such a hardy guy beside the prince.”

It wouldn’t have been difficult for him to overcome the Indestructible Vajra Divine Art if he could do it all over again, but everything had happened so quickly earlier that he had no time to react. Clearly, the Mongols wouldn’t slip up a second time either.

A Mongol soldier whispered into Prince Zhenjin’s ear. The prince was startled. “You’re the Guo third master. The Golden Saber Prince Consort is amazing indeed. His children are all top-notch martial artists.”

Even the Red Sun Reverend remarked, “I doubt that Guo Jing and the so-called Five Greats are any stronger than these two.”

The others knew that the Red Sun Reverend was merely salvaging his reputation, but they had to agree with him. Several of them had met Guo Jing, and they agreed with those words. It was baffling to them how two youngsters could be so strong. 

Prince Zhenjin continued, “I’ve treated you with courtesy. Don’t you think you should return the etiquette?”

Zu An sighed. “Pleasantries are meaningless when our paths diverge.”

Defecting to the Mongols can’t possibly be the way to clear this trial.

“Very well, I won’t hold back either. Once I hold you two hostage, breaching Xiangyang won’t be a problem.” Prince Zhenjin’s eyes heated up. Xiangyang was the biggest hurdle standing in the Mongol campaign against the Song. If he could contribute toward that, he would have a much greater stake on the throne. 

“I’ll confer on anyone who captures those two the title of Mongolia’s Number One Hero!”

The eyes of the Mongol soldiers lit up.

Zu An and Firework were powerful, but even ferocious tigers feared wolf packs. Not to mention they had powerful experts on their side as well. There was no need to fear these two.

Mongolia was well on its way to conquering the world. Claiming the title of Mongolia’s Number One Hero would grant tremendous honor and riches not just for them, but also for their next few generations to come.

Even the Quanzhen Sect’s disciples were revved up. This was an opportunity for them to revitalize the Quanzhen Sect. 

Sensing the ravenous gazes, Zu An and Firework pressed their backs against each other. 

“Shall we break out of the encirclement?”

They had the upper hand in solo duels, but the Mongols had them far outnumbered. They had only been able to hold their ground thus far because they caught the Mongols by surprise, but now that they were encircled, the situation had become very difficult for them.

Needless to say, the Mongols wouldn’t grant them a second chance to approach Prince Zhenjin. On top of that, Firework had greatly depleted her inner energy from the earlier fight.

As powerful as Zu An and Firework were, they were constrained by this trial world’s laws. 

“You stall the others. Give me ten seconds to kill him before we leave.” Firework eyed the Red Sun Reverend with cold eyes. How dare a mere ant covet my body?

Zu An understood her temperament. “Alright.”

He charged at Prince Zhenjin. Unsurprisingly, most experts rushed to stop him.

The more they fought, the more alarmed the experts became. Since they had the numerical advantage and at least five of them had inner energy on par with the two assailants, they initially thought they could subdue Zu An with ease, yet they struggled to breach his defense!

The way Zu An channeled his inner energy and moved was shockingly ingenious. 

Meanwhile, Firework found an opening to charge at the Red Sun Reverend. 

After witnessing Daoist Bai Sun injuring Firework earlier, the Red Sun Reverend had regained his composure. This woman is nowhere as powerful as I thought. Our inner energy is on the same level; she only has the advantage in terms of moves. 

So, he decided to leverage his strengths and abandon the battle of skill for one of inner energy, knowing Firework wouldn’t dare to face him like that. Otherwise, she could be stuck in place and left vulnerable, to the extent that even a simple Mongol soldier could claim her life. 

But his eyes soon narrowed. Much to his astonishment, Firework decided to face his palm.

“You’re courting death!”

The Red Sun Reverend felt slighted. Does she think she can defeat me instantly in a clash of inner energy? She has greatly exhausted herself; I have no need to fear her. Others will tear her apart if I can just stall her for even a moment.

As they met palms, the Red Sun Reverend poured out all his inner energy right from the start, hoping to catch Firework off guard and inflict severe injuries on her right away. To his surprise, however, Firework’s palm felt soft like cotton; there was no force whatsoever.

The next second, he felt a tremendous force rebounding onto him. It contained not just Firework’s inner energy, but the inner energy he'd unleashed earlier as well. 

How did she do it?!

The Red Sun Reverend was horrified. He quickly used his Death Seal to retreat from the clash, but it was all too late. The terrifying force tore through his veins, causing him to die on the spot.

Zu An was awed. As expected of Firework. She really only took ten breaths to execute a top expert whose inner energy was on her level. 

But his eyes quickly narrowed.

A Mongol soldier suddenly emerged from the crowd and unleashed a palm strike on Firework’s back just as she was inflicting the killing blow on the Red Sun Reverend. The impeccable timing and the immense force of the attack made it clear he was no ordinary Mongol soldier.

He was an expert on par with the other Mongol experts.

Zu An had never expected the enemy to copy his maneuver. It was unbelievable how neither Zu An nor Firework noticed the anomaly until that Mongol soldier made a move. The other party must have been a top-notch assassin who had been lying in wait all this time.

Firework realized what was going on, but the limitations imposed on her made it impossible for her to dodge this attack. She spurted fresh blood and crashed forward. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2787: Betrayal And Defense
chapters.body:
Chapter 2787: Betrayal And Defense

Firework sensed someone holding onto her and instinctively launched an attack, but she quickly stopped when she realized the other party was Zu An. 

She breathed a sigh of relief, only to be assaulted by fiery pain right after. It felt as if her meridians had all been ripped apart. 

The Mongol soldier who ambushed her didn’t give chase. He cautiously retreated and eyed them from afar. 

Zu An finally saw the other party’s appearance. The latter had a tall nose and distant eyes, an appearance that resembled Hu Feng[1]. He was clearly not from the Central Plains.

“You live up to your title as Persia’s Assassin King, Old Man Shanzhong. I’m impressed,” Prince Zhenjin complimented the man. 

The other experts grunted in indignation, but they were awed by Old Man Shanzhong as well. They had all seen what Firework was capable of; the latter had even killed the Red Sun Reverend with ease. Yet Old Man Shanzhong had inflicted severe damage on her by dealing a timely blow. 

With Firework’s injuries, the Mongols’ victory was sealed, but they chose to temporarily pause their assault lest Firework attempt a desperate counterattack—the last thing they wanted was to die in vain when victory was already theirs. Instead, the Mongols chose to encircle them from afar. 

Brief silence settled upon the battlefield.

Zu An stared at Old Man Shanzhong. He hadn’t expected to meet Persia’s Assassin King here, though it all made sense considering how Mongolia had conquered Persia as well. 

Prince Zhenjin said, “Young master Guo, your big sister is severely injured. Her life will be in danger if she isn’t treated right away. Why don’t you consider my proposal? I’ll make sure your big sister receives proper treatment.”

He had remained calm thus far because he knew about the experts hidden around him. With his victory certain, he tried to recruit Zu An and Firework once more. He understood how the carrot and the stick worked. 

“Young master Guo, I suggest you think things through before answering. You’re alone now. If we continue this fight, not only will you be unable to save your big sister, you’ll also be going up against all of us alone. You are a smart person. I’m sure you know what to choose.”

Zu An’s expression darkened. Prince Zhenjin was right. 

If Firework hadn’t sustained injuries, they could have broken out of this encirclement with ease. Now that he was alone, the chances of him breaking out of this encirclement alone were 50% at most. If he carried the wounded Firework with him, the odds would be even lower.

Firework understood that and coldly said, “Escape by yourself.”

Having survived by herself thus far, she didn’t expect anyone to protect her. Besides, they weren’t on close terms, either. It would be a stretch to call them friends; they had merely been making use of each other. There was no reason for Zu An to risk his life to save her.

There would be no disappointment if there was no expectation. 

But Zu An smiled and said, “We’re comrades. I won’t abandon you.”

Firework frowned. “We aren’t comrades. You can even say that I’m your competitor.”

Zu An calmly replied, “Since we came here together, we ought to leave together as well. As I’ve said, I’ll find a way for the four of us to leave this place together. I won’t abandon you here. If our roles were reversed, I’m sure you wouldn’t abandon me either.”

Firework sneered, “You overestimate yourself. I’d toss you aside in a heartbeat.”

“What a pity,” Zu An remarked, though he continued standing his ground.

Firework looked at him in contempt. Is he so obsessed with playing the hero that he doesn’t recognize reality anymore? I bet he’ll leave without hesitation the moment his life is in danger. 

She had seen such scenes play out far too many times. Even the closest friends and lovers eventually betray each other in times of desperation. 

She focused on circulating her inner energy to heat up, but she soon frowned. Old Man Shanzhong’s earlier blow had crushed most of her meridians, making it impossible for her to properly channel her inner energy to recuperate. 

I dominated the universe for so many years. To think I’d end up dying here. 

The lack of response from Zu An made Prince Zhenjin’s expression darken. He waved his hand and ordered, “Attack!”

The Mongol soldiers nocked their bows and unleashed a rain of arrows. 

Zu An kicked up two spears on the ground and wielded them. He twirled the spears around, deflecting the arrow rain. 

He knew it wouldn’t do for him to passively defend against the arrows, or else it was only a matter of time before he was turned into a porcupine. Thus, he stomped the ground, jolting the arrows up into the sky, and flung them out with his spear.

While the archers were dealing with his abrupt counterattack, he quickly grabbed Firework’s waist and dashed into the Mongol soldiers. 

Firework frowned. The heat on her waist made her uncomfortable. It wasn't the first time Zu An had hugged her in such a manner, but back then, she was a cat teasing a mouse. This was her first time being protected in such a helpless position. 

But what’s the point? He’ll still abandon me in the end. He might as well just dump me now and spare me the misery. 

As these thoughts surfaced in her mind, her expression grew increasingly colder. 

The Mongol soldiers and Quanzhen daoists attacked Zu An, but with the combat experience he had accrued, the latter dealt with them easily. 

Daoist Bai Sun and the other Mongol experts exchanged shocked looks.

Firework had already astounded them earlier, and they hadn't thought she would have an ally whose strength was on par with her. Just watching the pair's moves evoked an epiphany in them. They felt as if they could make a breakthrough if they went into closed-door training.

All of them had already reached a bottleneck in their martial arts, such that they had hardly made any advancement in the last few decades. Yet these two youths inspired them so much that they thought they could push toward higher ground.

That was how shocking the situation was.

Seeing over half of the Mongol soldiers and Quanzhen daoists crumbling in defeat, Prince Zhenjin turned to the Mongol experts and said, “I request you to join hands with my people and capture that man.”

He knew that these people were lofty grandmasters in their respective territories, and the only way they would join hands with one another was for him to make the request in person.

But he was wrong this time. 

After witnessing Zu An and Firework’s tremendous fighting prowess, these experts wouldn’t dare to put on airs. They had only held still thus far because they wanted the Mongol soldiers and Quanzhen daoists to wear down Zu An’s inner energy. 

Zu An and Firework’s moves surpassed their wildest imagination. Their only shortcoming was their comparatively weaker inner energy due to their young age. Their odds at victory went up the more Zu An expended his inner energy. 

Those Mongol soldiers and Quanzhen daoists weren’t dying in vain; their deaths had expended at least half of Zu An’s inner energy. Since Prince Zhenjin was making the request in person, the experts decided to make their moves now. 

The Master of the Vajra School dashed forth, confident in the defensive prowess of his Indestructible Vajra Divine Art.

Daoist Bai Sun exerted his Xuanming Divine Palm to inflict Zu An with his ice poison. 

Yang Lianzhenjia revealed inner energy stronger than the Red Sun Reverend's.

The biggest headache of all was Old Man Shanzhong, who prowled in the shadows like a venomous viper, ready to dish out a lethal blow whenever Zu An let his guard down. Zu An had to continuously keep an eye out for the latter. 

These four experts were highly experienced in combat, but Zu An calmly stood his ground against their joint offense. Thus, they quickly adjusted their strategy and focused on attacking the injured Firework instead. 

Under normal circumstances, such a fighting style would have been beneath them, but no one faulted their actions here. 

It worked. Zu An’s initial composure eroded away, and he began to struggle. It didn’t take long for wounds to appear on his body.

Firework’s expression turned even colder. “Enough. You have already shown enough camaraderie. You can scram now.”

Zu An smiled. “I’m not in the habit of abandoning my comrades, let alone a beautiful lady.”

Another injury was inflicted on his shoulder. 

Firework closed her eyes. “A licentious pervert is bound to lose his life to a woman.”

1. A Chinese poet in the 1900s ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2788: Dead Meat
chapters.body:
Chapter 2788: Dead Meat

The Mongols were confused by their conversation. What’s going on between those two?

“Are they really siblings?” Yang Lianzhenjia laughed eerily. 

“They sound more like a couple,” Daoist Bai Sun scoffed.

“Heh! Everyone says Guo Jing is a hero, yet his children engage in such a depraved act,” the Master of the Vajra School said with an excited glint in his eyes.

Old Man Shanzhong didn’t utter a word, but he continued lurking around Zu An and Firework, occasionally dishing out blows at their blind spots. 

Firework frowned, but she didn’t respond. She wasn’t truly Zu An’s sibling anyway. 

The same went for Zu An. He was more worried about the current crisis they were in. In order to protect Firework, he needed to expend himself more than usual, causing his inner energy to deplete swiftly. At this rate, he was going to empty his tank. 

The Mongol experts were well aware of that. Everything they did was with the intention of sapping his inner energy dry. 

Zu An tried to expose some openings to bait the Mongol experts into overcommitting, so he could kill off some of them and reduce the pressure on him. However, the Mongol experts were all wily old foxes. None of them fell for his trick.

If only there were a way for me to replenish my inner energy…

Firework suddenly asked, “Didn’t you have a skill to absorb energy?”

Zu An’s eyebrows shot up, knowing she was referring to the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra. She’s right. This world may ban supernatural powers, but the concept behind the skill holds true. This world has skills like the North Sea Divine Art and Cosmos Absorption Art[1] that can suck out other people’s inner energy. They might not be anywhere as powerful as the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra, but they prove that such skills are feasible in this world. 

Thus, he began altering the Taotie’s Heaven-Devouring Sutra so he could use it in this world. 

He had used the Heaven-Devouring Sutra for many years now and was extremely familiar with it, so it wasn’t tough for him to alter it. It didn’t take him long to come up with a skill similar to the North Sea Divine Art and Cosmos Absorption Art. 

It wasn't stronger than the Nine Yin Manual he had learned from Guo Jing and Huang Rong, but it would greatly increase his endurance in a prolonged fight. He could absorb the might exerted by his enemies into his inner energy, thus replenishing it. 

Yang Lianzhenjia and the others quickly noticed the anomaly. “I feel like my inner energy is being siphoned away whenever I execute a move.”

“I feel the same way, too.”

“Is that fellow using some kind of sorcery?”

… 

The overwhelming power displayed by Firework and Zu An earlier had already piqued their vigilance. Upon realizing Zu An was using some kind of weird power, they hurriedly backed off to a safe distance away lest they fall for his tricks.

The Master of the Vajra School was less cunning than his peers and was a step too slow in his retreat. Zu An managed to grab him by his shoulder. 

But the Master of the Vajra School wasn't worried. He channeled his Indestructible Vajra Divine Art, causing his body to glow with a brilliant golden light and his monk robe to puff outward. With his defenses raised to the maximum, he was confident that not even Old Man Shanzhong’s full-powered attack could hurt him. 

But Zu An had already come up with a countermeasure after suffering a setback from that. 

The Indestructible Vajra Divine Art, despite its grandiose name, wasn't particularly profound. Put in simple terms, the skill involved the compression of one’s inner energy around one’s body to an extreme degree, forming an invisible energy barrier. 

At the same time, the compression also increased the density of one’s bones and muscles, achieving an effect reminiscent of ki. With all external attacks spread evenly across the energy barrier, it created an illusion of indestructibility.

It wasn't too hard to overcome this skill. Zu An had already come up with seven solutions against it. But with powerful enemies lurking around, he opted for the fastest and simplest method.

A peculiar light glowed from his eyes. It was the Nine Yin Manual’s Soul Transference Method. It was a skill used to attack a person’s mind.

“Huogong Toutuo! Despite being a Shaolin disciple, you killed the Dharma Hall’s abbot and massacred dozens of your peers. Do you plead guilty?” 

Zu An’s mind was stronger than anyone else in this world, and he was talking about the Master of the Vajra School’s greatest grudge. 

The Master of the Vajra School was initially dazed, but his eyes quickly narrowed in hatred as he roared, “They deserved to die! They humiliated and beat me up back then…”

“Be careful, Master of the Vajra School!” the other experts shouted as they rushed over to rescue him. 

It was too late.

The Master of the Vajra School’s mind had already been compromised by the Soul Transference Method, and the Indestructible Vajra Divine Art required continuous channeling to maintain the energy barrier. This brief opening resulted in gaps appearing in his defense. 

His inner energy began flowing toward Zu An’s hand as if it were water leaking out of a breached dam.

“NO!”

The Master of the Vajra School screamed in misery. He tried his best to stop the outflow of his inner energy, but it was to no avail. His eyes lost their spirit, and his body became withered. In no time, he looked no different from an ordinary old man.

There was a cold flash of light. In an attempt to save him from Zu An’s suction skill, Yang Lianzhenjia severed the Master of the Vajra School’s arm, but it was already too late.

The Master of the Vajra School had been reduced to a dried-up husk, with his inner energy all sucked away. As a result, the severing of his arm only worsened his condition. His breathing became incredibly shallow. 

Zu An chuckled. “Tastes good. Who’s next?”

The experts felt goosebumps. 

Fuck! Who’s the antagonist here?

Seeing that he had successfully intimidated the Mongol experts, Zu An made use of this opening to carry Firework out of the encirclement. The two of them swiftly disappeared behind the mountain. 

With his inner energy suction skill, Zu An could have easily stood his ground against all these experts alone. But he needed to protect the severely wounded Firework from their enemies too. If the fight continued, Firework could very well lose her life.

So, he didn’t hesitate to flee the moment an opportunity arose. It wouldn’t be too late to settle the score with these people after rescuing Firework. 

He flitted across the mountain before abruptly stopping in front of an ancient tomb. 

An ancient tomb on the mountain behind the Quanzhen Sect. This should be the entrance to the Ancient Tomb Sect. 

By now, Zu An had already noticed that Firework’s meridians had been severed. It would be unwise to continue traveling with her severe injuries. Thus, he shouted, “Hero Yang, Miss Long, my second sister has been injured by the Mongols. Can we seek refuge at your home?”

To his bewilderment, there was no response. 

Even though Yang Guo was avoiding Guo Xiang to sever her feelings for him, upon seeing her life in danger, he should have shown up by now. 

Worried about Firework’s critical condition, Zu An decided to push the entrance open and enter the ancient tomb. 

The place had been armed with many defensive mechanisms that would have doomed most people, but Zu An had been through far more. These traps were child’s play in contrast. 

He soon arrived at a quiet stone chamber. He placed Firework down and began treating her. 

Firework spurted another mouthful of blood. She shook her head and said, “My meridians have been completely severed. My fate is sealed. You can’t save me. You’re wasting your energy.”

She was well aware of her physical condition. Such injuries would have meant nothing to her outside; she had countless ways to heal up. Unfortunately, there were no supernatural powers and miraculous pills here. She racked her brains, but it felt as if her only fate was death. 

1. These are all inner energy suction skills in Jin Yong’s wuxiaverse ☜

Translator's Thoughts

For those curious:

The Cosmic Absorption Art is Ren Woxing’s skill (from The Smiling, Proud Wanderer). It allows the martial artist to possess rich inner energy surpassing any of their peers, but absorbing the inner energies of many different martial artists can make one prone to inner energy deviation.

The North Sea Divine Art (from Demi-Gods and Semi-Devils) is a more advanced version of the Cosmic Absorption Art. ‘North Sea’ refers to the daoist concept of a theoretical sea in the far north that’s deep beyond measure. This skill allows one to absorb all kinds of inner energies and assimilate them impeccably without suffering the risk of inner energy deviation.

chapters.title: Chapter 2789: Someone I Know
chapters.body:
Chapter 2789: Someone I Know

Zu An fell into deep thought. He knew that much as well. 

Old Man Shanzhong lived up to his reputation as the Assassin King. His blow was fast, accurate, and precise. He could have saved the Red Sun Reverend, but he'd chosen to trade the latter’s life for a chance to ambush Firework. 

His bet had paid off. 

Were it not for Firework’s sturdy body and high inner energy, she would have died on the spot. Even so, she'd only delayed her death. It looked like there was no way to turn things around for her. 

Zu An racked his brains. He was sufficiently knowledgeable about medicine to concoct some pills, but this ki-lacking world was lacking in potent herbs. The strongest herbs he could get here were at most ginseng and lingzhi, which were nowhere near enough to treat such severe injuries. 

He pondered harder and asked, “Should I channel my inner energy into your body and help you connect your meridians?”

Firework coldly replied, “That might work for other meridians, but the ones around my heart are very fragile. You might have been useful with your previous cultivation, but now that we've both lost our supernatural powers, the slightest lapse in your control will only further damage my meridians and hasten my death.”

Zu An huffed impatiently, “I’m looking for ways to save you. Can’t you be less grumpy?”

“Did I ask you to do this for me?” Firework scoffed. 

Zu An frowned. Firework had displayed a wide range of emotions thus far, even as the demon spider, but this was the first time she was venting her emotions. Her current condition had to be affecting her a lot.

So, Zu An consoled her. “Don’t worry. This world might lack supernatural powers, but there is quite a bit that can be achieved through martial arts. There’s hope for your injuries. Dali’s Duan Family[1] specializes in the Yiyang Finger, which can heal all injuries, no matter how severe.”

Firework had been treating Zu An coldly thus far, but the latter didn’t lose his temper and even talked to her gently. This calmed her down a little, and she replied, “Dali is too far away. I won’t last the journey. You don’t need to be too bothered by this.”

Zu An knew she was speaking the truth. Firework was in no condition to make the journey.

All of a sudden, he heard light footsteps and put his finger up to his lips, signaling for her to remain quiet. He snuck behind the door and waited vigilantly.

The door suddenly opened, and a cold gleam flashed. 

Zu An swiftly flicked it away. It turned out to be a sword, which swiftly changed its trajectory to strike back at him, unleashing over a dozen stabs at once. 

Zu An had thought that given his vast combat experience, deep comprehension of energy, and sharp judgment, he was pretty much unrivaled in this world. He hadn't thought that someone in this world was capable of employing such profound swordsmanship. There were several close calls that he had to fumble to overcome.

Then, he suddenly exclaimed aloud. He finally got a good glimpse of his aggressor.

The sword also came to a halt. A beautiful pair of eyes gleamed in the dark environment. 

“Tingxue!” Zu An called out in delight. 

The other party had flowing long hair and wore a snowy robe. She exuded an ethereal air that sharply distinguished her from the secular world. 

A hint of emotion rippled in the white-robed woman’s usually nonchalant eyes. “It’s you…”

She also noticed Firework lying on the bed, and her eyes narrowed. A man and a woman staying alone in such dim lighting…

Zu An was exceptionally excited to meet a familiar face in a distant land. “Tingxue, what brings you here? What’s your relationship with the Ancient Tomb Sect? Are you… Xiaolongnu?”

His tone became peculiar. For some reason, he was very averse to the idea of her being Xiaolongnu, even if it was beneficial to the completion of her mission. 

Tingxue shook her head and replied, “No, Xiaolongnu is my mother.”

Zu An and Firework were both stunned.

Tingxue replied with a rare hint of awkwardness. “It’s just this world’s setting.”

Zu An replied with a smile, “It’s the same for us, too…”

He proceeded to share his and Firework’s identity in this world. 

“Guo Jing, Huang Rong… I’ve heard their names.” Tingxue took a good look at Firework. “So, you’re Guo Xiang. I heard from Xiaolongnu that you were courting Yang Guo.”

She was uncomfortable with calling those two ‘father’ and ‘mother’, especially since this was merely a temporary identity in a trial. 

Firework’s face reddened. “That’s the original Guo Xiang! I have nothing to do with… Cough cough…” She spoke with such agitation that it triggered her injuries, causing her to go into a coughing fit. Blood splattered over the floor. 

Tingxue was perplexed. “How did you sustain such severe injuries?”

While this world forbade supernatural powers, someone of Firework’s caliber wouldn’t be weak at martial arts.

“You still dare speak. Aren’t you the one who drew those guys here?” It had become clear to Firework that Tingxue was the gorgeous woman whom those Mongols outside had mentioned.

Tingxue blinked in confusion. Zu An explained the situation to her. 

“Ah, I didn’t know they were calling out to me. They have been very noisy, so I left the ancient tomb half a month ago to look for you. I didn’t expect to bump into you here.” Tingxue nodded in realization. 

If he learns that his half a month of sweet-talking has been in vain, Prince Zhenjin might just pop a vein out of sheer frustration.

“Speaking of which, we're here to fulfill the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan. We wish to borrow the Xuan Metal Sword from Yang Guo.” Zu An briefly shared the plan before asking out of curiosity, “Where are Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu?”

Considering the earlier commotion outside and their entry into the ancient tomb, those two should have appeared by now.

“They have gone out to explore the world,” Tingxue replied. “Yang Guo said something about how they had to overcome many tribulations before they finally got together, and that he wanted to cherish their every moment together. He didn’t want others to disturb them.”

“And that includes you as well?” Zu An retorted. 

Tingxue shook her head. “They see their daughter as a third party.”

“…” Well, this kind of fits Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu’s lovebird persona. They only have each other in their eyes. Zu An thought about their agenda here and was worried. “That’s a shame. Looks like it won’t be easy for us to obtain the Xuan Metal Sword.”

Tingxue replied, “You don’t have to worry about that. The Xuan Metal Sword is stored in the ancient tomb. Come, I’ll bring you to it.”

Zu An was surprised by how smoothly everything was going, but he turned her down and said, “We’ll fetch it later. Firework is severely injured. I need to find a way to save her first.”

Baffled, Tingxue whispered, “Aren’t you two enemies? Why are you saving her?”

Zu An knew she meant no ill will. It was a question voiced out of sheer rationality. “I promised her that I’d find a way to bring the four of us out of this trial world safely.”

Due to her severe injuries, Firework had closed her eyes to rest, but her eyelashes fluttered a little after Zu An said those words. 

“If you truly wish to save her, I do have a way.” Tingxue said. 

1. The Duan Family is the royal family of Dali. Their previous patriarch is the Southern Monk, one of the Five Greats. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2790: Strip
chapters.body:
Chapter 2790: Strip

“What is it?” Zu An was both overjoyed and confused. 

Even someone as knowledgeable about this world as he couldn’t find a solution, but Tingxue, despite having only arrived here not long ago, knew a way out?

“The ancient tomb has a Cold Jade Bed that was passed down from my ancestor. A daoist named Wang Chongyang got it for her to treat her severe injuries. It’s still in the tomb now. Come with me.” Tingxue turned around and left the room.

Zu An hoisted Firework up his arms and followed Tingxue.

Firework usually put on a lofty attitude, but now that she was lying quietly in his arms like a little chick, he found it hard to relate her to that domineering demon spider.

They soon arrived at a room. As soon as they stepped in, a surge of cold air rushed at them. Zu An felt Firework tremble, so he gently channeled a surge of energy into her body.

The warm energy coursing through Firework’s body made her feel comfortable. She opened her eyes to look at the man carrying her. This angle feels familiar. This was how he held me back at the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation, too.

Oblivious to her thoughts, Zu An was focused on the pristine bed not too far away. The bed looked like an amalgamation of ice and jade, and there was a layer of frigid white air above it. He could feel its chill even from a distance.

“I know this bed helps to hasten the growth of one’s inner energy, but shouldn’t someone injured steer clear of this bed? Otherwise, the cold energy might worsen their injury.”

When resting on a Cold Jade Bed, martial artists could instinctively circulate their inner energy, even when asleep. This greatly enhanced their training efficiency, potentially reaping a week’s worth of gains within a single day.

But those who were injured couldn’t channel their inner energy to protect their bodies. Approaching the Cold Jade Bed in such a state would only result in cold energy seeping into their bodies, thus worsening their affliction.

Return of the Condor Heroes had mentioned that the Cold Jade Bed could be used for recuperation, but it had failed to save Xiaolongnu. That was why Zu An hadn't thought of it right away.

“In order to tap into the Cold Jade Bed’s healing property, you might have to cultivate the Ancient Tomb Sect’s signature skill, the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra,” Tingxue replied. 

Zu An anxiously said, “Please save her, Miss Tingxue.”

But Tingxue shook her head and replied, “I can’t do it.”

Zu An was startled. Firework harrumphed. “Don’t beg her! Bring me out, lest I dirty her place.”

Tingxue knew Firework had misunderstood her words, so she explained, “It’s not that I don’t want to save you, but that I’m unable to do so. Cultivating the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra requires two people of opposing inner energy attributes. My inner energy carries the yin attribute, and so does Firework’s. I’d only worsen her injuries if I made a move.”

Firework realized she had misunderstood Tingxue and felt apologetic, but she was too embarrassed to admit it. “Since that’s the case, why don’t you let Zu An learn it? His inner energy carries the yang attribute.”

Zu An had channeled his inner energy into Firework many times now, and the warm feeling it evoked in her was extremely comfortable. With his vast knowledge, it shouldn’t be too difficult for him to learn a martial art in this world. 

Tingxue frowned. “But there’s a problem with cultivating this martial art.”

“How hard can the martial arts of this world be?” Firework was confused. 

“Well…” Tingxue’s cheeks reddened.

Zu An suddenly interjected, “Does the cultivation of the martial art require both parties to be naked?”

Tingxue looked at Zu An in astonishment. “How do you know?”

The Jade Maiden Heart Sutra was the Ancient Tomb Sect’s most prized martial art, and it had never been leaked to outsiders. 

“This fellow is very familiar with this world’s setting.” Firework harrumphed. “So, does the cultivation of this martial art really require its practitioners to strip naked?”

Tingxue nodded. “Cultivating this martial art will cause one’s body to heat up and release steam, so it has to be done naked in an empty area, so the steam can swiftly dissipate without any blockages. If the steam accumulates, it can potentially be fatal.

“The Jade Maiden Heart Sutra is divided into nine stages. The odd-numbered stages are called ‘Advancing Yin’ and the even-numbered stages are called ‘Retreating Yang’.

“The Retreating Yang stages can be paused whenever needed, but the Advancing Yin stages have to be completed in a single breath. Pausing in the middle of an Advancing Yin stage will result in a severe backlash. It’s a highly risky process. That’s why both parties have to channel their inner energies for protection.”

Firework harrumphed. “That’s obviously an unorthodox martial art.”

Tingxue didn’t argue against that. “Our ancestor developed this martial art to deal with the orthodox sects’ number one expert, Wang Chongyang. She made many trade-offs to maximize its prowess, making mastering the skill much riskier. The original host of this body learned the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra under Xiaolongnu’s tutelage.”

She then turned to Zu An and continued, “Firework is severely injured. You’ll have to train the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra to a level where you can treat her. I can guide you in learning the martial art, but we’re of different sexes. How can we train together without clothes?”

Her fair complexion reddened, adding a touch of color to the dull room. 

Firework teased, “I thought Nebula worshipers abandoned their emotions. It’s just stripping clothes. Are you embarrassed?”

Tingxue lowered her head. “Perhaps I’m still lacking.”

Firework was choked by her honesty.

Zu An chuckled. “That’s not an issue. There should be a camellia field outside the ancient tomb. We can cultivate there—the flowers will conceal our bodies from each other.”

He couldn’t believe he was using the method Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu had used back then. Tingxue probably didn’t know about that, as that was a secret between the couple, and they were too embarrassed to tell their daughter.

Tingxue blinked. “That sounds feasible.”

Firework rolled her eyes. Is that fellow dumb? Tingxue was already hesitating. If I played the pity card, she might have agreed to strip before him. Yet he’s not making full use of this opportunity. 

Zu An and Tingxue made their way to the camellia field outside the ancient tomb. He made sure to survey the area to check if there were any Mongols around. 

In truth, Zu An and Firework’s sudden appearance had intimidated the Mongols. Prince Zhenjin had felt threatened and hurriedly retreated with his subordinates. 

Firework was awestruck by the vibrant camellias that covered the mountain. There are actually so many beautiful scenes in the world. My obsession with exacting vengeance has caused me to miss out on a lot…

Tingxue shared the formula and the things to look out for when cultivating the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra with Zu An. “Have you gotten everything down?”

“Yes, I’ve gotten it down.” With Zu An’s strong mind, it was a piece of cake for him to remember and grasp this world’s martial arts. 

Tingxue glanced at Firework. “Aren’t you going to rest in the ancient tomb?”

Firework shook her head. “I hate dark places. I was sealed in one for too long.” You must be kidding me! They’re going to take off their clothes to cultivate. How can I miss such a show?

A tinge of redness touched Tingxue’s face. She sat down in the flowers and said, “Let’s begin.”

Zu An looked uncomfortable as well. No man could remain composed in such a situation, especially with Firework staring at him teasingly.

“Strip!” Firework exclaimed. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2791: Turning Tables
chapters.body:
Chapter 2791: Turning Tables

Tingxue’s eyebrows shot up. She picked up something amiss about Firework’s voice, as if she were some perverted auntie. She placed her finger on her robe, but she couldn’t bring herself to take off her clothes.

Firework scoffed, “You’re still lacking in the path of Nebula.”

Zu An seemed to have guessed Tingxue’s worry as he said, “Don’t worry, our divine senses don’t work in this world. It’s impossible to see through the flowers and bushes with our eyes.”

Their normal selves could see through trees with divine sense easily, which was why Tingxue was so awkward. 

Tingxue nodded before unraveling her waistband. Her robe slid down. 

Even through the flowers, Zu An could still see her smooth and radiant skin. His breathing became unnatural. He quickly reined himself in and undid his clothes.

“Come…” A pair of fair hands was extended toward him. 

Zu An raised his hands and met her palms. It felt like touching a piece of ice or fine jade, and it stirred an urge to pull her into his embrace. He was shocked that, despite his mental fortitude, he could still harbor such thoughts. In a way, it showed how alluring Tingxue was.

“Calm your mind. Circulate your inner energy using the formula I imparted to you,” Tingxue instructed. Her quivering voice revealed that she was nowhere as calm as she appeared. 

Zu An felt ashamed. He quickly reined himself in and did as he was told. Under her guidance, he was able to grasp the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra quickly. Steam soon rose from their bodies as if they were in a sauna.

Firework had come out to watch the show, but her severe injuries weighed her down. It didn’t feel like much at first, but even craning her neck over to take a better look was a challenge now. This made her feel frustrated.

That fellow Zu An does have a nice figure. Tingxue’s skin is also fairer and smoother than snow.

Firework became groggy due to her injuries, and it looked as if she had fallen asleep. 

It would have taken even a talented martial artist months to learn the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra, but Zu An’s knowledge far surpassed this world’s level, and Tingxue was aiding him. It took him around half a day to master the technique.

Tingxue opened her eyes. “You have mastered the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra. That’s probably as far as the Ancient Tomb Sect’s founder reached.”

Zu An was about to answer when he heard a crashing sound. Firework had collapsed to the ground. Shocked, he rushed over to support her. 

Tingxue’s face reddened. Due to Zu An’s abrupt movement, she caught sight of something she shouldn’t have in her peripheral vision. 

But Zu An couldn’t bother with that now. He hurriedly inspected Firework’s condition—her severe injuries had induced extreme weakness in her. Her body was burning up in a high fever.

Tingxue had already put on her robe by this point, and while tidying herself up, she asked, “How is she doing?” 

Zu An was in no mood to enjoy the sight. He quickly shared Firework’s condition.

“Bring her to the Cold Jade Bed for treatment,” Tingxue instructed.

“Alright!” Zu An belatedly realized he was still naked, and that made him feel awkward. 

While he was searching for clothes, Tingxue passed a robe over and said, “Wrap yourself up.”

Zu An quickly draped the robe over himself before carrying Firework and sprinting back to the Cold Jade Bed. 

The cold of the chamber caused the feverish Firework to snuggle closer to Zu An, as if only his body warmth could comfort her. 

“You have to channel the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra to treat her right away,” Tingxue said grimly.

“Miss, may I trouble you to start a fire by the side?” Zu An asked.

Tingxue did as she was told. By now, all three of them understood how the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra could be synergized with the Cold Jade Bed to treat severe internal injuries. The crux was to nourish Firework’s meridians with a flow of pure yang energy before neutralizing that energy with the Cold Jade Bed. 

The Cold Jade Bed served to dispel the heat and prevent any clots, lest the injuries worsen. 

But there was a limit to a martial artist’s inner energy in this world. It would be tough for an individual to overcome the Cold Jade Bed’s chill with inner energy alone, so it would be ideal to start a fire next to the bed. Zu An could draw the pure yang energy from the fire and amplify it with his innate yang inner energy to heal Firework’s injuries continuously.

It was theoretically similar to how Master Yideng healed others with his[1] Yiyang Finger. It was slower, but it came without the downside of temporarily losing one’s inner energy and being unable to move one’s hands for several years. 

Tingxue quickly gathered some dry branches and started a fire. 

Zu An carefully placed Firework down on the Cold Jade Bed, but the cold made her embrace Zu An more tightly. She was shivering so much that even her teeth were chattering. 

Zu An quickly infused some energy into her body to alleviate her chill. He was about to begin the treatment when Tingxue hurriedly stopped him.

“Do you intend to begin treatment just like that?”

Zu An was startled. “We can’t drag out her treatment any longer.”

Tingxue huffed. “I’m not stopping you from treating her, but no heat should be retained in your bodies during the crucial phase of the treatment. The two of you need to strip, just as you did when cultivating the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra.”

Zu An was stunned.

It dawned on him why Tingxue had told him to just drape a robe around himself earlier. He didn't have to put on clothes when he needed to strip very soon for the treatment. However, if he were to strip Firework, she would obliterate all of them when she regained her powers.  

Firework sensed what was going on and murmured, “No…”

Unfortunately, her feeble words carried little persuasiveness. 

Zu An looked at Tingxue for help, but the latter calmly replied, “Why are you looking at me? You can decide whether you want to save her or not.”

“Alright, I’ll do it.”

On the one hand, Zu An didn’t wish to see Firework’s death despite everything that had happened. On the other hand, he was unsure what the conditions to clear the Deity of Death’s trial were. It was possible that Firework’s character could play a crucial role.

So, he requested Tingxue to strip Firework, but she looked at him quizzically and asked, “Isn’t that unnecessary? You’ll still be baring your bodies to each other even if I strip her.”

Unlike in the shrubs, there was no cover on the Cold Jade Bed to shield their bare bodies from each other. They would only be more intimate, especially with the cold-averse Firework hugging him so tightly.

“May I trouble you to excuse yourself?” Zu An asked awkwardly.

“I need to be here to ensure the fire continues burning sufficiently. The slightest problem here may endanger both of you,” Tingxue replied with a straight face.

Zu An was speechless.

1. The Southern Monk, the abdicated emperor of Dali’s Duan Family. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2792: Another Step Forward
chapters.body:
Chapter 2792: Another Step Forward

It’s awkward enough to have to strip Firework, but I have to do it with Tingxue watching by the side? Is she doing this to exact vengeance on Firework?

But the reason she gave makes perfect sense. I do need to tap into the fire’s heat lest the Cold Jade Bed’s cold energy get the better of me.

“Alright.” Zu An was reluctant to do this, but Firework was already in a critical state.

“Firework, I’m doing this to save you. Please pardon me if I offend you in any way,” he murmured in Firework’s ear.

Firework’s mouth moved, but she was too feeble to speak.

Zu An quickly stripped her robe. It was as if the dim chamber suddenly got brighter. Even the Cold Jade Bed looked dull in comparison to Firework’s fair skin. 

Zu An quickly pulled himself together and treated her using the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra.

Firework, fearing the cold, instinctively snuggled up to Zu An like a koala hugging a tree. 

Zu An had a hand placed above the fire and the other holding her both hands, pressing down on a key acupoint of her ‘Ren Du’ meridians and gently transferring his yang energy over. 

Firework's meridians were so damaged that Zu An dared not exert any force. Her delirious condition also made it impossible for her to cooperate with him in the treatment. If he accidentally used too much force when transferring the yang energy, it could shatter her meridians and kill her.

Such precise control was much more tiring than fighting. It didn’t take long for sweat to seep down his forehead. 

Tingxue looked at the duo with a bizarre look. Somehow, their posture felt strange to her. Her cheeks began to heat up. 

This fire is too hot… 

She was starting to regret staying here. She was averse to fire as her cultivation skills all leaned toward the ice element, but she could only quell her discomfort and focus on maintaining the fire.
