Under Zu An’s patient treatment, Firework finally regained consciousness. When she realized she was naked in Zu An’s arms, she immediately fell silent. 

Feeling her trembling body, Zu An quickly explained, “It was an emergency. Hold yourself together. Don’t let everything go to waste.”

Firework coldly replied, “Do you take me to be some foolish woman who would cause a huge fuss over such trivialities at this critical moment?”

Zu An was surprised by how composed Firework was, but on second thought, she was a powerful entity far surpassing the likes of him. He should never have thought of her as an immature child who had never seen the world.

He did think their current position was a little awkward, however. “Why don’t you turn over now that you’re awake?”

Neither of them was clothed, and they were hugging each other. This was grueling for Zu An. He was sweating not just because he had to be exceptionally careful treating her meridians, but also because it was exhausting having to control his lewd thoughts.

Little did he expect Firework to turn down his request. “I refuse. I’m too injured to sit directly on the Cold Jade Bed and withstand its bone-piercing chill. It’ll only worsen my condition.”

Zu An was speechless. You’re going to continue sitting on my lap? Have you considered my condition? Though there was no way he would say those words aloud. Besides, Firework was indeed in a weakened state. 

“Since you’re awake, channel your inner energy based on the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra imparted to us by Tingxue. Cooperate with me in circulating my inner energy around your meridians.”

Given the severity of Firework’s injuries, it would be ideal for both parties to channel the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra together to ensure a precise flow of energy. Zu An had transferred his inner energy earlier to awaken her, so she could actively cooperate with him.

“Alright.” Firework nodded. 

She had gained a good grasp of the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra from listening to Tingxue’s explanation. She began cooperating with Zu An in channeling his inner energy around her body to nourish her meridians.

It was tough at first. Firework’s meridians were so severely injured that it was difficult for her to channel her inner energy, but Zu An patiently unblocked her acupoints one by one before slowly expanding her blocked meridians. 

Firework, with her deep understanding of energy and laws, was able to cooperate with him impeccably. It didn’t take long for the two of them to get into the flow of things. 

Surges of warm energy flowed into Firework’s body and nourished her injured meridians before they were dispelled into the Cold Jade Bed, such that they wouldn’t hurt her brittle meridians. 

Firework’s pale face regained some color. 

“I've recovered some inner energy now. Put me opposite of you,” Firework suddenly said. They were in extremely close proximity, so much so that she could feel Zu An knocking at her gate, ready to enter at any time. That stirred some feelings in her.

And the greatest taboo while undergoing treatment was to get distracted. That could potentially lead to cultivation deviation.

Zu An’s face reddened. He understood the reason behind her request, so he wouldn’t turn her down. He gently put her down on the other side. 

Firework closed her eyes and focused on circulating Zu An’s inner energy to heal her injuries, but her fluttering eyelids hinted that she was nowhere as calm as she appeared. 

Zu An felt awkward as well, knowing Tingxue was watching off to the side. That said, he had weathered enough storms to have the mental fortitude to adjust his state of mind and focus on the task at hand. 

Tingxue quietly averted her gaze. There was a red tinge to her fair skin. She deeply regretted choosing to stay in the chamber. She felt like a maid tending to the fire while the other two flirted with each other. 

She had chosen to stay as vengeance for the shame she felt earlier, but she suddenly felt as if her presence here was extraneous. The thoughts in her mind alarmed her. 

What’s going on? I should have lost most of my emotions after embarking on the path of Nebula. It’s rare for me to feel anything or harbor such thoughts. So why do I have so many miscellaneous thoughts in my head?

Is it because this trial world restricts my nebula power?

A bizarre silence settled upon the room. The only sounds that could be heard were the occasional crackling of the flame and the steam puffing from Zu An and Firework’s bodies.

Some time later, Firework suddenly opened her eyes and said, “The Jade Maiden Heart Sutra can be further refined.”

“You noticed it, too?” Zu An had also found the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra extremely familiar, since he had a cultivation skill similar to it. 

“How can I not notice when you have?” Firework rolled her eyes at him. “Since this skill requires two people to grasp it, why not make it a dual cultivation martial art? It will come very close to the level of a cultivation skill. Its prowess should surpass the Nine Yin Manual and the Scripture of Tendon Transformation[1].”

She suddenly asked excitedly, “Shall we give it a try?”

Zu An exclaimed, “Ah?”

1. Known as Yijin Jing, it’s a real daoist text containing mental and physical exercises to cultivate one’s ‘essence’. In Jin Yong’s wuxiaverse, it is a Shaolin martial art created by Dharma. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2793: Goes Against The Natural Order
chapters.body:
Chapter 2793: Goes Against The Natural Order

Tingxue raised her head. Her usually cold face carried a hint of astonishment.

Zu An was taken aback. “How do we test that?”

Firework looked at him with a devious smile. “I believe you have a lot of lovers. Are you pretending to be innocent now?”

Zu An’s face reddened. “I just find your words inconceivable.”

“Inconceivable? We’re too weak right now. We need to grasp every chance we can to become stronger.” Firework harrumphed. She was too used to being strong, to the point that her current vulnerability pricked at her nerves. 

She'd thought that with her current body’s martial arts foundation, paired with her vast knowledge, she was unrivaled in this world. But her recent injury knocked some sense into her. There were many formidable experts in this world. Perhaps they wouldn't be a match for her in a 1-on-1, but they still posed a threat when ganging up on her.

She had to resolve this problem!

Seeing how Zu An was looking at her weirdly, Firework coldly pointed out, “Have you not wondered why no one has cleared this trial in so many years? You saw those long stairs of white bones, right? Some of them might have been stronger than us. Do you think their strength or wits were inferior to ours?”

Zu An fell silent. That was indeed the concern he harbored. To this moment, he still had no idea what this trial was about. 

Firework continued, “Since we aren’t strong enough at the moment, we should do everything within our means to become stronger. If there’s a way to do so via dual cultivation, is there any reason why we shouldn’t do so?”

She turned to Tingxue and said, “It just so happens she knows the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra. You can also dual cultivate with her and raise her fighting prowess as well. That would put us in a better position to deal with whatever crisis we face ahead.”

Tingxue shot to her feet. “You can do it yourselves.”

She turned around and left, not bothering to tend to the fire anymore. 

Zu An and Firework exchanged looks. Firework looked at Zu An in confusion. “Isn’t she a Nebula worshiper? She’s supposed to evaluate the world with a completely impartial mind, devoid of emotions. Is she still bothered by her physical form?”

Zu An sighed. “You’re the libertine here.”

Firework harrumphed. “Are you doing it or not? If not for the special nature of this place, I wouldn’t have made this offer. You should know that you’re the only man whom I could barely stand in all these years.”

Zu An retorted, “Isn’t it because you didn’t have a chance to meet other men because you were trapped in the forbidden land?”

“You’re asking for it!” Firework angrily lunged forward and bit Zu An’s shoulder.

“Ah! Be careful, we’re still in the midst of treatment!” Zu An was alarmed. 

The Jade Maiden Heart Sutra was an unorthodox martial art in the first place; those who practiced it were prone to cultivation deviation. That was why there had to be no disturbances when channeling it. Yet Firework was still messing around. 

“Don’t worry, my inner injuries have healed up considerably. I can channel a bit of my inner energy now.” Firework coyly lay on Zu An’s chest. “Big brother Zu, am I really not attractive to you?”

Zu An was so accustomed to her domineering tone that her suddenly reverting to the sweet, coy tone she'd used when they first met sent shivers down his spine. He quickly pushed her aside and said, “Stop it. This is improper…”

Touching her supple skin made his heart skip a beat. Given their current situation, it felt inappropriate to touch her anywhere. 

“What’s the matter? We’re both willing parties. Or do you not want to?” Firework gently propped up Zu An’s chin while looking at him.

“Of course not.” Zu An pushed her hand aside. “You shouldn’t be talking about this now. I’m still treating your injuries.”

“Indeed, you’re treating my injuries.” Firework chuckled. “I’m sure you know my inner injuries would recover faster if we performed dual cultivation instead.”

Zu An was aware of that as well, but how could he conflate those two things?

“Now that you can channel your inner energy, you can treat yourself. I’ll be leaving first.” Having such a gorgeous beauty in his arms, at this rate, Zu An felt as if he would succumb.

But Firework grabbed his hand and stopped him. “You aren’t allowed to leave.”

Zu An sighed. “I don’t do it with just anyone.”

Firework widened her eyes in anger. “Are you implying that I’d do it with anyone?”

“That’s not what I mean. I just think that we shouldn’t be doing this…” Zu An would have never thought a day would come when he would struggle to guard his chastity against someone else. 

Firework suddenly laughed. “You say you don’t want to, but your body is honest.”

Zu An was stunned. He suddenly felt as if he had jumped genres to the romance section. Sis, did you accidentally take my script?

Firework was starting to get impatient. “You’re so wishy-washy. Are you a man or not?!”

Her look of contempt stoked Zu An’s anger. “Don’t regret uttering those words!”

“What do I have to regret?” Upon seeing that she had provoked his competitive spirit, Firework’s eyes curled in glee. “I’ll be on top.”

Zu An was stunned. “Impossible. It goes against the natural order of the world.”

Pah pah…

The two of them came to blows.

“Brat, are you taking advantage of my injuries now?”

“Are you telling me to wait for you to recover before fighting you? Do I look like a fool to you?”

Upon seeing that she was at a disadvantage, Firework reverted to her coy tone. “Aiya~ Big brother Zu, please go easy on me.” 

That sent shivers down Zu An’s spine. “Which is your true self?”

“They're all my real personalities,” Firework replied. “Or should I say that they're the personalities I had in different stages of my life? This was how I was in my younger years.”

“Cute…” Zu An was reminded of the young lady he met on the spaceship—she was eccentric but extremely endearing. 

“I didn’t expect you to be a lolicon.” Firework glared at him. 

Zu An nearly spurted blood. “Don’t talk rubbish! You’re obviously an adult. No, if we go by your real age…”

“Shut up!” Firework roared. Her hands had been restrained, so she could only lurch forward to seal his mouth.

Zu An was taken aback. He hadn’t expected their first kiss to happen like that. 

Embarrassment flashed across Firework’s face. As their lips parted, she gently murmured, “You’re hurting me. I haven’t fully recovered yet.”

Zu An hurriedly freed her out of concern of her injuries.

But Firework used this opportunity to flip him over, seal his acupoints, and claim the initiative. She looked at him gleefully. “It’s my victory.”

She leaned toward his ear and whispered a formula, her hair naturally cascading on his chest in the process. “Remember this formula. You’ll have to cooperate with me later.”

Zu An was speechless.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2794: I Am Not That Kind Of Person
chapters.body:
Chapter 2794: I Am Not That Kind Of Person

Zu An hadn’t expected her to ambush him like that. He could only glare at her in anger.

Firework grinned triumphantly. “I like those eyes. Continue looking at me like that.”

Zu An was stunned. This woman is insane!

“Lie down. I’ll do it myself,” Firework smiled. 

She frowned upon seeing her adversary, but she was a headstrong person. How could she admit defeat at a time like that?

Zu An inhaled sharply. She’s such a tenacious person. Even I'm feeling pain, let alone her. 

Firework leaned toward his ear and whispered, “Be glad. If not for this world limiting my body, you would never have been able to get inside me, no matter what you did.”

Zu An looked resigned. “To think I was taken by a spider demoness…”

Before he could finish speaking, Firework bit him and hissed, “Are you insulting me? I used to be a human, too!”

Zu An remembered what the Deity of Death spoke about her past. Curious, he asked, “What made you turn into a demon?”

Firework’s gaze turned cold, but she quickly reverted to her gentle demeanor and asked, “Can we not talk about unhappy matters at a time like this?”

Her overflowing charm and tenderness made it plenty clear that this wasn’t the time to be touching on mood-ruining topics, but Zu An didn’t forget about her injuries. “Let’s treat your injuries first.”

“I’m healing much faster than before now.” Firework smiled sweetly. 

The two of them interlocked their fingers and fully channeled the modified Jade Maiden Heart Sutra. Even so, her inner injuries were so severe that all her meridians were either shattered or clogged. Zu An struggled to channel his inner energy into her body. 

While her meridians had recovered a little after their earlier treatment, it would still take a long time to open up all of them.

It was a good thing Zu An wasn’t in a rush, so they could take their time. It also helped that Firework could channel her inner energy to some extent. Through their cooperation, they could precisely channel Zu An’s energy into her body to ensure a thorough recovery. 

Occasionally, they would encounter a meridian that was closed tightly. Zu An would first patiently probe the meridian with a small surge of inner energy, gradually expand it, then reinforce it.

As Firework’s meridians reconnected, her previously stuck inner energy converged like tributaries returning into a river, allowing her to better tap into her inner energy. She proactively channeled her inner energy to coordinate with Zu An. 

At the same time, Zu An didn’t need to tread as carefully now that Firework was in a better condition. Even so, he didn’t dare make a reckless move. Like a small stream flowing into a river, he patiently inspected every bit of her body for injured meridians to repair.

When he was almost done healing most of her meridians, his inner energy could finally flow openly through her body to expand and reinforce all her meridians, thus pushing her martial arts cultivation up another level. 

This raging flow of inner energy eventually returned to the vast sea. The vast sea evaporated into clouds, converged in the mountains, and returned as rain to replenish the river.

Just like that, the two of them formed a continuous cycle between themselves. 

Firework’s complexion grew increasingly red, a sign that she had recovered fully. 

The continuous flow of inner energy naturally unlocked Zu An’s acupoints, allowing him to regain control of the situation. Firework was initially reluctant to relinquish control, but her rejection was so weak that he got his way.

Their martial arts cultivation rose to another height.

Firework tightly embraced Zu An. Her domineering demeanor had softened considerably. “It’s a pity my powers are sealed, or else I could have pulled out Yani and Diodora to play with you. If you wanted, I could do Tingxue and Isabella as well.”

The thought of that made Zu An’s heart skip a beat, but he quickly put on a straight face and replied, “I’m not that kind of person. Don’t even think about tempting me with that.”

“Is that so? I don’t think your body is fooling anyone.” Firework laughed.

Zu An was so embarrassed that he harrumphed. “You don’t know what you’re messing with now…”

“Ahhh! I was wrong. Don’t…”

The two of them bickered. The cold chamber was filled with laughter.

Sometime later, Zu An and Firework stepped out of the chamber. They spent a long time searching the ancient tomb before they found the frosty Tingxue sitting in the forest outside. 

“What are you doing here? We were looking for you,” Firework asked.

Tingxue looked at the energetic Firework and remarked in surprise, “You’re all healed up?”

“That goes without saying. I personally modified that martial art, and I had the help of the Cold Jade Bed as well,” Firework replied with a smile. 

Zu An was stifled. Even though I got all sweaty and exhausted for you, you’re not giving me any credit?

Firework seemed to have sensed his frustration, and her eyes twinkled. “It’s also thanks to this lad. He has good stamina. You should give him a try. I guarantee he’ll bring you to new heights in your martial arts.”

Tingxue turned around. “I don’t need it.”

“There’s no need to be shy. You becoming stronger benefits all of us. Who knows what the clearing condition of this accursed Deity of Death trial is?” Firework suddenly grinned in mischief. “If you don’t become stronger while you can, you might regret it if I turn on you guys, if it turns out that only one of us can survive this trial.”

Tingxue’s face turned cold. “I’d have no regrets. That would be heaven’s will.”

“Tsk. How boring.” Firework wanted to see the Nebula worshiper succumbing to lust, but Tingxue remained unfazed.

Tingxue looked at Zu An with frigid eyes. She resembled Yan Xuehen so much that Zu An felt guilt-ridden, as if he had been caught cheating by Yan Xuehen herself. He wanted to say he'd been forced into it, but he couldn’t bring himself to say that.  

Tingxue suddenly asked, “If I’m not mistaken, the two of you are siblings in this world?”

Zu An was speechless.

Firework retorted, “…What nonsense are you spouting?! We were transported in here with our real bodies. We’re merely borrowing their identities. It’s not as if we’re really siblings.”

Tingxue nodded nonchalantly, but she appeared unconvinced. Firework was so frustrated that she nearly spurted blood.

Zu An interjected with a cough and hurriedly changed the subject. “Where’s the Xuan Metal Sword? Shall we obtain it first?”

Tingxue nodded before leading them on a convoluted path through the ancient tomb. “Yang Guo placed that sword here. Go ahead and take it.”

Inside a giant boulder, a heavy black sword sat silently, as if awaiting its fated owner.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2795: Dilemma
chapters.body:
Chapter 2795: Dilemma

“There’s something off about that sword…” Firework murmured.

Zu An walked over to draw the sword from the boulder, but it was shockingly heavy.[1]

“You’re right. It’s made out of a special material that contrasts with other weapons in this world.” Zu An remarked. 

They had seen many weapons along the way, but the Xuan Metal Sword was made out of an unusual material.

Firework stroked the weapon and soon concluded, “Its material seems to be from another world. No wonder it became a godly weapon in this world.”

“Is it possible for the other universal deities to have tampered with this sword?” Zu An had previously discussed with her why no one had cleared this trial in so many years.

“Unlikely. It’s part of the main storyline of this trial. The Deity of Death would never have become a universal deity if he couldn’t even sense such blatant tampering.” Firework spent a moment in thought before shaking her head. 

Zu An was awed. Given her stature, her judgment is much more reliable.

“Let’s take the sword out first. It’s part of our mission anyway,” Firework said. She clenched her fist and snorted, “That Mongol prince is lucky he escaped fast. Otherwise, I’d have settled the score with him.”

It had been a long time since she last suffered such severe injuries. If not for Zu An, she would have been dead by now. His determination to rescue her, even though it put him in grave danger as well, was part of the reason she was willing to dual cultivate with him.

She suddenly remembered how he had protected her from the Eight Barren Netherworld Suppression Formation, and that spurred some thoughts in her. She looked at him with tender eyes, only to see him whispering intimately with Tingxue. Her expression immediately soured.

Tingxue pulled out two swords from a wooden crate and passed them to Zu An. “Take the Gentleman Sword and Maiden Sword too. These two swords are made out of extraordinary materials as well. Just the Xuan Metal Sword might not be enough for you to craft the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber.”

The Gentleman Sword and the Maiden Sword were godly weapons Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu had obtained from the Loveless Valley, but the two of them had grown so strong that they didn’t need such weapons anymore.

Judging from her words, Zu An understood that she wasn’t planning to travel with them and was startled. “Tingxue, come to Xiangyang as well. This trial is too dangerous. We have to stick together for a better chance to overcome this crisis.”

Tingxue shook her head. “There’s no need for that. I’m accustomed to being alone. This ancient tomb suits me well.”

Firework walked over and asked, “Are you feeling uncomfortable because of what happened earlier?”

Tingxue ignored her and turned away.

Zu An was worried that they would bicker, so he quickly continued, “Our divine senses are sealed in this world, and there’s no effective way for us to communicate over a long distance, either. Let’s head to Xiangyang together and discuss a way to clear this trial.”

Firework harrumphed. “This brat insists on saving everyone from this trial despite its futility. I wouldn’t have wasted my time otherwise.”

Tingxue frowned in hesitation.

Zu An added, “There’s still Isabella. We don’t know her current whereabouts. It’d be easier to gather information about her in a city like Xiangyang. The Xuan Metal Sword belongs to the Ancient Tomb Sect, so it makes sense for you to join us as a representative of the Ancient Tomb Sect.”

“Fine. Let’s find Isabella as soon as possible.” Tingxue finally gave in.

Zu An was overjoyed. It would be easier for them to overcome this trial if they joined forces.

The three of them left the ancient tomb. Firework found a chance to sneak up to Tingxue and asked, “What’s your opinion of our circumstances? Do you think it’s possible for the four of us to survive this trial?”

Tingxue nonchalantly replied, “I don’t like to think about such stuff.”

“It doesn’t matter to you even if you die in the trial?”

“Everything in the universe eventually returns as dust to the nebula. Dying is merely a natural passage of life.”

Tingxue’s calm response made Firework feel as if she had punched cotton. I nearly forgot this woman is a Nebula worshiper. Of course she doesn’t care! 

To get back at Tingxue, Firework intentionally acted intimately with Zu An for the rest of the journey. Even when they stayed at inns at night, she would sneak into his room and cause a huge ruckus.

Zu An initially indulged in it, thinking that Firework was hooked on him. However, he soon realized her intention and was rendered speechless. She’s trying to cuckold Tingxue by getting together with me? But there’s nothing between me and Tingxue!

But Firework had a reason for doing so. She had heard from Zu An that Tingxue resembled one of his lovers, and she keenly sensed something peculiar between the two of them. That was why she was so enthusiastic about this.

This put Tingxue in a tough spot. I shouldn’t have agreed to accompany them to Xiangyang.

Zu An also faced a dilemma. Tingxue was clearly irritated with him, but the more he tried to console her, the louder the ruckus Firework made at night.

His ‘days of suffering’ only ended when they returned to Xiangyang. 

Guo Jing and Huang Rong were overjoyed when they learned that their children had brought back not just the Xuan Metal Sword, but the Gentleman Sword and the Maiden Sword as well. They were also amazed when they saw Tingxue. 

Huang Rong grabbed Tingxue’s hand and exclaimed, “Big brother Jing, they look so alike. Doesn’t this remind you of the first time we met Miss Long?”

“Indeed.” Guo Jing looked wistful, as if he had returned back in time. 

Even Guo Fu rushed over to peek at Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu’s daughter. Her lower jaw dropped when she saw Tingxue’s face. There were many words at the tip of her tongue, but they dissipated with an air of melancholy.

Tingxue felt uncomfortable by their amicability. She instinctively shuffled toward Zu An.

Huang Rong noticed her subtle movements and was initially startled before she revealed a heartened smile.

Guo Jing suddenly said, “Tingxue, Polu and Xiang’er can be considered your uncle and aunt.”

Zu An, Firework, and Tingxue were all speechless.

Even Huang Rong rolled her eyes at her husband. “Guo’er called Miss Long ‘little aunt’ back then as well, but there isn’t a big disparity in their ages. There’s no need for such formalities.”

Guo Jing smiled sheepishly as he realized he had misspoken. 

Firework couldn’t care less about this farce, so she quickly pulled the topic back on track.

Huang Rong instructed Guo Fu to call her maternal grandfather[2] over, using that as an excuse to send her away. Then, she explained, “We invited your maternal grandfather over. He’s highly knowledgeable, and he has been researching how to forge a godly weapon. He has experimented with other weapons and found two chokepoints.

“One, it’ll take more than an ordinary flame to melt the Xuan Metal Sword. He heard that there’s a mystical man residing near Sichuan’s E’mei Mountain who specializes in crafting Thunderbolt Bombs. That man might have a solution to this issue.

“Two, a fire strong enough to melt the Xuan Metal Sword would easily melt any metal clamps used to hold the weapon in place. We’ll need special tools to craft the godly weapon. The Ming Cult’s Sacred Fire Token is the only thing in the world capable of withstanding such heat.

“Thus, I’ll need you two to make a trip to E’mei Mountain and the Ming Cult. Xiang’er can visit E’mei Mountain, but the trip to the Ming Cult will be treacherous. Cult Leader Shi is a formidable martial artist, and he has many powerful subordinates. The Sacred Fire Token is the Ming Cult’s holy treasure; there’s no way he’ll give it to us, even if it’s a request from us. We’ll have to find a way to steal it.

“Unfortunately, your father and I cannot leave Xiangyang at this juncture, and your grandfather is not young anymore. If only Guo’er were around… I don’t know who I can entrust this task to.”

1. In Return of the Condor Heroes, the Xuan Metal Sword was known to be extremely heavy, weighing 41 kilograms—even top martial artists in this world struggled to effectively wield it as a weapon. ☜

2. Guo Fu’s maternal grandfather is one of the Five Greats, East Heretic Huang Yaoshi. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2796: Deepening Ties
chapters.body:
Chapter 2796: Deepening Ties

Firework was bewildered. She pointed to Zu An and asked, “Can’t you just send him?”

Given Zu An’s skills and wits, there was no better candidate than him.

Huang Rong was taken aback. “Polu’s skills are still lacking.”

Guo Polu’s aptitude was at the same level as his father[1], and he was nowhere as focused as his father. His martial arts were subpar at best. 

“He’s not weak by any means. He might have even surpassed the two of you,” Firework replied candidly. 

“Oh?” Guo Jing was overjoyed to hear that. He thrust his palm to attack Zu An, hoping to test the latter’s martial arts.

Zu An sensed the incoming attack and instinctively thrust his palm in retaliation. 

Huang Rong was horrified. Big brother Jing, you shouldn’t use so much inner energy just because Xiang’er made a passing remark! How can Polu withstand that attack?

Her eyes soon bulged in shock. The two men had exchanged over a dozen blows by now, but Guo Polu was standing his ground against Guo Jing. From time to time, Guo Polu would make an ingenious maneuver that astonished even Huang Rong. 

Guo Jing burst into laughter. “Polu has grown at a rapid pace. He’s almost on par with me now.”

Firework smiled. Zu An obviously held back earlier. 

Huang Rong frowned. She stared intently at Zu An. “Polu, how did your martial arts advance so much within this short span of time?”

Zu An was stunned. He had revealed too much earlier because he was worried Huang Rong would send someone other than him.

Huang Rong was an intelligent and skeptical person. She knew that the real Guo Polu would have never achieved such a level of mastery in his martial arts in his lifetime, let alone within this short span. It would be disastrous if she realized that he wasn’t the real Guo Polu.

Just then, Firework interjected, “He just dual-cultivated with Tingxue not long ago.”

Tingxue, who was nonchalantly watching the spectacle from the sidelines, was stunned.

Zu An was speechless.

Firework shot the two of them a glance. It’d be disastrous if Guo Jing and Huang Rong learned that she'd dual cultivated with Zu An. 

Zu An could only awkwardly nod in acknowledgment.

Tingxue was speechless. So this is how it feels to be angry. Her inability to explain the situation only further stoked her frustration. 

Huang Rong’s skepticism gave way to delight. “Ah, so it’s Tingxue’s credit. Polu is really too much…” 

She grabbed Tingxue’s hand and began trading pleasantries once more. 

To think that my son would bring not just the Xuan Metal Sword, the Gentleman Sword, and the Maiden Sword back, but also a daughter-in-law as well! I’m surprised an oaf like him knows how to win a woman’s fancy.

She hadn't shown anything earlier, but she'd initially felt a tinge of dislike toward Tingxue upon seeing that she resembled Xiaolongnu. Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu had left her with many traumas back then. But now, she was growing more and more fond of Tingxue. 

The Ancient Tomb Sect’s martial arts are indeed unorthodox. To think that the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra was a dual cultivation skill. No wonder Xiaolongnu, despite being Guo’er’s master, insisted on marrying him. 

Tingxue’s face turned bright red. She didn’t know what to do.

Huang Yaoshi, who had just arrived, was also overjoyed to hear the news. “Yang Guo’s daughter? Wonderful!”

He was more fond of Yang Guo than his slow-witted son-in-law.

Zu An couldn’t help noticing that the legendary grandmaster’s hair had turned white, and his vitality had greatly declined. His days were numbered. The lifespan of a mortal is short indeed.

The room buzzed for a while before they returned to the main topic at hand. “Rong’er and Jing’er were thinking of hiding the manuals in the weapons, but my experiments show that it’s not feasible—the manuals won’t be able to withstand the heat of the forging. I think it’s better to hide the manuals elsewhere and leave clues about their locations in the weapons.”

Huang Rong smiled. “Father, you’re more thoughtful. With the Mongols’ influence spreading all over the Central Plains, there’s nowhere safe to hide the manuals here. No matter how I look at it, it’s safer to hide the manuals across the sea, where the Mongols can’t reach it.”

“The Peach Blossom Island is located in the middle of the sea. It is tough for outsiders to find it, and the island is protected by formations constructed according to the principles of Qi Men Dun Jia[2]. Even if someone chances upon the island, they'll be trapped by the formations.” 

“Thus, we’re planning to hide the Nine Yin Sutra and the Book of Wumu on the Peach Blossom Island, and we’ll leave the map and the solutions to the formations in the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber.”

Zu An was amazed. Guo Jing and Huang Rong had put a great deal of effort into this plan. 

Huang Rong then turned to Tingxue and continued, “Someone has to supervise the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan to ensure the weapons do not fall into the wrong hands. There’s no one more suited for that role than the Ancient Tomb Sect. Your sect steers clear of the secular world, making it hard for the Mongols to find you. On top of that, your sect members are highly skilled and cold in nature.

“We currently aren’t able to find Guo’er and Miss Long, but your presence works as well. You can consult them about this when you return. I’m sure the righteous Guo’er and Miss Long will not turn away from this responsibility.”

Having Tingxue as her future daughter-in-law only made her more confident of her plan. 

Tingxue had no choice but to accept the request.

Huang Yaoshi said, “Xiang’er, you’ll head to E’mei Mountain to find the Thunderbolt Bombs. Polu, you’ll sneak into the Ming Cult and steal the Sacred Fire Token. Miss Tingxue, you can accompany my disciple, Cheng Ying, to the Peach Blossom Island to familiarize yourself with the route to the Peach Blossom Island and the formations there. This serves as an additional layer of insurance.

“The Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan spans a century; there’s bound to be hiccups along the way. They could go missing or fall into the wrong hands. So, if you find someone suitable in the meantime, you can guide them straight to the Peach Blossom Island without bothering with the Heavenly Sword or the Dragon Slaying Saber.”

Zu An nodded in agreement. Huang Rong and the others had considered so many different possibilities that it seemed nigh-impossible for the plan to fail.

Tingxue frowned. She instinctively looked at Zu An. It was only after seeing the latter nod that she finally gave her consent.

Huang Rong’s smile widened. 

Huang Yaoshi proceeded to brief Firework about E’mei Mountain’s mystical man, while Huang Rong briefed Zu An about the Ming Cult. 

Zu An was familiar with the Ming Cult a hundred years from now, but he knew nothing about this era’s Ming Cult[3].

He learned from Huang Rong that the Ming Cult’s current leader had the surname of ‘Shi’, and that it was only active in the Western Regions. Their limited activity in the Central Plains meant that little was known about them. Only the Beggar Sect, who had a past conflict with them, had more information. 

The Ming Cult originated from the Persian religion of Manichaeism. Many considered them to be an unorthodox cult, but it would be unwise to judge their morality solely from that. It was rumored that they had a formidable martial art known as the Great Cosmic Shift. 

Zu An jotted down all the Ming Cult’s experts that Huang Rong mentioned in his mind. At the same time, he wondered if the Ming Cult had already made contact with its main Persian sect, given that Old Man Shanzhong had come to the Central Plains.

It was getting late, so the group retired to their rooms for the time being. They would set off tomorrow morning.

Much to Zu An and Tingxue’s bewilderment, they were assigned the same bedroom. 

Xiangyang was severely lacking in resources from many years of war. To lead by example, the Guo Clan had dismantled their mansion’s pillars to repair the city walls, so there were already fewer rooms than before. The simultaneous arrival of Huang Yaoshi, Cheng Ying, and the others only worsened the shortage.

Huang Rong, taking into consideration that Zu And and Tingxue had already dual cultivated, chose to assign them the same room. This would have been considered rude in the past, but etiquette was a luxury when their country’s downfall was imminent. 

In fact, she thought it would be ideal if they could speed things up and leave some descendants for the Guo Clan.

This put Zu An and Tingxue in a tight spot. They stared at the only bed in the room, at a loss for words.

1. Guo Jing, despite being one of the strongest experts in the world, is known to be slow-witted and untalented. Huang Rong helped him a lot by convincing top martial artists to teach him. ☜

2. Qi Men Dun Jia is a divination book, but in the context of Return of the Condor Heroes, it is a set of principles that allows you to construct elaborate labyrinths that can confuse, trap, and even injure others. ☜

3. The Ming Cult only appears in ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’, which is set a hundred years from ‘Return of the Condor Heroes’ ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2797: Holy Maiden
chapters.body:
Chapter 2797: Holy Maiden

“You take the bed. I’ll sleep on the floor.” Zu An reacted fast to the situation. It would appear suspicious if he requested another blanket from Huang Rong, but it was no big deal for a martial artist of his level to do without a blanket.

Tingxue shook her head. “There’s no need for that. I have an idea.”

White silk shot out from her sleeve and pierced the wall, forming a hammock. She gracefully leaped into it. “Xiaolongnu taught me this. I thought it was interesting.” 

Seeing her rest on the silk hammock, Zu An was briefly dazed. He felt as if he was looking at the legendary Xiaolongnu in person.

Just then, the window opened. Zu An and Tingxue looked over and saw Firework sneaking in. 

Tingxue nearly slipped off her silk hammock. “What are you doing here?”

Firework laughed. “It wasn’t easy for me to create an opportunity for you two, but you’re squandering it just like that?”

Tingxue huffed, “Look at the mess you have made!” 

While covering up for Firework, she'd suddenly become the Guo Clan’s daughter-in-law. Somehow, she found herself unable to return to her usual composure. Emotions swayed her much more easily. She had never felt like this before.

“We’re friends. Shouldn’t we help each other out?” Firework replied with a grin. 

Tingxue coldly replied, “What are you doing here so late at night? Are you here to mess around with him again?”

“It’s called training,” Firework corrected her.

Tingxue was speechless.

She wasn't so dense as to be unable to tell that Firework was intentionally getting intimate with Zu An in front of her to provoke her. What kind of perverted inclination is that? She wasn’t about to play along with this nonsense, so she got up and went to the neighboring room.

But Firework grabbed her hand and exclaimed, “Where are you going? I won’t be able to explain it if others see me sharing a room alone with him.”

It would invite controversy if Zu An and Firework, who were siblings in this world, were spotted spending the night together. However, they could still explain it if Tingxue was here as well.

Tingxue’s eyelids twitched. “You want me to stay here to watch you?” 

It was one thing back when we were in different rooms, but you’re going too far now. She wrapped her hand around her sword sheath. 

“I’m just teasing you.” Firework laughed. “Since we’re about to part ways, I’m here to discuss things with you two.”

Zu An burst into laughter. Since our time in the ancient tomb, Firework has reverted to the bubbly girl I met on the spaceship. Could she be suffering from dissociative personality disorder?

“Do you find their plan feasible?”

Zu An replied, “I have to say that they've thought things through. I don’t think we could have done it any better.”

Firework frowned. “But none of the countless challengers before us has cleared this trial. Is there an intrinsic flaw in the plan itself, or is the difficulty in accomplishing the plan greater than expected?”

Tingxue finally expressed her view. “There’s no point thinking so much right now. All we can do right now is to do as we were told and attempt to craft the Heavenly Sword and the Dragon Slaying Saber first.”

“What if it’s a trap?” Unable to be as nonchalant as the Nebula worshiper, Firework turned to Zu An and asked, “You’re familiar with this world’s background, right? Did the plan succeed or not?”

“There were some twists and turns, but in the end, the plan succeeded.” Zu An summarized the story of ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’ for the two of them. 

“What could the problem be?” Firework was perplexed. “Based on what you have shared, I am the future founder of the E’mei Sect, so it makes sense for me to head to E’mei Mountain right now. That seems to be an inevitability of fate.”

“It doesn’t seem likely that you’ll face significant danger during your journey to E’mei Mountain, but you mustn’t let your guard down. Anything involving fate has to be taken seriously.”

“Don’t worry, Tingxue and I should be fine. What’s more worrying is your journey to the Ming Cult.” Firework had already learned about the Ming Cult’s association with Old Man Shanzhong, and the latter had proven himself to be a formidable assassin. 

“I’ll tread carefully,” Zu An replied. 

They continued their discussion through the night, but they still had no clue how to tackle this trial.

The following day, when Guo Fu called them out for breakfast, she saw them sleeping on the same bed and shouted in shock. That jolted Zu An and the others awake. 

Tingxue shot to her feet, whereas Firework was unfazed. 

“We chatted too late into the night, got tired, and fell asleep like that.”

The three of them were bound to mortal bodies in this world. Consecutive days of traveling had caused fatigue to build up in them. When the sky darkened, they fell asleep before they realized it. Needless to say, nothing happened among them. 

Guo Fu huffed, “Xiang’er, you aren’t young anymore. You shouldn’t be fooling around like that.”

She didn’t suspect anything because Tingxue was around as well. Her little sister had always been eccentric from a young age, so it was normal for her to not maintain the usual boundaries between men and women.

Before embarking on their journey, the three of them first paid a visit to Guo Jing and Huang Rong.

As Zu An rushed northwest to Kunlun Mountain, he couldn’t help replaying the conversation they had the night before parting ways. Based on what I remember, the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan succeeded in the end, so why has no one succeeded in the trial to date? 

He didn’t think that those who had come before them wouldn’t be able to obtain the Sacred Fire Token and the Thunderbolt Bombs.

Is the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan not the crux of the trial? Or has this world’s future changed, making the plan doomed to fail? How did the other universal deities tamper with this world?

Zu An had several conjectures in mind, but they needed confirmation. He continued heading toward the Western Regions. 

Around a month later, he finally arrived at the foot of Kunlun Mountain. The long journey made him realize how accustomed he was to flying around. It felt frustrating for him to have to travel by foot like that. 

Soon, he found the Ming Cult’s home at the vast Kunlun Mountain.

The Ming Cult of the present era was nowhere as powerful as they would be in the future. He encountered some guards along the way, but none of them could stop him. He snuck his way toward the Radiant Peak, only to find fewer guards around. Huh? Shouldn’t there be more guards as I approach the Radiant Peak?

He soon learned the reason by eavesdropping on the cult members’ conversations. A holy maiden from the Persian headquarters had graced the Ming Cult, so Cult Leader Shi had gathered all important figures at the main hall to welcome her.

Zu An was surprised. 

A holy maiden from the headquarters? Could it be Purple Dress Dragon King Daiqisi? But that can’t be. That’s a character from ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’, so she hasn’t even been born yet. 

He secretly knocked out a cult member, changed into his clothes, and snuck into the main hall. 

A crowd was gathered in the main hall—they were likely all in the Ming Cult’s upper echelons. Seated on the highest chair was a brawny old man. Judging from his clothes and the respect accorded to him, he was Cult Leader Shi.

Cult Leader Shi coldly said, “Treasure Tree King, the Central Plains Ming Cult may have descended from the Persian Ming Cult, but it has been centuries since we have laid our roots here. The Mongols seek to conquer the Central Plains, and we have sworn to fight them to the bitter end. So, how can we obey the Persian headquarters’ unreasonable order to serve the Mongols?”

Zu An swiftly understood the situation. 

The Persian headquarters must have sent an envoy down to persuade the Central Plains Ming Cult to surrender to the Mongols, but Cult Leader Shi refuses to obey the order. He’s a righteous person.

Zu An was initially worried for the Central Plains Ming Cult for defying the Mongols, but on second thought, they were located in the middle of the remote Kunlun Mountain. The Mongols’ army of hundreds of thousands wouldn’t be of much use here. It was no wonder they were fearless. 

A Persian with a sharp nose and distant eyes replied, “We have different aspirations. We won’t force you to comply since you aren’t willing to do so. I just hope this matter doesn’t impair the goodwill between us.”

The Persian was so understanding that Cult Leader Shi felt embarrassed. The Central Plains Ming Cult did have deep ties with the Persian Ming Cult, after all. 

The Persian continued, “Actually, we have another objective here. A respected elder from our Persian headquarters passed away a year ago. He was from the Central Plains, but he migrated to Persia and joined our Ming Cult. He contributed greatly to our Ming Cult and married a Persian lady, and the two of them had a daughter.

“That respected elder missed his homeland even on his deathbed, and he wished for his daughter to return to the Central Plains. Our cult leader respects his will and commanded me to escort his daughter to the Radiant Peak, hoping that the Central Plains Ming Cult would take good care of her.

“Isabella, come here and pay respects to Cult Leader Shi.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2798: A Clue
chapters.body:
Chapter 2798: A Clue

The Persian envoy stepped aside for a young woman to make her entrance. She was so gorgeous that her presence lit up the room.

The Ming Cult’s main hall should have been a solemn place where important decisions were made, but the young woman’s entrance invited gasps of astonishment. Her beauty felt out of this world. 

Zu An was both surprised and delighted. Despite tapping into the Beggar Sect’s vast information network, he hadn’t been able to find any clues on Isabella all this time. He hadn’t expected to reunite with her here.

Isabella was dressed in Persian clothes, giving her an exotic touch. She stepped forward and gracefully bowed to Cult Leader Shi. Her manners were flawless, as one would have expected from the Acadias’ esteemed big miss.

Upon seeing her, Cult Leader Shi’s heart softened. “Holy maiden, since your father was from the Central Plains, you should treat this place like your home, too. From now on, you’re the Central Plains Ming Cult’s holy maiden. Heed my order, cult members. You are to show utmost respect to the holy maiden from this day onward!”

“Yes, cult leader!” the cult members replied in unison.

Isabella’s appearance alleviated the tense atmosphere. 

The members of the Ming Cult warmly welcomed the envoys from the Persian headquarters with a banquet. To avoid alarming the esteemed holy maiden, Cult Leader Shi instructed a maid to settle her in a quiet residence.

Zu An snuck out of the main hall and followed the maid to the courtyard. It looks like the Ming Cult regards the headquarters’ holy maiden highly. This residence is almost on par with the cult leader’s residence.

While the maids got busy tidying up the place, Isabella sat down alone in the room and sighed. She gazed at the distant mountains outside the window, but she was in no mood to admire this majestic scenery. Instead, she pouted as if she had suffered a grievance.

“Why is our holy maiden brooding alone here?” a teasing voice echoed.

Isabella was frightened, but the voice sounded oddly familiar. “Am I dreaming? Why do I hear his voice?”

“I didn’t expect to bump into you here. It feels like a dream to me as well.” A figure slipped into the room. 

Isabella quickly clasped her about-to-gasp mouth. “Big brother Zu, it really is you!” She was so excited that she immediately leaped into his embrace.

Feeling her trembling body, Zu An also embraced her tightly. “I’ve finally found you.”

He suddenly narrowed his eyes. “Shh, someone’s coming.”

He nimbly leaped out of the window. It would surely cause a huge uproar if the cult members were to see their pure holy maiden embracing a man. 

Several maids entered the room. “Paying respects to the holy maiden. Our cult leader has commanded us to serve the holy maiden from this day onward. Please instruct us if there’s anything you require here.”

Has the holy maiden… been crying? Well, she did leave the place where she grew up and traveled a long distance to get here, so she might be feeling homesick. 

Isabella traded some pleasantries with them before anxiously sending them out. 

“Big brother Zu! Big brother Zu~” She rushed to the windowsill and called out with a quivering voice, afraid that their earlier reunion was merely an illusion.

“Why are you sniffling?”

Zu An's gentle smile pierced through Isabella’s defenses, and she began sobbing in his arms, venting the fear and horror she had been accumulating all this time.

Zu An patted her shoulder and gently consoled her.

Some time later, Isabella, now feeling a little embarrassed, raised her head and wiped off her tears. “I made a fool of myself, big brother Zu.”

“Not at all. If you didn’t react after seeing me, I’d be the one crying.”

“You’re teasing me~”

The two of them spent a while reminiscing about the past before Zu An asked her about what she had been through ever since entering the trial.

After entering the trial, Isabella had assumed the identity of the Persian Ming Cult’s holy maiden. She was respected within the cult, but due to the foreign environment, all the scheming individuals who approached her with ulterior motives, and her failure to find any clues about Zu An despite secretly searching for him, she couldn’t shake off her anxiety.

On top of that, she'd suddenly been forced to leave Persia and travel to a distant place called the Central Plains. She'd thought there was no way she could find Zu An and the others anymore, but little had she expected to reunite with him here. 

“What’s the Persian Ming Cult’s motive for visiting the Central Plains Ming Cult?” Zu An didn’t believe their bullshit story about sending their holy maiden back to her motherland.

“Their foremost agenda was to persuade the Central Plains Ming Cult to surrender to the Mongols, but Cult Leader Shi refused to do so,” Isabella replied. “Their secondary goal is to obtain the secret manual, Great Cosmic Shift.”

“Great Cosmic Shift?” Zu An was confused. “Didn’t the Central Plains Ming Cult inherit the Great Cosmic Shift from the Persian headquarters? Why are they looking for it here?”

Isabella explained, “Persia has been ravaged by war for many years now, resulting in the original manual of the Great Cosmic Shift going missing. That’s why the Persian headquarters covets the copy in the Central Plains Ming Cult. There’s no way the Central Plains Ming Cult would relinquish a martial art as powerful as the Great Cosmic Shift to them, so they instructed me to settle down here and find a chance to steal it.”

She paused for a second before adding, “At all costs.” Her face reddened in embarrassment and anger. 

Zu An understood what that meant for a young, beautiful maiden. He secretly berated the Persian headquarters for being despicable and shameless in his mind before further asking, “Do you know the conditions to clear this trial? Could it be to steal the Great Cosmic Shift?”

Isabella spent a moment in thought before replying, “I don’t think it’s that simple. Stealing the Great Cosmic Shift is merely a part of it. The final goal should be to become the leader of the Persian headquarters.

“There are a total of three holy maidens in the Persian headquarters, including me, and the cult leader of every generation has always been chosen from the three holy maidens. The other two have been dispatched to Eastern Europe and Western Europe, and they have been entrusted with arduous tasks as well.

“The cult leader position will be passed on to the one who contributes the most to the sect. I suspect that stealing the Great Cosmic Shift won’t be enough; I’ll have to persuade the Central Plains Ming Cult to surrender to stand a chance at victory.” 

Isabella noticed Zu An’s prolonged silence and was intrigued. “Big brother Zu, do you have different conditions for clearing the trial?”

“I’m afraid that my conditions for clearing the trial might be contrary to yours…” Zu An replied with a bitter smile before sharing the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan.

Isabella was horrified. “How can this be?!”

To think they were in opposite camps! Didn’t this mean that one of them had to die here?

Zu An consoled Isabella, “It might not be that bad. I’ll find a way for all of us to survive.”

He'd still been confused a moment ago, but learning about Isabella’s mission gave him some peace of mind. Even though they were in opposite camps, there could still be a way for both of them to win. 

He was more concerned about Firework and Tingxue’s missions now. 

Firework’s mission is probably about the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan, or the founding of the E’mei Sect, but what about Tingxue? Is her mission to supervise the plan? Isn’t that too simple?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2799: Secret Tunnel
chapters.body:
Chapter 2799: Secret Tunnel

“Do you know about the Sacred Fire Token?” Zu An asked after some thought.

Isabella nodded. “The Sacred Fire Token is a sacred treasure of the Persian Ming Cult. It’s made from platinum mixed with xuan metal and diamond sand. It’s about 60 centimeters long, has a texture resembling jade, and is extremely hard. You’ll recognize it at a glance.

“Six Sacred Fire Tokens were crafted and passed down to the Central Plains Ming Cult back then. Such sacred treasures are wielded only by the cult leader, so you might have to work on Cult Leader Shi.”

Zu An said, “I don’t think Cult Leader Shi was carrying the Sacred Fire Token earlier. He must have placed it elsewhere.”

“It could be in his room, but I have no idea where it is.”

“It could also be in the secret tunnel. The Radiant Peak has a secret tunnel that only the cult leader can access. Anyone else who enters will be executed. It’s the perfect place to store the Sacred Fire Token.”

“Hm? Big brother Zu, how do you know about that?” Isabella was surprised. Not even the Persian Ming Cult knew about the Radiant Peak’s secret tunnel. 

Zu An briefly shared why he was knowledgeable about this world with her.

Isabella widened her eyes in surprise. “Big brother Zu, you have such a profound connection with the Deity of Death’s trial. It looks like you’re the most likely candidate to clear the trial.”

“No matter what, I’ll find a way to save everyone.”

“I believe you can do it, big brother Zu.”

Zu An was surprised. He'd thought it would take him more effort to convince Isabella, but the latter believed him right away.

Isabella noticed his expression and smiled. “I've seen you pulling off the impossible many times in Dreamland. I trust you.”

In truth, Zu An wasn't very confident despite his declaration. This was a universal deity’s trial, after all. But looking at Isabella’s gleaming eyes, he suddenly felt a surge of courage. 

The two of them continued chatting about what had happened to them respectively after they parted ways, before Zu An took his leave to scout the Radiant Peak.

By the time he returned, it was already nightfall. “Isabella, I found the entrance to the secret tunnel. Let’s investigate it together. We might find the manual for Great Cosmic Shift there.”

“Alright.” Isabella’s eyes lit up. She felt more at ease being with Zu An than staying in her room alone. 

Firework and Tingxue had spent their lives in combat, but Isabella, being born into a powerful background, rarely had to fight. Her combat style revolved around her overpowered treasures, but that put her at a disadvantage in this trial world, where supernatural powers were banned.

Isabella wasn't weak compared to the majority of the martial artists, but it would be a stretch to consider her a top-notch expert. On their way to the secret tunnel, Zu An often had to carry her and employ his movement skills to avoid being discovered by guards along the way.

But Isabella enjoyed it. Her grandfather had built many amusement parks for her across the universes, but even those didn’t bring her this much joy. 

The thrill of possibly being discovered at any moment and the security she felt from Zu An’s embrace felt overwhelming. If not for the inappropriate circumstances, she might have been shrieking by now.

The two of them soon arrived at the secret tunnel. There were no guards stationed here, as it was a forbidden area.

They pushed open the stone door and entered it. A winding path appeared before their eyes. They advanced carefully, but contrary to their expectations, it wasn't booby-trapped.

They traveled around a hundred meters before reaching the end. Isabella examined their surroundings and remarked in surprise, “We seem to have reached a dead end. It doesn’t look like there are any mechanisms installed here.”

Zu An probingly touched the wall before saying, “Please step aside for a moment.”

He channeled his inner energy into the wall and pushed it aside by twenty centimeters, revealing another passageway. Then, he said, “I’d have expected no less from the Ming Cult’s forbidden area. The other mechanisms in the tunnel are delicate, but this stone door has to be opened with brute force. No matter how smart an intruder is, unless their inner energy is strong enough, they won’t be able to enter.”

Isabella was awed. “I hadn’t expected these people from a lower world to be so cunning. We mustn’t let our guard down here.”

Zu An nodded in agreement. Knowing that universal deities could have tampered with this trial world, he'd never treated the people of this world as countryside bumpkins. 

The two of them entered the passageway.

Isabella surveyed their surroundings as she remarked, “There are many forks here. It seems to be a labyrinth.”

“It’s a basic labyrinth,” Zu An replied. Having witnessed major formations in the Myriad Worlds, this labyrinth was indeed nothing in comparison. It didn’t take long for them to find the right path to advance deeper.

Soon, they arrived at a wide room. Its layout hinted that it was the cult leader’s private training room.

Zu An searched the room, but much to his disappointment, he didn’t find the Sacred Flame Token or Great Cosmic Shift manual. 

Come to think of it, in ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’, one of the later cult leaders, Yang Dingtian, died in this secret chamber while carrying the Great Cosmic Shift manual. If I’m not mistaken, the manual is a piece of goatskin leather. I’m many years too early to be finding the manual here. 

I bet Cult Leader Shi carries the manual on him everywhere he goes. 

Zu An shared his deduction with Isabella. While the two of them were planning their next move, they suddenly heard voices and hurriedly darted into the neighboring stone chamber to hide. 

Footsteps approached. There appeared to be two people.

“Cult leader, those Persians are definitely up to something. We should take this chance to deal with them once and for all!”

“That won’t do. They are from the headquarters, and they have come as representatives for the Mongols. The Mongols will retaliate against the killing of their envoys. Back then, Khwarazm was a considerable power in Central Asia, but they killed a Mongol envoy, and that provoked an all-out attack from Mongolia, resulting in their country’s downfall.”

Isabella and Zu An exchanged gazes. One of them had to be Cult Leader Shi, and the other had to be among the Ming Cult’s upper echelon, though his exact identity eluded them. 

“Cult leader, the killing of the envoy was an excuse. The Mongols would have conquered Khwarazm regardless. The Persians are clearly here as scouts. If we allow them to return, the Mongols will have information about our location and geographical terrain. By the time the Mongolian army reaches our base, it’ll be too late for regrets!”

“Things have not come to that point yet. We’re so remotely located that it isn’t worthwhile for the Mongols to invade us. They sent a holy maiden here to serve as a hostage to ease our minds.”

“That holy maiden harbors ulterior motives. I sent someone to check her residence, and she isn’t there! Who knows where she has gone to wreak havoc!”

Hearing those words, Isabella stuck out her tongue. She was in quite a predicament. 

“I need to think things through.” Cult Leader Shi sounded vexed. 

“Cult leader, it’ll be too late if you continue hesitating!”

A sinister laugh suddenly echoed. “It’s already too late.”

Translator's Thoughts

Editor Felis: Anyone else get the song in their head? "Secret tunnel~"

chapters.title: Chapter 2800: Stealing Treasure
chapters.body:
Chapter 2800: Stealing Treasure

Zu An had secretly taken a look. 

The person who was conversing with Cult Leader Shi was a middle-aged man. He was likely the Ming Cult’s Radiant Left Envoy, judging from his position earlier in the main hall. I think his family name is Yi or something. 

In the organization, the Radiant Left Envoy was second only to the cult leader. It made sense for Cult Leader Shi to bring him into this secret tunnel.

But who could the other person be?

Cult Leader Shi and Left Envoy Yi were astounded as well. “Who is it?”

Several figures stepped in.

Zu An glanced at Isabella. The intruders were the Persians who had escorted her here. 

Isabella was stunned as well. She had no idea what those Persians were doing here, but regardless, it was bad news for her. Whether those Persians succeeded or not, it would put her in a spot, especially since she had to settle down here. 

“Treasure Tree King, the Three Envoys of Wind, Cloud, and Moon, what are you doing here?” Cult Leader Shi’s eyes were cold. It wasn't a joke that anyone who barged into the secret tunnel without the cult leader’s permission would be executed. 

Zu An eyed the Persians. The Three Envoys of Wind, Cloud, and Moon were two men and one woman. The two men had vicious eyes, whereas the woman wore a veil and had a figure that was more voluptuous than the women of the Central Plains. 

Since their ages don’t match, they can’t be the same Three Envoys of Wind, Cloud, and Moon in ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber'. They must have always been two men and one woman.

“We would never have known about your secret ploy against us if we hadn’t come here,” the Treasure Tree King sneered in halting Chinese. 

Left Envoy Yi roared in anger, “Don’t you know that those who barge into the forbidden area will be executed without mercy?”

The Three Envoys of Wind, Cloud, and Moon suddenly launched an attack. 

Left Envoy Yi was a top expert; he couldn’t have climbed to his current position otherwise. 

He quickly defended himself, but the three envoys used unorthodox martial arts that were hard to read, and their teamwork was impeccable. One swept his legs. One grabbed his hand. One attacked his acupoints. There was no chance for him to counterattack at all. 

It didn’t take long for the three envoys to defeat Left Envoy Yi. It happened so abruptly that Cult Leader Shi couldn’t make it in time to support him.

Left Envoy Yi felt so ashamed that he would have dug a hole and buried himself on the spot. He had always been proud of his strength, believing himself to only be second to Cult Leader Shi in the Ming Cult. Yet he was swiftly curbed by these enemies.

He felt icy-cold inner energy gush into his acupoints, assaulting him with excruciating pain and preventing him from channeling his inner energy.

Zu An clearly saw what had happened. The Three Envoys of Wind, Cloud, and Moon’s martial arts were so unorthodox that they could easily curb most enemies unless one was significantly stronger than them.

Cult Leader Shi’s expression turned grim. He was shocked by the three envoys’ martial arts as well. “What do you mean by this?”

The Treasure Tree King laughed. “Cult Leader Shi, we’re doing this out of self-defense, lest you join forces to kill us. You don’t have to worry about Left Envoy Yi’s safety. All you have to do is to defect to the Mongols. That’d make you my superior in the future. I wouldn’t dare offend you.” 

Cult Leader Shi’s expression darkened. “I believe I made myself clear earlier.”

“You’re putting me in a difficult spot.” The Treasure Tree King shook his head. “There are voices in the headquarters who want to slowly advance our goals and buy time for the holy maiden to find the Great Cosmic Shift, but I don’t see the need to play the long game here.”

Zu An and Isabella exchanged looks. Looks like there are diverging opinions in the Persian Ming Cult. The Treasure Tree King might be a supporter of another holy maiden, and he’s doing this to foil Isabella’s mission.

Cult Leader Shi harrumphed. “I knew you harbored ulterior motives. I should have made a move earlier.” 

He secretly retreated while lamenting. This was the Central Plains Ming Cult, his home turf. No matter how powerful the Treasure Tree King and the Three Envoys of Wind, Cloud, and Moon were, they would be doomed with everyone charging at them at once. 

The only problem was that the other cult members were forbidden from stepping into the secret tunnel. If he died here, no one would find him for a long time.

But the Persians saw through his intention. The Three Envoys of Wind, Cloud, and Moon quickly spread out to block his path of retreat.

“Does the great Cult Leader Shi plan to escape like a downtrodden dog?” the Treasure Tree King sneered.

Cult Leader Shi harrumphed.

All of a sudden, he lunged at the veiled woman. He was planning to take down the weakest Radiant Moon Envoy as quickly as possible to unravel the Three Envoys of Wind, Cloud, and Moon’s collaboration formation. 

But the Radiant Moon envoy was guarded against his assault. She dodged his attack with an unorthodox movement skill while scoffing, “Tsk tsk tsk. Is Cult Leader Shi interested in me? But I’m not sure if an old man like you has the stamina to take me on.”

Cult Leader Shi’s face darkened, and his attacks became even more ferocious. Faced with great pressure, the Radiant Moon Envoy couldn’t maintain her smile anymore. 

The Flowing Cloud Envoy and the Profound Wind Envoy quickly reinforced her. The three envoys had been working together for such a long time that they could read each other’s intentions. Their teamwork was impeccable.

Cult Leader Shi was able to stand his ground despite simultaneously fighting three opponents, though.

“Is that the legendary Great Cosmic Shift? It’s unfathomable indeed.” The Wind, Cloud, and Moon Envoys keenly sensed their inner energies being directed against their own comrades, which made it seem as if they were fighting among themselves instead.

Cult Leader Shi harrumphed. Their martial arts are too unorthodox. I can’t read their moves. Somehow, they're always able to dodge my attacks from unbelievable angles, causing my fatal blows to miss. And that Treasure Tree King is eyeing me like a hawk from the side. 

As the fight dragged on, he grew increasingly agitated. 

Meanwhile, Zu An watched the battle with a frown. 

Cult Leader Shi is in a terrible position, but for some reason, the only top martial art he's using is the Great Cosmic Shift. His other moves are strong in their own right, but they aren’t top-notch.

Why isn’t he using the Nine Heavens Palm? That’s the move that skyrocketed Cult Leader Yang Dingtian to the top of the martial world in the future—though he failed to best Zhang Sanfeng.

Even the head of the Shaolin Temple’s Three Elders lost an eye to the Nine Heavens Palm! His two juniors spent decades developing the Vajra Evil Subduing Ring, but they dared not confront Yang Dingtian to avenge their senior.

So, why is Cult Leader Shi not using the Nine Heavens Palm? Does he plan on catching the Treasure Tree King by surprise?

But Zu An soon rejected that conjecture. 

The Treasure Tree King found an opening and hurled his fist into Cult Leader Shi’s back. 

Cult Leader Shi had planned to use Great Cosmic Shift to transfer the force behind the fist to the Three Envoys of Wind, Cloud, and Moon, but he found himself unable to move. The four Persians took advantage of that opening to strike Cult Leader Shi’s acupoint.

Pu!

Cult Leader Shi spat blood. He crumpled to the ground, unable to fight anymore. 

“Cult leader!” Left Envoy Yi was enraged, but he was unable to do anything about it. 

Sensing Cult Leader Shi’s confusion, the Treasure Tree King explained with a smile, “Great Cosmic Shift originates from our Persian headquarters. We might have lost the main manual, but we still have texts to understand its unique traits. You have only reached the third layer of Great Cosmic Shift; there’s no way you could have transferred my attack.”

Cult Leader Shi’s face turned pale. To think that I’d die here due to a moment of carelessness. 

“I reckon he’s carrying the Great Cosmic Shift manual with him. Radiant Moon Envoy, search his body.” The Treasure Tree King was worried Cult Leader Shi had a final ace up his sleeve and dared not risk his life.

“Yes!” The Radiant Moon Envoy rushed forward to search Cult Leader Shi’s body. “Oh? I didn’t expect someone as old as you to have a decent figure.”

Soon, her eyes lit up. She lifted a piece of lambskin and exclaimed, “Found it!”

The Treasure Tree King was overjoyed. He was about to take it when a shadow flashed across the room and snatched the lambskin. 

Only then did the crowd realize there was another young man in the room.

“Who are you?!” the Treasure Tree King roared in anger. Does the Central Plains Ming Cult still have other hidden experts? He must have been hiding here for some time now, but to think that none of us noticed him!

Cult Leader Shi and Left Envoy Yi traded perplexed looks. That guy isn’t a member of our Ming Cult. What is he doing in the secret tunnel?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2801: A Forced Compromise
chapters.body:
Chapter 2801: A Forced Compromise

Zu An couldn’t be bothered to waste his breath. “Did you bring the Sacred Fire Tokens here?”

Cult Leader Shi and Left Envoy Yi were taken aback. We thought he was here to reinforce us, but it looks like he’s after the Sacred Fire Tokens.

The Treasure Tree King and the Three Envoys of Wind, Cloud, and Moon were enraged. How dare this fellow covet our Sacred Fire Tokens? The former yelled, “Hand that goatskin to me, and I’ll spare your life!”

Even the great leader of the Ming Cult and its Radiant Left Envoy had been subdued by them. He didn’t think a young man in his twenties could be stronger than them.

Zu An shook his head. “That won’t do. My friend needs the Great Cosmic Shift manual.”

Isabella chuckled from the shadows, her laughter bringing a tinge of sweetness to the dull secret chamber.

“You’re courting death!” The Treasure Tree King thrust his palm at Zu An.

Zu An stood his ground and confronted the palm head-on. 

“Be careful! There’s something peculiar about his inner energy!” Cult Leader Shi warned them. While this mysterious fellow didn’t appear to be an ally, he harbored greater animosity toward the Persians. The Treasure Tree King’s peculiar inner energy had overwhelmed him with pain earlier, resulting in his eventual incapacitation.

“It’s too late!” the Treasure Tree King sneered. His inner energy morphed into thin needles to pierce Zu An’s palm acupoints. 

It was their signature skill, Bone Penetrating Needles. It involved manifesting one's inner yin energy into needles as thin as a hair, transmitting it into an enemy’s body and repeatedly piercing the enemy from within. The pain inflicted was so great that it would briefly stun anyone. They also had another skill called the Yin Gale Knife. They could manifest their inner energy as invisible blades to pierce the enemy’s hand acupoints to inflict considerable damage. 

It was with these attacks that they'd subdued Cult Leader Shi and Left Envoy Yi with ease. Their unorthodox martial arts played a part, but these skills were the ones that had allowed them to catch their enemies by surprise.

But Zu An took his palm without flinching in the slightest. 

“Impossible!” The Treasure Tree King was certain he'd hurled out a few Bone Penetrating Needles, but they seemed to be ineffective against Zu An. 

Bewildered, he quickly manifested more Bone Penetrating Needles, but the moment he channeled his inner energy, it began gushing out toward Zu An’s palm like a breached dam.

To his relief, the Three Envoys attacked at that moment, forcing Zu An to release him.

Sheesh, the simplified version of the Heaven-Devouring Sutra I modified for this world is nothing compared to the real deal. Otherwise, I could have sucked the Treasure Tree King dry there and then. 

Even so, that brief contact left the Treasure Tree King sweating profusely. He anxiously shouted a warning, “Be careful, he’s capable of sorcery! Avoid physical contact with him!”

The Three Envoys were startled. Their attacks required physical contact to send their Bone Penetrating Needles and Yin Gale Knives into their enemy’s body. Otherwise, their power would be greatly decreased. But they had also seen what happened to the Treasure Tree King earlier, so they obediently changed their moves. 

The Three Envoys employed bizarre martial arts that martial artists of the Central Plains struggled to deal with, but with his sharp eyes, Zu An easily deciphered them.

The trio hurriedly changed their moves, but in the midst of doing so, they revealed a huge opening for a brief instant. Zu An unhesitatingly charged in and struck the Wind Envoy and Cloud Envoy, causing the two of them to collapse like sacks of mud. 

Horrified, the Radiant Moon Envoy immediately turned around to flee, only to find herself immobilized. To her horror, Zu An’s palm was already on her shoulder. 

Cult Leader Shi and Left Envoy Yi gasped in shock. Having crossed blows with the Persians, they knew that they were difficult opponents. Yet Zu An had made quick work of them.

“Spare me, young master. I’m willing to serve you…” The Radiant Moon Envoy attempted to seduce Zu An. Her beauty had never failed her. She had even charmed the Flowing Cloud Envoy and Profound Wind Envoy, which was why the other two died first even though she was the weakest in the group. 

“My women are a hundred times better than you.” 

Zu An was about to end her when a voice suddenly spoke up, “Please have mercy!”

Isabella held up her skirt and rushed out. Faced with Zu An’s questioning gaze, she quickly explained, “Big sister Radiant Moon took good care of me at the Persian headquarters.”

Zu An recalled that there were two other holy maidens in the Persian Ming Cult. Isabella will need helpers to become the cult leader. So, he nodded. “I’ll spare you for Isabella’s sake.”

“Thank you, great hero! Thank you, holy maiden!” The Radiant Moon Envoy was extremely relieved to have survived this crisis. 

The Treasure Tree King was infuriated. “Isabella, you traitor! How dare you collude with outsiders against us?”

Isabella’s expression turned cold. “Our cult leader ordered me to infiltrate the Central Plains Ming Cult to obtain the Great Cosmic Shift, but your actions have compromised my position in the Central Plains Ming Cult. Who is the traitor here?”

Zu An was impressed by Isabella’s air of majesty. He was so accustomed to her little sister vibes that he nearly forgot that she was the Acadias’ big miss, a powerhouse in the Universal Conglomerate. 

The Treasure Tree King sweated profusely. He could tell that Zu An was on close terms with Isabella, and he happened to be the supporter of another holy maiden. Seeing that he had lost the upper hand, he roared and feigned an attack, only to abruptly turn around and flee. After seeing what the latter was capable of, he didn’t dare to confront Zu An.

But when he realized he was being dragged back into the chamber, fear swiftly gripped him. 

Zu An wasn’t about to let him escape. He pulled the Treasure Tree King into his grasp with the simplified Heaven-Devouring Sutra and siphoned his inner energy dry. 

Earlier, he had chosen not to siphon the inner energies of the Three Envoys, as it would risk revealing an opening for his enemies to exploit, but since the battle had already ended, there was no harm absorbing the Treasure Tree King’s inner energy to bulk up. The stronger he was, the more prepared he would be for future crises.

When they saw the brawny Treasure Tree King withering in an instant, Cult Leader Shi and Left Envoy Yi felt shivers run down their spine. 

Zu An looked at the duo. “Where are the Sacred Fire Tokens?”

Cult Leader Shi raised his head and declared, “Go ahead and kill me. I’ll never hand you our cult’s sacred treasure!”

Isabella interjected, “He just saved your lives. Isn’t that worth the Sacred Fire Tokens?”

Cult Leader Shi snorted, “How can I trade the Sacred Fire Tokend for my life? I wouldn’t be able to face my predecessors when I die!”

Left Envoy Yi nodded in agreement. “Our cult leader is right. Kill us if you want to. We don’t need your pity!”

Isabella wanted to continue persuading them, but Zu An stopped her and said, “It’s also fine by me if you don’t want to hand over the Sacred Fire Tokend. I’ll just kill the two of you, strip your bodies, and tell your people that you were messing around in the cult’s sacred land. How does that sound?”

Both Cult Leader Shi and Left Envoy Yi were horrified. “How dare you!”

“You despicable scum!”

The more they berated Zu An, the colder his expression became. In the end, Cult Leader Shi had no choice but to surrender. “I’m powerless to stop you from stealing the Sacred Fire Tokend. Do you dare leave your name behind?”

“Me?” A thought suddenly popped into Zu An’s mind. “I am from the Beggar Sect.”

He remembered that in ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’, the Beggar Sect had obtained the Ming Cult’s Sacred Fire Tokens from their 31st Cult Leader Shi.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2802: A Ray Of Hope
chapters.body:
Chapter 2802: A Ray Of Hope

Upon mentioning the Beggar Sect, Zu An felt as if he had had an epiphany, but that feeling passed him by before he could grasp it. 

“The Sacred Fire Tokens are in my bedroom,” Cult Leader Shi suddenly said.

“Bring me there.” To stop them from playing any tricks, Zu An layered more restrictions on Cult Leader Shi and Left Envoy Yi.

Cult Leader Shi and Left Envoy Yi traded helpless and fearful gazes. They weren’t afraid of death, but this fellow was too despicable.

So, they left the secret tunnel and made their way to Cult Leader Shi’s room.
