Despite bumping into several cult members along the way, both Cult Leader Shi and Left Envoy Yi knew they would only be dragging their cult members down by asking for help, so they didn’t bother putting up any resistance.

The cult members thought that Cult Leader Shi was bringing the Radiant Left Envoy, the holy maiden, and the Persian envoys to his room for a private conversation, so they didn’t probe into it.

Cult Leader Shi opened a hidden compartment built into his room and took out six Sacred Fire Tokens. 

Zu An took the tokens. They felt heavy, and they had a peculiar texture that felt like an amalgamation of metal and jade. There was no way to fabricate such a material. 

“I’ll borrow your cult’s Sacred Fire Tokens. You can be assured I’ll return them to the Radiant Peak once I’m done with them.” Zu An stowed away the Sacred Fire Tokens. He didn’t explain further as he didn’t want to reveal the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan. 

Cult Leader Shi harrumphed. “I only have myself to blame for my weakness. Should a cult member surpass me in the future, you can be certain he’ll knock on the Beggar Sect’s doors.”

Zu An simply smiled, not bothering to argue. He took the goatskin containing the Great Cosmic Shift manual and handed it to Isabella. 

Isabella stared at the blank goatskin in a daze. The sharp-witted Radiant Moon Envoy bit her finger and scattered her blood on it. Only then did pictures and words surface on the goatskin.

Isabella passed the goatskin to Zu An. “Big brother, why don’t you practice it too? This is the Ming Cult’s highest martial art. It should prove to be useful in this world.”

Indeed, the Great Cosmic Shift likely wouldn't be much in the Myriad Worlds, but it was definitely one of the strongest martial arts within the restrictions of this trial world. So, Zu An didn’t turn down her offer. 

“You should study it, too.”

Isabella leaned on his shoulder, and the two of them studied the manual together. Moments later, their expressions darkened. 

Zu An leaped away and knocked out the Radiant Moon Envoy and Left Envoy Yi. Then, he glared coldly at Cult Leader Shi and asked, “Where’s the real Great Cosmic Shift manual?”

Cult Leader Shi laughed. “That’s the real one.”

“Impossible! This manual only contains the first three layers!”

The parts detailing the subsequent layers were so badly damaged that there were only bits and pieces left, and the choice of words was so obscure that it was impossible to comprehend them without context.

Cult Leader Shi sneered, “Did the Persian Ming Cult, despite being the creators of the Great Cosmic Shift, lose their manual too? In the history of the Central Plains Ming Cult, only the powerful Cult Leader Zhong has been able to reach the fifth layer. Only a small handful of cult leaders had been able to reach the fourth layer. Furthermore, we have faced a great deal of infighting over the years. We only have the first three layers now.”

Isabella believed his words, but Zu An’s expressiom darkened. “Impossible. There has to be a complete Great Cosmic Shift manual. Where did you hide it? Don’t force me to resort to violence!”

Cult Leader Shi’s face reddened in anger. “I’d have practiced it if I had the complete manual; then I wouldn’t have been captured by the likes of you people!”

Isabella pulled Zu An aside and whispered, “I don’t think he’s lying. Big brother Zu, you don’t have to worry about me. It looks like I’m fated to fail my mission.”

Zu An patted her head. “It’s still too early to give up, Isabella. There has to be a complete Great Cosmic Shift manual in this world. Have you forgotten the plotline of the ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’ I shared with you?”

Isabella was startled. “Ah, you’re right. Could there be another copy of the Great Cosmic Shift hiding somewhere?”

“Impossible. The Great Cosmic Shift manual is strictly passed down only through the line of cult leaders. There can’t be a copy of it.” Cult Leader Shi refuted that conjecture. 

Zu An pondered for a long while. “Cult Leader Shi, I’ll be staying at your Ming Cult’s Radiant Peak for a while. I hope you don’t mind.”

Cult Leader Shi was speechless. Will you leave if I say that I mind you staying?

Obviously, that was impossible. Zu An stayed behind, taking up residence beside Isabella. 

In Isabella’s room, Isabella worriedly asked, “Big brother Zu, do you think Cult Leader Shi will rally the experts of the Ming Cult to encircle us?”

“You don’t need to worry about that. Cult Leader Shi is a smart man. He knows it’s futile to send his cult members at us, and there’s no need to take such a risk since we don’t plan to destroy the Ming Cult. He’ll weigh the pros and cons and decide on the optimal course of action.”

“Then why did you choose to stay behind?”

“I need time to think things through. I have an idea what the goal of the trial is.”

“Oh? What is it?”

“It won’t work if I say it aloud.”

“You’re terrible, big brother Zu.”

Just like that, Zu An stayed on the Radiant Peak for half a month.

…

“Impossible!” Cult Leader Shi exclaimed while staring at the goatskin presented to him. 

It contained the subsequent layers of the Great Cosmic Shift manual, which should have been lost to time.

“There’s nothing impossible about it. I found the full manual,” Zu An said.

Cult Leader Shi was skeptical. Who knows if you fabricated it? 

He browsed the manual and found that the subsequent layers were aligned with the first three layers he had grasped. He had spent many years studying the Great Cosmic Shift, and he couldn’t confidently say that this manual was fake. 

He wanted to study the goatskin more, but Zu An sucked it back into his palm. “I’m not giving you the full manual for free. There is a condition.”

“What is it?” Cult Leader Shi’s eyes heated up. He would even step down as the cult leader if it meant completing the lost layers of the Great Cosmic Shift.

“Join the Mongols.” 

“Dream on!” Cult Leader Shi was infuriated. “I thought the Beggar Sect was persistently holding the line against the Mongols thus far, but to think you people are already thinking about surrendering.”

“Calm down, Cult Leader Shi. I’m merely asking you to surrender in name.”

“Surrender in name?”

“How much longer do you think Southern Song can hold on against the Mongolian invasion?”

Cult Leader Shi pondered. “At most a few years more.”

“Once Southern Song is destroyed, the Mongols will have the manpower to clear away surrounding flies. How long do you think the Ming Cult can last against the Mongol army?” 

“Hmph! Kunlun is a vast mountain. The Mongols will suffer huge casualties if they invade our Radiant Peak. The Mongol Khan won’t be so foolish.”

“What if they execute the surrendered soldiers of Southern Song instead?”

Cult Leader Shi was startled. Cold sweat rained down his back. He had turned down the Persian envoy because he knew it simply wasn't worthwhile for the Mongolians to send their troops to attack the Ming Cult.

But it was very possible for the Mongols to execute the surrendered Song soldiers instead. That was an effective way to reduce the size of the Song army, thus making it less likely for the Han to overthrow them in the future.

“But if we surrender to the Mongols, we’ll become a laughingstock.”

“Only a coward dies in vain. A hero stands firm amid criticisms to accomplish their goal,” Zu An argued. “Besides, you’d just be feigning surrender. You have a holy maiden here to help you strike diplomatic relations with the Mongols; there would be no reason for the Mongols to interfere in the distant Radiant Peak. The barbarians won’t be so lucky as to last beyond a century. Who knows? The Ming Cult might just play a critical role in overthrowing the Yuan Mongols.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2803: Tingxue Needs To Have A Child
chapters.body:
Chapter 2803: Tingxue Needs To Have A Child

Cult Leader Shi thought Zu An had come here for the Sacred Fire Tokens, but somehow, the latter was now strategizing for the Ming Cult against the Mongols. He couldn’t fathom what Zu An’s true goal was. 

“It doesn’t matter who I am. The important thing is for you to choose what’s beneficial to the Ming Cult.” Zu An briefly hesitated but chose against revealing his identity. Guo Polu was an unknown figure in this world. Leaving his name here could affect the trajectory of events. 

“You snatch our Ming Cult’s Sacred Fire Tokens and Great Cosmic Shift manual, yet you claim to be working in our interest?” Cult Leader Shi was bubbling with anger.

“You would have been killed by the Persians if not for me. I saved your life. Once you were dead, the Persians would have taken over the Ming Cult and brought it under the Mongols’ rule. The Ming Cult would have become nothing but a shell of its former self.

“I'm not only pointing out a road for the Ming Cult’s survival, but also offering to return the lost Great Cosmic Shift to you. Shouldn’t you be thankful to me?” 

Cult Leader Shi fell silent. He couldn’t refute what Zu An had said, and he slowly calmed down from his initial anger. 

“Don’t worry, the Sacred Fire Tokens will be returned to the Ming Cult in the future,” Zu An spoke so confidently that his words felt prophetic. 

Cult Leader Shi wondered if Zu An was an immortal who'd descended to the mortal world. On top of wielding martial arts surpassing that of the legendary Five Greats, Zu An had also filled up the incomplete portions of the Great Cosmic Shift manual, and he spoke compellingly as if he could see the future. 

“How can you ensure that our Ming Cult won’t be annexed by the Mongols?”

Seeing that Cult Leader Shi had finally loosened his stance, Zu An called Isabella over to discuss the finer details. 

Following that, he bade Isabella farewell. “I have to bring the Sacred Fire Tokens to Xiangyang, so I can’t accompany you to Persia. Can you handle those matters by yourself?”

Isabella replied with a sweet smile, “Rest assured, big brother Zu. You've settled the matters of the Great Cosmic Shift manual and the Central Plains Ming Cult’s surrender to the Mongols for me, and I have the Radiant Moon Envoy to aid me now. If I still can’t become the cult leader, I might as well buy a slab of tofu and smash my head into it.”

Zu An nodded in agreement. Isabella was the Acadias’ big miss, and he had witnessed her socializing prowess from how she'd easily dealt with the experts coming from all over the Myriad Worlds. There was indeed no need to worry about her. 

“Remember, you have to withhold the Great Cosmic Shift manual from the Persians. They can’t know about it.” Zu An knew how the plotline progressed. Decades later, the Persian Ming Cult would dispatch the Purple Robe Dragon King over to the Central Plains Ming Cult to steal the Great Cosmic Shift again.

“You can put your mind at ease. I know how to deal with it.” Isabella bit her lips and embraced Zu An tightly. “Big brother Zu, I don’t know if this will be our final meeting.”

Zu An fell silent. It was one thing for them to be separated, but if they couldn’t clear this trial, all of them would die.

“Don’t worry. I’m confident things will work out.”

“Mm! I trust you, big brother Zu.”

… 

Zu An rushed back to Xiangyang. Firework and Tingxue were already waiting for him. Both E’mei and the Peach Blossom Island were closer than Kunlun Mountain, not to mention he had spent an entire month there. 

Guo Jing and Huang Rong looked visibly relieved when they saw him. Even Firework and Tingxue felt as if a boulder weighing on their hearts had been released. Despite knowing that few in this world could pose a threat to him, they'd still been worried by his late return.

Zu An entrusted the Sacred Fire Tokens to Guo Jing and Huang Rong for the forging of the Heavenly Sword and the Dragon Slaying Saber. “I promised Cult Leader Shi I would return the Sacred Fire Tokens to them after we’re done.”

Guo Jing was overjoyed. “That goes without saying! Cult Leader Shi is magnanimous to lend us the Ming Cult’s sacred treasure!”

Huang Rong was perplexed. She hadn't thought Cult Leader Shi would lend out the Sacred Fire Tokens, but the proof was right before their eyes. She didn’t dwell on it too much since they were in a rush to forge the sword and saber, though.

Firework pulled Zu An and Tingxue to her room and asked him for updates.

“Tell me what happened on your side first.”

“Tingxue went to the Peach Blossom Island and familiarized herself with the route and formation. Nothing noteworthy happened, other than her pummeling perverts along the way. E’mei Mountain’s Thunderbolt Bomb was tricky, but something of that caliber can’t stump me.” Firework had a wistful look on her face. “But E’mei Mountain’s cloud sea left me with a deep impression. It was as mesmerizing as the fireworks I saw when I was sixteen.”

Firework snapped out of her daze and groaned, “Ugh, that’s a memory from this body’s original owner. How annoying!”

Zu An was surprised to hear that.

“Quick, tell us what happened on your side,” Firework urged. Tingxue also looked at him with curious eyes. 

Zu An briefly shared what he'd been through with them. Firework exclaimed in surprise, “To think you actually met Isabella! I have to say you have quite the luck!”

“Why didn’t Isabella return with you?” Tingxue was confused. 

Zu An told them about her mission.

Firework frowned. “Her mission is at odds with ours?”

“Not necessarily.” Zu An smiled. “Her mission was to obtain the Great Cosmic Shift and convince the Central Plains Ming Cult to surrender to the Mongols, so she could become the leader of the Persian headquarters. That’s not at odds with our mission, and I resolved it for her…”

He shared the details with them.

Firework frowned. “Won’t we be goners if she clears her mission first?”

“It isn’t that easy to clear the trial,” Zu An said. “I had an epiphany while I was at Kunlun Mountain. Perhaps those who challenged this trial before us failed because they refused to work together, thus resulting in their failure. Think about it. Each of us was assigned a different mission, with varying levels of difficulty. How is this a fair assessment?”

Firework frowned. “I see where you’re coming from, but how can the trial decide who is worthy to succeed the Deity of Death? No one wants to become a foil to another person.”

Zu An shook his head. “I’m unsure of the details right now. We can only take it a step at a time.”

There was no lack of smart people who'd challenged this trial before them, and some of them must have thought about cooperating with one another. Yet no one had succeeded to date. That suggested there was another crucial factor leading to their failure.

“What’s your plan?” Firework asked.

“Your mission is easy. On top of ensuring the successful forging of the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber, your other mission should be the establishment of the E’mei Sect. But things are a bit trickier for her.” Zu An looked at Tingxue with worried eyes. 

“Isn’t her mission just to supervise the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan? That’s the easiest mission of all. I don’t see how that can be tricky.”

“There’s a huge gap in the timeline. The Yuan Mongols will only be overthrown eighty years later, but given her mortal body, she won’t survive that long in this trial world.” Zu An paused before hesitantly adding. “She needs to have a child.”

Tingxue was stunned.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2804: A Crucial Figure
chapters.body:
Chapter 2804: A Crucial Figure

Firework widened her eyes in bewilderment. She glanced at Tingxue, followed by Zu An, before finally asking, “Are you saying that because you covet her body?”

“Of course not! The Ancient Tomb Sect needs to have a successor for us to close the loop decades from now,” Zu An exclaimed indignantly. He turned to Tingxue and asked, “Do you have any siblings, or rather, does Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu have other children?”

“Not at all.” Tingue’s face darkened. 

Zu An was put in a spot. They needed to have successors to ensure the plan went smoothly. 

Firework laughed. “Now I know why the powerhouses who came before us failed the trial. Cooperation is tough for them, and they are too proud to leave offspring with the natives of this backward world. To make things worse, this world limits their lifespan, so there’s no way they could last eighty years.” 

She turned to Tingxue and suggested, “Why don’t you get your parents to grind out a handful of siblings for you? I heard Xiaolongnu was the number one beauty of the martial world in her era. Is that Yang Guo still a man if he doesn’t make her pop out a dozen kids?” 

Dark streaks appeared on Tingxue’s face. “According to my body’s memories, those two spend their days immersed in each other as if they exist in their own world. They think I’m merely an accident, a third party who disturbs their peace. They've been traveling around the world ever since I was very young.”

Zu An was speechless.

Firework said, “…No wonder my Guo Xiang couldn’t find Yang Guo.”

Zu An was worried. “I’m not sure they could have kids even if they wanted to. They should be in their fifties now.”

Those two had faced so many tribulations along the way that they were no longer young by the time they finally got together, and many years had passed since then. Even top martial artists had to obey the rules of the world. 

Firework remarked with an enigmatic smile, “Since those two can’t do it, I guess you’ll have to do it with Tingxue.”

Tingxue was speechless.

Zu An huffed. “Can you stop stirring chaos at a time like this?”

Firework laughed. “Founding the E’mei Sect and becoming a nun doesn’t sound too bad now.”

Tingxue placed her hand on her sword. Firework finally changed the topic and asked, “Are there any other difficulties aside from this?”

“There is. We have to find someone called Yang Dingtian.”

“That shouldn’t be too hard if we use the Beggar Sect’s information network. How old is he? What are his unique traits?” Firework had gained a deeper insight into this world from Huang Rong in recent days. 

“Let me see. I don’t think he’s been born yet.”

“Are you messing with me?”

“Of course not. He might not have much screentime, but he plays a pivotal role in the ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’-verse.” Zu An proceeded to share Yang Dingtian’s background in ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’ with Firework and Tingxue. 

“Can’t you just tell me everything in a single shot? You’re telling me things a droplet at a time like a leaky tap.” Firework clenched her jaws in anger.

“The worldbuilding is too deep. I only remember certain details when I encounter them,” Zu An replied with a sigh. 

Tingxue, who had been quiet all this while, spoke up, “You mentioned that this world is a mix of historical events and the contents of several novels. Why don’t you tell us everything you know about this world while we’re staying here?”

She wasn’t about to blindly believe everything Zu An told her. The suggestions he had been randomly dropping were almost enough to give her a heart attack. Who knew what else he would suggest in the future?

Firework nodded in agreement. “That’s right. Tell us everything you know. We might be able to spot details you have overlooked and fill in the gaps.”

She felt nervous making that proposal. Zu An’s greatest edge over them in this trial world was the information he wielded, and that made him the most likely one to clear this trial. If he refused to divulge the information he knew, it would mean all his talk about them clearing the trial together was lip service, and he still prioritized himself above all things else. Despite having lived long enough to grow skeptical of others, she still felt nervous, as if she was afraid that she would be disappointed by his response.

Zu An answered without hesitation, “You’re right. We should brainstorm to check for gaps.”

Firework laughed. He’s looking cuter and cuter.

Zu An was oblivious to the changes in her state of mind. He shared the wuxiaverse created by Jin Yong with them, going from book to book in chronological order. He raked up details from the novels from the dredges of his mind and shared them in elaboration. His mind was much stronger than before he transmigrated, allowing him to recall most details.

Time passed swiftly.

Halfway through, Guo Fu dropped by to check on them and saw them chatting together on the bed. She was amazed by how close they were, but at the same time, she was also perplexed as Guo Xiang and Guo Polu had never been this close before. Even I’m feeling envious now. 

Also, hasn’t Xiang’er been searching for Yang Guo all these years? Why is she getting close with his daughter?

The situation didn’t make sense to her. 

It went without saying that the trio noticed Guo Fu’s arrival, but they were too busy brainstorming the possibilities in the plotline, so they couldn’t be bothered with her. Fortunately, Guo Fu got bored shortly after and left. 

The following day, they realized that they'd fallen asleep on the same bed again, but they didn’t feel as awkward as they did on the first time. On the contrary, they were becoming used to it. 

“Other than Yang Dingtian, the Ming Cult’s future Radiant Left Envoy, Yang Xiao, is also a problematic one. How did he learn Peach Blossom Island’s Divine Flick? Is he Yang Guo’s descendant?” Firework asked with a frown while tidying herself up. 

“That’s unlikely. The age doesn’t match, and Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu don’t have a son. Even if…” Tingxue paused as her face reddened. “Even if I have a child, I doubt his surname will be Yang.”

Firework looked at Zu An. 

Zu An replied with a frown, “That’s weird. The Yellow Dress Maiden in Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber definitely goes by the surname of Yang.”

“Then there’s no way to explain it. It’s unlikely Xiaolongnu can have more children. Could it be a child between Yang Guo and another woman? If so, who could it be?” Firework quickly racked her brains. It was unlikely to be her, since she became a nun in this world’s timeline. 

A beautiful face suddenly popped into her mind, and she asked with a frown, “Could it be Guo Fu? She’s a bit younger, and she's maintained herself well. I think she can still have a child. Last night, you mentioned that Yang Guo and Guo Fu were betrothed to each other, right?”

“But Guo Fu and Yelu Qi haven't had a child even though so many years have passed.” Tingxue nonchalantly voiced her disagreement as if they weren’t talking about her father.

“Tsk. They either don’t love each other enough, or that Yelu Qi is lacking as a man. I think Guo Fu has the right figure to have a child,” Firework casually remarked. 

Zu An was rendered speechless. “I doubt it. There are theories floating around, like Yelu Qi surrendering to the Mongols when Xiangyang finally fell, but Guo Fu refused to follow him and was eventually rescued by Yang Guo. But those are just guesses. I don’t think someone like Yang Guo would betray Xiaolongnu.”

“What if Xiaolongnu dies prematurely? She was inflicted with severe injuries and lethal poison several times, so her body could already be at its limit. That could be why Yang Guo abandoned his daughter in favor of taking Xiaolongnu around the world,” Firework said. 

Tingxue blinked. Those words seem to make sense. 

Zu An pondered the suggestion. “We’ll have to find Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu to confirm that.”

“Indeed. Yang Guo is a key character,” Firework said. “Yang Dingtian is likely related to him, possibly his illegitimate son or disciple. Also, the Huashan Sect is quite the peculiar one. You mentioned that the Sect was founded by Quanzhen Sect’s Hao Datong, but why would someone from the Quanzhen Sect be well-versed in the Jade Maiden Swordsmanship? 

“And those Nine Swords of Dugu are an enigma as well. There’s no such thing in this world. Yang Guo was the only one who had any connection with Dugu Qiubai, so he might be behind the Nine Swords of Dugu.”

Translator's Thoughts

Details on the Yellow Dress Maiden:

The Yellow Dress Maiden is an enigmatic character who only appears a few times in the story, but she easily subdues Zhou Zhiruo, who is one of the strongest characters in the novel by the end. 

She reveals her surname is Yang, and she used the complete Nine Yin Manual. These details reveal that she is Yang Guo’s descendant. 

Quick summarized timeline:

>Guo Xiang (Firework) founded the E’mei Sect, which is a sect of nuns

>Zhou Zhiruo joined the E’mei Sect and later became its leader. She inherited the Heavenly Sword and learned the secret behind the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Sabre (By striking the sword and the sabre against each other, you can shatter both swords and acquire what lies within)

>Zhou Zhiruo acquires the Dragonslaying Sabre, strikes the two swords against each other, then learns the simplified version of Nine Yin Manual.

>Later, she was jilted during her marriage and became a villain. She joined a martial arts conference, revealed her skills, and sought to become the leader of the martial world.

>The Yellow Dress Maiden swoops in and easily subdues Zhou Zhiruo because she has the complete version of Nine Yin Manual.

Nine Swords of Dugu

Nine Swords of Dugu is one of the strongest skills in another of Jin Yong’s works, ‘The Proud, Smiling Wanderer’. It consists of nine stances that can be mixed and matched to allow extreme flexibility in one’s swordsmanship, allowing one to counter every move in the world. 

Dugu Qiubai is a character in Return of the Condor Heroes. 

Yang Guo chanced upon Dugu Qiubai’s sword grave and learned that Dugu Qiubai was an unrivaled, master swordsman. Dugu Qiubai was so powerful that he cast away his sword and opted for a tree branch as his weapon, yet he remained undefeated until his death.

Dugu Qiubai’s name can be translated into “Lonely, begging to be defeated”.

—

PS: Sorry for the information dump in this arc. The whole Jin Yong’s wuxiaverse makes more sense if you watch Chinese wuxia dramas before 2010. Honestly, you don’t really have to read the information dump - I’m just adding it in for extra information for those who feel their fingers itch when they see random names being thrown around.

chapters.title: Chapter 2805: Dying For Love
chapters.body:
Chapter 2805: Dying For Love

Zu An was impressed. You can totally tell she’s a powerhouse who's lived through the ages. I did consider these issues, but I couldn’t pinpoint the heart of the problem the way she did. Yes, the main issue does lie with Yang Guo. 

He asked Tingxue, “Do you have any idea where the two of them went?”

Tingxue shook her head. “I've never seen them since arriving in this world. All I know about them comes from my memories.”

Zu An was speechless. What kind of parents are those two?!

So, they turned to Huang Rong[1], hoping to tap into the Beggar Sect’s information network to find Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu.

Huang Rong was astonished. She eyed Firework with suspicion. 

Zu An knew that Huang Rong must have thought that her daughter was still pining over Yang Guo, so he explained, “Tingxue has something urgent she needs to consult her parents about.”

Huang Rong chuckled. These two lovebirds must be looking for Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu to receive their approval for their relationship. This would complicate our family ties. Yang Guo can be considered a nephew to me, but now he’s going to become our in-law.

Even so, since the world was already in chaos, she couldn’t care less, and she was very satisfied with Tingxue as her daughter-in-law, too. Thus, she summoned the disciple in charge of maintaining the Beggar Sect’s information network.

Unfortunately, Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu were very elusive. The Beggar Sect could only identify a couple of places where the couple had appeared a while back. 

Still, Zu An was relieved. This was better than having no clues. Considering how iconic the couple was, they'd likely be able to follow the pair's trail if they headed down in person and asked the locals. So, he bade farewell to Huang Rong.

Firework wanted to tag along, but Huang Rong stopped her and said, “Xiang’er, there are more details regarding the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan that we need to discuss.”

She was worried her daughter still hadn’t forgotten Yang Guo. 

That relationship was never going to work out in the first place. What if Guo Xiang somehow wins Yang Guo over? That would be a catastrophe! The older sister marries the father, while the younger brother marries the daughter? That’s completely unacceptable!

Firework saw through Huang Rong’s thoughts. She considered outright ignoring Huang Rong, but on second thought, they needed someone to stay here to keep an eye on the progress of the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan. 

On top of that, she needed to prepare for the establishment of the E’mei Sect as well. In light of all these things, she agreed to stay behind. 

Before Zu An left, she secretly pulled him to one side and whispered, “Make full use of this opportunity to knock up Tingxue.”

Zu An was stunned.

Firework was irritated by Zu An’s startled expression. “Why are you acting innocent with me?” 

“That’s not what I mean. Don’t you feel jealous in the slightest?”

“Don’t worry, I’m a magnanimous woman. Besides, we need her child to clear this trial. I’m sure you don’t want her to bear another man’s child, right?”

That thought pricked Zu An’s heart. Somehow, he was extremely averse to that idea. 

Firework laughed at his reaction. “You two are fated to be, and Tingxue harbors goodwill toward you. It all comes down to your charisma.”

… 

After leaving Xianyang, Tingxue kept looking at Zu An as if she had something to say but was hesitant. In the end, she mustered her courage and asked, “What did Firework ask you before we left Xianyang?”

Zu An smiled. “You used to not care about such matters.”

Tingxue was momentarily stunned before she slowly nodded. “You’re right. I have gotten emotional since coming to this world.”

“You're a human at the end of the day. The path of Nebula demands you to abandon all feelings, but that goes against nature’s will. Why don’t you take this opportunity to relinquish it now?”

“I don’t know why I chose this path back then, but there must be a reason behind that.” Tingxue wasn’t distracted by his abrupt question. “You haven’t answered me yet.”

“Promise me you won’t get angry.”

“Why would I be angry? I forgot anger before coming to this trial world.”

“She told me to take this chance to knock you up.”

“!!!”

“You promised me you won’t get angry! Aiya, stop hitting me!”

… 

Despite the trail of clues provided by the Beggar Sect, Zu An and Tingxue struggled to gather information about Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu. Nevertheless, they could tell that the two lovebirds had only stayed at each area briefly, and their traveling became increasingly rushed.

In the end, the trail of clues ended at a town. 

Zu An and Tingxue had to take out a map to discuss their next step.

While studying the routes, Tingxue suddenly exclaimed, “Doesn’t it look like they’re heading toward Mount Zhongnan’s Ancient Tomb Sect?”

Zu An’s eyes lit up. It did look as if Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu were heading for the Ancient Tomb Sect. “Perhaps they got tired of traveling, so they decided to return and look for you.”

“That’s not how they were in my memories. I have a bad feeling about this. Let’s quickly return to the Ancient Tomb Sect to take a look.”

“All right.” Zu An reined in his smile. He also felt unnerved by Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu’s rushed travel—it felt like an ominous premonition. 

So, the two of them traveled day and night in a hurry, but it still took them half a month to arrive at the Ancient Tomb Sect.

To their disappointment, there was no one there. 

“No, someone has been here.” Tingxue sensed an anomaly. She made her way to her room and found a letter there. She tore it apart, and her expression darkened. 

“What’s wrong?” Zu An asked, feeling concerned.

Tingxue handed the letter to him. Zu An took a look and his heart sank.

It was Yang Guo’s will.

It turned out that Xiaolongnu’s body had taken a toll from all the lethal poisons she had been inflicted with, and it didn’t help that her foundation was already impaired due to her cultivating on the Cold Jade Bed at a young age. She could initially shrug it off due to her strong inner energy, but as soon as she gave birth, her condition had dropped off a cliff.

Yang Guo had been taking her around the world to search for medicine to treat her and to find warm places for her to recuperate. Unfortunately, he couldn’t stop the inevitable. Judging from the rapid deterioration of her condition, Xiaolongnu knew that her days were numbered, so she'd wanted to return to her roots. Her ancestor grandmother, master, and the others were all resting in the ancient tomb too. 

Yang Guo had pushed down his sorrow and hurriedly took her back to the ancient tomb. 

Xiaolongnu had gritted her teeth and held on, but to her regret, she failed to see Tingxue when she returned. With her wish of returning to the ancient tomb fulfilled, she breathed her last.

Yang Guo had succumbed to his pain and ended his life in the same coffin as his wife. Before he died, he left a will expressing his apology to Tingxue. At the same time, he left behind his martial arts insights in recent years, hoping that Tingxue could learn them and protect herself.

Zu An and Tingxue rushed to the coffin room. Only when they saw the lovebirds, who continued to embrace each other even upon death, did it register to them that this was all real. 

Zu An felt as if his head had been struck by a hammer. What’s going on? Why did they die? What’s going to happen to the plotline now? Are our theories all wrong? Or have other universal deities tampered with the trial so much so that it’s no longer possible to clear it?

Tingxue was stunned. Despite only being their daughter in name in this trial world, she couldn’t stop sorrow from engulfing her. Two streams of tears flowed from her eyes. 

So, this is what sorrow feels like. 

She was so shaken up that she spurted blood and fainted. 

1. The ex-leader of the Beggar Sect. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2806: The Nine Swords Of Dugu And The Nine Heavens Palm
chapters.body:
Chapter 2806: The Nine Swords Of Dugu And The Nine Heavens Palm

Alarmed, Zu An rushed forward to support her. Her hands were icy cold.

He immediately realized this was the backlash from cultivating the Ancient Tomb Sect’s Jade Maiden Heart Sutra. The Ancient Tomb Sect had a unique cultivation skill, but it came with a huge downside—the practitioner couldn’t feel extreme bouts of emotions. Otherwise, they risked cultivation deviation.

Zu An quickly supported Tingxue back to her room and channeled his inner energy to heal her.

Moments later, Tingxue regained consciousness. “Thank you.”

“There’s no need for thanks between us.”

Tingxue flashed him a faint smile before her eyelids drooped. “They might not be my real parents, but I couldn’t hold back my sorrow after witnessing their death.”

“Unlike plants, we’re born with hearts,” Zu An said wistfully. “Anyone would be shaken when witnessing a love that transcends even death.”

Tingxue looked dazed. “I may have been alone all this time, but I have never felt lonely. But now that they are dead, I don’t have anyone else in this world.”

“What do you mean by that? You still have friends like me, Firework, and Isabella,” Zu An hurriedly interjected. 

Tingxue sighed. “I’d already be thankful if Firework didn’t provoke me.”

Zu An suddenly felt uncomfortable as he remembered what Firework had told him before they left Xianyang. He quickly changed the topic and said, “This will be tricky. Their deaths prove that our previous guesses are off.”

Most of their plans had been banking on Yang Guo. His death meant that there was no way for them to accomplish those plans anymore.

“That’s not necessarily so.” Tingxue picked up Yang Guo’s will. “Look at this…”

Zu An was so shaken up by Yang Guo’s death that he hadn’t read the latter half of the letter, which contained the martial arts Yang Guo left for his daughter. Other than the Ancient Tomb Sect’s martial arts, there were also the Nine Yin Manual, the Toad Skill, and most important of all, the Melancholic Palm. 

Yang Guo was well aware that his Melancholic Palm was a unique martial art built on his yearning for his wife, so there was no way for him to impart it to others. Thus, he sought to create a new set of martial arts.

He was a unique individual who had inherited the skills of the Five Greats and grasped Dugu Qiubai and the Ancient Tomb Sect’s essences. His understanding of martial arts far surpassed that of any of the other Five Greats.

He'd thus put together the strengths of different experts to create a sword skill and a palm skill. He then named the sword skill the ‘Nine Swords of Dugu’ because the greatest influence behind the sword skill was Dugu Qiubai, and he didn’t want his invincible predecessor buried deep in the mountains to remain unknown. As for the palm strike, he'd named it the ‘Nine Heavens Palm’.

Unfortunately, due to Xiaolongnu’s abrupt death, he hadn’t completed the sword skill and the palm skill. However, he had completed a finger skill. 

The finger skill had been inspired by Reverend Yideng’s Yiyang Finger, but Yang Guo had tapped into the unique traits of the Nine Yin Manual and the Ancient Tomb Sect’s soft inner energy to create the ‘Yiyin Finger’. It was nowhere as profound as the Nine Swords of Dugu and Nine Heavens Palm, but it would be enough for his daughter to protect herself.

Zu An was shocked beyond words. He absolutely hadn’t expected Yang Guo to be the creator of these martial arts. He'd thought it was a coincidence at first, but everything made sense. There had been much discussion about the origins of these martial arts, but indeed, Yang Guo was the most likely person to have created them. 

That explained how Yang Dingtian had become unrivaled in the martial world, pummeling the Three Elders of Shaolin Temple until they didn’t dare to leave, even though his inner energy was only strong enough to grasp the fourth layer of the Great Cosmic Shift. 

It was because the Nine Heavens Palm had the same origin as the Nine Swords of Dugu. It wasn’t reliant on one’s inner energy. 

Tingxue spoke up. “There’s still a problem. These martial arts should have appeared in the future, but Yang Guo passed away before he could complete them. We’ll have to find a way around that.”

Zu An burst into laughter. “That isn’t a problem. Yes, Yang Guo might be the only one in this world who could have created these martial arts, but neither you nor I are from this world. How can we be stumped by something like this?”

Tingxue thought that made sense. With their vast knowledge, they could fill up the missing bits in the martial arts, and the final products would be more potent than what Yang Guo could have ever created.

“We can also easily circumvent Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu’s deaths—we just have to create another Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu.” Zu An’s eyes gleamed brightly as he thought of another plan. 

“How do we do that?” Tingxue was confused. 

“The Condor Hero was traveling around the world with a mask on, so most people don’t know what he looks like. All I have to do is to put on the same mask. You greatly resemble Xiaolongnu, so it shouldn’t be tough for you to impersonate her,” Zu An said as he dug out Yang Guo’s mask.

Tingxue blinked. He wants to be my father, and I have to play my mother? “This feels weird. Do we have to do this?”

“We have to. We transmigrated into this world assuming the identities of people who exist here. But there was next to no information about Guo Polu even in the original plotline. He vanished after Xianyang collapsed, with rumors that he had died in battle. It’d break the plotline if I continued my activities as Guo Polu, and that could lead to us failing the trial.

“It’s the same for you, too. We’re the only ones who know about Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu’s deaths. This is the perfect situation for us to assume their identities,” Zu An replied. 

“All right.” Tingxue didn’t oppose this course of action. 

The two of them began studying the incomplete Nine Swords of Dugu and Nine Heavens Palm. They had to concede that Yang Guo was extraordinarily talented to have created such profound martial arts in this world. These two martial arts had already surpassed this world’s ceiling. 

It was fortunate that Zu An and Tingxue were experts from the Myriad Worlds, and they were both adept in swordsmanship. After days of studying, they eventually completed the Nine Swords of Dugu. 

Tingxue remarked, “If someone intelligent learns this swordsmanship, they can exert formidable prowess even if they lack inner energy.”

Zu An was also satisfied with their work. However, he said, “The Nine Heavens Palm is slightly lacking. We specialize in swordsmanship, after all. If Firework were here, she could have done more with it.”

“You miss her?”

Zu An was startled by that question. He couldn’t believe such words could have come from that icy beauty.

Tingxue’s face reddened. She quickly changed the subject. “Don’t worry, the Nine Heavens Palm is only slightly inferior to the Nine Swords of Dugu. It’s more than enough for one to reign supreme in the martial world.”

Zu An agreed with her judgment. Unlike the more influential Nine Swords of Dugu, the only person known to wield the Nine Heavens Palm was Yang Dingtian. There was no need for them to fuss too much over it.

Zu An’s ears suddenly twitched. “Hm? I hear fighting sounds coming from the Quanzhen Sect. That’s weird.”

“Let’s take a look.”

The two of them snuck out of the ancient tomb and soon discovered members of the Quanzhen Sect pursuing fifteen daoists who were dressed in Quanzhen uniforms.

Is it infighting?

The ones being chased were at a huge numerical disadvantage. They would have been dead by now if not for a youth, who looked to be in his twenties, protecting them with his outstanding martial arts.

“Zhang Baojun, this is our Quanzhen Sect’s internal affair. Are you sure you want to get involved?” one of the pursuers roared in anger. 

The youth calmly replied, “I’m only here to aid a friend. Not all Quanzhen disciples wish to pledge allegiance to the Mongols. You don’t need to go this far.”

Zu An was surprised. This youth is a future legend of the martial world—Zhang Sanfeng[1]!

1. In real life, Zhang Sanfeng is known as a legendary daoist credited with creating Tai Chi. In Jin Yong’s wuxiaverse, he appears in Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber as a head of the Wudang Sect. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2807: The Plan To Close The World’S Loop
chapters.body:
Chapter 2807: The Plan To Close The World’S Loop

“We’re merely following fate. It’s foolish to stand against the tides of change.” The pursuing Quanzhen disciples had no plans on continuing the debate and soon continued their assault.

Zhang Baojun had yet to master his martial arts and struggled to simultaneously deal with multiple opponents. Just as they were about to be captured, several rocks suddenly flew out and struck the Quanzhen disciples’ weapons. 

The Quanzhen disciples were stunned. Who is capable of using such small pebbles to knock away all our weapons from a far distance? Is it East Heretic Huang Yaoshi?

Two figures stepped out. The man wore a withered mask, whereas the woman looked pure and ravishing. 

“Yang Guo, Xiaolongnu…” Some Quanzhen disciples had met Xiaolongnu back then and recognized her at a glance. They were perplexed how she hadn’t aged despite years passing, but it was normal for fairies to remain perpetually youthful.

Zhang Baojun exclaimed, “Big brother Yang!”

He'd received pointers from Yang Guo back then and benefited greatly from it. 

Zu An nodded. It’s a good thing I have the mask with me. As long as I hide an arm, it shouldn’t be too difficult for me to impersonate Yang Guo[1].

“Quanzhen’s values have regressed so much that its disciples are ganging up on others now. Scram now before I change my mind!” Zu An growled with an intentionally deeper voice. His imitation of Yang Guo wasn't necessarily accurate, but most people wouldn’t know better, except for a small handful of people who knew him well.

Relieved, the Quanzhen disciples immediately turned tail and fled. The Condor Hero’s reputation had grown resounding over the years, not to mention that the Quanzhen disciples had witnessed his prowess in person during the battle at Chongyang Palace then. 

Zhang Baojun and his friends from the Quanzhen Sect stepped forward and thanked Zu An for saving their lives. Zu An traded some pleasantries with them while asking about the situation.

It turned out that the Quanzhen Sect’s defection to the Mongols had been extremely controversial within the sect. Most disciples were averse to siding with the Mongols, though they were also worried that rejecting the Mongols would bring a crisis upon them. 

This move had prompted the remaining Quanzhen Seven Masters to leave and establish their own sects. For instance, Hao Datong’s line founded the Huashan Sect, while Sun Bu’er founded the Tranquil Sect.

The current Quanzhen Sect was run by the third-generation disciples. There were many hot-blooded ones among the young disciples who didn’t want to serve the Mongols and wanted to break free of the Quanzhen Sect as well.

Naturally, the Quanzhen Sect’s leadership wouldn’t permit such movements. They didn't have the influence and the power to stop the Quanzhen Seven Masters, but they could definitely curb the younger disciples.

As a result, an internal conflict had broken out in the Quanzhen Sect. 

It was also then that Zhang Baojun had received a request from his friends to help them leave the Quanzhen Sect. Unfortunately, the Quanzhen Sect’s leadership caught wind of their plans and sent people after them.

No wonder they say the Wudang Sect was an offshoot of the Quanzhen Sect. Zu An uttered some words of motivation before asking, “What are your plans?”

Zhang Baojun looked confused. “I don’t know, but surely there must be a place for us in this huge world.”

Zu An smiled. “Why don’t you go to Mount Wudang?”

Zhang Baojun blinked before replying heartily, “All right.”

Zu An imparted the Quanzhen Sect’s swordsmanship and inner energy methods to them, so they could preserve their roots. That would serve as the Wudang Sect’s foundation for their rise in prominence in the future.

The young disciples were extremely grateful to Zu An. Most of the Quanzhen Sect’s martial arts had become incomplete by their generation, and it didn’t help that their leadership were hoarding the key martial arts. As a result, they had hardly learned any of the Quanzhen Sect’s martial arts. 

Zu An pulled Zhang Baojun aside for a private conversation. “I have a portion of Wang Chongyang’s Innate Energy Art, which is highly compatible with you. Do you want to learn it?” 

Wang Chongyang had inscribed not just the Nine Yin Manual, but also a portion of his Innate Energy Art in the ancient tomb.

Zhang Baojun was more than happy to learn the martial art of someone who had been the world’s strongest martial artist, but he was also concerned that it was unethical to secretly learn another sect’s martial art.

Zu An reassured him with a smile, “Rest assured. Given its current state, Wang Chongyang wouldn’t want his martial art to be passed down within the Quanzhen Sect. He would be happier if you could bring glory to his martial art. However, there’s a flaw in the technique. You’ll have to remain chaste for your life. You won’t be able to have a wife or children.”

Wang Chongyang hadn't gotten together with Lin Chaoying[2] not just because of their pride, but also because of his Innate Energy Art. 

South Emperor Duan Zhixing of the Five Greats had once had many concubines in his imperial harem, but it was because he'd begun practicing this skill that he began to distance himself from women. That led to Ying Gu cheating on him and him becoming a monk.

Zhang Baojun was stunned to hear those words. A beautiful woman flashed across his mind. But Heroine Guo only fancies big brother Yang. She wouldn’t care about someone like me. So, he replied, “My heart is fully committed to the dao. I don’t plan to marry or have children.”

Whether in real history or Jin Yong’s wuxiaverse, Zhang Sanfeng had remained chaste his entire life. So, Zu An imparted the Innate Energy Art’s sutra to him. 

Zu An didn’t complete the Innate Energy Art for Zhang Baojun, knowing that the latter could comprehend something deeper from it. The inspiration from that would spur him to create even more martial arts. 

Zhang Baojun quickly memorized the sutra. While feeling thankful to Zu An, he couldn’t help but ask, “Big brother Yang, second miss Guo has been looking for you all these years.”

He'd met Guo Xiang more than a decade ago in the Shaolin Temple, when she was still traveling around in search of Yang Guo. It was then that she'd given him an arhat doll. While he had learned some Shaolin martial arts from the arhat doll, it was her radiant smile that was imprinted in his mind. 

“I know.” Zu An looked at Zhang Baojun and sighed. 

In the original story, Guo Xiang had conceded defeat to Xiaolongnu. She'd lamented not having met Yang Guo earlier, or else she could have protected him as a sugar mommy[3], and that could have led to them getting together instead.

But in truth, Guo Xiang had met another ‘Yang Guo’, but failed to realize it and lost her opportunity to be a sugar mommy.

Zhang Baojun glanced at the gorgeous Tingxue and thought Yang Guo’s decision was understandable. Once again, he thanked Zu An before setting off to Mount Wudang with his Quanzhen friends. 

The current him probably couldn’t have imagined that he would build on the Innate Energy Art and Nine Yin Manual to create the Pure Yang Infinite Art and become a true grandmaster. 

“Why do you treat him so well?” Tingxue asked after Zhang Baojun and the others left.

Zu An replied, “There’s an eighty-year gap between the end of ‘Return of the Condor Heroes’ and ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’. In this period, the old Five Greats passed away one after another, and the Mongols took over the world. The martial world inevitably descended into chaos.

“There’s a famous saying in the future that Zhang Sanfeng devoted sixty years of his life to quelling demons. Naturally, we can’t let him remain weak.”

He had already deciphered the crux of this trial. The world was incomplete, and his job was to pick up the missing pieces, stitch them together, and ensure this world’s trajectory proceeded in the direction that was intended.

No matter how intelligent or powerful the previous challengers were, there was no way they could have accomplished this feat without knowing about the trial world’s background. 

—

Brief History of Wang Chongyang and Lin Chaoying

Both are characters who died before Return of the Condor Heroes.

The two of them were martial arts prodigies who were in love with each other, but they were rivals as well, and refused to get together because of their immense pride. 

Lin Chaoying thought Wang Chongyang scorned her, so she created the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra to counter the Quanzhen Sect’s moves.

When Lin Chaoying died, Wang Chongyang paid her a visit in the ancient tomb and was shocked to see the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra inscribed on the wall. He tried to devise a counter against the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra but was unable to do so, forcing him to acknowledge Lin Chaoying’s talents.

Later, Wang Chongyang came into possession of the Nine Yin Manual and realized it was highly compatible with the Quanzhen Sect’s skills. He finally came up with a counter against the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra, so he returned to the ancient tomb and inscribed his solution against it (which contains bits of the Nine Yin Manual and the Innate Energy Art).

1. Yang Guo only has one arm. His other arm was chopped off by Guo Fu. ☜

2. She’s the founder of the Ancient Tomb Sect. ☜

3. Xiaolongnu was responsible for training Yang Guo when he was weak, and could be considered responsible for his success, hence ‘sugar mommy’. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2808: Xianyang Collapses
chapters.body:
Chapter 2808: Xianyang Collapses

Tingxue nodded, though it was unclear if she understood what Zu An was getting at. “Where should we head next?” 

She wasn't fond of thinking too much into things—Nebula worshipers were emotionless machines in the first place. She liked how she didn’t have to speculate too deeply when with him.

“Mount Hua!”

Mount Hua wasn't too far away from Mount Zhongnan. It only took them three days to arrive there.

Looking at Mount Hua’s treacherous cliffs, which looked as if someone had hacked cleanly through them, Tingxue remarked, “It is a little short, but there are few mountains as steep as this in the Myriad Worlds.”

Zu An chuckled. The mountains in this world were short compared to those in the Myriad Worlds, which easily reached tens of kilometers tall. This was a world where supernatural powers were forbidden, after all. It would be hard for such tall mountains to grow without the nourishment of ki or spirit energy.

The two of them made their way up Mount Hua. Unlike the modern-day mountain, which had paved routes, the trial world’s version was dangerous to climb. It was fortunate that Zu An and Tingxue were strong enough to overcome most obstacles, so it didn’t take them long to reach the top of Mount Hua, where the Huashan Sect was located.

The current era’s Huashan Sect was still shabby. Hao Datong had long passed away, and there were only young disciples left in the sect.

When they saw Zu An and Tingxue, these young disciples put up their guard, having deduced that they were Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu from the duo’s appearances. Clearly, they knew about Hao Datong accidentally hurting Grandma Sun[1], which resulted in the latter’s death. 

They thought Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu had come to the Huashan Sect to exact vengeance for Grandma Sun.

But that wasn't Zu An’s intention.

He explained that he hadn’t come to exact vengeance. Rather, he had great respect for Wang Chongyang and didn’t want his martial arts to disappear with time, so he'd journeyed to Mount Hua to offer them some martial art manuals.

The Condor Hero had built up a good reputation over the years, and Zu An told them about Zhang Baojun rescuing a line of Quanzhen disciples as well. These details helped to dispel the Huashan disciples’ doubts, and they welcomed the pair as honored guests.

Zu An soon learned that the Huashan Sect had already developed the Purple Haze Divine Skill.

It turned out that Hao Datong had felt helpless after encountering so many top-notch experts. While the Quanzhen Sect’s inner energy method was calm and righteous, it lacked power. Thus, he put his heart into developing a new inner energy method to amplify the power of Quanzhen inner energy. 

However, the Purple Haze Divine Skill had side effects. Those who overexerted themselves in the long run would find their skin turning purple.

The Quanzhen Seven Masters are still quite capable. Each of them found their own path and developed unique martial arts. Their martial arts might not be top-notch, but they're easily high-grade.

Since the Huashan Sect’s inner energy problem had been resolved, Zu An imparted to them the Quanzhen Sect’s swordsmanship, whereas Tingxue imparted to them the Ancient Tomb Sect’s Jade Maiden Swordsmanship. 

The future Huashan Sect did have the Jade Maiden Swordsmanship and the Virtuous Lady Swordsmanship, after all. Zu An was certain that those had been passed down from the Ancient Tomb Sect. 

That day, the Huashan disciples witnessed a white-robed fairy executing indescribably beautiful swordplay. That was a sight they would never forget in their lives. They unanimously agreed to name the Huashan Sect’s main peak Jade Maiden Peak. 

After they passed down their respective sword skills to the Huashan disciples, Zu An asked if they had a disciple named Feng Qingyang[2], but unfortunately, everyone shook their heads. 

Zu An figured that Feng Qingyang hadn't been born yet. I might have to return to Mount Hua again decades from now.

The two of them bade the Huashan disciples farewell and began wandering Mount Hua.

While walking past a cliff, Tingxue suddenly exclaimed. She walked up to a boulder and saw the words ‘Cliff of Remembrance’ inscribed on it. “This handwriting looks familiar.”

Zu An inspected the handwriting and said, “It’s likely from Guo Xiang. She traveled the world searching for Yang Guo. She might have heard about Yang Guo witnessing the showdown between Hong Qigong and Ouyang Feng on Mount Hua’s peak, so she came here to try her luck. She didn’t find Yang Guo here, so while watching the scenery in a daze, she suddenly felt sentimental and left these words behind.”

Tingxue sighed. “Miss Guo is truly a sentimental woman. Yang Guo is a heartless man.”

Zu An laughed. “Is it fine for you to speak about your father like that? He would have been cheating on your mother if he'd accepted Guo Xiang.”

Tingxue rolled her eyes. “You know they aren’t my parents.”

“Your expressions are more animated than before. You look better now.”

“…Where do we go next?”

“We’ll head to the imperial palace. We have to find a eunuch.”

“???”

“It’s about time to create the Sunflower Manual. That skill should be inspired by the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra as well. We have to close that loop as well.”

“Is it that demonic skill that requires practitioners to castrate themselves?” Tingxue frowned. “How did the Ancient Tomb Sect’s martial arts get distorted like that?”

“The Ancient Tomb Sect’s martial arts are unorthodox. Practitioners of the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra are vulnerable to cultivation deviation, especially if they didn’t have a mentor to guide them. Those who obtain an incomplete copy are prone to be misled,” Zu An replied with a smile.

“Practicing the Innate Energy Art requires one to consume all kinds of pills, which may lead to unbearable heat building up within one's body. Similarly, cultivating the Jade Maiden Heart Sutra can easily stoke one’s lust. If the Sunflower Manual originates from those two, it’s not that surprising that it demands its practitioners to castrate themselves. Let’s look into it on the way.”

“Alright…”

So, Zu An and Tingxue traveled during the day and discussed the creation of the Sunflower Manual in the inns at night. Neither of them had ever learned such a bizarre skill, so it wasn't as easy for them to create it. 

At the start, the two of them still stayed in separate rooms, but they often stayed up late to discuss the details of their plans, so they eventually just booked a single room and slept on the same bed when they were exhausted. They'd already shared a bed in Xianyang anyway. 

Of course, the two of them slept with their clothes on. 

Some time later, they arrived at Lin’an. When they saw how the capital was still merry despite the ongoing war, the two of them sighed. This explained why the Song Dynasty was ailing. 

That said, neither of them was in the mood to worry about the country. They slipped into the imperial palace and looked around, but they couldn’t find a suitable eunuch.

Later, it struck Zu An that even the humblest eunuch would have become a master after decades of training, and such a character would have undoubtedly left his mark in ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’. That didn’t check out.

Most likely, he would have to find a suitable successor for the Sunflower Manual later on, similar to the case of the Nine Swords of Dugu.[3]

Just then, they heard news on the street, saying that the Mongols had encircled Xianyang, and the reinforcements dispatched by the imperial court had been crushed. Xianyang was on the verge of collapse.

Without hesitation, Zu An and Tingxue hurried back to Xianyang. But by the time they returned, the place had already collapsed. 

Guo Jing and Huang Rong had realized that there was no way to turn the tables anymore, so they urged Lu Wenhuan, the general guarding Xianyang, to surrender to the Mongols and open the city gates in exchange for the people’s safety. 

Then, they'd ended their lives because they didn’t want to bow to the Mongols. The Guo manor was set ablaze.

This was aligned with the world’s intended trajectory. Zu An had planned it with Firework beforehand—she would leave with the Heavenly Sword ahead of time, while the Dragon Slaying Saber would fall into the hands of the masses and be fought over by different factions. 

In addition, Guo Polu also vanished after the battle in Xianyang, with rumors that he had died fighting against the Mongols.

These things were all planned out beforehand, but Zu An still felt melancholic witnessing it in person. Huang Rong and Guo Jing must have been disappointed that I didn’t return…

Just then, there was a ruckus in the city. A lady carrying something in her arms was being chased by the Mongols. A few members of the Beggar Sect fought to their dying breath to escort her out of Xianyang, but it didn’t take long before they were massacred by the Mongol soldiers.

The Mongols slowly approached the lady, their faces twisted into perverse smiles. What a gorgeous lady. We've struck gold this time!

The lady was Guo Fu, and the item she was carrying was the Dragon Slaying Saber!

1. Grandma Sun took care of Xiaolongnu while she was growing up. ☜

2. Feng Qingyang appeared in ‘The Smiling, Proud Wanderer’ as a swordmaster wielding the Nine Swords of Dugu. ☜

3. In Jin Yong’s wuxiaverse, the Sunflower Manual was created by a eunuch. To close that loop, Zu An is seeking to create a Sunflower Manual and pass it on to the eunuch. But if he passed on the Sunflower Manual now, the eunuch would have become an expert by the start of ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’ and would have played a huge role in that story. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2809: You’Re Going To Use Her To Give Birth?
chapters.body:
Chapter 2809: You’Re Going To Use Her To Give Birth?

Zu An was taken aback. Why is the Dragon Slaying Saber in her hands?

But he had no time to think deeper into it. Guo Fu was already surrounded by the Mongol soldiers. To avoid being humiliated, she raised her dagger and thrust it toward her chest. 

Zu An hurriedly flicked his finger to knock away her dagger.

Guo Fu turned around and saw the iconic masked man with a single arm. For a brief moment, she was stunned. “Big brother Yang…”

Zu An had made sure to disguise himself to avoid others from recognizing him as Guo Polu, but he hadn’t expected even Guo Fu to fail to recognize him.

The Mongol soldiers were infuriated at someone getting in their way, but their eyes lit up in delight when they noticed Tingxue. What amazing luck we have today! First that beautiful lady, and now another gorgeous babe!

Some Mongol soldiers cheered. They rushed at Tingxue, fearing that their peers would get to her before them.

Tingxue’s face turned cold. She drew her sword and slew her way through the soldiers. 

Zu An made his way to Guo Fu’s side and asked, “Are you all right?”

“Big brother Yang!” Guo Fu stared at him in a daze. All her grievances and fear she had suppressed in recent days gushed up in her heart, and tears began streaming down her cheeks. She lunged into his embrace and started crying.

Yang Guo had already saved her and Yelu Qi once in the great battle of Xianyang back then. She hadn’t expected to bump into him again now that Xianyang had been breached, and she was cornered.

She thought about how she had wrongly placed her trust in Yelu Qi, and complicated emotions swirled in her mind. Why did I treat him like that in the past? Things could have been different otherwise.

Zu An was speechless.

Even Tingxue also looked over, intrigued by the situation on their side. 

Zu An shrugged. He was confused as well.

Tingxue’s expression became increasingly colder as her blows grew sharper. It didn’t take her long to massacre the soldiers around her and return to their side. 

“Miss… Long…” Guo Fu looked awkward. It suddenly struck her how inappropriate it was for her to be hugging Yang Guo in front of Xiaolongnu. “Miss Long, why do you look so young… Tingxue?”

Zu An gently tapped Guo Fu’s neck, and the latter fainted. “We can’t reveal our identities here. Let’s take her away with us first.”

More Mongol soldiers were rushing over after hearing the news, and there was no lack of experts among them. Even with their current level of martial arts, it would be disastrous if Yang Guo and Tingxue would be encircled by them.

Tingxue nodded. She glanced at the Dragon Slaying Saber and asked, “What about that?”

Zu An grabbed the Dragon Slaying Saber and flitted some distance across the city before casually tossing it into a hut to hide it. According to the world’s trajectory, the Dragon Slaying Saber fell into enemy hands.

He couldn’t simply hand it over, as the Mongols would have suspected that something was wrong with it. It would be more believable if they searched the city and chanced upon it.

During the chaos, the two took Guo Fu out of Xianyang and temporarily settled down in a cavern in the mountains.

Tingxue eyed Zu An. “How do you intend to deal with her?”

“We need to figure out what happened to her first.” Zu An recalled how she was carrying the Dragon Slaying Saber earlier. Something must have happened. 

“But she already recognized me. She might figure out your identity from that.” Tingxue was worried. According to their plan, Guo Polu should have died when Xianyang was breached, as there was no other record of him in history. If Guo Fu recognized him, it could lead to unexpected complications.

“That’s simple.” 

Zu An released Guo Fu’s acupoint, causing the latter to slowly regain consciousness. When she met Zu An’s gaze, he quickly used the Nine Yin Sutra’s Soul Transference Method.

Guo Fu looked completely dazed, but her eyes swiftly regained their clarity. By then, she was already convinced that the duo before her were Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu.

Zu An asked, “What happened? Why were you fleeing with the Dragon Slaying Saber? Where’s Yelu Qi?”

Guo Fu shouldn’t have known about the Heavenly Saber and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan.

Guo Fu’s eyes reddened. “Big brother Yang, I have severed ties with that fellow. He… He defected to the Mongols!”

Zu An and Tingxue exchanged looks, though they weren’t surprised. They had anticipated such a possibility.

Guo Fu continued with tears flowing down her cheeks. “I recently learned that he had been leaking information from Xianyang to the Mongols. If not for him, Xianyang wouldn’t have been breached so quickly.”

“He would have been of considerable status even if he had joined the Mongols. The two of you have been on good terms all these years. How did you end up like that?” Zu An was perplexed. 

He wasn't surprised that Yelu Qi had defected to the Mongols. He was surprised that the latter hadn’t taken Guo Fu with him, though.

“He wanted to take me with him, but how could I be with a traitor? Our parents guarded Xianyang for many years!” Guo Fu spat hatefully.

Zu An was surprised. He'd always thought Guo Fu was a bumpkin who only cared about herself, but who could have thought she had such spirit in her?

“Did he cause your parents’ death?”

Guo Fu shook her head. “He didn’t. He used another name, Yelu Zhu, when dealing with the Mongols to avoid staining my parents’ reputation, especially since he’s the leader of the Beggar Sect. He even tried persuading my parents to surrender, promising to ensure their safety. But my parents refused to join the Mongols and chose to end their lives instead.”

“When I learned that he was planning to hand the Dragon Slaying Saber, which my parents and grandfather painstakingly crafted, to the Mongols, I immediately stole and escaped with it. However, the Mongols quickly noticed it and pursued me. That’s when you appeared and saved me.” 

Zu An was stunned. It was recorded in history that Yelu Zhu was Yelu Chucai’s oldest son, but who could have known that Yelu Qi was actually Yelu Zhu?

Yelu Qi did have a conscience after all. He and his father were Mongol officials, so it wasn’t surprising for them to support their homeland. Guo Jing and Huang Rong had already guessed as much, or else they wouldn’t have excluded Yelu Qi and Guo Fu from the Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber Plan. 

But news would still have eventually gotten out. 

There had to be people in the Beggar Sect who knew about Yelu Qi’s betrayal. In the first place, Yelu Qi was a distinguished young master, whose disposition was at odds with the Beggar Sect. This could be where the conflict between the Beggar Sect’s Clean Clothes Faction and Dirty Clothes Faction started from…

“Speaking of which, where is the Dragon Slaying Saber?” Guo Fu suddenly recalled this matter and searched the area.

Zu An explained, “There were Mongol soldiers all around Xianyang. We had to fend them off while retreating; there was no way we could have carried such a heavy saber with us then. So, we hid it in one of the residences.”

Guo Fu sighed. “Big brother Yang, you should have left me there and taken the saber instead.”

Zu An was surprised. This was unlike Guo Fu’s personality. But on second thought, she had gone through a lot within such a short time—her parents’ deaths, her siblings’ disappearance, and her husband defecting to the enemy side. It was normal for her to seek death. 

Knowing that, Zu An dared not leave her alone here. “What do you plan to do from now on?”

“I don’t know.”

“You can travel with us in the meantime.”

… 

The group made their way to E’mei Mountain.

Firework was initially delighted to see them, but upon seeing Guo Fu, she looked at Zu An with a perplexed gaze. “So, you've made up your mind to have her bear your child?”

Zu An was stunned.

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2810: Firework’S Plan
chapters.body:
Chapter 2810: Firework’S Plan

“What nonsense are you spouting?” Zu An was dumbfounded.

Firework tilted her head in confusion. “Why else did you bring her here? She’s completely useless except for her beauty… though I have to say, even though she’s no longer a young lady, she is still ravishing. She fully inherited her mother’s beauty. Is that why you saved her?”

Zu An huffed. “She’s our big sister in this world. Am I to turn a blind eye to her plight?”

“But rescuing her complicates things. What do you intend to do with her? And how are you going to explain your identity to her?” Firework frowned. It would be inconvenient for them to discuss matters if they had to constantly bring an outsider with them. 

“I’ll bring her around for now, lest she suddenly decides to end her life. You don’t have to worry about my identity. I used the Soul Transference Method to implant a belief that we're Yang Guo and Xiaolongnu in her,” Zu An explained.

Firework looked at him with a crooked smile. “You aren’t planning to take responsibility for her?”

Zu An was speechless.

Firework eventually said, “Fine. You can keep her for the time being.”

“How are preparations going on your side?” Zu An asked.

“I’m almost done. I’m ready to establish the E’mei Sect whenever required. I've also created the E’mei Sect’s martial arts.” Firework remarked with a sigh, “Guo Xiang is such a sentimental woman. It feels like every bit of the E’mei Sect has Yang Guo’s shadow in it.”

Be it the Spirit Fox of the Black Marsh or Guo Xiang’s future disciple, Abbess Fengling, they were all named after Guo Xiang’s experiences with Yang Guo back then. 

With this thought in mind, she glared at Zu An and snorted, “That Yang fellow is too much. He wouldn’t have let down so many women if he was a philanderer like you!”

Zu An was speechless. Actually, I respect Yang Guo’s devotion to Xiaolongnu, although it is also true that he held up the lives of many women. But then again, it is this feeling of regret that makes the characters memorable to the readers. 

“What’s the situation with Zhu Ziliu, Dawu, and Xiaowu?”

“Zhu Ziliu and Xiaowu traveled to the Western Regions and established the Red Plum Blossom Mansion there,” Firework replied. “But Dawu’s wife is Yelu Yan, and the Yelu Clan refuses to leave the Central Plains. It’s for the same reason I couldn’t send Guo Fu off ahead of time, either.”

Zu An nodded in agreement. Everything was going according to plan. They weren’t worried about Dawu and the Yelu Clan’s fate, since this was only a trial world.

Firework suddenly nudged Zu An and asked, “How are things on your side?”

“I've settled Wudang and Huashan. As for the Nine Swords of Dugu and Sunflower Manual, I might have to wait a few more decades before passing them down…”

“That’s not what I’m asking. How are things between you and Tingxue? Is she pregnant yet?”

Zu An’s face reddened. “You’re thinking too much.”

Firework’s face grew stern. “Stop being so wishy-washy. This concerns whether we clear the trial or not. Settle it as soon as possible.”

Zu An was stunned.

In the days to come, the three of them kept an eye on the movements across the world.

Great Song lost its momentum after Xianyang was breached. The Mongols traveled down the river and defeated Southern Song’s final stronghold at Dingjiazhou. After that, they marched straight toward Lin’an, easily crushing everything along the way. 

Southern Song’s empress dowager led the majority of the officials to surrender to the Mongols. Some loyal officials tried to escape with the little emperor, but they were wiped out in the Battle of Yamen. An official named Lu Xiufu jumped into the sea with the little emperor in his arms, and the two of them died… 

Over at Persia, Isabella was promoted to the leader of the Persian Ming Cult for retrieving the Great Cosmic Shift and persuading the Central Plains Ming Cult to surrender to the Mongols.

In the Central Plains, the martial world moved in the direction they'd designed. Many foreign experts settled in the Central Plains after the Mongols conquered the world. Many of the world’s original experts died in battle or of old age. It was truly an age of chaos, and the people suffered greatly. 

Soon, however, a Wudang youth stepped forward to quell the evildoers with his supreme sword, upholding justice in the world.

The Ming Cult’s Cult Leader Shi passed away, and the Radiant Left Envoy took over his position. 

The E’mei Sect was established and took in female disciples.

Huashan Sect slowly grew from a minor sect with just a few members into a powerhouse… 

Everything progressed smoothly, except…

“What are the two of you doing? Why aren’t you sleeping with each other?” Firework glared at Zu An and Tingxue.

Tingxue kept her silence. Zu An sighed. “Such things require building up to the moment. How can you make a child just because you want to?”

“That wouldn’t have been a problem in the past, but we’re in mortal bodies now. Waiting is a luxury we can’t afford!” Firework gritted her teeth in anger.

Zu An looked at the embarrassed Tingxue and said, “Why don’t we adopt a child and nurture her into the Ancient Tomb Sect’s successor?”

His current relationship with Tingxue was above that of friends, but beneath that of lovers. They still lacked impetus. 

“That won’t do.” Firework turned down his proposal. “Our survival is at stake here. Who knows if there will be a problem if the Ancient Tomb Sect’s successor has no blood ties to you? We can’t take this risk.”

“So be it. Death is nothing scary anyway.” Tingxue stood up and left. 

The three of them had more or less completed their missions. The Ancient Tomb Sect’s successor fell under Tingxue’s jurisdiction.

Zu An shrugged. 

Firework clenched her teeth. If it had been in the past, she would have already knocked out Tingxue, stripped her naked, and delivered her onto Zu An’s bed. She was irked by how the two of them were roleplaying innocent youths here. 

But this world had sealed her supernatural powers, resulting in her being roughly equally matched with Tingxue. It also annoyed her that Zu An refused to cooperate with her in subduing Tingxue. This left her with no way to proceed.

She knew it could be disastrous if they continued dilly-dallying. Just then, though, a thought popped into her mind, and she smiled cunningly.

That night was a sleepless one for Zu An. He rolled around his bed, tugging his clothes, feeling unbearably hot. “What’s going on? Is this cultivation deviation?”

Just then, he heard light footsteps. He could tell it was Firework, and it made him chuckle a little. She really likes to sneak around. 

His blanket was suddenly lifted. A warm and smooth body slipped in and hugged him tightly.

“It’s cold…” a gentle voice murmured.

Zu An immediately realized something was amiss. She's…

Just then, Firework suddenly appeared like a specter to seal his acupoint.

“Again?” Zu An was both infuriated and horrified. 

Firework stood by the windowsill with a gleeful smile. “It’s finally done. You and Tingxue insisted on roleplaying innocent youths. I couldn’t stand it anymore, so I had no choice but to help you out.”

Zu An was speechless. You aren’t helping anyone here. You should have at least delivered Tingxue in! Why did you bring Guo Fu in instead?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2811: The End
chapters.body:
Chapter 2811: The End

“Don’t worry. I've checked her body—it’s definitely good for childbirth. Have a pleasant night, the two of you.” Firework chuckled before leaping out the window.

Zu An was speechless.

“Big brother Yang, I’m cold…” Guo Fu embraced him tightly with her feverishly hot body. Perhaps due to her inner energy, her skin remained as supple as a youth. 

Zu An narrowed his eyes. She isn’t thinking rationally. Someone must have drugged her. It has to be Firework’s doing as well. She’s truly a demoness; she doesn’t think things through before doing them.

He clenched his teeth in anger. In his current state, he couldn’t even get up and push away Guo Fu. All he could do was to channel his inner energy to unseal his acupoint as soon as possible.

But Guo Fu was already wrapping herself around him. Perhaps due to the drug or the series of blows she'd suffered, she murmured, “Big brother Yang, did you know that I fell in love with you at first sight back then? But I was young and didn’t know better. You weren’t as attentive to me as Dawu and Xiaowu, so we often fought.

“I was thrilled when I met you again in the Hero Tournament. I reacted angrily when I heard my father was going to betroth me to you, but in truth, I was secretly overjoyed. Yet you said that you were going to marry your master instead. I felt humiliated in front of everyone else. Since then, I've hated you.

“Everything that happened afterward was a result of that. I’m sorry. I know I’m letting Miss Long down, but I can’t hold myself back. Please don’t reject me…”

… 

Hearing a beauty pour out her feelings left Zu An dazed. 

She'll be inconsolable if she learns that Yang Guo is dead too. Her parents are dead. Her siblings are scattered around. Her husband betrayed her. Yang Guo’s death might be the last straw that causes her to crumble for good. It might be better for her to live blissfully in an illusion than to cruelly reveal the truth to her. 

Guo Fu displayed her feminine charms and tenderly took in his everything. Tears of joy streamed down her eyes.

… 

In a distant forest, Tingxue coldly eyed Firework. “Aren’t you being too cruel to her?”
