Firework was unfazed. “I had no other choice. You refused to get off your high horse, so I had to resort to another measure.”

Tingxue was speechless.

Firework leaned in and asked, “Why? Does it hurt you to see the man you love sleep with another woman?”

Tingxue turned away. She couldn’t be bothered to respond. 

Firework continued, “Her original fate was to fall into the hands of the Mongol soldiers. We saved her life and offered her a sanctuary for her soul when she was in despair. How are we being cruel to her? If anything, we're her benefactors.”

Tingxue frowned. She didn’t agree with that, but she couldn’t find the words to retort against it. 

Firework continued, “Besides, do you think that the Soul Transference Method is really that effective in this world, which forbids supernatural powers?”

Tingxue widened her eyes in astonishment. Indeed, their old selves could have easily controlled the mind of a mortal, but it would be hard to pull it off impeccably in this world.

“No matter how dumb Guo Fu is, she’s bound to notice something over time. Yet the effects of the Soul Transference Method remain even after so long. This can only mean one thing—this is what she wants. She’s subconsciously keeping herself hypnotized because she doesn’t want to wake up from this lovely dream and face the hell known as reality,” Firework coldly replied. 

Tingxue harrumphed. “I shouldn’t have expected anything less from the demoness who controlled Dreamland for many years. You’re skilled at weaving dreams for others.”

“Thanks for your compliment.”

“What do you intend to do next?”

“Have them work overtime to bear a baby.”

“…”

“You’ll raise the child as her mother.”

“???” Tingxue couldn’t maintain her nonchalance anymore. “You intend to kill her?”

Firework coldly pointed out, “Your mission is to leave behind a successor for the Ancient Tomb Sect, so that child has to be a descendant of the Condor Heroes. The world needs a Yellow Dress Maiden who believes that she’s the descendant of the Condor Heroes, not the offspring of Yang Guo and Guo Fu.”

“I’ll raise the child, but don’t you dare kill her,” Tingxue said before taking her leave. Her cold voice carried unwavering conviction.

Firework looked at Tingxue’s departing silhouette with a smile.

… 

In order to ensure that her childbearing plan went smoothly, Firework closely supervised Zu An and Guo Fu for the next few months.

Fortunately, Guo Fu did have a constitution that was good for childbearing.

Zu An had been perplexed as to why Guo Fu and Yelu Qi had no offspring despite being married for so long, but the truth was now apparent to him. Guo Fu was in love with someone else, which had made her instinctively reject Yelu Qi.

The Yelu Clan had been on the decline, so Yelu Qi desperately needed Guo Jing and Huang Rong’s influence. Thus, he'd played along with Guo Fu and put on the appearance of a model couple before the world.

No wonder he defected to the Mongols. The situation must have been incredibly stifling to him.

After suffering a series of major setbacks, Guo Fu had grown more mature. She was even willing to lower herself before Tingxue, fearing that the latter harbored a grudge against her for all the hurt she had caused them in their earlier years that would tear them apart. 

She didn’t have her parents’ protection anymore, and she was no match for ‘Xiaolongnu’. She was well aware that if a conflict broke out between them, ‘Yang Guo’ would side with his beloved ‘Xiaolongnu’. 

So, she kept an extremely humble posture and finally convinced 'Xiaolongnu' to accept her. 

While living in the ancient tomb, Guo Fu swiftly discovered that ‘Yang Guo’ and ‘Xiaolongnu’ were nowhere as close as the world thought. More often than not, the two of them spent their time alone in their respective rooms.

Soon, she began straightening her back again, as she came to believe that she was the one whom ‘Yang Guo’ was truly in love with. She even tried picking a fight with ‘Xiaolongnu’, only for the latter to beat her up. She reverted to her humble attitude afterward. 

Nevertheless, she still felt indignant. 

I might be no match for Xiaolongnu alone, but I should be able to deal with her if I have my little sister’s help. I heard that Xiang’er became a grandmaster and founded the E’mei Sect. 

On that note, she did feel apologetic to her little sister. She knew that her little sister harbored deep feelings for Yang Guo, having devoted more than a decade of her life to scouring the world in search of Yang Guo, yet she'd slipped in and gotten together with Yang Guo before her. 

When she received Guo Fu’s letter requesting help, Firework rolled on the floor in laughter. 

But she did miss Zu An, and she'd already taken in Abbess Fengling as her disciple anyway. So, she used the pretext of looking for a remote place to train to leave E’mei Mountain and visit Mount Zhongnan’s ancient tomb. 

Guo Fu was initially overjoyed to have found an ally. She proactively tried to matchmake her little sister with ‘Yang Guo’, not knowing that the two of them were already together. 

To her dismay, her little sister was closer to ‘Xiaolongnu’ and refused to help her. To make things worse, after her little sister joined them, ‘Yang Guo’ stayed in her room less often. This filled her with regret. 

On the whole, though, the four of them lived in harmony. 

Seasons came and went. Decades passed. Guo Fu passed away after living a fulfilling life. Zu An and the others had also grown old and ailing.

Over the years, Zu An had been steadily completing his missions.

He'd found the young Yang Dingtian and imparted the Nine Heavens Palm to him.

He'd secretly taken in Cheng Kun as his disciple and imparted to him the Yiyin Finger.

He'd found a youth named Feng Qingyang in the Huashan Sect and imparted the Nine Swords of Dugu to him.

He'd also found an oppressed eunuch in the imperial palace and imparted the Sunflower Manual to him. 

He'd also secretly arranged for the old Cheng Ying to take in a disciple named Yang Xiao and impart to him Plum Blossom Island’s Divine Finger Flick. 

Meanwhile, Lu Wushuang quietly honed the Five Poison Art she'd learned from Li Mochou back then at the southern border and eventually founded the Five Poisons Sect.

… 

Everything had gone according to plan, but Zu An felt increasingly uneasy. 

He felt as if he had overlooked something, but that was impossible. The three of them had been constantly discussing their plans, and they had already considered everything. 

Shortly after, however, they received news that Isabella had died in Persia. Perhaps struck by that revelation, Firework and Tingxue passed away shortly after as well. 

Zu An sat on a chair in a courtyard, watching the setting sun. He could feel his life force slowly seeping away. He was at his limit as well. 

But why? Why can’t I see any signs of us clearing the trial even at this point?

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2812: Overthrowing Everything
chapters.body:
Chapter 2812: Overthrowing Everything

Will the trial only end when we''re all dead?

But what if the trial remains uncleared even after I close my eyes and pass away? That would spell all our deaths.

Zu An’s unease deepened at that thought. 

No, I must recall what I've overlooked. Otherwise, the decades we've spent planning everything out would have been in vain.

He thought about his encounter with the Deity of Death, as well as everything that had happened after they entered the trial. Not a single challenger had cleared this trial to date, and he didn’t think he was smarter than the powerhouses of the Myriad Worlds. 

Granted that he had a huge advantage, knowing the trial world’s background, there was no guarantee that his interpretation of the trial was accurate. 

Above all, he still had no idea how the other universal deities had tampered with this world.

Memories flashed across his head before finally settling on the duel with the Mongolian Prince Zhenjin on the mountain behind Chongyang Palace. He had finally figured out what was amiss.

The timeline is wrong!

It’s one thing for us to encounter Daoist Bai Sun and the Master of the Vajra School, but why did the Red Sun Reverend appear in this world as well? Not only is he not a character in Jin Yong’s wuxiaverse, but the timeline doesn’t fit as well.

The Red Sun Reverend is a powerhouse who appeared in the final years of the Yuan Dynasty, so how could he have been Prince Zhenjin’s vassal? Heck, the Yuan Dynasty wasn't even formed then!

There’s also that white-haired man with the flute and that seductive woman. They should be ‘White Hair’ and ‘Ravishing Beauty’ from Huang Yi’s novel. Those are characters who appear in the final years of the Yuan Dynasty as well!

Their appearance here suggests that this world’s timeline has been plunged into chaos. 

My solution assumed that this world would follow the normal timeline. If the other universal deities hadn’t tampered with the trial world, it would have been the right answer. But if other universal deities have truly tampered with things here, it won’t be surprising if we fail this trial. 

That must have been that being’s doing—the Deity of Chaos and Enigma Mi Li!

She must have tampered with this trial world because she doesn’t want someone to inherit Death’s legacy. She did it so discreetly that there would be no way for one to realize that something was amiss unless one was familiar with its background.

The Deity of Annihilation probably tampered with the Mongols. Their act of killing and plundering is perfectly aligned with the deity's beliefs. With the Mongols strengthened by Annihilation's divine power, Zhang Wuji and Zhu Yuanzhang won’t be able to overthrow the Yuan Dynasty like they did in ‘Heavenly Sword and Dragon Slaying Saber’.

I wonder how the other universal deities tampered with this trial world…

It’s a pity I discovered it too late. I’m already on my last legs. What else can I do?

Zu An’s eyes, which had grown turbid with age, suddenly regained their clarity. 

No, there’s still time. I can still do something about it. I have the perfect tool for that. I am the envoy of not just the Deity of Chaos and Enigma, but the Deity of Memory as well! 

Since the other universal deities were able to tamper with this trial world, it stands to reason that I can tap into their powers here as well.

Along with this realization, a mysterious surge of power flowed into Zu An’s body, temporarily delaying his death. He took out the Spring Autumn Brush and the Time Annal.

Since this world is already in chaos, I might as well stir the pot more!

He activated his divine sense, and the Brush of Spring and Autumn began furiously scribbling in the Time Annal. 

“This is a world of distorted spacetime, where the Mongols, the Jin, the Qing, the Liao, Western Xia, Southern Song, Dali, the Tibetans, the Oirats, Tshongkha, the Karakhanids, the Persian Ming Cult, Goguryeo, Fusang… all simultaneously exist.

“It is primarily based on Jin Yong’s 14 novels…

“Ouch! It hurts terribly! Song Qingshu slowly roused from his sleep, feeling as if all his bones had shattered…

“In the end, Song Qingshu united all factions together to defeat the Mongols and rebuild his homeland…”

“What should I call it? Under the Madam's… No, that won’t do. I’ll call it The Master Player instead!”[1]

This book’s content is even more chaotic than this trial world. I shall offer this as tribute to the great Deity of Chaos and Enigma.

This book records everything that happens here. I shall offer this as tribute to the great Deity of Memory.

This book stirs the desires of those who read it. I shall offer this as tribute to the great Deity of Bliss and Desire. 

This book encourages a proclivity for reproduction. I shall offer this as tribute to the great Deity of Reproduction.

This book quells chaos to re-establish order. I shall offer this as tribute to the great Deity of Order.

This book records the annihilation of countless nations. I shall offer this as tribute to the great Deity of Annihilation.

This book describes beauty beyond measure. I shall offer this as tribute to the great Deity of Love and Beauty. 

This book’s emperor displays a tenacious desire for longevity. I shall offer this as tribute to the great Deity of Longevity. 

This book contains ingenious schemes and politicking. I shall offer this as tribute to the great Deity of Knowledge.

This book’s protagonist resolutely protects his loved ones and the Divine Continent. I shall offer this as tribute to the great Deity of Protection.

Last but not least, I shall offer the countless souls who have died in war in this book to the great Deity of Death!

…

Zu An had no idea which universal deities had participated in the crusade against the Deity of Death, so he showed his respect to all of them except for the Deity of Nebula. Considering how they had little interest in everything, it was unlikely that the Deity of Nebula would participate in the crusade against the Deity of Death anyway.

There’s nothing I can offer that would interest the Deity of Nebula anyway.

Several surges of divine light flowed into Zu An’s body. Everything began to collapse until the trial world was reduced to a single white dot.

Zu An found himself standing in the middle of a monotonously white world. His previously aged body had reverted to its youthful form, and he'd regained his supernatural powers as well. 

The skull stairway appeared before his eyes once more.

“Come up!” a divine voice boomed. 

Zu An slowly climbed the stairway a step at a time. He soon reached the end and was met with a pair of gigantic red eyes. 

“You’re fated with death. I didn’t think those fellows would tamper with my trial. That explains why no one has been able to clear my trial in such a long time.” The Deity of Death was both infuriated and heartened. “Good. You were born to be the Deity of Death. Are you willing to inherit Death’s authority?”

“Where are my companions?” Zu An asked.

“They’re dead. They didn’t clear the trial.”

Zu An narrowed his eyes. “That’s not true. They played a crucial role in the completion of the trial. I couldn’t have cleared this trial without their assistance. Going by the rules you set, they should be qualified to inherit Death’s authority as well.”

The Deity of Death coldly replied, “Only one person can inherit Death’s authority.”

“Do you truly believe I’d be able to inherit Death’s authority?”

“What do you mean?”

“The universal deities tampered with your trial because they don’t want to see a new Deity of Death. I might have cleared your trial, but my actions must have drawn their attention. I wouldn’t be surprised if some of them are listening in on our conversation.”

“…” A long while later, the Deity of Death said with a resigned tone, “I have lost too much of my powers to tell if they are watching us or not, but your words make sense.”

Zu An finally continued, “Entrusting Death’s authority to a single person is no different from a child carrying gold into a busy market—it’ll only lead to death. You might as well split Death’s authority into multiple pieces and entrust them to those who have contributed to the clearance of the trial.

“That would allow Death’s authority to be passed down. At the same time, the other universal deities would view the situation more favorably, as it would ensure that Death’s authority remained under their surveillance, since my companions are worshipers of other universal deities.”

The Deity of Death was silent for a long while. “This means Death’s authority might never become whole ever again. There might never be another Deity of Death.”

“At least hope will be preserved.” Zu An smiled. “I don’t think you have the strength to protect me from the other universal deities in your current state.”

1. This is a webnovel on Qidian. Its original name was Under the Madam's Skirt, then it was later renamed to The Master Player. ☜

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2813: The Universal Deities’ Reactions
chapters.body:
Chapter 2813: The Universal Deities’ Reactions

The gigantic red eyes flickered with hesitation. It took some time before the Deity of Death finally made up his mind. “You must truly love those women.”

“Why do you say that?”

“No one else in the Myriad Worlds could reject the allure of a universal deity’s authority and instead request that it be split into pieces just to save a few women,” the Deity of Death replied with a sigh.

“This has nothing to do with love. I promised them that we'd overcome the trial together, and they put their utmost trust in me. I don’t want to break my promise,” Zu An replied calmly. 

Of course, the threat posed by other universal deities also played a huge part, or else he couldn’t have withstood the temptation so easily. 

“Forget it. You’re the only promising candidate I've met in so many years. Maybe you’re right,” the Deity of Death said. The gigantic red eyes split into four rays of white light, with the biggest ray flowing into Zu An’s body. 

Zu An’s body shuddered. He was gripped by an unprecedented sensation that was far more profound than what he'd felt when he obtained the Cultivation World’s World Law Beacon. It felt as if he had the Myriad Worlds in his grasp, but this authority was obscured by a veil. 

It must be because my piece of Death’s authority is incomplete. 

The remaining three rays of white light descended to other areas. Moments later, Firework, Tingxue, and Isabella appeared before his eyes. They experienced similar sensations as well. 

Firework stared intently at Zu An with a furiously beating heart that refused to calm down. After everything she'd been through, she had lost all her trust in others . It was only because she knew that it was nigh impossible for her to survive the Deity of Death’s trial, and that Zu An had proven himself to be trustworthy so far, that she'd chosen to make an exception and trust him this once.

That said, she'd already been prepared to be backstabbed once more. Zu An had promised to save all of them, but she was skeptical about it. 

She had gained some grasp of Zu An’s character by this point. If there were no competing interests at stake, Zu An could still choose to save all of them. But the same couldn't be said if there were competing interests.

For instance, if only one of them could survive this trial, she hadn't thought Zu An would give up his life to save the others. 

Little had she expected Zu An to go this far just to score some chicks! He'd actually given up Death’s authority! 

Other powerhouses wouldn’t have hesitated to massacre entire universes to obtain Death’s authority, but he'd chosen to share it with them!

Tingxue had a complicated expression as well. She'd already returned to being a Nebula envoy and had regained her usual impassiveness, but this was a universal deity’s authority they were talking about here…

That man fulfilled his promise to save all of us and even gave something this precious to me. 

She recalled how they had lived together as a couple for the past few decades, and that left ripples in her heart, which should have long become still like an old well. 

Isabella was nowhere as subtle as Tingxue. She excitedly dove over to embrace Zu An. “Big brother Zu, I knew you’d succeed!”

Zu An dotingly patted her head. “It's been tough on you, having to stay alone in Persia all those years.”

“It was lonely, but thinking about you gave me strength.” Isabella hugged Zu An’s arm while radiating a blissful smile. She didn't mention Death’s authority, but there was no way the Acadias’ big miss wouldn’t know about its value.

Suddenly, the world shook. The winding skull staircase began to crumble. The skulls shrieked miserably as they desperately tried to reassemble the staircase, but they still dropped to the ground and shattered into dust.

The gigantic red eyes in the sky vanished. The Deity of Death’s aura could no longer be felt. Everyone present understood that the Deity of Death was gone for good. 

The group was transported back to the forbidden land’s earth vein. 

Zu An was about to speak when a divine light suddenly flashed, and he found himself transported into a special space. Sensing a familiar aura, he hurriedly bowed. “Paying respect to the exalted Deity of Chaos and Enigma.”

Countless stars in the sky twinkled as if depicting the Deity of Chaos and Enigma’s expression. “You have done well this time. Your book, ‘The Master Player’, has been written to my liking. The world setting and timeline are beautifully chaotic. But don’t you think you’re being too greedy, trying to please all the universal deities?”

Zu An’s heart skipped a beat. He hurriedly said, “I’m overwhelmed by your magnanimity in protecting someone as humble as me. I’d have been reduced to nothing otherwise.”

“You need not put yourself down. Your unique take has pleased quite a few of them. At least the Deity of Bliss and Desire and Deity of Reproduction are happy with you. However, if the Deity of Love and Beauty were still around, they would have smashed your head in. You made so many beautiful women follow the same man.”

Zu An smiled sheepishly in response. Looks like I buttered her up the wrong way. 

“The others can’t be bothered. How can the shallow schemes of your book’s characters catch the interest of the Deity of Knowledge? It’s the same for the others, too.”

“What about the Deity of Annihilation?” Zu An asked. The Deity of Annihilation had supported the Mongols in this trial world, but Zu An had written the Mongols as antagonists who were defeated in the end. He'd offered the demise of countless countries as tribute to the other party, but that wouldn't necessarily be enough to placate them. 

“They were  infuriated, of course. They want to destroy you. Don’t worry, I stopped them.” The Deity of Chaos and Enigma suddenly changed her tone and asked, “But what surprised me is that Meng actually stood up for you as well. Can you tell me why?”

The question was asked in a nonchalant tone, but it made Zu An sweat profusely. 

“I beg your pardon, exalted Deity of Chaos and Enigma. The Deity of Memory summoned me and conferred me as her envoy. I didn’t dare reject her.” Zu An didn’t hide anything, knowing that the other party could uncover the truth if she investigated it. It would be wiser to be honest to show his loyalty.

“Interesting. I’d have never expected a Time Scribe to become a Mirror Scribe as well.” The stars in the sky twinkled, as if reflecting Mi Li’s good mood, but she proceeded to calmly drop a dangerous question right after. “Who do you prefer between the two of us?”

Zu An instinctively sought to flatter Mi Li, but a thought popped into his mind, and he quickly changed his words. “You are both exalted entities whom I admire greatly, but a humble ant like me truly dares not comment about the universal deities.”

“You are a cunning one. Now that you possess half of Death’s authority, you aren’t an ant anymore.”

“I am first and foremost your envoy.”

“Hahaha! You are a Chaos and Enigma envoy, but you are also a Memory envoy. On top of that, you possess Death’s authority. What remarkable chaos this is. You’re indeed suited for my path. Fine, I shan’t make things hard for you.”

The countless stars in the sky vanished. Mi Li had left.

Zu An barely managed to breathe a sigh of relief when his surroundings morphed once more. This time, he was transported into a peculiar space filled with waterfalls of numbers. Taking a closer look, the flowing numbers were unusual, resembling a combination of 0s and 1s, yet not exactly so either.

It was the Deity of Memory!

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2814: A New Skill
chapters.body:
Chapter 2814: A New Skill

“Paying respects to the exalted Deity of Memory!” Zu An was starting to get used to this.

The surrounding numbers flickered as if assessing Zu An. “Why didn’t you say that you liked Mi Li more earlier?”

Cold sweat rained down Zu An’s back. She was listening in. He quickly replied, “I am the envoy of not just the exalted Deity of Chaos and Enigma, but the Deity of Memory as well.”

“I see. Then why don’t you like me more?”

Zu An sweated profusely. These two universal deities are… He quickly reined himself in and replied, “It’s the utmost honor of a humble ant like me to receive the grace of a universal deity. I’d be stepping out of line to dare evaluate supreme beings of your stature.”

“Mi Li is right. You’re a cunning one.” The numbers flickered. “You are the envoy of two universal deities and the wielder of half of Death’s authority. You aren’t an ant anymore.”

Zu An carefully replied, “Exalted universal deity, please be assured that I have no intention of resurrecting Death. That is why I opted to divide its authority.”

You must be kidding me! Even the Deity of Death was killed by the universal deities. I'm nothing in comparison!

“You don’t need to be nervous. If not for our tacit approval, you couldn’t have retained half of Death’s authority,” the Deity of Memory replied. 

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. “Allow me to express my utmost gratitude to you for saving me from the Deity of Annihilation.”

“I protect my worshipers. You have done well, recording so many important events. I am very satisfied.” The Deity of Memory was in a good mood.

Zu An took the chance to ask, “May I ask how I should deal with Death’s authority? I don’t feel any different from before.”

“Possessing half of a universal deity’s authority elevates your stature. Just as there are countless civilizations in the Myriad Worlds, there are countless power level systems as well. Those power level systems mark a difference in quantity, such as the difference between 100 and 1. There is no fundamental change in its meaning. A change in stature, however, can give rise to a fundamental change from 0 to 1.

“It is extremely difficult for those of a lower stature to hurt those of a higher stature, and you now possess the highest stature in the Myriad Worlds. You might have only inherited half of Death’s authority, but you’ll still benefit greatly from it,” the Deity of Memory patiently explained.

“Thank you for your guidance, exalted universal deity!” 

Zu An had gained some insight into power and laws when he obtained the Cultivation World’s World Law Beacon and became its god. Cultivators were divided into tiers, ranging from first rank to earth immortal. But to a higher being, there was no fundamental difference among those cultivation levels.

The concept of stature was likely related to one’s ability to control, alter, and even create laws. No matter how powerful a cultivator was, they were still reliant on the energies around them and had to obey the laws of physics in each world. A higher entity could render a powerful cultivator helpless just by altering a law.

The stream of numbers suddenly flickered, and a book fell from the sky and floated into Zu An’s hands. The Deity of Memory said, “That trial world was created by the Deity of Death before his demise. He left a secret manual for his successor. It should have been impossible for anyone to clear the trial, and I also withheld the manual.

“However, I have browsed the manual; it has nothing to do with Death’s path. He must have had a plan in mind, but now that he has completely fallen, it’s no longer worth dwelling on that. I’ll give you the manual.”

But Zu An didn’t dare take it. “I dare not casually browse through something left by the Deity of Death.”

The numbers in the surroundings flickered. “There’s nothing for you to fear. The Deity of Annihilation was the one who wanted to bring down the Deity of Death. The rest of us had only just attained our deityhood and still harbored fear toward Death, so we tacitly permitted and even aided Annihilation. We are not the same entities we were back then. Death can no longer faze us. Of course, I wouldn’t have handed this manual to you if you weren’t my worshiper.”

Zu An quickly understood what was going on.

The other universal deities no longer feared Death, but they still wouldn’t permit the rise of another Deity of Death. Had it been anyone else, they would have never been permitted to clear the trial, or inherit the Deity of Death’s legacy. 

Thanks to him being fortunate enough to become both Memory and Chaos and Enigma’s envoy, Zu An was the only exception. At the same time, the others who participated in the trial were highly regarded worshipers of the other paths. That was why the universal deities had let them off. 

Zu An took the manual and saw three words written on its cover—Song of Righteousness. He was taken aback.

“Yes, it’s the one you’re thinking about. Following Southern Song’s collapse, Prime Minister Wen Tianxiang rejected the Yuan Mongols’ invitation and wrote the Song of Righteousness. Despite being a mortal, Wen Tianxiang’s Song of Righteousness resonated with the world. Even I have a high opinion of it. The current Song of Righteousness is elevated from its previous state, and it’s compatible with you.”

“Compatible with me?” Zu An was confused.

“I sense that you have an ability resembling Soulspeak, but using it incurs a hefty price, so you can’t use it under normal circumstances. The Song of Righteousness nurtures a righteous energy, which can be used to offset the cost of your ability. Now that you possess half of Death’s authority, you should be able to use it.”

Zu An was overjoyed to hear that. “Thank you for your bestowment, exalted Deity of Memory!”

This was indeed invaluable to him.

“Allright. Remember to record more important events like this…” 

Hearing that the Deity of Memory was about to leave, Zu An hurriedly asked, “Exalted Deity of Memory, may I know how I can travel through spacetime to reunite with someone whom I have lost touch with?”

He'd visited Dreamland in search of a solution, but Dreamland turned out to be a sham. That left him at a loss. But thinking about it, there was no one better to pose this question to than the universal deities of the Myriad Worlds.

The stream of numbers froze. “Who do you want to meet?”

“An old friend.”

“A lover, I see.”

Zu An was speechless. I forgot she can read my thoughts.

“Why didn’t you ask Mi Li?”

Zu An dared not to speak ill of the Deity of Chaos and Enigma, so he replied, “You are the exalted Deity of Memory. You must possess all kinds of records about the Myriad World.” 

“There’s no use bootlicking me. I do know the answer, but I am not obliged to answer your questions. It makes me wonder who is the universal deity here.”

Cold sweat rained down from Zu An. “Exalted Deity of Memory, please pardon my brashness.”

“I’ll tell you how you can find the person you want to meet when you find something better to offer to me.” With that, the stream of numbers vanished, and Zu An returned to the forbidden land’s earth vein. 

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2815: Love Sage
chapters.body:
Chapter 2815: Love Sage

Zu An wiped off his cold sweat. The universal deities sure are capricious. He quickly put a halt to those thoughts lest those entities overhear them.

He flipped open the Song of Righteousness. 

“In Heaven and Earth there exists righteous energy, pervasive in all forms and things. Below, it manifests as rivers and mountains; above, as the sun and stars. Within mankind, it is called the noble spirit, vast and mighty, filling the universe. When the imperial path is pure and peaceful, it harmonizes all in the imperial court…” 

Zu An heard a voice echoing in his ears, bringing him enlightenment. Righteous energy began to sprout within him. 

New secret manual detected: Song of Righteousness. Do you want to absorb it?

Zu An was overjoyed to hear the long-awaited voice. According to prior experience, the Keyboard System absorbing the manual wouldn’t cause the latter to lose its original function, so he chose to absorb it.

The Song of Righteousness transformed into a stream of light and embedded itself onto the Keyboard’s F9 key.

New secret manual collection. New function unlocked: Title System.

The system will select the most suitable title for you based on your performance in major events.

In this dungeon, you have staked everything to protect your female friends, going to the extent of relinquishing the indescribably invaluable authority of Death to rescue them. The extent you’re willing to go to score a girl is no different from a Love Sage.

Love Sage: You can more easily earn a woman’s goodwill. Damage inflicted on women is reduced by 50%. Damage received from women is reduced by 70%.

Zu An was initially overjoyed to have unlocked a new keyboard skill, only to be flabbergasted by the title he obtained. What kind of title is that?! More easily earn a woman’s goodwill? How is that even measured?

At least there are numbers on the damage reduction, but that only means I’ll deal half as much damage to women in the future. As for being hurt by women… 

Zu An suddenly thought about the Heiress’ Ball of Delight, which made him immune to damage from women richer than him. This 70% damage reduction appeared humble in comparison, but if used well, it could prove to be an invaluable asset.

Will the damage reduction apply to the bedroom as well, though? That would be a gigantic nerf for me!

“Big brother Zu? Big brother Zu?” Isabella called out.

Zu An snapped out of his daze and noticed the three women staring at him with weird eyes. 

“Big brother Zu, you were standing in a daze earlier, smiling to yourself. You mumbled something about a nerf. What do you mean by that?” Isabella’s clear eyes were brimming with overflowing curiosity. 

Faced with such clear eyes, Zu An’s face reddened. “It’s nothing…”

Firework clicked her tongue. “Isn’t it just being summoned by a universal deity? There’s nothing to hide here.”

The flow of time inside a universal deity’s domain was different from that of the real world. During Zu An’s conversations with the Deity of Chaos and Enigma and the Deity of Memory, an instant had only passed in the real world.

Zu An smiled. “I was indeed summoned by a universal deity…”

“You’re amazing, big brother Zu. You were summoned by a universal deity multiple times within such a short span!” Isabella’s voice was filled with admiration. The people of the Myriad Worlds viewed it as an honor to be summoned by a universal deity. It symbolized greater strength and potential.

“You have a portion of a universal deity’s authority inside you now. That lad must be out of his mind to share something like that,” Firework grumbled, though her twinkling eyes reflected her excitement.

“I don’t know how I can repay you, big brother Zu…”

“Stop. Are you going to betroth yourself to him next?” Firework interjected.

“Don’t talk nonsense!” Isabella stomped her feet. She looked adorable.

Zu An smiled. “We've braved life and death situations together now. Saying such words only makes us look distant.”

Isabella nodded. She bit her lip, not knowing what to say. 

“Big brother Zu, why don’t you follow me back to the Universal Conglomerate? I remember you were looking for something. We have branches all over the Myriad Worlds. I’m sure we can help you find what you’re searching for.” Isabella looked at Zu An with expectant eyes before adding, “Big sister Firework and big sister Tingxue, you should come along as well. I’ll make sure to host you all well.”

Firework sneered, “Tsk, your invitation is obviously only directed at that fellow. We’re just add-ons. I’m not interested.”

Isabella felt indignant. “Big sister Firework, I have no such intention.”

Tingxue replied, “I’ll pass. There’s something I need to investigate.”

Zu An was surprised. It was no surprise for a Nebula envoy like Tingxue to be uninterested in the Universal Conglomerate, but to think she would be so concerned about a certain matter.

Just then, Firework suddenly spoke up. “It’s getting late. Why don’t you rest in my territory first? Dreamland’s accommodations are quite well-known across the Myriad Worlds.”

The others looked at Firework with weird eyes, knowing that Dreamland was a dead city, with practically its entire populace being her clones. 

Firework harrumphed. “Relax. I’m in a good mood after finally being freed. I no longer need their souls. I’ll return them.”

“Didn’t you say they were dead?” Zu An was surprised. 

“You believed that? It was merely part of my background setting as Dreamland’s big miss,” Firework replied with a gleeful grin. “I merely extracted their souls and infused mine into their bodies. That’s much easier than creating them from scratch.”

Zu An breathed a sigh of relief. Despite his intimate relationship with Firework, he'd still been jarred by her lack of hesitation to massacre entire worlds. Her willingness to spare Dreamland’s people is a good start. I need to advise her more.

“Big sister Firework, can you free Uncle Alfred?” Isabella looked at Firework with teary eyes.

“Don’t worry, he’s alive. I have no interest in such old souls.”

“You’re the best, big sister Firework!” Isabella was glad that Zu An had dealt with Firework, or else she had no idea how she could deal with such a terrifying enemy. 

Firework casually waved her hand and pulled out countless cocoons from the darkness using silk threads. These cocoons contained the guests who had been on board the frigate.

The guests remained on edge even after they were freed.

“Where’s that demon spider?”

“Did big miss Isabella save us?”

“We have to leave this place right away. We’ll be dead meat if that demon spider finds us.”

… 

Those people looked as if they were scared out of their wits. 

Isabella noticed Firework’s twitching eyelids and quickly spoke up. “It’s big sister Firework, big brother Zu, and big sister Tingxue who saved us all. You have nothing to worry about anymore. That demon… Cough cough, I mean, we’re out of danger now.”

Isabella had noticed that Firework had conjured Yani and took it as a sign that she wasn't planning to reveal her true identity to the guests, so she played along with that. 

The guests thanked Firework, but they still left in a hurry as they couldn’t shake off their fear. 

The frigate they'd traveled on had been destroyed, so Isabella pulled out a tiny ship model from her bag and tossed it into the water. It swiftly expanded into a gigantic ship. 

Everyone boarded the ship and embarked on their journey back to Dreamland. 

While the guests remained fearful for their lives, Zu An began conversing with a beautiful woman wearing a red dress. It was Mi Li, who had been absent for a long time. 

“You've been gone for so long that I thought you were dead.”

“It’s a good thing you didn’t ask them for the materials to reforge my body, or else I might have really died.”

Translator's Thoughts

chapters.title: Chapter 2816: Turning Tables
chapters.body:
Chapter 2816: Turning Tables

“I’m not a fool. I've noticed how you disappear each time a universal deity appears. There’s no way I’d talk about you in front of them.” Zu An looked at Mi Li with confusion. “Are you on bad terms with the universal deities? Otherwise, why are you afraid of catching their notice?”

Mi Li harrumphed. “How can I defy the universal deities? I just wanted to avoid trouble.”

“Considering how they can read my mind and memories, I’m surprised that the universal deities never noticed you or the Keyboard System.”

“You have to thank that Keyboard System of yours. It partially shielded you from the universal deities’ probing, allowing you to retain some secrets before them.”

Zu An was surprised. The Keyboard System can even fool universal deities? I wonder who created it.

“Where did you hide in the Deity of Death’s trial world? I was worried that you’d get killed by the laws.” 

“I was hiding in the Tai’e Sword. Many universal deities were observing that trial world, so even the Keyboard System dared not make an appearance. Otherwise, they might have detected its existence while collecting Rage points. Rage points are soul pulsations, after all.”

Zu An narrowed his eyes. “You’re very familiar with the Keyboard System.”

“Of course. I'm contracted with you.” Mi Li yawned. “You’re smart to have divided Death’s authority into multiple parts. Otherwise, the other universal deities would have killed you, even if you had the backing of Chaos and Enigma, as well as Memory.”

“Come to think of it, I'm quite fated with Death.” Zu An thought about how he had become the master of the Netherworld across different timelines. “Now that Death’s authority is fragmented, does it mean there will never be a new Deity of Death?”

“Once you grow strong enough, you can recollect the other pieces of Death’s authority and become the new Deity of Death,” Mi Li replied with a smile. “It just so happens that the other three pieces are with those women, and they're on good terms with you. On that note, I can’t believe you didn’t score Tingxue despite spending so much time with her in the trial world. The Keyboard System must be blind to label you ‘Love Sage’.”

Zu An was speechless. He hadn’t expected Mi Li to even know about his title. He really had no secrets in front of her. 

“Tingxue appears to lack emotions, but she has a strong will. We might be on good terms, but we’re lacking something. It feels like our relationship may unravel if I take a step forward.”

“Tsk. You were just too busy indulging in Firework and Guo Fu’s bodies. That Guo Fu looked a lot like Huang Rong when she was younger. No wonder you worked so hard plowing her.”

“…”

“There’s Isabella as well. It can’t be more obvious that she has maxed out goodwill for you. You can practically see hearts in her eyes. Yet you didn’t take the opportunity to push her down.”

Zu An was annoyed. “It was already a struggle to find a way out of that godforsaken trial. I didn’t have the energy to be thinking about all these things.”

“Was it?” Mi Li looked at him with an ambiguous smile, her feet shaking provocatively. It was so irritating that Zu An wanted to pull her feet down. “Back to the topic. Instead of recombining Death’s authority, you can also use your half of Death’s authority to forge your own path. You might just become a new universal deity.”

Zu An was surprised. “I can become a new universal deity that isn’t the Deity of Death?”

“Of course. Each authority symbolizes a divine jurisdiction. It’s like a ticket to the dining table, though in your current state, you’re only qualified to stand next to the table. Whether you can secure a seat and which seat you’ll get depends on how you grow in the future,” Mi Li replied. 

Zu An fell silent. He began pondering his path for the first time. Are there still incomplete paths in the Myriad Worlds that no universal deities have reached the end of?

Mi Li suddenly yawned. “That’s all for now. Your little girlfriend is here. I’ll go back to sleep, lest I hear annoying clapping sounds.”

She vanished into thin air. 

Right after, someone knocked on the door before gently pushing it open. A woman with voluptuous legs strutted in. “Sir, I’m here to thank you for saving my life.”

Zu An’s jaws dropped. The one who had entered the room wasn’t Firework but Dreamland’s guard captain, Yani. He still remembered how Yani had valiantly struck down a Death envoy and blasted a hole in the sky. 

However, he quickly snapped out of his daze and asked, “Firework, what are you doing?”

Yani was confused. “Sir, what are you saying? I’m Yani.”

“Stop playing.” Zu An harrumphed.

Yani chuckled. “Tsk, how boring. Do you not like me in this form? I guess you prefer smaller girls. Lolicon!”

“That’s slander!” Zu An was irritated. “What are you up to?”

Yani casually sat down beside him and wrapped her hand around his neck. “I wasn’t able to show you the real me because my supernatural powers were sealed in the trial world. Now that I've regained my powers, it’s only right for me to let you have a taste of it.”

Zu An was speechless.

Before he could process what was going on, Yani grabbed his shoulder and pushed him down onto the bed. She towered over him, her beautiful face exuding valiance and charisma. Her shirt button began to groan due to her movements, and it looked as if it could snap at any moment. “Do you prefer the coy Firework, or the valiant Yani?”

“Aren’t they both you?” Zu An found this situation utterly ridiculous. Those two women felt completely unrelated to each other, yet he knew that they were both the same person. 

“If you like Yani more, I’ll accompany you more as her in the future,” Yani whispered into his ear, teasing him. 

Zu An was mesmerized. Unlike the bubbly Firework, Yani resembled a general who had conquered the battlefield. Her disposition, the sensations from her body, and even her fragrance felt completely different.

He felt an urge to protect Firework, but when it came to Yani, he felt a desire to conquer her.

“I can feel it. You’re moved.” Yani looked at him with a mischievous grin.

Zu An flipped over and pinned her beneath him. “You aren’t going to be on top of me.”

“Hmph. The trial world is one thing, but do you think you can defeat me here?” Yani’s eyes gleamed with pride. She wrapped her legs tightly around Zu An and flipped him over, pinning him down beneath her. 

“???”

“…”

Fighting sounds filled the room as they competed for supremacy. 

Some time later, Yani suddenly exclaimed, “Hold on! How did you suddenly become so strong?”

Zu An looked down on Yani from above in glee. His Love Sage title surprisingly came in handy here—reduces 70% damage from women! Furthermore, Yani had held back to avoid hurting him, so that gave him a chance to stage a comeback. 

Translator's Thoughts
